narrative_en: Please, my son Kwame was involved in a car accident here in Damongo this afternoon. He was crossing the road near the market when a pragyia hit him and he fell down. His leg is paining him and we can see some cuts and scratches on it, but he is able to move it small so we don't think it is broken. Baffour is my name, and I just want someone to advise me on what to do for him now.
narrative_ewe: Meɖekuku, nɔvinyɔnu, ViŋɔNye Kwame wò kpe accident ɖe Damongo afisia egbea. Eƒu ʋɔ xoxo kple avenɔƒe la nɔƒe ɖe, pragyia ɖe ɖo eŋu eye ewu ƒu. Afɔe le eyi nam, eye míakpɔ aɣɔtsitsi kple nyɔdzidzoe le afɔ ma la dzi, gake etɔ ŋu gbugbɔ le afɔ la ŋu kpɔ, nenye ɣleti vɔ o. Ŋkɔnyɔnu nye Baffour. Megblɔ nám, ate ŋu wɔ nɛ dɛ fifia abe?
narrative_ga: Ofainɛ, dɛbi bi Kwame — accident ehi eŋ Damongo afɛɛ yanɔ. Ɔhɛ kpee ni le market kɔŋ ashi, pragyia bi ebo eŋ, ebumo fɛɛ. Gboo ehii eŋ baŋ, miihu cuts ni scratches ni le gboo eŋ ashi — ŋmɛɛ, ɔtumi move gboo eŋ kakra, yɛ naahɛ ŋ fracture o. Baffour — yɛɛ mi ŋmɛ baŋ. Chale, mibɔ oniŋ ni boa mi — hɛ mido hiɔ eŋ ashi ni?
narrative_dagbani: N daa, m bia Kwame, o kpɛŋ accident Damongo ha, nyɛnni yi. O bee nyɛŋ tarigi market niri, ka pragyia di o, ka o gbini. O tiŋ yɛligu o daa, ka ti ŋmɛ n'o tiŋ zuɣu sunsuŋ ayi bi, ama o bee tiŋ saɣim biɛla, a n'ti mi o tiŋ kpaa. M yuli la Baffour, ka m bori la ŋun be o yɛltɔɣa maa kuri maa ni o maa ti mala lali.
narrative_fante: Me pa wo kyew, me ba Kwame na accident bi baa no ha wɔ Damongo afternoon yi. Ɔretwam ɔkwan no ho kɔ market no nkyɛn no, pragyia bi bɔɔ no na ɔtuu fam. Ɔnan yɛ no yaw na yɛhunu cuts ne scratches bi wɔ so, nanso ɔtumi yi no kɔ so kakra, enti yɛnnwen sɛ ɛbrokɔ. Me din de Baffour, na mepɛ sɛ obi kyerɛ me sɛ menyɛ deɛn ama no seesei.
narrative_gurene: N tɩɩma, m bia Kwame, o nyɛ accident Damongo ka, yɛlim buɣim la. O la bee nyɛŋ zug sanga kɔ ayi market zeŋ, ka pragyia pɛ o, ka o bɔɔrɩ fam. O nʋɔrɩ yɛligu o la, ka tɩ nyɛ bɔbɔ yɛsɔr sɔŋa o nʋɔrɩ zug, ama o tara a nʋɔrɩ zɔa bilbiisi, ka tɩ nan nyɛ o nʋɔrɩ ka kpɔ. M yʋʋrɩ la Baffour, ka m bɛ yɛl saka nɩ o ninsaal tara m sɔŋɔ ŋwana m tol o ka yɛlim.

narrative_en: "Please, I want to report something that happened here in Tamale central. My neighbor's boy, Kwame, he was in a trotro and another vehicle hit it from the side — not too serious, thank God, but he hit his neck and head on the ground when he fell. He's a young boy, maybe around seventeen or so. Dela is my name, I'm the one calling. The boy is sitting down now, he's conscious but he's saying his neck is paining him small."
narrative_ewe: Meɖekuku, meɖi gblɔ wo se nudzadzra si wòwɔ afisia Tamale central. Ɖevi si le xɔla ŋuti, Kwame, ele trotro dzi, eye akɔ bubu wo ɖo e ŋɔ le ɣeyiɣi me — magblɔ be enyo egbe o, eye akpe na Mawu, gake ewu ƒu, eye ɖo kple ta wo ɖo ƒu anyigba dzi. Ɖevi ƒe ɣeyiɣi le esiaɖeka ƒe ɣeyiɣi gbɔ. Dela wɔe nye ŋkɔnyé, meyɔna ŋutɔ. Ɖevi la ɖo eme fifia, eɖo edzi, gake egblɔ be ɖo yi eyi nam dogo dogo.
narrative_ga: Ofainɛ, mɛ pɛ shɛ mɛ bɔ report bii shi nɔ happen ni Tamale Central. Emi agbo dɛbi, Kwame, ono le trotro mu — kaa bii fɛɛ ni le nɔŋ tsɔɔ ŋ from the side — enye too serious o, Nyɔŋmɔ shukru, kɛ wɔ obumo shi kpee nɔŋ, ɔnɛk kɛ ŋmɛi fɛɛ ni hit ground. Oblɛ bii — maybe around seventeen so. Dela yɛ mi din — mɛɛ ni frɛ shɛ. Dɛbi nɔŋ chɔkɔ sɔ adɛɛ, oni le, kɛ ɔtɛ shɛ ɔnɛk ehii ŋ small.
narrative_dagbani: N daa, n bɔri kɔ n yɛl' bia doŋ din kɔŋ ka sɔŋ Tamale central. N yaba biɛla, Kwame, o nini trotro biɛla doŋ ka kaɣa ŋun kuli nin duli o ka o gbaŋ o ni — ka ka yɛlgu tiŋ daan, Naawuni sɔŋ o, ama o ŋun ni o gbuni ka o gbini ka o duli zuu. O biɛla daa, gaaribiga, o ku maaŋ sheli ayɔpɔin pia. Dela n yuɣu, maa frɛ ŋɔ. Biɛla o chɔɣi daa ka o nyɛ maa ŋɔ, o ti soli, ama o yɛra o zuu ŋun ni o yɛligu o ka o yɛra o ka ka daan tɔɣiŋ.
narrative_fante: Me pa wo kyew, mepɛ se meka biribi a esii ha Tamale Central. Me hɔ baakofo ba, Kwame, ɔtene trotro mu, na kar fofor bi bɔɔ no fã — ɛnyɛ den paa, Awurade ase, nanso ɔtuu fam a, ɔbɔɔ ne kɔn ne ne tiri fam. Ɔyɛ abofra, ne mfeɛ bɛyɛ du nson anaa saa. Me din de Dela — mene onipa a efrɛ no. Abofra no tene ase seesei, ɔhunu ne ho, nanso ɔkeka se ne kɔn yɛ no yaw kakra.
narrative_gurene: N tɩɩma, m bɔrɩ a koe nɩ accident bi nɩ n zɔŋ Tamale central. M yɛɣa bia, Kwame, o nɩ trotro pʋɔ la, kaɣa ŋɔ tɩtɩ naa bʋɔrɩ o la dɔɔ fɩela — ka o yɛligu dee, Yinɛ n tɩ, la o kpɛŋ fam la, o tuŋ nɛ o ŋun bɔɔrɩ fam. O bia yuu, o nɛ ayɩla-nɩ-yɩɩbu nɩ sɔɔ la. Dela la m yuori, mam la bee o n bɔrɩ fo. Bia nɩ zɩŋɩ la, o nyɛ la, la o yɛɣa o tuŋ yɛligu dee o la.

narrative_en: My brother Mensah was knocked by an aboboyaa near the Takoradi market area, not too long ago. The bike hit him and he fell badly, and his head and neck area got hurt in the fall. He is conscious and talking, but he is complaining of serious pain in his neck and there is some swelling on the side of his head. I am Nhyira, I am with him right now — we need someone to come and help us because we are not sure if we should move him.
narrative_ewe: Allo! Meɖekuku, kpe ɖe ŋunye! Nɔvi-ce Mensah, aboboyaa du i Takoradi market ŋɔ — kaba kaba fia. Motor du i, ewu ƒu akpokplɔ, eye ta kple ɖo ŋɔ yi ŋu dede. Eɖo edzi, le ŋɔ kple ame, gake eyi ŋu xɔxɔ le ɖo ŋɔ, eye ta ŋɔ fe ɖeka le sisi dede. Nyem Nhyira — meɖo ŋu xɔ ŋu tsɔ esia. Meɖi ame aɖe aɖu ɖe ŋuɖe eye adi kpe ɖe ŋutsoa — edzi alo miaɖe mia nu o, efoa nu be miewu i xɔ akɔ alo miaɖo to i le fea o. Meɖekuku, frɛ ambulance aɖu egbe sia egbe!
narrative_ga: Hɛloo, ofainɛ boa mi — ɔba mi Mensah, aboboyaa bumo no kɛ Takoradi market area ni, tɛŋ kɛ yɛ. Motor no bumo no maŋ, obumo wɔ fɛɛ, kɛ ŋmɛi kɛ ŋmɛi fɛ naa area no hii no wɔ fall no mu. Oni le kɛ okan, nye ŋmɛi fɛ kpee no hii no hɛɛ fɛɛ, kɛ swelling bi wɔ ŋmɛi naa shishi. Miinɛ Nhyira, mi wɔ kɛ no sɔɔ yɛ — mɛhe mɔ bi mli shi boa mii, biiŋ yɛmiŋ shiŋ tɔɔ no ba kɛ enyɛ haŋ mɔ ni yɛ naŋ bɔ no yaŋ.
narrative_dagbani: Allo! N daa, sɔŋ maa! M nua Mensah, aboboyaa yuli o Takoradi market saɣim puuni, ka o gbini biɛn, o ŋun ni o kɔɣili yɛligu o biɛn. O maa daa bɛ yɛligu, amaa ka yɛligu daa — o ŋun daɣa luni o dooi, ka o kɔɣili yɛligu o biɛn kaana. O nyɛŋ o maa yɛla, o guri, amaa ka yɛligu daa n tiim o ni. Maa yuli Nhyira, m bɛ o chɛɛ saha. N bori la duɣu maa ni — di yɛli wula ŋun n waa n zaa bɛ o saha — ka ti niŋ ti soli ka ti o pɛla, bala ka ti yii o din bɛ ziɛm. N daa, frɛ ambulance ka yi bɛ yi maa sɔŋ!
narrative_fante: Me nuabarima Mensah, aboboyaa bi bɔɔ no Takoradi market area ho, enye akyiri kɛse. Bike no bɔɔ no na ɔtuu fam pintinn, na ne tiri ne ne kɔn ho nyinaa yɛɛ no bɔne wɔ saa subu no mu. Ɔte hɔ na ɔrekasakasa, nanso ɔreka sɛ ne kɔn yɛ no yaw kɛse, na ne tiri fa bi so nso ahyɛw. Me na me din de Nhyira — mete ne ho seesei ara. Yehia obi mmra mmoa yɛn, efisɛ yɛnnim sɛ yɛnsesa no anaa yɛmfa no ankɔ baabi — yɛnsuro sɛ sɛ yɛde no bɛkari a, bɛyɛ no bɔne.
narrative_gurene:
M pio Mensah, aboboyaa bɔ o Takoradi market la saɣim — ka ka tɩɩ bilim. Motor la bɔ o, o bɔɔrɩ wʋsɩ, ka o zuɣu ne o noo saɣim yɛligu n mɩnɩ. O ko a nyɛ al'a, o goŋ gori, ama o gori o noo yɛligu ka yɛligu wʋsɩ — ka o zuɣu saɣim la ɣum gbim bee o.

Mam yɛ fo, m yɛtɔ Nhyira — mam bee o yin. Ti mɩɩrɩ sɔŋ, bala ti ko a nyɛ la bɛ ti tɩɩ o yisi. Yi mam! Frɛ ambulance — sɔŋ mam!

narrative_en: Please, something very serious has happened here in Axim. My neighbor's son, Issah, he was hit by a car on the road not far from the house, and they brought him back here but the boy is not doing well at all. His leg is very badly hurt — it looks broken and there is too much blood, we are afraid. I am Mawuli, I live just nearby, and the parents are here but they don't know what to do. Please we need help to come quickly because the boy is just a small child and he is in a lot of pain.
narrative_ewe: Meɖekuku, nudzadzra gã aɖe wɔe fifi afisia Axim. Ɖevi ame ŋutsu si le agbɔme nyɔnu aɖe ƒe ŋutsu, Issah, akɔ aɖe tso ŋu dzi Ʋɔ dzi, Ʋɔ si ga megbɔ xɔ o, eye wotsɔ woe de ɖe afisia megbe, gake ɖevi ɖeka la sɔ nyuie o ke o. Afɔ ɖe ŋu eƒe wu vɔ—eye eɖo abe ɣleti vɔ ene, eye dzi katã le etsotso, miaɖe ame ɖeka o. Yeyem nye Mawuli, mele ɖe he agbɔ, eye eƒe nɔvi tsitsiwo le afisia, gake wogblɔe wo be wodzra o. Meɖekuku, kpe ɖe ŋumia nyuie, woava fifi, efiaɖe ɖevi la ye ɖevi ʋevie kpe ɔ, eye eyi nam gã gã.
narrative_ga: Ofainɛ, something very serious ahappen yɛ ha Axim. Me neighbor ŋ bi, Issah, kaa bumo no kpee ni hɔ close fɛɛ house, na wɔ baa no akɛ ha — ɛbaa dɛbi no wɔ sane o. Ŋ gboo hii no kɛkɛ — enɔ fracture ni shwe etsotso no koraa, yɛ suro. Mɛ yɛ Mawuli, midzɔ ha nearby, na ŋ papa na ŋ maame wɔ ha ɛbaa yɛ sane wɔ nyɛ nɛ nii o. Ofainɛ, yɛ hiɛ bo ni bi ba faa wɔ fast because dɛbi no oblɛ fɛɛ ni ŋ gboo ehii no kɛkɛ. Frɛ ambulance — boa mi!
narrative_dagbani: N daa, yɛla dabɔɣu kura bɛ tɔɣim ka din yiɣim ka Axim. N pɛlɣu biɛla, Issah, kaɣa nɔ o nyɛŋ zugini, yili ka din so ni — ka o nyɛŋ gba di bɛ so ni yɔɣu. Ka din nɔ o di maa kaa bɔɣa diri. O tiŋ yɛligu yɛligu — a nyɛ ka o tiŋ gbinni, ka ziim bee o paɣa, ti suri doo. M yɛli ni maa Mawuli, n daa yili bɛ ka din so ni. O ma ni o ba bɛ kaa, ama bi ŋmani bɛ n-kpihi daa mɛŋ naa. N daa, ti bori sɔŋ — yi bɔɣa di ŋma ti gba, bala bia yini kura o nyɛ, ka yɛligu kama bee o.
narrative_fante: Me pa wo kyew, asɛm kɛse bi abɛba ha wɔ Axim. Me hɔhoɔ ba, Issah, kar bi bɔɔ no wɔ ɔkwan so a ɛbɛn efie no, na wɔde no baa ha, nanso abofra no ho nni yiye koraa. Ɔnan yaw no paa — ɛte sɛ ɛkrakra, na mogya retu no dodo, yɛsuro. Me din de Mawuli, mɛtese hɔ ara, na ɔna ne ɔse wɔ ha nanso wɔnnim dɛ wɔnyɛ dɛn. Me pa wo kyew, yehia mmoa mmra ntɛm ara, efisɛ abofra no yɛ abofra ketewa paa na ɛyɛ no yaw paa.
narrative_gurene: N tɩɩma, fɔ nɩ yɛligu kʋlʋŋ bee n yɛ Axim. M zoa biɛla, Issah, kaɣa bɔ o nyɛŋ zuɣu, yɛ ki la yir la kuri, la a bi da o nɩ. O nʋɔrɩ a yɛligu wʋlʋm wʋlʋm — a nyɛ o nʋɔrɩ gbɛŋ la, la ziim bee o wʋlʋm, ti nɩ zɔrɔ. Mam yɛtɔ la Mawuli, m nɩ bɩe la n zɔŋ tʋɔrɩ, la o ba nɩ yɛ la, la a bɩ nyɛ b sɔŋ b mɩŋ. N tɩɩma, sɔŋ mam, yi n yɛ ka pɛlɛŋ pɛlɛŋ — bia la ka biɛla doo, la a yɛligu bee o wʋlʋm wʋlʋm.

narrative_en: Please, I want to report an accident that happened on the Kumasi-Tamale road, somewhere near Ejura. My name is Naa Dedei, and I'm calling about a young man called Nii who was involved in a road accident not too long ago. It seems a vehicle hit him and he got badly hurt around his chest and stomach area, but he is still conscious and responding. He's not in the best state, but he's managing small. Please, someone should come and help because he needs attention quickly.
narrative_ewe: Meɖekuku, meyi na woɖe accident si wòwɔ ɖe Kumasi-Tamale ʋɔ dzi, xexeame Ejura kpodzi. Ŋkɔnyenye nye Naa Dedei, eye mefɔ telefon sia afi si ŋku la le ŋutsu ƒe dɔwɔwɔ si wɔɖe Nii dzi — nye ŋutsu kɔkɔe. Accident wɔe ɖe eŋu le ʋɔ dzi ana ko, eye enya akɔ deka yi ʋo dzi eŋu. Eyi nam ʋuʋu ɖe eƒe ɖevi ŋɔ kple abɔ ƒe ɖuɖu dzi, gake eɖo edzi hã, eye ese ame ƒe gbe. Eɖo edzi o geɖe hã — gake esia ko agbe le eŋu. Meɖekuku, mifrɛ ame abe wo ka va kpe ɖe eŋu abe, afɔfɔ kpɔ eŋu le nyuie, emeɖi kpɔ dɔwɔwɔ enumake.
narrative_ga: Ofainɛ, mebi report accident bi eyɛ Kumasi-Tamale road, somewhere near Ejura. Mi din bi Naa Dedei, mifre aba nɔŋ Nii — oblɛ bi obumo accident kɛ road no mli, ni ehii te. Kaa bi etsu mi ni, enyɛ ehii mi kɛ gbɔɔ kɛ shi areas too — enyɛ bad paa. Kɛɛ oni le, orenya response, so enimɔ gbɛ o. Ofe kɛ struggle small, kɛɛ oremanage. Ofainɛ, nɔŋ bi obi eba boa mi haaa — ni ehi attention fast fast. Frɛ ambulance, chale, akpee!
narrative_dagbani: N daa, n bɛ yɛ ni n yɛl yin accident din kpɛŋ Kumasi-Tamale nyɛŋ zuɣu, Ejura kɔbiŋ. Maa nyɛŋ din Naa Dedei, ka n bɛ yɛ ni zoli Nii boro — o yɛ dieri biɛla, ka accident kpɛŋ o nyɛŋ zuɣu, ka ka daa kpɛm. Kaɣa bi o, ka o yɛligu ka yɛligu — o digindi ni o pam daa yɛligu tɔɣi. Amaa o ti soli kɔbi — o bɛ yɛ ni zaŋ ni o bɛ yɛ ni yɛla. Ka o nyɛ ka nyɛla, amaa o bɛ mini tuma. N daa, yini nyɛ n yi kɔ o sɔŋ — ka o yɛlgu biɛn ni o bori la nyɛla kpɛm kpɛm.
narrative_fante: Me pa wo kyew, mepɛ sɛ meka accident bi aho, na ɛbaa Kumasi-Tamale road so, kɔse Ejura nkyɛn. Me din de Naa Dedei, na merɛfrɛ afa obarima ketewa bi ho, na wɔfrɛ no Nii. Ɔkɔ hyɛɛ accident bi mu kakra ara seisei. Ɛte sɛ kar bi bɔɔ no, na ɛyɛɛ no yaw paa wɔ ne bruwa ne ne yafunu ho, nanso ɔwɔ hɔ daa na ɔteɛ asɛm. Ne ho nsɛe koraa, nanso ɛnyɛ saa yiye. Me pa wo kyew, obi mmra boa no, efisɛ ɔhia mmoa ntɛm ara.
narrative_gurene: Please, I want to report an accident that happened on the Kumasi-Tamale road, somewhere near Ejura. My name is Naa Dedei, and I'm calling about a young man called Nii who was involved in a road accident not too long ago. It seems a vehicle hit him and he got badly hurt around his chest and stomach area, but he is still conscious and responding. He's not in the best state, but he's managing small. Please, someone should come and help because he needs attention quickly.

narrative_en: Please, something serious has happened and I need help urgently. My name is Appiah, and I am calling about an old woman called Abena who was working near the Kete Krachi road close to Dambai — she was carrying heavy loads as part of her daily work when something went very wrong and she got badly hurt in her chest and stomach area. She is in a lot of pain and cannot move properly, the injury looks very bad. Please we need someone to come quickly because she is elderly and this is too serious for us to handle alone here.
narrative_ewe: Meɖekuku, nudzadzra gãtɔ aɖe wɔ ɖe afisiame, eye meɖi kpɔ ŋunye vovovo. Ŋkonye nye Appiah, eye mefrɛ ŋdi Abena dzi — nyɔnu srɔ̃ aɖe wɔ dɔ kple woƒe asi ɖe Kete Krachi ʋɔ ŋgɔ, ɖe Dambai kɔkɔe. Ele nuviwo tɔtɔ be dɔwɔwɔ ŋɔliŋɔli ake, gake nuto gãtɔ wɔ, eye eyi nam vovovo ɖe woƒe ɖevi ŋɔ kple abɔ ɖe eme. Eyi ŋunye dzo, eye mate ŋu yi fe ɖe nyuie o — nuvi si wɔ eƒe ame la xɔ na le gã. Meɖekuku, miaɖi ame aɖe tso afisi aɖe va vovovo, esi nyɔnu srɔ̃ ia ne, eye nudzadzra sia le dukɔ wu mia ŋu be miakpɔe ame ɖeka o. Woava ko!
narrative_ga: Ofainɛ, something serious aba shi, mi hia bo mli kɛ nowɔ. Mitsɔ Appiah, mi frɛ frɛ shikpɔŋ biaa wɔ Abena, gbɔmɔ nyɛ, shi wɔ akɔ nɔ kpee Kete Krachi, ni shi ko Dambai fɛɛ. Oshɔ load kɛkɛ wɔ ejiɛ work fɛ, ni abɔ o kɛ tɛtɛ — gbɔɔ ni shi — ehii o kɛ daadaa. O hii pɛpɛɛpɛ, enimɔ move gbɛ. Chale, injury bi yɛ fɛɛ serious — gbɔmɔ nyɛ ni yɛ no too — mɛmɛ le hia ofainɛ. Ofainɛ, frɛ ambulance shi ba nowɔ — yɛ tumi handle no heni yɛ bɔ o.
narrative_dagbani: N daa, ka yɛligu daa kuli ŋmɛ ka n bori sɔŋ dɔɣinli. N yuli din yɛ Appiah, ka n frɛ ni kpɛma poga yini, o yuli din yɛ Abena. O bee Kete Krachi nyɛŋ doli, Dambai doli yɔɣu, ka o gbini o tuma kama — o bee tali shɛli pam bali ka ka yɛligu pahi o dɔɣinli. O digindi ni o pam ka yɛligu naɣi o dɔɣi, ka ka yɛligu ti ka o so bi nyi dini yɛ niŋdi. O kum daa naɣi, ka bi zaŋ lahi naa. N daa, yi frɛ bia yini o ka o di paɣa, barka, bala o nindaan ŋɔ ni ka ka saɣiri daa, ka yɛligu kpahi. Ka yi bi di zaŋ ŋan ti naa yɛŋ pam ka ti bi bee ŋmɛ ti sɔŋ o gba ŋa.
narrative_fante: Me pa wo kyew, biribi a ɛyɛ den paa aba, na mehia mmoa ntɛm ara. Me din de Appiah, na merɛfrɛ ho fa opanyin baa bi a wɔfrɛ no Abena — ɔreyɛ adwuma bɛn Kete Krachi ɔkwan no ho kɔse Dambai. Ɔno na ɔresorɔ ayɛ duru paa wɔ ne dwumadi mu, na biribi a ɛyɛ hu paa baa so — ne bruwa ne ne yafunu mu na ɛyaw no yiye yiye. Eye no yaw paa, na ɔntumi nnyina ne ho yiye. Nkurofa a ɛyɛ hu paa — injury no hyɛ hu. Me pa wo kyew, mhia obi a ɔbɛba ntɛm ara, efisɛ ɔyɛ opanyin na ɛnyɛ biribi a yɛtumi hyɛ ase ara yi. Mboa yɛn!
narrative_gurene: N tɩɩma, sɔŋ mam! Bʋɔrɩ bee tɩ la ka m yɛligu ka m kpɛŋ sɔŋ. M yʋɔrɩ Appiah, ka m bee yɛligu Abena yɛlɩ — o yaa nindaan, ka o bɛ ka o mʋ be Kete Krachi nyɛŋ nanga Dambai. O bee tʋma o tʋma o vɛɛrɛ, o kpɩɩka kpɛŋ tɩya boo, ka bʋɔrɩ ka o bee yɛligu zɔŋa. O yɛligu a pʋʋrɛ nɛ o koba fãã — yɛligu bee o zɔŋa ka a nyɛ yaa kpɛŋ tuma. O bɛ tɩ tɩŋa laafɩ, o bɛ tɩ tum laafɩ. N tɩɩma, tʋm nɩ bia nɩ bʋɔrɩ la! Abena yaa nindaan ka a tuma yaa zɔŋa — tɩ bɛ tɩ tʋm laafɩ tɩ yɛla la tɩ manɛ. Frɛ ambulance bʋɔrɩ ka la wa!

narrative_en: My brother Hamza was knocked by a pragyia this afternoon here in Salaga. I am Yakubu, I am calling on his behalf because he is resting now. The bike hit him on his side and he fell down, but he got up by himself so it's not too serious. He has some pain around his chest area and a small bruise there, but nothing looks broken or anything like that. We just want to make sure he is okay and know if we should take him somewhere.
narrative_ewe: Allo! Meɖekuku, kpe ɖe ŋunye. Nɔviwi ame Hamza wɔ accident kple pragyia ɖe Salaga fɔli afisia. Nye nye Yakubu — mekɔ ɖo na eŋu ɖo ne, elabena ede duɖuia siwo ne. Motor xo we ɖe edzidzi to fɔli, eye ewu ƒu, gake eye ede dzi ɖoe ame — ame me gblɔ o be nudzadzra lolo wɔ na e alo be nusiame ɖe eŋu o. Eɖo edzi, eye ese eyi ŋu ɖe ɖevi ŋɔ ŋɔ dzi nu keke, eye le afe ɖeka aɖe sia xɔ gbɔ sɔsɔe o — gake ɣleti ɖeka o, nudzaɖe kɔkɔe ooo. Mienoa ŋu gbɔ trɔ na nyi be nedie edzi ŋutɔ, eye mele mi ŋu wɔ biabia be mia ɖo e yɔ afe si aɖe alo eɖo ŋu mia kpɔ.
narrative_ga:
Hɛloo, ofainɛ boa mi. Ɔjogbaŋŋ mi Hamza, pragyia bi kpee ni ŋ afɛɔ yɛ Salaga. Mii nyɛ Yakubu — mii frɛ ni shi ŋ ji, afi ebu shi ehii ni ŋ nɔ akɔɔ nɔ.

Kaa — pragyia bi, ehii Hamza ni ŋ gbɔɔ fɛɛ, ebumo, kɛ otsoo ni ŋ kɔɔ bɔɔ — ɔmii ŋmɛi eni bo o. Shi enyɛ naagbe o, ni ji nɔ, gbɔɔ fɛɛ ehii ni ŋ kakra, kɛ bruise kakra wɔ hɛɛ — kɛ ni mli kɔi fracture bi anaa something like that o. Nyɛ wɔ nɔ le.

Chale, wɔ pɛ shia nɔŋ. Wɔ bi nyɛ Hamza le kɛ o — kɛ wɔ pɛ nyɛ mɔ ni wɔ yaŋŋ ni ŋ shia hospital bi, anaa ni wɔ du hɛɛ nɔ. Bɔ wɔ ni mɔ ni wɔ nyɛ?
narrative_dagbani: Allo, n daa. M dabi Hamza, pragyia yuli o gomfi tuma be Salaga. Mi Yakubu la, n frɛ ni o yɔɣu, barka o bɛ chɛɣim doo. Kaɣa biɛla gbuni o nyɛla, o gbini, ama o fɔ o mɛɣim ko o kpɛli, ka a yɛlɣu ka kpaga. O digindi nyɛla ka yɛligu dɔɣu, ka nyɛla kpabi biɛli be bee o doo, ama a nyɛ a yɛligu ka o tiŋŋi be yɛlgu wula wula. N nyɛ ka tiŋŋi kpata kpata kɔrila daa. Bii ka ti bɔri shɛli maa ni, ti bɔri nyɛ ka O lee kuri, ka maa ni ti nyɛ ka ti naa o nyɛla yili, ka a bɔri ka ti zom ka wula.
narrative_fante: Me nuabarima Hamza, pragyia bi bɔɔ no awiabere yi ha wɔ Salaga. Me ne Yakubu, merefrɛ wo n'afa no ho efisɛ ɔda ho sreɛ seesei. Bike no bɔɔ no n'afiri ho na ɔtuu fam, nanso ɔsɔɔre n'ankasa so, enti ɛnyɛ den paa. Ɔwɔ yaw tiaa n'abrowafo ho na bruise kumaa bi wɔ hɔ, nanso hwee nkae sɛ fracture anaa biribi te saa. Yɛpɛ sɛ yɛhunu sɛ ɔho yɛ yiye, na yɛpɛ sɛ yɛnim sɛ yɛnsɛ de no kɔ baabi anaa dea?
narrative_gurene: Mam di Hamza, o zɔŋ pragyia dɩ wɛltɩ o naam yɩɩra. Mɩ yɛ Yakubu, m bɩla fo yɛ o ninsim tɩɩ, bɩ o bɛ nyɩnka naam. Pragyia dɩ bɔɔ o nɩŋgbaan fɛɛ, o kpɛŋ fam, a bɩ o zɔŋ o mɩŋ sɔŋ fɛɛ o yɩgɩ — ɔ nɛ, ka biisi daa kɛŋ. O ka yɛligu a nɩŋgbaan pɔɔ kɔ o poore ninsim, a bɩ bruise bilɩ bee o sɔ, a bɩ ka bɔŋ kɔ daa kpɛ biisi. Tɩ ŋan tɩ bɛ bɩ tɩ nyɛ a o sɩɣa sɩɣa, a bɩ tɩ nɛ, tɩ da ta o hospital?

narrative_en: Please, something serious has happened here in Wa Central and I need help urgently. My name is Agyeman, and I'm calling about a young man called Owusu — he was hit by a car on the road just a short while ago. The boy is not doing well at all, his leg is badly injured, it looks very broken and there is a lot of blood. He cannot stand or move the leg at all, and he is in a lot of pain, just lying there on the ground. Please, we need an ambulance to come here fast before the situation gets worse.
narrative_ewe: Meɖekuku, nudzadzra gãtɔ wɔ fifia kaba Wa Central, eye meɖi kpɔ ŋunye akpokpɔ. Ŋkɔnyem nye Agyeman, eye mecɔ telefon sia ɖe ŋutɔ ŋutsu fofome aɖe si wɔanɔ Owusu — akɔ dze esi eƒu ʋɔ dzi fifi si va yi. Ɖevi la mehã o tɔtrɔ, eƒe afɔ vɔ gãgã — ele ɣleti vɔ abe nane ɖe eye dzi le etsotso le ebe dzi. Mate ŋu ʋɔ afɔ la o, mate ŋu yi edzi o, eye eyi ŋunye gãgã — eli ƒu feme be. Meɖekuku, miɖi ambulance aʋɔ yi fifia kaba eye emigble ɖe miaɖe bena nu la megblẽ o.
narrative_ga: Ofainɛ, something serious abɛ happen fɛɛ Wa Central nii, mɛhɛ help urgently. Me din dɔ Agyeman, nɛ mehɛ call yɛɛ young man bi, wɔn frɛ no Owusu — kaa bumo no kpee fɛɛ, enyo short time adɔɔ. Oblɛ no enimɔ nyɛ adɔɔ o chale, ono gboo ehii no paaa — enimɔ look like fracture fɛɛ, nɛ shwe etsotso paaa. Enyɛ taa gbɔɔ o, enyɛ taa move gboo no o, nɛ ni hii no paaa — oshie fɛɛ kpee so pɛɛ. Ofainɛ, yɛhɛ ambulance ni bɛ jɛkɛ ko fast fast, eha situation no gbaa woko.
narrative_dagbani: N daa, yɛla kɔrigu pam bɛ zaa ka o bɔri la Wa Central kɔŋ, ka n bori sɔŋ daabiɛ. N yuli ni Agyeman, ka n bɛ yɛ ni Owusu yɛla. Bia waa, Owusu, kaɣa daa o kuna nyɛŋ zuɣu ʒɛm ʒɛm ka ka o kuli o ni. Bia o nyɛla zaɣa kpɛŋ pam — o tiŋ daa ti yɛligu kpam, o tiŋ kpɛli la, ka ziim bee o dɔɣu pam. O nan gari tiŋ koli amaa o tiŋ zooi, ka yɛligu kɔrigu bee o pam — o gbini ŋɔ zuɣu wuhibigu. N daa, yi frɛ ambulance ni bi bi miri zaŋ da zɔ maaŋ, ka o bɔri la yɛla kuli ku ti kpɛ ka ni o.
narrative_fante: Me pa wo kyew, biribi serious aba ha Wa Central na mhia mmoa ntɛm ara. Me din de Agyeman, na merɛfrɛ ho fa ɔbarima ketewa bi a wɔfrɛ no Owusu — kar bi bɔɔ no wɔ ɔkwan so seesei ara. Owusu ho nnyɛ koraa, ne nan ayaw paa — ɛte sɛ fracture badly na mogya pii retu. Ɔntumi ngyina ase anaa sesa ne nan koraa, na ɛyɛ no yaw kɛse, ɔda fam hɔ pɛɛ. Me pa wo kyew, yɛhia ambulance bi mmra ha ntɛm ara ansa na situation no nnante yiye.
narrative_gurene: N tɩɩma, gɛri gari kɛ bi maŋ ka a yɛligu teŋ Wa Central, ka m tɩ sɔŋ nɔɔŋ. M yʋɔri Agyeman, ka m frɛ bia bi yʋɔri Owusu — kaɣa bugi o nyɛŋ zug ka O kɛŋ ka bi daa. Bia ka bee la yɛligu, o nɔɔrɛ bugi wʋlʋ, a nyɛ ka o nɔɔrɛ kpali biig ka ziim bee o wʋlʋ. O waari tʋm o nɔɔrɛ bɛɛ kpɛŋ be fam, ka o yɛligu wʋlʋ, o kɛŋ fam tɛn fɛɛ. N tɩɩma, ti tɩ ambulance wa ka yi nɔɔŋ yi nɔɔŋ n ka gɛri a yɛligu po.

narrative_en: Please, I'm calling to report something that happened to my brother Kwabena here in Yendi. He was doing some work at home — cutting something on the roof I think — and a small piece of it fell and hit him on the back of his head. Appiah is my name, I'm the one calling. He said it's not too painful, just a small cut, but there's a little blood so we wanted to make sure it's okay.
narrative_ewe: Meɖekuku, mefrɛ mi be maɖo nunya aɖe mi wu, nudzadzra aɖe wu ɖekɔ Kwabena Yendi ɔ. Nɔ le ŋɔ le agbe adaŋu le fie, ele vu le ta ŋɔ ɔ, be nudzodzro aɖe vɔ͂ wo kpo wu ta agbeŋɔ dzi ɔ. Nyɔ be Appiah, ame si le frɛ emi ɔ nye. Ɖekɔ Kwabena gblɔ be eyi nam geɖe o, vuvɔ aɖewɔ ɔ ka, gake dzi le le eso ɔ, meto ŋu le wo mia be maxɔ kpɔ be nane la ɖe eŋu me nyuie.
narrative_ga: Ofainɛ, mifɛɛ frɛ shɛ mi hɛ yɛ bɔɔ ko nɔŋ mi nuŋ Kwabena kɛ Yendi. Oshi oshwa oshi kɛ fie — oshi gbaa bii ko kɛ atifi mi mɔ — nɔ ko bii naa bumo ehii ŋmɛi ashi. Appiah na mi din, mi na onimɛ frɛ. Oshi bɔ ni hii kɛɛ ko lɛ, cut kakra bo, ŋmɛi ashi, engbɛɛ shwe tsotso kakra, yɛ bɛ jɛ frɛ shɛ ni nyɛ kɛ kpɔɔ lɛ gbi lɛ oyɛ.
narrative_dagbani: N daa, m frɛ ka m wilim daa bi yɛligu be nini, be n doo Kwabena din gbini ka bɛ Yendi. O be o yili la ka o kuli kuli kpɛna ka o te wula — o gbɛra a taɣi din be sunsuŋa la saɣim — ka kaɣaɣa biɛla nini kpiɣi o ŋun gbɔŋ. M yuli la Appiah, maa n frɛ la. O yɛlɔ ka ka yɛligu daa naŋ o tee daa, ka kpɛma kpɛma daa, ka ziim biɛla bee o, ka bɛ n ti bɔri in kpɛm doli ka ti nii in ka o ŋmaa wuhigu.
narrative_fante: Me pa wo kyew, merɛfrɛ ama mekaa biribi a esii me nua Kwabena ho ha Yendi. Ɔreye adwuma wɔ fie — ɔrekutwa biribi wɔ dan so, na me susu sɛ kakra bi tuu fam bɔɔ no tiri akyi. Appiah na yɛfrɛ me, me na merɛfrɛ. Ɔse ɛnyɛ no yaw paa, cut kakra na ɛyɛ, nanso mogya kakra retu, enti yɛpɛe sɛ yɛhwɛ sɛ ɛho te sɛn.
narrative_gurene: N tɩɩma, m frɛ fo a yɛl yɛla a maalɩ m pio Kwabena a Yendi. O bee o ŋɔ yir gbɛɛŋ — o sɔɣɩ yɛla a yir ŋɔ zuɣu, m mɩnɛ — la tɩɩ biɛɣɩ kpɛŋa a o ŋun poore a o kpeŋa. Appiah la m yʋʋrɩ, mam la o frɛ nyɛ. O yɛl a yɛligu ka yɛ kʋ peeba, tɩɩ biɛɣɩ siebo la, la ziim bee o biɛɣɩ, kʋ a bee tɩ daa o a yɛla ŋɔ ti yɛ sɩɣɩ.

narrative_en: "Please, something very bad happened to a young girl called Felicia on the Kumasi-Cape Coast road, near Mankessim. Some men attacked her, they beat her seriously and her arm is in a very bad condition — she cannot move it at all and it is swollen and bent in a wrong way. I am Boateng, I was passing in my car and I saw her on the roadside crying, nobody was helping her. Please you people should send somebody fast because the girl is losing a lot of blood and she looks like she is going to faint."
narrative_ewe: Meɖekuku, nudzadzra gãtɔ ame wòwɔ nyɔnu ɖevi ɖeka be Felicia yɔ le Kumasi-Cape Coast ʋɔ dzi, kpɔkplɔ Mankessim. Amewo etɔ̃ame wòyi eŋu, wòtu eŋu gãgã eye asi ŋɔ la wòme nyo o — emate ŋu tsi asi la ŋu o eye esi la vɔ̃ eye ewu gble ɖe fe bubu dzi. Nyem nye Boateng — me le akɔ dzi, mewu ʋɔ la dzi abe akɔ ɖe gɔme ene mɔ anye ka. Mekpɔ eŋutɔ le ʋɔ ɣedzeɣedze to nyagbe, ame aɖeke mele kpe ɖe eŋu o. Meɖekuku, mi frɛ ambulance va asi eye mi zã ɣeyiɣi, efufu dzi amewo gbɔ o, nyɔnu ɖevi la dzi gɔ le etsotso gãgã eye mele nunya be eɖo vovotoɖe dzi.
narrative_ga: Ofainɛ, something very bad ehi Felicia — oblɛ bi — wɔ Kumasi-Cape Coast road la, Mankessim fɛɛ. Nnɔɔ bi bɔɔ ŋ wɔ shɛ, wɔ blɛɛ ŋ kɛkɛ, nɔ gbɔ la enyɔ fɛɛ — enyɛ nmi move ŋ bi o, enɔŋ ebo nyɛ, nɔ kpoo wɔ mɛi mɔkɔ. Mɛ nye Boateng — meloo kaa wɔ kpee la, mhui ŋ wɔ kpee fɛfɛ, enyeli, ŋmɛ bi hɛ bo ŋ o. Ofainɛ, ɛi mi nɔɔ, mi mitsɛ onukpa bi fast waa, bɔ dɛbi la shwe etsotso bebii, ni nɔɔ enyeli kɛ efaint.
narrative_dagbani: N daa, kuli bɛ yɛligu daa kɔŋ Felicia, o nyɛ biɛla, o kɔŋ Kumasi-Cape Coast nyɛŋ zuɣu, Mankessim kɔŋ. Nniyɔɣu dima gbini o, o gbaali o yɛligu daa, ka o nuu — o nuu yɛligu daa ŋmɛɣim, o ti tɔli o nuu fili kuri, ka o nuu kpɛŋ kɔbiili, o zɔɣim kuri. N nye mi Boateng, m bɛ m kaɣa zuɣu m nuu nyɛŋ tia, ka m nyɛ o nyɛŋ dii, o yilibu bɛ, o bɛ dini, ka o ziim ɣeya ŋmɛɣim, ka o nyɛ be ti soli. N daa, yi tuma yɔɣu yini naa naa, ka o ziim ɣeya seeɣim, ka o nyɛ o maa yɛla ŋmɛɣim, ka o ti soli.
narrative_fante: Me pa wo kyew, biribi bɔne paa na asi abofra baa bi a wɔfrɛ no Felicia wɔ Kumasi-Cape Coast ɔkwan no so, Mankessim nkyɛn. Mmarima bi na wɔsɔɔ no, wɔbɔɔ no yiye paa — na ne nsa no ho yɛ bɔne ara — ɔntumi nnye no mpo, na ano ahoahoa na ano akyinkyin bɔne bɔne. Me din de Boateng, na merekɔ me kar mu na mehunuu no ɔkwan nkyɛn no so na ɔresu, na obiara amma no boa. Me pa wo kyew, mo mboa me na monsoma onipa bi ntɛm ara ɛfiri sɛ abofra baa no retu mogya paa, na ne ho kyerɛ sɛ ɔbɛkolapuse.
narrative_gurene: N tɩɩma, yɛligu kʋŋ daa wa Felicia yɛ — o yɛ bia pɔɣa. Yɛligu bee Kumasi-Cape Coast nyɛŋ zuɣu, Mankessim saɣim. Nidaam dɔɣim o, o bʋɣɩ o yɛligu teŋ — o nu yɛ kʋŋ — o tɔɔrɩ o a puŋ, nɛ a wa yɛlɩ, a boŋa, a yɛ beem wʋsɩ. Mɩ yɛ Boateng — m daa bee m kaɣa nyɛŋ zuɣu la m nyɛ o nyɛŋ gɔbɩ, o sɔla, neb kʋŋ bee o sɔŋ. N tɩɩma, yɩ frɛ nidaam yoli yoli, bʋɣɩ ambulance la wa, bʋɣɩ o zuɣu — bia pɔɣa ziim ɣeya teŋ teŋ la o nyɛ bee o ko a nyɛ.

narrative_en: My name is Asare, I'm calling to report something that happened to a young lady called Elinam. She was cooking jollof at her flat here in Adenta — the pressure from the pot made the lid fly up suddenly and hot steam and water splashed on her forearm. The skin there is red and blisters are already coming up, and she is in a lot of pain. She's conscious and talking, but the burn looks serious and she says it keeps getting worse. We need to know what to do for her right now.
narrative_ewe:
Ŋkɔnyé nye Asare. Meɖo egɔ na miawɔ aɖe si wɔ Elinam. Nyɔnu ɖevi aɖe — Elinam wɔe — le jollof ɖe ŋu le eƒe flat ŋu afisia le Adenta. Agbagba si le ƒomevi ŋu ʋoa ŋgɔ vovovo, eye ƒomevi ƒe ʋu gbagba ƒo dzi, eye steam kpekpe kple dzi gbãtɔ feƒe tso ŋu lo asi ƒe ɖaɖa ŋu — ame nɔ ʋa na ya tututu loo.

Ame ko la, asi ƒe ŋgɔ si gbãtɔ feƒe ƒo dzi la, adome dzi le avã ʋu dzi, eye blister — ame no woɖoa la — le ɖo gbagba fifia. Eyi nam kpui. Eyia ɖeke, Elinam eɖo edzi, le ŋu le gbe la me, le taɖo ŋu. Gake gbãtɔ feƒe la gbɔ la kpɔ sɔsɔ tɔ, eye mia xɔ aɖe si gblɔ be eyi nam va nyim nyim. Miɖi nya be miaɖe ŋu na eŋu kakaɖe fifia — miaƒe wɔ nenu la?
narrative_ga: Hɛloo, miiŋ nyɛ Asare — mifɛɛ report bii shi oblɛ baa dzɛɛ Elinam. Oni le, oshɛ noko, nɔ wɔ jollof kokoko kɔ flaatɛ ŋ ni le Adenta. Pressure ni le pot ŋ nu shi — lid bumo bumo kɛkɛ, steam kɛ nishɛ ekpoo ebumo gbɔ ŋ mli ni le forearm. Skin dzɛɛ le ŋ kɛ ni color nyɛ red, kɛ blisters dan shɔŋ ko le ŋ. Ehii ni hii — hii fɛɛ. Oni le, oshɛ noko, nɔ kɛ mɛ oshi, nɔ wɔ ni hii ehii ni fɛɛ kɛ eboro boro. Chale, burn ni dɔɔ fɛɛ — ni serious. Kɛ bi mɛ, mɛ hɛ mɛ ni wiɛ dzɛɛ mɛ ni sɛ ofainɛ nɔɔ bo Elinam hi la — mi ni hiɔ mɛ wala nɔ?
narrative_dagbani: Allo, m yɛligu la Asare. N bɛ yɛ ni frɛ ti saɣim dɔɣite chaŋ ka bini biɛla da. Pag biɛla yɔɣiri din din, o yɔri la Elinam — o bɛ Adenta, o yiri dabari din. O bɛ nyuŋ jollof daa, o na kata bee ka shɛli din din, ka kata kpɛŋ zuɣu salima, ka nyuŋ kpihinli ni zie ka pa o nuuri din yɛligu — nuu din bɛ pam ni wari. Ka ŋun din ziim duɣu din bɛ pɔɣim pɔɣim yɔ, ka chirigu bɛ daŋ faa o nuu zuɣu. Ka yɛligu paɣa Elinam — o yɛligu daa paai ka o yɛ o bɛ nyɛ ka biri nyɛ n-zɔŋ. O bɛ yɛm yɛligu, ka o bɛ gbɛya, ka o bɛ goli, amma o yɛ ka yɛligu daa bɛ zaŋ paai. N bori la m nyɛ sɔŋ maa — dimi suɣulo, ti yɛ ti n tuma Elinam chaŋ zaa yɛ ka ti paai kpaa?
narrative_fante: Me din de Asare, merefrɛ mun sɛ biribi aka ɔbaa ketewa bi a wɔfrɛ no Elinam. Ɔrekyekye jollof wɔ n'apartment no mu ha wɔ Adenta — pressure a ɛwɔ potɔ no mu no maa n'atiridii no tu fam ntɛm ntɛm, na steam na nsuo a ɛhyehye no tuu n'ensam no so. Anidie a ɛwɔ hɔ no aye kɔkɔɔ, na blisters nso reba saa ara, na ɛyɛ no yaw paa. Ɔte hɔ na ɔrekan, nanso burn no ɛte sɛ ɛyɛ den, na ɔka sɛ ɛyɛ no yaw kɛse ara saa. Yehia sɛ yɛhunu deɛ yɛbɛyɛ ma no seesei ara.
narrative_gurene: M yɛŋ la Asare. M bɔɔrɩ fɔn tuma a fo sɔŋ yɛla paɣa biɛɣa yenbo a yɛŋ la Elinam. O la bee jollof nɩŋ o flat pʋɔ Adenta, la kʋ nɩŋ pɔt pressure sɔrɩ la, a o gura bɔɔrɩ fɔ ŋɔɔ, la tɩɩm nɩŋ pʋɔ yoori bɔɔrɩ o nuŋ ɣaŋ ka sɔrɩ. O nuŋ ɣaŋ pɔɔlɩ nyaa la bɔɔ yɛla, la ʋ pɔrɩ la gʋriɩ ka bee o bɛɛ. O yɛligu daa so ziŋa. O nyɛ la, o gomsi la, la tia nyuu la bee yɛligu sɔŋa — o yɛligu la kɩɛ ka kpɛŋ. Sɔŋ mam la fo nii, bii yɛla y'a nɩŋ ka o ra?

narrative_en: My son Kojo got hurt today while he was helping at the carpentry shop here in Assin Fosu. A piece of wood fell on his arm and cut him small, so there is some bleeding but he is still moving his hand fine. I am Kwasi, I am his father. We have cleaned it and tied a cloth around it, but I just wanted to report it because he is still a young boy and I am not sure if we need to bring him somewhere.
narrative_ewe:
Allo, meɖekuku, kpe ɖe ŋunye. Nyeŋutsu Kwasi — ŋutsu si le gbe fia. Ɖevim Kojo xɔ dzo egɔme egɔme fifia, eƒe akɔ ɖe carpenter dukowo la nu le Assin Fosu. Aʋe ɖeka do ƒu eƒe asi dzi, ko eʋo asi aɖeke kakaka, gake dzi le etsotso geɖe. Mede ko xɔɔ ko agbe, eƒe asi ƒu lo dede — eble asi nyuie ko.

Míaɖo dzi adze eʋo ta ʋe, míakpa devi nyui aɖe eƒe asi dzi. Gake Kojo ɖevi gbãtõ ko — eye mate ŋu se be mele ɖe míaga ɖe asiwo o, ke mede ŋkeke. Ekema metia gbe de wo wò me be míabe devi ɖe asiwo o, ke wò ƒe asi ko wɔ le eyi ŋuɖoɖo.

Meɖekuku, kpɔ mia be mídze ƒu na emi o? Asiwo megbe be?
narrative_ga: Hɛloo, chale. Me biɔ Kojo, ɔbumo ŋ ni wɔ afɔ. Ebumo ni ŋ hɛ carpentry shop shi le Assin Fosu. Nɔ wɔɔ, wood kɛlɛŋ bumo ŋ gbɔ ŋ ji, abi ɔteetee ŋ ni shwe etsotso kɛkɛ — ɛnyɛ big big o, ni ŋ gbɔ nimɔ gbɛ atɔ. Yɛ haŋ shwe ŋ ji, yɛ kitee ntɔŋ ŋ bii gbɔ ŋ ji. Me biɔ Kwasi — me nɔ ba ŋ. Kojo dɛbi kɛkɛ, chale, ni mitsɔ shi wɔ yɛ bɔ ŋ hospital anaa yɛ tɛɛ hɛ yɛ hiɛ ŋ wɔ tsɔbɔ o. Ofainɛ, bɔ mii shi — yɛ de ŋ hospital anaa?
narrative_dagbani: Allo, N daa. M bi Kojo o gbɛ yɛlɣu zaŋ bɛ, waa nɔŋ kuli ka o bee sɔŋ carpentry shop ŋɔ ni Assin Fosu. Kpɛŋgu yini o dɔli nuu zuɣu ka o kpɛm yɛŋ bini o din ni, ka ziim bee nabiɛla, ama o nuu bee goli n'ɔ daa — o nuu bee tiŋ ŋɔɔni. Mali maa nyɛŋ la Kwasi, m bi baaba. Ti ŋmasi o tiŋ ŋɔ yɛlɣu ka ti kaɣ kpaŋ zaŋ di koli, ama N di labi yɛla n zaŋ diri ti ma, barka o gbɛ bia daa ka N ba ŋmani n bori la ti zaŋ o nyɛla yili wala ayi.
narrative_fante: Me ba Kojo nyaa awu nnɛ bere a ɔreboaboa ɔpanyin no adwuma wɔ carpentry shop no mu ha Assin Fosu. Dua bi tuu ɔkwan baa ne nsa so ama ɛtwa no kakra, enti mogya retu no kakra, nanso ɔrekɔ ne nsa yiye ara. Me din de Kwasi, mene ne agya. Yɛawasow ne nsa no na yɛde ntoma asie ho, nanso mirehwɛ sɛ ɛsɛ sɛ yede no kɔ hospital anaa dabi, efisɛ ɔyɛ abofra ketewa ara na minnim sɛ ɛho hia anaa.
narrative_gurene: M bia Kojo wa yɛligu yɛlga bi a karpenteri yir a Assin Fosu. Zɩɩm ligri wa wa nɩɩm a o nↄŋ, a o ziim ɣeya bilisi, ama o nↄŋ bee o viim la. Mɛ yɛ Kwasi, o ba. Ti sɔŋ ka ti yii zɩɩm ka ti kɔŋ atɔrɔ a o nↄŋ, ama m dat a sɔŋɔ niyaŋ kelim bie a, o yɛ bia bilisi, ka m ko nyɛ sɩd ka ti maal tɩ daŋ o Yɛla yir.

narrative_en: Please, something very bad has happened on the Cape Coast-Elmina road. A young boy, Appiah, was knocked down by a car and he is in a very serious condition. His leg — I think it's his leg or maybe his arm — is badly hurt, like it's broken and there's a lot of blood. I am Maame, I was passing when I saw him lying on the roadside and people have gathered but nobody knows what to do. Please send help quickly, the boy needs urgent attention right now.
narrative_ewe: Meɖekuku, nudzadzra gãã aɖe wɔ ɖe Cape Coast-Elmina ʋɔ dzi. Ɖevi ŋutsu aɖe, Appiah, akɔ aɖe zã eŋu, eye edze ɖe ʋɔ ŋɔ abe ame si le tsitre ene. Afɔe — mese be afɔe wɔm, ke nɔeŋu le asie — evɔ dukpe, eye dzi wɔ etsotso gãã. Nyem nye Maame, me le ʋɔ la dzim, eye megbɔ eŋu hã eke le ʋɔ te la dzi. Amewo dzo bɔbɔm le eŋu ko, gake amegã aɖe se nu wɔ o. Meɖekuku, tsia kpɔ ŋunye enumake — frɛ ambulance bena wava gbã gbã, ɖevi la hiã kpe enumake fifia!
narrative_ga: Ofainɛ, something very bad aba Cape Coast-Elmina road ni. Oblɛ bi, Appiah, kaa bumo no mli, no onyemi le gbɛɛ — situation serious paaa. Ono gboo — mitse gboo kɛ gbɔ, eyɛ kɛ fracture ni shwe etsotso paaa. Miye Maame, mibɔ kpee ni mihui no edɔŋ kpee ŋ ni nii kɛ ehuu dɔŋ, ŋmɛne onyɛ amɛ hɛɛ ni otsi bo shikpon. Ofainɛ, frɛ ambulance akɛ mli, oblɛ ni urgent attention yɛ mli — chale, mli mli!
narrative_dagbani: N daa, ka tɔŋ kura kuri nyɛ Cape Coast-Elmina nyɛŋ zuɣu. Biɛla doo, o yɔɣu be Appiah, kaɣa gbɛ o, ka o nyɛ yɛla ka a yɛligu daa. O tiŋ — m gba o tiŋ, wala waa o nuu — ka o yɛligu daa taɣi, n kuli ka o tiŋ paɣ, ka ziim bee o taɣi. Mba yɔɣu be Maame, m bɛ nyɛŋ zuɣu, ka m nya o gbɛŋ nyɛŋ doo, ka niba duri ka bɛ nyɛ bɛ n daa ni bɛ di yɛlim ban tuma. N daa, yi tili sɔŋ pam be, biɛla la bɔri sɔŋ zaa ka yen!
narrative_fante: Me pa wo kyew, biribi bɔne paa aba ɔkwan so a ɛkɔ Cape Coast-Elmina. Ɔbarimaba bi, Appiah, kar bi bɔɔ no wɔ ɔkwan so na ne ho yɛ den paa seesei. Ne nan — mese ne nan anaa ne nsa — wɔ mu yaw kɛse, sɛ fracture na mogya retu no bebree. Me na me din de Maame; merenkɔ na mahunu no da ɔkwan nkyɛn, na nnipa ahyia ho nanso obiara nnim deɛ wɔbɛyɛ. Me pa wo kyew, soma mmoa mmra ntɛm, abofra no hia mmoa seesei ara!
narrative_gurene: N tɩɩma, bʋɣʋm bi yɛligu bee la Cape Coast-Elmina nyɛŋ zuu. Bia biɛɣa, Appiah, kaɣa kpɛŋ o, ka o bɛri mi wʋlwʋl pɛɛlɩ. O nɔɔrɛ — m nyɛ la o nɔɔrɛ baŋ, baŋ o nɩɩrɛ — a yɛligu wʋlwʋl, a nyɛ la a sɔrɩ, ka ziim ɣeya pɛɛlɩ. Mam yɛ fo, mim yɛ Maame — m da bee nyɛŋ zuu ka m nyɛ o bɔɔrɩ fam, ka niba la bee yin bɩ ka o nyɛ la wa bee la pɛɛlɩ. N tɩɩma, sɔŋ mam be zɔŋ — frɛ ambulance wa be zɔŋ, bia a tɩɩma sɔŋ be yinni yinni.

narrative_en: Please, I want to report something that happened here in Dambai. My neighbor's daughter, Fatima, she was knocked by an OA taxi on the road — small small accident but the girl got hurt. My name is Edem, I'm the one calling. She has a cut and some swelling on her leg, but she is walking small so we don't think it is too serious. We just want to make sure she gets checked properly.
narrative_ewe: Meɖekuku, meɖi ŋlɔ nusiwo wòwɔ afisia Dambai. Ɖomenyala ƒe ɖevi nyɔnu, Fatima, OA taxi ƒe akɔ dzo ŋu eƒe ʋɔ dzi — accident le nu kakaka o, gake ɖevi la xɔ te. Ŋkɔnyem enye Edem, meyɔna wò. Eƒe afɔ dzi tsi, eye eƒe afɔ gbɔna — gake eɖoa afɔ kakaka, nenye míeye be nuɖoɖo la megblɔ akpa o. Míeɖi be woatsɔ eŋu kpɔ nyuie, be woakpɔ be eƒe ŋu le teƒe gehã.
narrative_ga: Ofainɛ, mɛ bi kɛ report bɔ ni etsɔɔ wɔ ha Dambai. Mɛ neighbor bi nyɛ dɛbi, Fatima — OA taxi bumo ŋ wɔ kpee — accident no trɔ small small, nɛɛ dɛbi no ehii. Mɛ din nɛ Edem, mɛ nɛ ofrɛ. Ebi cut wɔ ŋ gboo, kɛ swelling bi wɔ ŋ gboo, nɛɛ otsɔɔ ke walk small small — hɛnɛ yɛ lɛ enyɛ serious paa. Yɛ bi kɛ yɛ nɔ check ŋ wɔ hospital ofainɛ.
narrative_dagbani: N daa, n bɛ yɛ ni din n yɛla zuɣu bi tuma Dambai ka. N yagba biɛla, Fatima, OA taxi kaɣa bɔ o nyɛŋ zuɣu — accident bɛ din zoli, amma biɛla dɔ yɛligu. N yuɣu din Edem, maa n bɛ lɛrbu. O tiŋ zuɣu, o kpɛma yɛla be ka o tiŋ daa nyali chɛŋ, amma o tiŋni be ka n da nyɛ din ka yɛligu din n zaɣa. N naa bɛ bori la dɔɣite nyɛ o la maŋ, ka o gba chɛŋ o ka sola.
narrative_fante: Me pa wo kyew, mepɛ sɛ meka bɔne bi a ɛsii ha wɔ Dambai. Me hɔhoɔ ba, Fatima, OA taxi bi bɔɔ no wɔ ɔkwan so — accident kakra, nanso abofra no huri. Me din de Edem, me na mɛfrɛ. Wɔatwa no wɔ ne nan so, na ɛyɛ so kɛse kakra, nanso ɔnantew kakra, enti yennhunu sɛ ɛyɛ dɛm paa. Yɛpɛ sɛ oduruyɛfo bi hwɛ no yiye ara.
narrative_gurene: N tɩɩma, m pɛ kɛ m yɛl mam kɔŋ a pʋɔ bee a Dambai. M yir nɩŋ bibia, Fatima, OA taxi dɔɔ o nyɛŋ zuɣu — accident kɛ ka yɛla pʋɔ, la a bia yɛligu. M yʋɔrɩ Edem, mam yɩɩla. O nɩŋ zɔɔŋ kɛ a pʋ yɩɩla bee o nɩŋ zuɣu, la o tɩɩna bee o nɩŋ la, yɛ be ka yɛla boorɩ ka yɛla ka yɛla. Be pɛ kɛ o kɛ a hospital la o nyɛ sɔŋ tɩɩna.

narrative_en: My name is Mawuli, I am calling to report about a young girl, Sena, who got hurt while working. She was doing some farm work by the roadside on the Kumasi-Sunyani road, somewhere close to Techiman, and something heavy fell on her arm — I think it may be her lower arm, it is swollen and she cannot move it well but she is still conscious and talking. It is not life-threatening but she is in pain and needs to be seen by a doctor soon. Please someone should come and help her.
narrative_ewe: Allo! Nyeŋu nye Mawuli. Meɖi gblɔ mi be ɖevi nyɔnu aɖe — Sena — eyi nam le dɔwɔwɔ me. Ele adaŋawɔwɔ le ʋɔ ŋgɔ le Kumasi-Sunyani ʋɔ dzi, fɛfɛ la Techiman ŋgɔ, eye nubloblo aɖe wu ƒu eƒe asi — meɖi vo be asi ɖeɖe la wòyɔ — asi vɔ kaba, esia gã, eye mate ŋu ɖu asi ɔ nyuie o. Gake eɖo edzi la, egblɔ gblɔ. Esia kpɔ ame ku nu o, gake eyi nam nyuitɔ, eye eɖi dɔkita akpɔ eya ko. Meɖekuku, miƒle nuto be woava kpɔ eye woakpe ɖe eŋu. Akpe!
narrative_ga: Hɛloo, me din bi Mawuli. Mifiɛ kɛ mibɔ ŋ oblɛ nyɔŋ kɛ ni shia, Sena — onya wɔ lɛ work wɔ tsɔɔ. Oba farm work lɛ kpee shishi wɔ Kumasi-Sunyani road lɛ, somewhere faako Techiman, nɛ something heavy bumo ŋ gbɔ lɛ — mishi ni lower arm ni onya. Gbɔ lɛ swollen, entsɔ gbɔ lɛ kɛkɛ o, nɛ ehii ŋ pɛpɛɛpɛ — nɛ ehii mi chale. Nɛ gbeke, oni le, oshishi, onibi le. Ni life-threatening o, nɛ gbeke ehii ŋ kɛɛ, osua doctor bi lɛɛ no. Ofainɛ, onuɔ bi baa kɛ boa Sena. Boa mi!
narrative_dagbani: M yɛligu di Mawuli, n frɛ ka n di yɛli di bia pɔɣ, Sena, o gbini o nuu zuɣu ka o nuu daa zɔli, ka o ti polo naawuni o nuu paɣim naɣa. O di baŋ taɣi bɔri kpɛm zuɣu Kumasi-Sunyani nyɛŋ ka Techiman doli, ka o di kuli kpaɣa yiri ka o gbini o nuu zuɣu — m tɔɣa di o nuu ka paɣim ŋa la o nuu yɛligu, ka nuu ŋa daa yiŋ, ka o ti polo o nuu paɣim naɣa. Amaa o nyɛ o yɛla, o ti yɛ la o nyɛŋmi ka o ti bɛri o la, amaa o yɛligu ka o bori dɔɣite n nya o dali. N daa, mɔɣ la o sɔŋ, naa n yɛli daa fini dama paɣa kuri ka o toliya.
narrative_fante: Me din de Mawuli. Merefrɛ mo sɛ mereha mo nhoma fa saa onipa bi ho — abofra baa bi, Sena, na waa hurt ne ho bere a ɔreye adwuma. Ɔreye farm work akyire Ɔkwan ho — Kumasi-Sunyani road so, beae a ɛbɛn Techiman, na biribi duru a ɛhyɛ so maa ɛtuu ne nsa so — mete sɛ ɛyɛ ne nsa ase no — na ɛama ɛabɔ mu, na ɔntumi mfa ne nsa no yiye, nanso ɔte hɔ a ɔnim ne ho, na ɔrekasakasa. Ɛnyɛ dangerous koraa, nanso ɛyɛ no yaw paa na ɛsɛ sɛ oduruyɛfo bi hunu no ntɛm ara. Me pa wo kyew, obiara a ɔbɛtumi ba no mmra mmoa no.
narrative_gurene: M yɩɩrɩ Mawuli. M frɛ sɔŋ fo wʋm a tuma dɔɔrɩ bi pɔɣɩ yenno — o yɩɩrɩ Sena — o yɛligu a o sɔŋ tuma a o. O la nyɛŋ bʋɔrɩ ka o sɔŋ tuma pʋgɩ, nyɛŋ a bee Kumasi la Sunyani, a bee Techiman nɩŋa. Tɩɩŋa zɔŋ o nɔɔbʋɔrɛ kpɛŋ fam — m niŋ o nɔɔbʋɔrɛ tɔɔ bee — ka o nɔɔbʋɔrɛ sʋɔrɩ ka o kaa tɩɩmɩ la o naŋ. Amaa o ko nyɛ la o la gʋl kpɛŋ. O zi ka pɛ bɔɔrɩ o ŋɔɔbɔ wʋlɔɣɔ, amaa ka yɛligu bee o la o sɔŋɔ kɔɔ nyɛ daktar pɔɣɩ. N tɩɩma, ŋɔɔ yi la sɔŋ o.

narrative_en: My name is Kofi, I am calling to report something that happened to my younger brother Asare at our house here in Cape Coast. Some boys came and they started a fight with him, and one of them hit him on the head. He has a small cut there and his neck is a little sore, but he is conscious and talking. It is not too serious but I wanted to make sure somebody knows about it.
narrative_ewe: Allo, nɔvinya, nyegblɔ Kofi. Mefrɛ wò le esia nyateƒe ɖe nudzadzra siaa ŋu si wɔ ɖe nɔviam Asare ŋu afime le Cape Coast. Aƒetɔ̃wo ava, wo dze kpekple kple eya, eye woɖe ŋu tso asi ŋu ta dzi. Ta dzi le ebi gã kakaka, eye ɖo yi ŋusẽ dzí, gake eɖo edzi, ale kple gblɔgblɔ. Nyanya me le gã o, gake meva ɖe meɖi be ameɖe na nya esia.
narrative_ga: Hɛloo, mi name ye Kofi. Mifra lɛ report bii shi naanɔ mi yɛ Asare — o ye mi manye oblɛ. Shi naanɔ ehii lɛ eba Cape Coast, yɛ ni shia kɛ. Boys bi nɔkɛ hɛ, wɔhɛ Asare fight, nɔ bi shwɛ eni ŋmɛi ni. Ŋmɛi ni, cut kekele bi le oshi, nɔ onyɔ bi hii ni wɔ — kaa, neck ni wɔ hii bii. Ɛɛ, oni le, oyɛ kasa — enimɔ gbɛ o. Enye serious pɛpɛɛpɛ, chale, kɛ mi pɛɛ shia se obiara nim shi.
narrative_dagbani: Allo, m yuli di Kofi. N bɛ yɛ frɛ ka n yɛl'a shɛli din nini paɣa n dan yɔŋ n doo Asare zaŋ ni tɔ yili kɔŋ Cape Coast. Biɛriba yɔbu nini bɔri ka bi zaŋ bi duɣini o, ka o yini o nuu zaŋ o ŋun. Ka yɛligu daa bi kpɛŋ o ŋun zɔŋ biɛla, ka o gozoriya shɛri daa yɛligu dini biɛla. Ama o ti soli kɛ o bɛ yɛla. Ka yɛli ni yeli daa ka ŋani ŋun yɛla, ŋmani o zɔŋ bee ni yɛli, amaa n nɔ maa ni kuli ŋuni n yɛl'a shɛli.
narrative_fante: Me din de Kofi, na merefrɛ mo sɛ mehyɛ nsɛm bi mu a esii wɔ me nuabarima kumaa Asare ho wɔ yɛn fie ha Cape Coast. Mmarima bi baa ha na wɔhyɛɛ fight bi ase ne no, na wɔn mu baako buiɛ no wɔ ne tiri so. Wɔatwitwa no kakra wɔ hɔ na ne kɔn yɛ no yaw kakra, nanso ɔrenya ne ho yiye na ɔreka asɛm. Ɛnyɛ den paa, nanso mepɛɛ sɛ obi nte ho.
narrative_gurene: Mam yɛ fo, mam yʋʋri Kofi. M frɛ frɛ sɔŋ mam kɛ m tɩɩ fo yɛli fo bʋɔrɩ sɛba a wa yiri. M deem Asare, o bee Cape Coast kɛ yiri naŋ. Biis nɩŋa wa kɛ bɩ daa o yɛlɩm, kɛ o yenno o ŋun dɩga. O ŋun pɔa yɩɛ pʋrɩ nɩŋa, kɛ o nɔɣɔ yɛligu pʋrɩ pʋrɩ. Ama o ko nyɛ, o bee yɛlimu. Ka bʋɔrɩ ka zɔri o yɛligu la. M bɔɔrɩ ŋɛn nɩŋa a sɔŋ mam tɩɩm bia nyɛ bʋɔrɩ yɛla.

narrative_en: Please, I am calling to report something that happened to an old man, Kwabena, along the Cape Coast-Elmina road. He got burned, I think some hot liquid or something touched his arm — it doesn't look too deep but the skin is red and he is in some pain. I am Ofori, I was passing by when I saw him and stopped to help. He is elderly so I was worried, but he is conscious and talking. We just need someone to come and check him properly.
narrative_ewe: Meɖekuku, mefrɛ ɖe asi le nudzadzra si wɔ na agbenyaga ɖeka, Kwabena, le Cape Coast kple Elmina ʋɔ dzi. Eɖo tsi kata alo nubibla ɖeka wu asi — me nya nyuie o, gake ƒo nyuie o, eme vɔ gã o. Asi le ŋgɔgble dzi eye eyi nam geɖe. Ŋkɔnye enye Ofori — me le ʋɔ dzi le núdze le megbɔ eƒe si akɔ kpe ŋunye. Srɔ̃ la eƒu agbe eye eɖo edzi, egblɔ na míe. Medi amewo aƒo ŋú abe ɖɔɖɔe abe nye ameɖeame ene o — mele kpɔ ame si awo gɔmedzedze eye anye nane le eŋu. Meɖekuku, frɛ ame ame ava kpɔ eƒe ŋu nyuie.
narrative_ga: Ofainɛ, mitsɛɔ call bi frɛ shikpon ni ehiɛ gbɔmɔ bi, Kwabena, loo Cape Coast-Elmina road ŋ. Ehia eyi ni ehɔ ŋ — mitsɛɔ hot liquid bi kɛ shianɔ bi bumo eŋ gbɔ ŋ — enimɔ deep o, yɛ skin ni shoo jɛɛ, kɛ ehii mi shi naŋ. Miŋ Ofori — mibii kpee ŋ meko, mi hɛ no mi kpee ni boa no. Gbɔmɔ oo, ano sane mi nibii — yɛ oni le kɛ otsumo. Yɛ tsɛ onukpa bi ni oyi shi kɛ check no wala.
narrative_dagbani:
N daa, m bɛ yu shɛli maa ku laɣim bia kpɛma yɛla — o yɛliŋ Kwabena, o gbini Cape Coast-Elmina nyɛŋ ŋɔ soo. Kɔɣim bɔri o nuu — m gbini o yɛ kɔɣim shɛligu nɔɣim bo daa, kaa yɛ tiim naa shɛligu. O nuu kuli gbɛhiɣu, amaa a ti yɛ zoli daa. Ka yɛligu o nuu. M nyɛ ko yɛ tiim daa ni a zoli daa, amaa ka yɛligu.

Maa nyɛliŋ Ofori. N bɛ gbini nyɛŋ ŋɔ yɔ, ka n nyɛ o, ka n zani n sɔŋ o. Kpɛma maa, n nyɛ o ka ka n yɛ niŋ yɛligu, amaa o maa yɛla daa — o bɛ nyɛ soli, o bɛ yɛli. Ka saan, ti bori dɔɣite o naa yɔɣɔsim nɔɣim ku nyɛ o ni gani daa.

N daa, yi frɛ ninbandee naa ambulance yi na o nyɛ daa.
narrative_fante: Me pa wo kyew, merefrɛ sɛ maka biribi a ɛsii opanyin bi, Kwabena, ho — ɔkwan a ɛkɔ Cape Coast-Elmina no so. Ogye ogyam — mese ehwehwe sɛ nsuohyehye anaa biribi a ɛhyew no kaa ne nsa ho. Ɛnyɛ dodo ase, na ne ho nhoma ayɛ kɔkɔɔ, na ɛyɛ no yaw kakra. Me din de Ofori — meretwam na mhuu no, na mesiee mboa no. Ɔyɛ opanyin, enti mebɔ hu, nanso ɔte ne ho na ɔreka asɛm. Yehia obi a ɔbɛba hwɛ no yiye.
narrative_gurene: N tɩɩma, m frɛ sɛbɩ ade a zɔɣ Kwabena, nindaan, a nyɛŋ Cape Coast ne Elmina chɛɛ. O yɛba ka bɔbɔ — m nyɛ ka zom kɔba a gʋŋ a ŋɔ tɩŋ a nʋɣa, ka a yɛligu kɔ la tɩɩ. Ka a pɔlɔ yela ka a nyɛ yem yem, ama a ko a yɛligu bɩ bɔɔrɩ ŋɔ. O nɔŋ o yɛla kɔ la m, ade m Ofori — m nyɛŋɔ o la, m yɩɩma m sɔŋ o. O yɛ nindaan ne mam la biɛ, ama o ko a nyɛ sɔŋɔ ka o gori. N tɩɩma, sɔŋ tuma bia sɛbɩ o wa nyɛ o na a yɛla kɔ.

narrative_en: My daughter Naa Akorkor got burned this morning here at our home in Sunyani. She was playing near the kitchen and she touched the hot pot and it fell on her arm. The skin is blistering and she is crying a lot, but she is still awake and talking. Please, I am Fatima and I need to know what I should do for her before we can reach the hospital.
narrative_ewe: Allo! Meɖekuku, kpe ɖe ŋunye! Ɖevinyɔnu ce Naa Akorkor xɔ ɖutsi gbã egbe afi sia Sunyani. Eƒu ame ɖe kpotso ŋu, eye woƒo aga vevie si le dɔwɔƒe kɔŋ le ŋu, abe wona ane eye aga do agbenɔƒe dzi agbɔ wu eƒe asi. Afɔɖeɖe le eƒe ŋu dzi tso ɖutsi ƒu. Naa Akorkor le gble kaka, gake eɖo edzi eye ele ɖoɖo. Meɖekuku, menyé Fatima, eye meɖi nyá be mase na efoa nu wu ele asi eye wóte ŋu yi Asiwo. Woaɖe mia ƒu akpa ka?
narrative_ga: Hɛloo, boa mi! Dɛbi bi, Naa Akorkor, shɛ ehii — ebumo hot pot gbɔ dzɔ gbɔ la. Saa gbɔŋ, eni le kitchen shi, otsɔɔ kaa ni pot la ekɛ yɛ wɔ mli, emɔ ebumo gbɔ dzɔ. Gbɔ la skin etsoo blisters, oni le, one ni edwɛ pɛɛ — kɛ etɔŋ yɛle ehe kɛkɛ. Ofainɛ, mi nyɛ Fatima — kɛ mibɔ shia ni mitse mli ni manɛ kɛ obi nmii hospital mli? Ni bo mi — nii manɛ ni mɛ boa ni dɛbi la agbaawɔ hospital ka le?
narrative_dagbani: N daa, o nyɛ Naa Akorkor, m biɛla puɣa, o gbari ka tɔɣim nyim datɔ zaŋ, o maa Sunyani, ti yili kura. O bɛ pam yɛligu chɛŋ doo din bɛ nyɛla yili kɔŋ, ka o nuu tii kpan ko, ka o kpɛŋ o nuu zuɣu. O nuu zuɣu nyɛ ti kua ka pam, ka zim biɛba bɛ bee, ka o wuhi daa — ka o yɛli bia yɛligu wuhim, ama o nyɛ soli, o bɛ yɛ ka o yɛla. N daa, maa nyɛ Fatima. N bori shɛŋ bɔri la: bɛ lana maa n pam din m naa naŋ n biɛla nyɛla yili sɔŋ tii ka ti fara naa nyɛla yili.
narrative_fante: Me ba Naa Akorkor hyew sɛ ɔbɔɔ no anɔpa yi ha fii yɛn fie Sunyani. Ɔregyina aburow mue ho na ɔkaa apɔtɔ hyew no na apɔtɔ no tuu gu n'nsa so. N'nhoma no wɔ bɔbɔ a ɛreba so na ɔsu paa, nanso ɔhunu adeɛ na ɔkasa. Me pa wo kyew, mene Fatima na mehwehwɛ sɛ ɛsɛ sɛ meyɛ deɛn mma no ansa na yɛato hospital.
narrative_gurene: Yinɛ! M bia Naa Akorkor sɔɔra sa soba o yɛligu wɔɔ. O bɔɔrɩ Sunyani, yɛ yir nɛ, n sɩɩ. O bɔɔrɩ dukuɣu nɛ, ka o sota yɛɣɩ sɔŋ kpee ka o bee yɛligu sɔŋ, ka o taɣa kpaa — o nuo taɣa kpaa ka o taɣa o nuo yeli ka aŋ bɔɔrɩ o nuo zuri. O nuo ziia bee, ka a ŋɔɔna daa ŋmaa o nuo so. O bee kpee — o yɛligu ka o yam mɛ, ka o nyɛ ka o baa yeli. N tɩɩma — mɛ yɛliɣu Fatima, ka m bʋɔra a sɔŋ mam nyɛ mam yele be mam bia yɛɣɩ nɛ, mam sɩɛŋ ka ti tum hospital.

narrative_en: My husband Selorm fell this afternoon at our house here in Nhyiaeso. He was trying to climb down from somewhere and he just slipped and landed hard on his side. I'm Ama, I'm his wife. He said his chest and stomach area is paining him small but he can still move around, so I don't think it's too serious. We just want to know if we should bring him somewhere or what we should do.
narrative_ewe: Allo, meɖekuku. Nyɔnya nye Ama, ŋutifafa nye Selorm ewu ƒu fifia azãsiƒe afimɛ afi la Nhyiaeso. Eyi ɖe ame ƒe dzidzi le nu bubu dzi eye woɖo ƒu vivi. Eɖo ɖevi ŋɔ kple abɔ ŋɔ dzi — ame ŋɔ la le enyiyã o, gake etso ɖe nukae me. Eyi nam be eyi ŋutsu ŋɔ small, gake edzi le ŋutititi — atu ɖe agɔ ŋutɔ. Mate kpɔ agbe be evɔ nyui o, gake nyɔnya mate ŋu aɖe sia ɖe aɖe o. Mele be mia ɖe ŋu yi asiwo la o mɔ? Abe, mia dze asi ɖe akɔ na wo yi, ame ne gblɔ mia se? Meɖekuku, kpe ɖe ŋumia.
narrative_ga: Hɛloo, ofainɛ boa mi. Ni wɔ mi shikpon Selorm, obumo akwaa yɛ nɔ kɛ yɛ dan ni Nhyiaeso. Oheɛ ŋ ehiɛ ni kpee le bii, obumo gbaa fɛɛ ni gbɔɔ wɔ kɔŋ. Mii ŋ Ama, ni yɛ ŋ shikpon. Ɔ shi ni gbɔɔ wɔ shi hii ni — ni enye hu toto toto, la ɔ tumi tswa tswa dɛɛ. Chale, miti suɔ enye bone pii. Yɛ pɛɛ shɛ ni yɛ ni nyɛ — yɛ fɛɛ ni bɛ ɔ fie yɛ hospital, abii yɛ ni nyɛ dɛ?
narrative_dagbani: Allo, N daa. M biɛm Selorm gbini yɛsili kɔŋ ni ka ti yili ka Nhyiaeso. O nye kɛ o bori fuli chɛ bɔŋ ŋun zo kana, ka o kpɛŋ lahibi, ka o gbii o tali puŋ. N yɛligu Ama, n nyɛ o pɔɣu. O yɛli ka o digindi nɛ o pam bɛri o biligi yɛligu, ama o zaai tuna, o bee chɛ paɣari. Ka m nyɛ ka o yɛla ti kpɛ daa tooi. Ti bori n bɛ yɛ ni, ti yi o kana tɔ dɔɣite yɛla, kaana ni ti mali ka ti di baŋ saha ŋun n ti bɔri?
narrative_fante: Me kunu Selorm tuu fam nnora awia yi ha yɛn dan mu wɔ Nhyiaeso. Ɔreyɛ adwuma bi so ɔrehwie ase na ɔsuruu na ɔkɔtoo ne nkyɛn so dennen. Me din de Ama, meyɛ ne yere. Ɔse ne bruwa ne ne yafunu fa yɛ no yaw kakra, nanso ɔtumi kyina ne ho, enti menna adi sɛ ɛyɛ serious papa. Yɛpɛ sɛ yɛhunu sɛ yɛmmɛn no bɛba hospital anaa yɛnyɛ dɛn.
narrative_gurene: My husband Selorm fell this afternoon at our house here in Nhyiaeso. He was trying to climb down from somewhere and he just slipped and landed hard on his side. I'm Ama, I'm his wife. He said his chest and stomach area is paining him small but he can still move around, so I don't think it's too serious. We just want to know if we should bring him somewhere or what we should do.

narrative_en: Please, something happened here in Bantama this afternoon. My neighbor's son, Issah — he was eating at a roadside food stall when the kerosene stove there flared up suddenly and the heat hit his chest and stomach area. It's not too serious, just the skin is red and sore, and he's awake and talking fine. Kweku here, I was nearby when it happened. Can someone come and check him because we are not sure if it needs treatment?
narrative_ewe: Meɖekuku, nuɖoɖo ɖeka wɔ afisia Bantama egbe fifia ŋdi sia. Ŋutɔ aƒetɔ ɖevi, Issah — ele ɖuɖu dzie agble ƒe dukɔwo dome, gake kerosene stove si le afe ɔ tsɔ fie vovovo kpɔ, eye dzodzo ɔ dze n'ɔ ɖevi ŋɔ kple abɔ dome. Ame nyuie o, gake akpa ŋu le tsotso, eye eɖo edzi eye ele ƒoƒo be yemedze ŋu gbɔ ɔ. Ŋkeke sia Kweku wò hã le afe sia si nusiwo wɔ ɔ. Ame aɖe ate ŋu va ɖo n'ɔ su, elabena mia nyekpe se be ede asiwo bena mele ɔ?
narrative_ga: Ofainɛ, something happen here Bantama this afternoon. Neighbor mi ba, Issah — ono ne di adɛ roadside food stall kɛ kerosene stove no flare up suddenly, kɛ heat no tse ono gbɔɔ kɛ shi faafo. Enyɛ serious pɛpɛɛpɛ, skin no red kɛ sore, kɛ oni le — ono kasa yɛ fine. Kweku dɛ, mibɛ kɛ ono shi naa ni happen. Onipa bi yaa bɛ check ono, kakra, because wɔ nyɛ shɛ ni sɔ treatment anaa?
narrative_dagbani: N daa, bɛ kɔri ka yɛla kuli paɣa Bantama yɛ ka zaa. N yigban biɛla, Issah — o di zaɣa nyɛŋ doo zina ka kerosin mɛ gbɛŋ o zuɣu tuma tuma, ka yɛligu kuri o digindi ni o pam zuɣu. Ka yɛla ka yɛli sɔŋ sana kana, so ka o niŋ gbɛŋ nyɔri ni ka yɛligu o, ka o nyɛ soli ni o yɛlimdi sɔŋ. Kweku yɛ ka N be ka yɛla paɣa n naa o so. Dɔɣite doo waɣila ki nyɛ o, bala ka tia ni bɛ ni o zaɣa sɔŋ ka bii sori?
narrative_fante: Me pa wo kyew, biribi asi ha wɔ Bantama yi awiber yi. Me hɔ dwumayɛfo ba, Issah — ɔredi aduan wɔ ɔkwan ho aduan stan mu na kerosene stove a ɛwɔ hɔ no hyew kwa na ogya no bɔɔ n'apɔw — ne bruwa ne yafunu ho no. Ɛnyɛ den paa — ne ho ho nko ara na ɛyɛ kɔkɔɔ na ɛyɛ no yaw kakra, na ɔrewɔ hɔ na ɔrekan asɛm yiye. Me ne Kweku, na mewɔ hɔ bere a ɛsii no. Obi bɛtumi aba nhwɛ no anaa? Ɛfiri sɛ yɛnnim yiye sɛ ɛhia treatment anaa dabi.
narrative_gurene: Please, something happened here in Bantama this afternoon. My neighbor's son, Issah — he was eating at a roadside food stall when the kerosene stove there flared up suddenly and the heat hit his chest and stomach area. It's not too serious, just the skin is red and sore, and he's awake and talking fine. Kweku here, I was nearby when it happened. Can someone come and check him because we are not sure if it needs treatment?

narrative_en: "Please, something happened on the Bolgatanga-Bawku road, near Navrongo side. A young man, Kweku, he was a passenger in a dropping taxi and a tipper truck hit them, I don't know exactly how it happened but by the time we reached there he was sitting on the ground holding his head and his neck was paining him. He is conscious but he looks confused and there is a small cut on his head that is bleeding. My name is Adwoa, I am one of the people who stopped to help him, please we need someone to come because we are not sure how serious it is."
narrative_ewe: Meɖekuku, nudzadzra ɖe le Bolgatanga-Bawku ʋɔ dzi, ʋɔ si le Navrongo ŋɔ. Ɖevi ŋutsu ɖeka, Kweku, eƒe ŋkɔ enye Kweku, ewo taxi dropping me woɖo ƒe eme, eye tipper truck ɖeka tso xo wo. Mese gɔme nyuie o be nuɖoɖo la ɖo aleke, gake ŋkeke si míaƒo ɖe fia la, míakpɔ be ele ɖo funu le afɔƒe dzi, etso eƒe ta xo. Eyi ŋ eƒe ɖo nam dzi. Eɖo edzi, gake ebu ebu vovovo le, nane le eƒe xɔdzidzi me la. Gbɔ le eƒe ta dzi kpekpekpe ɖeka le tsotso dzi. Ŋkɔnyame enye Adwoa. Míele ame siwo wɔ ame la siwo tsɔ asi ɖe eŋu. Meɖekuku, miaɖi be ame na va fifia, elabena míekpɔ gɔme nyuie o be nu si wɔ ame la ku etɔ̃ si le didi.
narrative_ga:
Ofainɛ, something happen Bolgatanga-Bawku road no, near Navrongo side. Ŋmɛi kɛ oblɛ bi — Kweku — ɔyɛ passenger wɔ dropping taxi bi nu, tipper truck bi bɔ wɔn, minyɛ shɛɛ nyɛ noko bi ehii mi, kɛ ɔnyɛ noko bi ehii mi — na ɔkɛ yɛ jɛŋ akɛ wɔn fɛɛ kɛ oshii bi, ɔjɛ kuntsung ŋmɛi nu, nɛɛ ŋmɛi koo ni hii ŋ. Ɔni le, kɛ ɔyɛ le shwane — noko bi ɛtɔŋ enimɔ gbɛ le ŋ — shi tɔŋ nibii. Ŋmɛi nu cut kɛ shwe etsotso ŋ — enye kɛ bii bii fɛɛ ni, kɛ shwe no etsotso.

Miŋ yɛ Adwoa — miŋ yɛ ŋmɛi wɔ nɔ bi kɛ wɔ stop wɔ bo ŋ. Ofainɛ, yɛ hia someone ni bi ba — mitsɔ shɛɛ enye serious noko bi ŋ, kɛ yɛ hia ambulance ba fast fast. Frɛ ambulance ba, ofainɛ!
narrative_dagbani: N daa, yɛla gbini ka Bolgatanga-Bawku nyɛŋ zuɣu, Navrongo doli. Saɣim bia, Kweku, o bɛ la dropping taxi koom, ka tipper truck ŋmɛ bɛ, n nan nyɛ tuma nins ka o gbini ka o ti kpɛŋ wɔri, n yɛli ka n nan kuli bɛ, o bɛ pam gbini, o zuu yuli o ŋun, ka a yɛligu bee o kɔliɣu. O nyɛ o maa yɛla, a maŋa o nyɛŋga o yiɛ ka yɛla kpeya. Zuu o ŋun zuɣu gbɛligu kaama bɛɛ ka ziim bee o. N yuli nyɛ Adwoa, maa bɛ bɛ la nima nins ka ti sɔŋ o. N daa, ti bori nibɛ din naa ka ta, ka ti nan nyɛ ka kuli yɛla tuma daa wɔri ka bala.
narrative_fante: Me pa wo kyew, biribi abɛyɛ Bolgatanga-Bawku ɔkwan so, Navrongo bɛn ho. Okunafo bi, Kweku, ɔyɛ passenger wɔ dropping taxi mu — tipper truck bi bɔɔ wɔn. Minnim sɛɛ nkyin na ɛyɛɛ ɔhɔ, nanso sɛ yɛduu hɔ a, ɔtee ase wɔ fam no rekyea ne tiri, na ne kɔn no reye no yaw. Ɔnim ne ho, nanso ɔte sɛ confused, na krasɛ cut kakra bi wɔ ne tiri so a mogya retu firi mu. Me din de Adwoa — meyɛ obi wɔ nnipa a yɛsuu ase na yɛboa no no mu. Me pa wo kyew, yehia obi mmra ha, efisɛ yennnim yiye sɛɛ ɛyɛ den koraa.
narrative_gurene: N tɩɩma, bilɩŋ bee Bolgatanga-Bawku nyɛŋ zi, Navrongo nɔbila. Bia bila, Kweku, o bee dropping taxi pʋɔ, ka tipper truck bɔɔ biem o bɩ. M ko nyɛ sɩd naaŋ nɩ bɛ bee la, la amɩ yi ka ŋ bɩ yi kɔŋ a, o bee pɛɛya fam, o nunuu dee o nuu, ka o kʋŋ yɛligu nɩ. O ko a nyɛ amá, la o lee lɩɩ kʋn. Ka kʋkɔba yɔɣɔ bee o nunuu zi, ka ziim bee o. M yɛligu Adwoa, mɩ bee nɔ bɩla nɩ yi yi sɔŋ o. N tɩɩma, ŋ nɩ tuma kʋŋkɔm a nɩ yi — bɩ nyɛ la o yɛligu nɩ dee naa.

narrative_en: Please, I want to report something that happened to a young boy here in Ho town centre. His name is Kpodo, and he was doing some work — I think he was helping carry goods or doing some kind of labour job — when something fell and hurt his arm. Fatima is my name, I'm the one calling; I was just nearby when it happened. The boy's hand is a bit swollen but he is still able to move around, so it doesn't look too serious. Someone should still come and check him because he is young and we want to make sure he is okay.
narrative_ewe: Meɖekuku, meɖi bu ŋuɖoɖo ɖe nane wɔa ɖe Ɖevi ŋutsu ŋu afisi le Ho agbɔta me sia. Ŋkɔe nye Kpodo. Esia wɔa dɔ — mehiã be eƒe adzro wɔ ablɔ anyi, alo eƒe dɔkɔkɔ anyi le dɔ la me — gake nu ɖeka wuia do si adze ɖe asi ŋu, ma yi ŋunye. Fatima nye ŋkɔe, nye ame si wɔ telefon la; mele fifia hã be nu si wɔ la tɔ. Ɖevi la ƒe asi xɔ bobe geɖe, gake elé tɔtrɔtɔ ɖe ye ɖo, eye enyo nyuie bisã. Gake meɖe kuku na ŋutɔ aɖe aƀu ava kpɔ ɖe eŋu, elabena ele vɔvi eye miaɖi nyɔ be eɖo edzi nyuie.
narrative_ga: Ofainɛ, mɛ bɛ report bɔɔ shi wɔ He ni le afɔ. Oblɛ bi name nɔ Kpodo, eye onyɛ work — mɛ suɛ ɔ lɛ boa carry goods ɛnyɛ labour work bi — akɛ bɔɔ bi bumo wiemɔ ehii nɔ gbɔ. Fatima nɔ mɔ yɛ name, mɛnɔ lɛ frɛ; mɛ le shikpong kɛ ni enyɛ. Oblɛ nɔ gbɔ swollen kakra, kɛ ɔni le fɛɛ eye ɔtumi kɛ mli — enyɛ gbɔɔ ehii o. Kɛ chale, onipa bi nyɛ bɔ bɛ check nɔ — oblɛ dɔŋ eye yɛ pɛ sane ɔ le fɛɛ.
narrative_dagbani: N daa, n bɛ yɛ ni n yɛla a din kuli ka biɛla yɛ la ka o be Ho dɔɣiri. O yɛligu din bi o Kpodo. O bɛ ka tuma — n yi mi ka o bɛ sɔŋ ka o nyɛ kɔɣim kaa o bɛ tuma la tuma biɛɣu — ka kɔɣim yini gbini ka a yɛligu o nuu. N yɛligu din bi maa Fatima, n naŋa bi frɛla la. N bɛŋ daa ka o nuu mi shee biɛɣu, amma o maŋ tibu tiŋa, di nini pa yɛ kpali kuli kana yɛligu nyafuli. Amma, dɔɣite pam yini ni wa yɔhi o, bala Kpodo bi nyafuli, ka ti bɔri ka o nya timali.
narrative_fante: Me pa wo kyew, mepɛ sɛ meka biribi a asi ha wɔ Ho town centre. Abofra na ɛfa no — ne din de Kpodo. Ɔreyɛ adwuma bi — mese ɔreboa kari nneɛma anaasɛ labour work bi — na biribi tuu gu so biaa huruu no nsa mu. Me din de Fatima, mene nea ɛfrɛ no; na mewɔ ho kɔse a ɛba. Abofra no nsa ho abɔborɔ kakra, nanso ɔtumi nante dada, enti enhu sɛ ɛyɛ hu paa. Nanso obi ba nhwɛ no, efisɛ ɔyɛ abofra na yɛpɛ sɛ yɛhunu sɛ ɔho yɛ.
narrative_gurene: N tɩɩma, M bʋɔrɩ sɔŋ mam kɛ m gom ade a yɛlɩ bee bia anaŋ Ho taŋa zʋɣʋ. O yʋɣʋrɩ Kpodo, a o laa tʋʋma — m nyɛ o laa sɔŋ kɛ nɔɔna yiisi tɩɩs sʋka, walla o laa tʋʋma biig biig — a ade bʋɔɔrɩ o nɔɔ zʋɣʋ, a a yɛligu o nɔɔ. Mam yʋɣʋrɩ Fatima — mam n frɛ ɔ yɛlɩ nɩ; m laa bee wʋllɩ zʋɣʋ a gɛm la daa wa. Bia nɔɔ bɔɔrɩ biig biig, a o tɔɔna nyɛ wʋllɩ, la ka nyɛ sɔɔŋ pɛɛlɩ. Amaa nindɔŋ a wa nyɛ o, bala o bia dɛɛ, a tɩ bʋɔrɩ kɛ o di laafɩ.

narrative_en: Please, something happened on the Cape Coast-Elmina road and I need to report it. My sister Adwoa was in a trotro that got hit by another vehicle — the car just came from nowhere and knocked into them. She has a cut on her head and she's been complaining that her neck is paining her a lot, but she's awake and talking. I'm Selorm, I'm with her now by the roadside. We need somebody to come and help us because the bleeding is not stopping.
narrative_ewe: Meɖekuku, nudzadzra ɖe afɔ wo Cape Coast-Elmina ʋɔ dzi eye meɖi gblɔ ame mi. Nɔvi nyɔnu ce Adwoa le trotro ŋu bena akɔ bubuawo de wo dzi — akɔ la va fito le xɔxɔe me eye ede wo ƒu tegbee. Dzi le etsotso le eto dzi eye eyi ŋutɔ ɖo le eto ŋkeke bubu. Gake eɖo edzi eye eto le gblɔgblɔ. Ŋkɔnyé enye Selorm, mele eŋu fifia ɖe ʋɔ ŋkɔ. Meɖekuku, mia hiã ame aɖe aʋa kpe ɖe mia ŋu, elabena dzi la dzotso o.
narrative_ga: Ofainɛ, bɔ accident bi etso Cape Coast-Elmina road so, mi nyɛ report etso. Minaaŋ Adwoa ni trotro bi dzɛ — kaa bi ba fɛɛ kɛ etia wɔ. Ŋmɛi ni shwe etsotso, kɛ oni nɔkɔ nɔ ni enyɛ nii ehii mi kɛkɛ — ŋɔ ojekoo kɛ otsɛ gbo. Miŋ Selorm, mi le ni tsɛ kpee dzɛ ŋɔ. Mifɛɛ onii bi ba bo mi ha, boys, shwe entsotso o — entso ngmɛŋ.
narrative_dagbani: N daa, n bɛ yɛ ni yɛli n maa yɛla dali Cape Coast-Elmina nyɛŋ zuɣu ka n tɔ report o. N puna Adwoa naa trotro yibo ka kaɣa dali o kɔri yɛ ni ka o bɔri buɣim — kaɣa ŋun yeli o piɛla ka o kɔŋ di o. O ŋun gbini ka ziim bee o, ka o n'yɛli o kuu yɛligu daa, ka a yɛligu ka o yɛli a bee daa. Ama o bee soli ka o bɛ yɛla. Maa nyɛ Selorm, n be o kɔŋ dali nyɛŋ duli. Yi maa sɔŋ — yi tili ŋun dali ka yi maa sɔŋ, barka ziim bee o ka o ta gani.
narrative_fante: Me pa wo kyew, biribi asi Cape Coast-Elmina ɔkwan so na mehia sɛ mɛka ho asɛm. Me nuabaa Adwoa naa ɔwɔ trotro mu a kar fofor bɛbɔ won — kar no baa firi baabi koraa na ɛbɔɔ won. Wɔataa ne tiri so na ɔreka sɛ ne kɔn yɛ no yaw paa, nanso ɔda ne adwene so na ɔreka asɛm. Me ne Selorm, mɛka ne ho seesei ha fa ɔkwan ho. Yehia onipa bi obɛboa yɛn ɛfiri sɛ mogya a retu no no nnyae.
narrative_gurene: N tɩɩma, sominu bɔɔrɩ Cape Coast-Elmina nyɛŋ soo, a mam yɛ n yɛla. M pio Adwoa bɛ trotro nɩ, ka kaɣa baŋ o la — kaɣa a yi nyɛŋ ɣeeŋ ka o wa tɩ trotro la. O ŋun waare, ka o bee bee o ŋun dune a yɛligu wʋlɩ, ama o koo aŋa ka o yɛla. Mam yɛ mɩɩ Selorm, m bɛ ne o la nyɛŋ yoro. Ti daama nɩŋa a wa sɔŋ ti, bala o ziim a yala ka a kaa kpɛŋ.

narrative_en: Please, something very bad has happened here at our family compound in Tamale. My brother Boateng, he was knocked down by a car on his way back home, and they brought him here but his leg is in a very bad condition — it is twisted and there is a lot of bleeding that we cannot stop. He is a young boy, maybe nineteen years, and he is crying and shaking and we are very scared for him. I am Hamza, I am the one calling, please we need help to come here fast because we don't know what to do.
narrative_ewe: Meɖekuku, nufe gãtɔ aɖe wɔe afisia ɖe miafe ƒome le Tamale. Nɔvime Boateng, akɔ aɖe tso eŋu le ʋɔ dzi gake eye wòtsɔe de afisia, gake eƒe afɔ le nu vɔ̃ gã me — ewɔ dzoto eye dzi le etsotso ko eye mate ŋu dzɔ o. Ɖevi geɖe la, efoa ƒe ewo kple ɖeka abe, eye eyi nam, eƒu agbe ko eye enya dzidzɔ si. Míe ŋutɔwo, medi egblim. Mídzí vevie eƒe dzɔdzɔ. Nyem la Hamza, nye ɖe ne wò frɛ. Meɖekuku, kpɔ miŋu vite, efɔ̃ afɔ aɖeke le miŋu o eye miegblɔ abe nɛ wòwɔ na mi o.
narrative_ga: Ofainɛ, something very bad abɔɔ wɔ ha Tamale, we family compound ni. Minu Boateng, kaa bumo no kpee wɔ ye ni ba fie, na wɔ ba no bring am come ha — ŋ gboo ni le condition kɛɛ kɛɛ, ni twisted na shwe etsotso ŋ na mitsɔ stop am o. Oblɛ ni no, maybe nineteen years, na oni hii na oni shɛkɛ shɛkɛ na yɛ ji bii kɛ no. Mi yɛ Hamza, mi yɛ one ni frɛ wɔ — ofainɛ, yɛ hia help ba ha fast fast bɔɔ, bɔ yɛni nyɛ bii ni etɔ ŋ o.
narrative_dagbani: N daa, yɛla kan daa mɔɣli daa bɛ tikuri ka o ti tuma nyɛla waa. M daa yaba Boateng, kaɣa gbɔ o n-daa o be sa yili saɣim, ka bɛ kpe o wa ama, ka o tiŋ yɛligu ka a ka yɛligu — o tiŋ yiɣisi ka ziim bee o ka ti pa ka ti nyɛ koo ti zaŋ zaŋ. O bia din, biɛla, o pahi pia ni awɛi lan be, ka o soi be ka o yuɣu yuɣu ka ti nyɛ o ti maa yɛla daa ni yɛligu. M din bɛ Hamza, m din bɛ bi nyɔɣim. N daa, sɔŋ maa ya ti wa malo duu ka ti nyɛ bee din ti bɛ tuma ka ti dabiɣi.
narrative_fante: Me pa wo kyew, biribi bɔne paa abu ha wɔ yɛn fifo compound no mu wɔ Tamale. Me nuabarima Boateng, kar bi bɔɔ no wɔ ɔkwan so bere a ɔresan akɔ fie, na wɔde no brɛɛ ha, nanso ne nan yɛ bad paa — ɛpɛpɛɛpɛ na mogya retu no, na yɛntumi nsi no. Ɔyɛ aberante koro, ebia mfe dunna so na ɔha wɔ no mu, na ɔretetew na ɔrewosow, na yɛsuro ama no paa. Me ne Hamza, me na mereka saa, me pa wo kyew, yɛhia mmoa mmra ha ntɛm ntɛm efisɛ yɛnnim deɛ yɛbɛyɛ.
narrative_gurene: N tɩɩma, something wʋlʋm yɛligu a nɩ nɔ yir kom la Tamale. M pio Boateng, kaɣa bɔɔ o nyɛŋ zuɣu o la yir pɛlɛ, la b'a de o bɩ la. O nɩŋ la ka ka yɛligu wʋlʋm — o nɩŋ bʋlɩ la ka ziim bee o ka ti be a tʋl o la. O yaa bia, a bia sɔɔ, wa nɩ bi-nɔ ayima nɔ (nineteen), la o naa naa ka o sɔɔsɔɔrɩ la ka ti gbeŋ wʋlʋm o zuɣu. Mɛ yɩɩra nɩ Hamza, mɛ la a tuma la. N tɩɩma, sɔŋ ti — yi yiri nɔ wʋlʋm ka ti be a nyɛ bii la ti na maalɩ.

narrative_en: Please, something very bad has happened here in Asokwa, Kumasi — an old man called Owusu nearly drowned and we are very scared for him. He is breathing but it is very slow and weak, and his head and neck look very swollen and strange, like something is badly wrong inside. I am Kwame, I am his neighbor, and I was the one who pulled him out of the water but he is not responding well at all. Please we need help to come here quickly because this man is old and he looks very serious.
narrative_ewe: Meɖekuku, nudzadzra gãtɔ aɖe wɔ ɖe afim Asokwa, Kumasi — srɔ̃ aɖe, Owusu wòtɔɣe, eƒe tsi tsɔ eɖe eme blewu, eye míewɔ ɣeyiɣi dzi eŋu tadekeme. Edzi ƒu agbe, gake eƒu agbe le blewublewu kple efɔɔfɔ, eye eƒe ta kple ɖo ɖɔ abe nu bubu aɖe le eƒe nu me ene. Nyeŋu Kwame, mele eƒe agbetɔ me, eye nye wɔe tsɔ eɖe tsi me vaɖe ɣeyiɣi, gake eŋlɔ abɔbɔ me nyuie o. Meɖekuku, mïdi kpɔ ŋunye dɔɔ, wòa va fii kpɛkpɛ, esi ŋu srɔ̃ sia nye, eye eƒe dɔ le ŋutɔgã o.
narrative_ga: Ofainɛ, something very bad abɔ wa ha Asokwa, Kumasi — gbɔmɔ bi wɔ Owusu, eni nearly drowned mli, naanɔ yɛɛ okpaka hei gbɔi. Eni hɔmɔ le, kɛ hɔmɔ shɔ slow wɔ weak kɛkɛ, naanɔ ŋmɛi wɔ eni gbo shɔ very swollen wɔ strange — chale, something dzɛ badly wrong inside ŋ. Miŋ Kwame, mi ŋmɛi neighbor ŋ, naanɔ mɛ yɛ ŋ lɛ ni jɛi nsɔŋ mli, kɛ eni responding well o shi. Ofainɛ, yɛ hia help ni bɔ wa ha fɛɛfɛɛ — gbɔmɔ ni, naanɔ eni shɔ very serious kɛkɛ.
narrative_dagbani: N daa, ka yɛligu paɣibu daa ti yɛra ka dini Asokwa, Kumasi — nindaan yɔŋ Owusu, o kpɛŋ pii chɛɛ ka o ti soli ka o ti ku. O nyɛ o maa yɛla ayi yɛla kum, ka doo nyɛ o yɔŋ ŋun ni gbini ka o diɣim bee o zoli bi zoli ŋani — o paɣ ka yɛligu be din yɛri o ŋun ni o yɔŋ ka nyɛ nya ka ka ti doo zuɣu yɛla. Mali maa Kwame, m bɛ o doli ni, ka m maŋ yua o jiŋ pii chɛɛ ka o ti bi nyɛ yɛli ŋan dam. N daa sɔŋ maa yeli sɔŋ o — yi frɛ ambulance yi na bi lahi, ka nindaan din dɔɣiri ka o nyɛri yɛligu paɣibu.
narrative_fante: Me pa wo kyew, biribi bɔne paa aba ha wɔ Asokwa, Kumasi — opanyin bi a wɔfrɛ no Owusu nearly drowned na yɛsuro no paa. Ɔrehome dada, na ɔhome no yɛ slow na yɛ weak paa, na ne tiri ne ne kɔn ahyew na ehu sɛ biribi bɔne paa wɔ ne mu. Mene Kwame, meyɛ ne neighbor, na me na mepullii no fii nsuo mu, nanso ɔnka nsɛm koraa. Me pa wo kyew, yehia mmoa mmmra ha ntɛm paa efisɛ onipa yi yɛ opanyin na ne state yɛ serious paa.
narrative_gurene: N tɩɩma, zuɣu bee dee pɛlɩm ka bɩɛla fɔ Asokwa, Kumasi — nindaan o da frɛ Owusu, o nɛɛr kaa lee a zi biɛri o. O bee nyɛŋa, bɛ o nyɛŋa ka yɛlem tɩɩm, tɩɩm wʋnɛ, ka o ŋun ne o nɔŋ nɩɩga pɛlɩm, a nyɛ ka zuɣu bee dee a bʋɔrɩ o tɩɩna. M yɛtɔ mam Kwame, m bee o yɛsim, ka mam n da bɔrɩ o zi biɛri, bɛ o ko a nyɛ sɔŋsɔŋ. N tɩɩma, sɔŋ mam — frɛ ambulance ka wa fɔ ka n yɛlem, bala bia nindaan nɛ ka o nyɛ pɛlɩm tɩɩm.

narrative_en: My name is Issah, I'm calling to report an accident that happened here in Suhum. An old woman, Adwoa, she was crossing the road when a trotro clipped her and she fell down. It didn't look too serious, but her leg is hurt — she said it's paining her and there's a small cut there. She can move it small small but she needs someone to come and check her properly.
narrative_ewe: Nyem ŋkɔ nye Issah. Mefrɛ mi abe nudzadzra siwo wòwɔ afim Suhum ene. Nyɔnu srɔ̃ ɖeka, Adwoa, eɖo ɔ na nu le ʋɔ dzi wòna wo le trotro ɖeka kpɔ ɛ la, eyata edo ƒu. Nudzadzra la megblɔ na o sesẽ hã, gake afɔ ɖeka yi ŋu ɛ — Adwoa ɖe ŋukɔ be eyi nam, eye ɔ̃gbɔ kpe ɖe eƒe afɔ la dzi ɖeɖe. Enya ɖe afɔ la nu eye ekpɔ afɔ la dzi kakaka o, gake edzina wɔ wo le nu. Meɖi ɖe ame aɖe avas afim eye woakpɔ ɛ fifia fifia, eye woagblɔ ne nyawo le nu ko.
narrative_ga: Mii nyɛ Issah, mirefrɛ mɛ report accident bi shi Suhum hɛ. Gbɔmɔ bi — Adwoa — ɔreyɛ kpee tsɔɔ na trotro bi kpaa no, na obumo fɛɛ. Enimɔ bi serious paaa o, shi gboo no — obi ni hii no mli, na cut kakra bi le hɛ. Otumi yi gboo no kakra kakra, shi oyɛ need sɔmɔ ba lɛ check no pɛpɛpɛ.
narrative_dagbani: M yuli di Issah. N bɛ yɛ nso ka m yi yi frɛ ka m yɛl amba accident kɔɣim ka o maa wuhibi Suhum ka. Kpɛma be, Adwoa, o maa zaa nyɛŋ boli ka trotro maa pɛli o tooi ka o gbini. O maa nyɛ ka yɛligu ka kato ka, ama o tiŋ ka yɛligu — o yɛla ka o tiŋ yɛligu daa, ka kɔrili biɛla bɛ ka din. O tiŋ maa, o zaŋ o wuhibi o tooi nni, ama o bori la nindaan bɔŋ ka o ni o naɣiri tuma.
narrative_fante: Me din de Issah. Mefrɛ sɛ merekyerɛ wo accident bi a ɛsii ha wɔ Suhum. Opanyin baa bi, Adwoa, ɔretu kwan no mu na trotro bi bɔɔ no na ɔtuu fam. Ennyɛ sɛ ɛyɛ den paa, nanso ne nan yɛ no yaw — ɔkae sɛ eye no yaw na na krasee kakraa bi wɔ hɔ. Ɔtumi yi no kakraa kakraa, nanso ɔhia onipa bi sɛ ɔbɛba na ɔbɛhwɛ no yiye.
narrative_gurene: M yɔɔrɩ Issah. M frɛ fo sɩ m tɩɩ fo accident bi bɔɔrɩ kɔ Suhum. Pog nindaan, Adwoa, o laa nyɛŋ na wa trotro bɔɔ o, o kpɛŋ fam. Ka a yɛligu la zuɣu kpɛŋ — o yɛligu a nɩ a nɩ, ka a gʋrɩ bilim bee o. O tɩŋ a laa nɩ la bilim bilim, la o daama ned wa nɩ wa gori o sɩ sɩ. N tɩɩma, frɛ ned wa kɔ.

narrative_en: Please, something very serious has happened here in Techiman. My neighbor's daughter, Abenaa, she was knocked down by a vehicle on the main road just this afternoon — the car hit her and she fell very badly. Her leg is broken, I think, because it is bent in a way it should not be and she cannot move it at all, and there is a lot of blood. Ramatu is here with me and we are both trying to help her but the girl is just lying there, she is not fully awake. Please send help quickly, this young girl needs the hospital now.
narrative_ewe: Meɖekuku, nudzadzra gã aɖe wɔ ɖe afim afi Techiman. Abenaa, ŋutɔ agbɔla ɖevi, ɣlɔ̃ akɔ ɖe Ʋɔ gã dzi egɔme sia — akɔ la ƒo eɖe ta bo ewu ƒu ko ŋutɔ. Mɔfɛ ame nya be eƒe afɔ ɣleti vɔ, efɔ wòle ŋkeke me la nu, eye mateŋu siasii o, eye dzi le etsotso gã aɖe. Ramatu le afim ŋunye kple nyem, miaƒo mia ŋu ɖe eŋu, gake ɖevi la feŋ ko, eɖo edzi ne ko, eɖo edzi gã o. Meɖekuku, gbɔ kpɔ ɖe mia ŋu enumake — ɖevi nyɔnuvi sia hiã asiwo ko fifia!
narrative_ga: Ofainɛ, something very serious abɔ Techiman ha. Neighbor mi dzɛŋ, Abenaa, kaa bumo ŋ wɔ kpee gbɔɔ afternoon ha — kaa ni bumo ŋ mli, obumo wɔ yɛ mli tumɔ. Gboo ŋ, mi suɔmɔ fracture gbɔ, shiaŋ ni otsoo bent way ŋ se bi sɔɔ, okɛ ɔmɔ gbeka gbeka hɛ, nɔɔ shwe etsotso pii pii. Ramatu le ha kɛ mi, yɛ nyɛ mli boa ŋ, kɛ dɛbi ha eshii ko enimɔ gbɛ shɛ. Ofainɛ, frɛ ambulance mli blɛ kɛ speed — oblɛ ha ehia hospital sɔɔ, chale, now now!
narrative_dagbani: N daa, ka yɛligu paɣa daa pahi kɔ Techiman. N yɛmdi biɛla, Abenaa, kaɣa daa gbɛ o tɔ nyɛŋ korikori be yenna — kaɣa daa kali o, ka o gbini doo paɣa. O tiŋ daa yiɣi, m tɔɣi, bɔ o tiŋ boli ka ka yɛl taɣi, ka o be tumi o yɛ yi kuli, ka ziim bee o paɣa. Ramatu bɛ kɔ ni ma, ti nɛɛ ti di sɔŋ biɛla la, ama biɛla la bɛ kali kɔ zuli, o be so so — o nyɛ o maa yɛla nyɔɣim. N daa, yi frɛ ambulance sɔɣim, yi yi biɛla la kpe nyɛla yili saha la, o bori sɔŋ yinna.
narrative_fante: Me pa wo kyew, asɛm kɛse bi aba ha wɔ Techiman. Me hɔhoɔ ba, Abenaa, kar bi bɔɔ no wɔ ɔkwan kɛse no so nnɛ awiabere yi — kar no bɔɔ no ma ɔtuu fam yiye. Me susu sɛ ne nan broken, efisɛ ɛkeka mu bɔn na ɔntumi nsakra no koraa, na mogya pii retu firi ho. Ramatu wɔ ha ne me, yɛn mmienu yɛ saa na yɛrɛboro sɛ yɛboa no, nanso abofra yi da hɔ pɛ ara — ɔnhunu hwee pɛpɛɛpɛ. Me pa wo kyew, mboa mmrɛ yiye — abofra yi hia hospital seesei ara!
narrative_gurene: N tɩɩma, somdi zɔŋ pɛɛlɩm be a yi la Techiman. M yɛŋ bia, Abenaa, kaɣa bɔɔ o nyɛŋ zɔŋ yɛla la wʋntɔ — kaɣa na bɔɔ o la, o kpɛŋ fam zɔŋ pɛɛlɩm. A nʋɔrɩ ŋmɩɩrɩ, m tɩɩm, bala ka o nʋɔrɩ zɔɔ nɩ ka o nan tol la bee, la o nan tol ka la zɔŋ, la ziim bee o pɛɛlɩm. Ramatu be ne mam yin la, la tɩ yɛlɛ ka sɔŋ o, la bia la kʋŋ bɛ, o ko a nyɛ zɔŋ. N tɩɩma, sɔŋ mam yɛ ka wʋntɔ — bia buŋ hospital yɛla la ka yin.

narrative_en: Please, something has happened to my father-in-law here in Adum, Kumasi. Kwabena fell down this morning — he slipped near the doorstep and hit his head on the ground. He is an old man so we are very worried, and his neck is paining him too and he cannot turn it well. Abenaa here, I am his daughter-in-law, and right now he is awake but he is confused small and complaining of serious headache. We need somebody to come and check him because he cannot get up on his own.
narrative_ewe: Meɖekuku, nudzadzra aɖe wɔ ame ŋunye fiafia afisia Adum, Kumasi. Kwabena edo ƒu ŋdi sia — ewu ƒu agble ɖevi ƒo ŋɔ eye eta wua ƒu ɖo. Ele srɔ̃ de, nenye mieɖi nu gɔme didi, eye eƒe ɖo yi nam hã, kple mate ŋu trɔ aɖe nyuie o. Nye Abenaa, nye eƒe atsu ŋutsu nyɔnu, eye fifia Kwabena eɖo edzi gake egblɔgblɔ le afɔliha kple eyi nam ɖe eƒe ta dzi sesẽ. Medi ame aɖe atrɔ de ame kpɔe, afa ele ŋu ɖe zi ɖe eƒe ŋuti abe eƒe ŋutefe ene o.
narrative_ga: Ofainɛ, something happen ni mɛ ni father-in-law shi Adum, Kumasi. Kwabena bumo sɔɔ — obu slip near doorstep ni obɔ ŋmɛi oshie fɛɛ ground. Ogbɔmɔ nɛ, so yɛ worry pɛpɛɛpɛ, ni ŋmɛi gbiŋ ehii nɛ too — okaŋ turn ni ŋmɛi gbiŋ yɛ blɛ o. Abenaa ni mɛ, mɛ daughter-in-law nɛ, ni saa sɔɔ oni le, kɛ o confuse small ni o complain serious headache. Yɛ hia person aba check nɛ, because okaŋ sɔɔ ŋ gbɛ oshie nɛ o.
narrative_dagbani: N daa, bɛ kuli kuli nyɛri ka n saɣim paɣa ba Adum, Kumasi. Kwabena gbini wuntɔ — o silimi Nyɛŋa baŋ yili nii, ka o ŋun di bɛɣi bugili zuɣu. O nyɛ nindaan, di yɛli ti bɛ yɛrila ka o ni gɔli o ŋun zuɣu, ka o ti pɛl o nuu paɣila. Maa nyɛ Abenaa, m nyɛ o biɛla paɣa, ka noŋ noŋ o nyɛ o ziɣila amaa o nyɛ maa ti gorimdi, ka o yɛligu daa o ŋun baga baga. Ti bori ŋun yi ni yi wa o nyɛla, bala o ti pɛli o mɔɣa buɣu saa.
narrative_fante: Me pa wo kyew, biribi aba m'aware wura agya ho ha wɔ Adum, Kumasi. Kwabena tuu fam anɔpa yi — ɔsɛɛ kɔ n'ani wɔ entrance no ho na ɔbɔɔ ne tiri ase fam. Ɔyɛ opanyin enti yɛsuro paa, na ne kɔn nso eye no yaw na ɔntumi ndan no yiye. Me ne Abenaa, me ne no daughter-in-law, na seesei ɔnye nin a nanso ɔkyiri kɔ kɔ na ɔka sɛ ne tiri pae no yaw paa. Yehia obi a ɔbɛba na ɔhwɛ no, efisɛ ɔntumi nso ne ho enti nkosi anaa ɔntumi nso.
narrative_gurene: N tɩɩma, bɩ yɛ nɩ la Adum, Kumasi a. M kɔɔbga Kwabena bɔɔrɩ zaabu — o kpɛŋ fam deo yiri nyɔɔrɛ tɩɩŋa la a o ŋun kpɛ tɩɩŋa. O yɛ nindaan, n tuma wʋnɛ tɩɩ wɔ ŋa la a zaam. A o kɔŋ nɩ yɛligu la o la aa tʋŋ nɩ la sɔŋŋa. Mam yɛ la Abenaa, m yɛ o poga biia. La wɔ, o yɛ nyɛna ama o bʋɔ wora wora la a o ŋun yɛligu ka zaa. Tɩ yɛ bɩ nyɛ nɩ a wa nyɛ o la, bʋɔ o yɛla, bʋɔ o aa tisi a pɔɔ ŋo a n tuma ne be.

narrative_en: My name is Ofori, I'm calling to report something that happened on the N1 highway near Kasoa. An old man, Yaw, he fell down — I think he slipped by the roadside — and he's hurt his leg badly, maybe his arm too. He can move small but he's in a lot of pain and he can't stand up on his own. He's not a young man at all, so we are worried about him.
narrative_ewe: Ŋkɔnyé nye Ofori. Mefrɛ wo be metɔ nuxɔxɔ siwo woa kpɔ eye mele N1 highway ŋu la gbɔ Kasoa. Srɔ̃ aɖe le eŋu, Yaw — ewu ƒu ɣeyiɣi si le ʋɔ ŋkɔ la gbɔ — eye afɔawo yi eŋu dukɔ, sub nɛ asiwo me hã. Ate ŋu yi go go go, gake eyi nam dukɔ eye mate ŋu ɖo etame o. Ame la nyo ko o — srɔ̃ ye nɔe — eye midzɔ eŋu ŋutɔ.
narrative_ga: Mii dzɛŋ Ofori, mifre lɛ shi N1 highway, Kasoa kɛŋ. Gbɔmɔ kɛ, Yaw — obumo kpee ŋmɛŋmɛ — mitsɔɔ ne oshishi kpee ŋmɛi. Ogboo ehii nɛ wu, ogbɔ fɛɛ ehii ne — mitsɔɔ. Oni le small, onyɛŋ mli le, nɛ otsɔ yɛ obua ogboo ko. Gbɔmɔ ŋ otsɔ ko, chale — eniɛ shwɛ ni kɛmi na.
narrative_dagbani: M yuli di Ofori. M frɛ ka m yi yɛla daa yɛla bi ka daa kpɛŋ N1 highway zuɣu Kasoa kpeŋ. Nindaan biɛn, o yuli di Yaw — o gbini nyɛŋ doo, m gbɔŋ o gbɛ nyɛŋ dalinli — ka o tiŋ yɛligu daa, n ta ka o nuu biɛ yɛligu. O bɛ zaɣa ka bi zaɣim o yɛligu yɛlili, ŋun ka o so zaɣa o ti mali yɔɣim. O nyɛ kpɛma, di yɛ maa ka ti lee n bɛri o mali.
narrative_fante: Me din de Ofori. Merefrɛ mo na merekyerɛ mo biribi a esii fam wɔ N1 highway no ho, Kasoa nkyɛn. Opanyin bi — Yaw — ɔtuu fam wɔ ɔkwan no nkyɛn. Mese ɔsrɛrɛɛ fam. Ɔnan no yɛ no yaw paa — na ɔnsa no nso bebrebe ara. Ɔtumi keka mpaemu kakra, nanso ɔntumi nnante na ɔntumi nsi ɔne ne ho. Eye no yaw paa. Onye ɔbarima foforo o — ɔyɛ opanyin paa — enti yɛsuro no ho.
narrative_gurene: M yɩɩrɩ Ofori. M bɔɔrɩ frɛ yɩɩla tɩ ŋmɩnɩ N1 highway zuɣu, Kasoa nɩŋa. Nindaan bi, Yaw, o bɔɔrɩ fam — m lɛ o kpɛŋ nyɛŋ nɩŋa o bɔɔrɩ — a o nɩŋa yɛligu a yaga. O nɩŋa ka a yɛligu pɔŋa, sɩ o nɩŋa gʋŋa nɛ. O tʋma ka nɩŋ bila, la ka o tõ a yɛligu a yaga a o ka tõ ŋmani o mɩnɩ. O ka bia rɛ, sɩ ti lɛ o maŋa.

narrative_en: Please, something very serious has happened here close to Adidome on the Ho-Accra road. An old man, Wiredu — he's my father-in-law — was crossing the Volta on a wooden canoe and the boat capsized. We managed to pull him out of the water but he went under for too long. He is breathing but it is very weak and he cannot respond when we call his name. His chest looks swollen and wrong. I am Dzifa, please we need help to come here fast because he is old and we are very afraid.
narrative_ewe: Meɖekuku, nudzadzra vevie teƒe aɖe wò le afisia Adidome dzi, Ʋɔ si ɖe Ho na Accra. Srɔ̃ aɖe, Wiredu — eye eya nye nyrɔ̃ŋutsu nye — ewo Volta ta le akpa ƒe kɔkɔ dzi eye akpa ɖoa ƒu. Míakɔ mía ɖe eŋu le tsi me gake etɔ̃ dzo le tsi me dzi sesẽ. Eƒu agbe kɔkɔ gake ƒu ɖe la vɔ̃ vovotɔ, eye emate ŋu ŋlɔ amewo gbe o, afi si míefrɛ ŋkɔe tso. Ɖevi ŋɔ si le eŋu la, ekpɔ abe enyo gbã o, ewu tso. Nyɔŋkeke, mele Dzifa. Meɖekuku, míeɖi kpɔ ŋunye sõsõ, wòa va afisia fifia, ɖe eya srɔ̃ kaka eye míele egbɔ vevie.
narrative_ga: Ofainɛ, bɔɔ kɛ serious ni shikpon yɛ ha Adidome fɛɛ, Ho-Accra road ni loo. Gbɔmɔ ŋɛŋ, Wiredu — ɔyɛ mi naaŋ naa — ɔyɛ crossing Volta wɔ canoe ni loo, naanɔ canoe etiaŋ. Yɛ habu amo ni yɛ wɔ shwe ŋ lɛ, kɛ ɔyɛ under water too long. Ɔhɔmɔ le kɛ hɔmɔ ɔyɛ very weak — naanɔ yɛ frɛ ni kɛ yɛ frɛ no ŋɛŋ, ɔnimɔ loo. Ni gbɔɔ ɔyɛ wɔ — ɛyɛ swollen, enimɔ straight. Miŋ yɛ Dzifa. Ofainɛ, boa mi — yɛ hia help mi ha fast fast, kakra, bɔɔ kɛ ɔyɛ gbɔmɔ kɛ yɛ hu shɛ bi. Frɛ ambulance — chale, please!
narrative_dagbani:
N daa, bɛ nyɛ koom daa yɛri ka o bee tɔɣim ka o ti shɛli. N bori la a mɛ sɔŋ maa.

Adidome kɔɣim, Ho-Accra nyɛŋ zuɣu, ka accident nyɛ ŋan. Nindaan yini — o yuɣu bi Wiredu — o bee m puɣu baŋ yiri. O zaɣa koom Volta zuɣu gbɛŋ biɛla zuɣu, ka gbɛŋ o kpɛŋ koom ni. Ti sɔŋ ti zuli o bi koom ni, ŋun n'ti nyɛ fɛɛri, amma o zaɣa koom ni ka kaan daa.

O sim daa, amma o sim bee ni yɛlim yɛlim — o sim daa la nampalli. Ka ti frɛ o yuɣu, o ti leei. O digindi nyɛ o maa yɛla — ka o nyɛ pam dali ka o ti tiim.

Maa yuɣu bi Dzifa. N daa, yi maa sɔŋ — yi wa ŋan ka peeni peeni! O kpɛma la, ka ti zibi daa.
narrative_fante: Me pa wo kyew, biribi dee ɛyɛ hu paa na ɛaba ha, Adidome ho, Ho-Accra kwan so. Opanyin bi, Wiredu — ɔyɛ me kunu agya — na ɔretu Volta so wɔ kyen dua bi mu, na kyen no dane fam wɔ nsuo mu. Yɛatumi twe no firi nsuo mu, nanso ɔkɔ ase nsuo mu ara sɛ ampa. Ɔrehome, nanso ɛyɛ ketewa ara, na sɛ yɛfrɛ ne din a, ɔntu so nkyere yɛn hwee. Ne bruwa no — ɛhwɛ sɛ ɛapono na ɛnyɛ yiye. Mene Dzifa. Me pa wo kyew, mmoa yɛn mmru ha ntɛm, efisɛ ɔyɛ opanyin na yɛsuro paa.
narrative_gurene: N tɩɩma, bʋʋd bi yɛla a wa yɛ ka a ka Adidome, Ho-Accra nyɛŋ zuɣu. Nindaan, Wiredu — o la m sorrɔ ba — o la bee Volta nɔɔ dɔɔrɩ kaɣa yɛlɩ, ka bo yel dɔɔrɩ ka o bɔɔrɩ nɔɔ pʋɔ. Ti sɔŋ o pɔɔ nɔɔ la, la o la bee nɔɔ pʋɔ yɛlba. O nyɛm bee o, la o nyɛm tɔɔ ka bɛ, ka ti bɔ o yʋɣʋ la ka o yɛ tɩ. O pʋsʋŋ gʋl ka a nyɛ yɛligu. Mim yɛ Dzifa. N tɩɩma, sɔŋ tɩ wa ka o la nindaan ka tɩ sɔŋɔ yɛl kɩtɔ — tɩ sɔɣɩ tɩ yɛl dɛɛ.

narrative_en: My brother Ibrahim had an accident — he was riding in an aboboyaa that overturned this afternoon near our house here in Mamprobi. He fell and hurt his leg, but he is walking small small so I don't think it is too serious. I am Abigail, I live with him. We just want to know if he should go to the hospital or if we can manage it at home because the leg is swollen a little bit.
