narrative_ewe: Allo, meɖekuku, kpe ɖe ŋunye. Nɔviwi ame Ibrahim woƒe accident le egbe egbe fifia ŋdi — aboboyaa si eƒoa le eme wɔ nu, ewu ƒu kple eƒe afɔ yi vɔ ɖe Mamprobi fifia ko, agbe ŋutɔ yiyi ŋu. Eɖo afɔ le dɔdɔ le eme, gake ele ɖo be adze afɔ ɖe eme kakaka — le asi ɖeka me, mate ŋu be nuvi si yi vɔ ɖea nyuie ko. Nyɔ̃ŋutsu ŋɔŋlɔ nye Abigail, mele xɔ ŋu kple Ibrahim fifia. Midzro be miane: Ele aɖe be eyi ɖo Asiwo, alo miate ŋu ɖa nu eƒe asi le xɔ ŋu, ehe afɔ a dɔ kpɔe dze ɖe zi ɖeka ɖeka, gake aɖaŋu afɔ la wɔa kpɔ tsɔtsɔ le eme. Meɖekuku, ɖo ŋkume nami. Akpe.
narrative_ga: Hɛloo, ofainɛ boa mi. Ade mi naaŋ Ibrahim obumo accident — ɔwɔ aboboyaa mu, aboboyaa no tuiŋ ehia ŋ afɛ ŋ gbɔɔ Mamprobi ni, awurɔ yi. Obumo ehumo, ni gboo hii ŋ — kɛ ɔyɛ akɛ ɔtoo tee tee, enyɛ serious pɛɛ, mitsɔ ni. Mi nyɛ Abigail, mibɔ shi ŋ kɛɛ. Yɛŋmɛi shia nyɛ: ɔnyɛ ni he hospital bii, abi yɛdzɛ manage ni aboɔ yi? Gboo no bɔ swollen kakra.
narrative_dagbani: M doo Ibrahim accident nyɛ — o bɛ aboboyaa mu ka o kpɛŋ ka o ŋmari ka pɛlim zanzan bi zaa yɛndi, ka mi n'yili dali Mamprobi. O tiŋ yɛligu, amaa o nyɛŋ bi zanzan ŋa, ka n sori kaa mali yɛligu ŋaha paɣibu. Mali m yuli bi Abigail, n to ni o yili. Ti bori bala ka ŋ ni gosi ti ka Ibrahim maa ma nyɛla yili wa, anaa ti tumi yɛ yiŋ ziɣi tuma, kimali o tiŋ pam bi zanzan, ka o tiŋ vuni biɛɣa.
narrative_fante: My brother Ibrahim had an accident — he was riding in an aboboyaa that overturned this afternoon near our house here in Mamprobi. He fell and hurt his leg, but he is walking small small so I don't think it is too serious. I am Abigail, I live with him. We just want to know if he should go to the hospital or if we can manage it at home because the leg is swollen a little bit.
narrative_gurene: M saya Ibrahim accident ma bɔɔrɩ — o bee aboboyaa a kpɛŋ fam awakening ŋa zaa yɛl yɛla kʋɔrɛ ka ne yir la Mamprobi. O bɔɔrɩ ka o nɩŋ yɛligu, ama o bee nyɛŋ la nɛɛ nɛɛ, ka mam ko mɔɔ o yɛligu bee zaa pɛlɛ. Mam yɛŋ la Abigail, m bee o yir la. Tɩ bɛ yam nyɛ bee o sɛ kʋ kɔ hospital, naa tɩ tɩɩ tɩ sɔŋ o yir la, bʋɔ o nɩŋ fuu nɛɛ nɛɛ.

narrative_en: Please, something very serious has happened on the Kumasi-Sunyani road, close to Techiman side. My grandfather, Mawuli, was in an accident — a car hit the vehicle he was traveling in and he has been badly injured in his chest and stomach area. The old man is in a lot of pain and he cannot even sit up on his own. I am Hadija, I am here at the scene with him now and we are trying to get him to the hospital but we need help fast because his condition is very serious.
narrative_ewe: Meɖekuku, nudzadzra gãtɔ aɖe wɔ ɖe Kumasi-Sunyani ʋɔ dzi, ƒe Techiman gbɔ. Mawuli, nye tɔgbuɖuɖu, le accident me — akɔ aɖe ƒe Mawuli le eme kpɔ akɔ bubu, eye woɖa ɖo ɖevi ŋɔ kple abɔ ƒe te te. Srɔ̃ la eyi nam nyuitɔ, eye mate ŋu eye ŋkeke ɖeka me nku ɖo esiwo o. Nyenye Hadija, mele ɣe sia le eŋuti fifia, mele egbɔ, eye miɖiɖi ɛ yi asiwo, gake miɖi kpɔ dɔwɔna go akpɔkplɔ, abe ne ɖe eƒe ʋɔ la, ame ƒe nyawo le dzetɔ nyuitɔ.
narrative_ga: Ofainɛ, chale, something very serious abɔ Kumasi-Sunyani road no so, Techiman side fɛɛ. Me gbɔmɔ, Mawuli, accident abɔ no — kaa ebii kaa wɔ eyi ŋ tɔɔ, naanɔ ɛ gbɔɔ kɛ shi ehii no wɔ waa. Gbɔmɔ no ni hii waa waa, ɛ tumi gbɔmɔ le ŋmɛi sɛ o. Me nyɛ Hadija, mɛ le fɛɛ ŋ tee kɛ nɔ nii, yɛ hee yɛ ba no hospital, kɛ yɛ hii bo fast, chale, because ɛ condition no serious waa.
narrative_dagbani: N daa, ka yɛligu paɣa daa kɔɣi Kumasi-Sunyani nyɛŋ din, Techiman taɣi. N kpɛma, Mawuli, o kɔŋ accident pam — kaɣa din gbaai kaɣa din o bɛ a pun din, naa o digindi ni o pam yɛligu o naɣa daa. Nindaan din bɛ yɛligu paɣa wuhim, ani o ti tumi o gbini o maŋ ni. Mia Hadija, m bɛ kɔŋ ti ni o yɛndi, ka ti bori la yi maa sɔŋ ti o kpɛ nyɛla yili, ama ti bori sɔŋ yɛl paɣa, bimba o yɛla lan ka ti yɛli a ka yɛligu paɣa daa.
narrative_fante: Me pa wo kyew, biribi dee ɛyɛ den paa aba Kumasi-Sunyani kwan so, Techiman fam ho. Me nana, Mawuli, ɔkɔ accident mu — kar bi bɔɔ ne karro a na ɔreba no mu, na wɔakɔ wu no yiye ne ne bruwa ne yafunu mu. Opanyin no ɛyɛ no yaw paa na ɔntumi nkɔ ntena ase ne ho ankasa. Me ne Hadija, mewɔ ha scene no so ne no seesei, na yɛretɔ ho a yɛde no kɔ hospital, nanso yɛhia mmoa ntɛm paa ɛfiri sɛ ne state no yɛ den paa.
narrative_gurene: N tɩɩma, bɩɩ fɔɔ ka vɩɩya a yɛli sɔŋ wʋlʋm a Kumasi-Sunyani nyɛŋ zug, Techiman ninnɛ la. M kpɛŋ bia, Mawuli, o bɔɔrɩ accident pʋɣʋ — kaɣa dɔɔrɩ kaɣa nɩŋ o be o pʋɣʋ, ka o yɛligu n kɩɩmɩ o pɔɣɔ la ka o pʋʋrɛ la zug. Nindaan o yɛligu ka yɛligu wʋlʋm, o kɔŋ tɛ a zɔb a o meng ye. Mam yɛ fo, Hadija yaa mam, m be bʋɣʋ ka waa la Mawuli la, ka ti yɛri a yi o waa hospital, ama ti sɔŋ tɩɩm ka o yɛla n kɩɩmɩ wʋlʋm. Sɔŋ mam! Frɛ ambulance!

narrative_en: Please, something bad happened in our compound here at Dichemso. Some boys attacked Owusu, the old man who lives near the entrance — they beat him on his head and neck area. I am Amoah, I am his neighbor, and when I heard the noise I ran outside and found him sitting on the ground, he was confused and his neck was paining him badly. He is not unconscious but he is not fine at all, the old man needs help.
narrative_ewe: Meɖekuku, nuƒoƒo dzetɔ wɔ le ɖe yɔ ŋɔ afime si le Dichemso. Ɖeviwo — vevitɔwo — wua Owusu, srɔ̃ la si nɔ ɖe anyigba ŋkɔm la ŋutɔ ŋu — wusu eŋu ta kple ɖo ŋɔ ɣesiaɣi. Nyenye Amoah, mele eƒe agbetame, eye gake hã mekpɔ ʋovovo la megbɔ dzi o, mede agbe megɔ dzi eye mekpɔ ame la ɖo anyigba dzi, ɖo wu eŋu, eye naɖoɖo meŋu o. Eɖo edzi, gake wɔwɔ meŋu o fe — eyi enam tee ɖo ŋɔ. Srɔ̃ la le ɣlɔ̃ kpe ɖe ŋunye nugbɔ — Meɖekuku, djro nami!
narrative_ga: Ofainɛ, something bad bi happen ni yɛ compound hɛɛ Dichemso. Boys tɛɛ bi attack Owusu, gbɔmɔ bi shi dze near entrance — wɔ hyɛ no ni ŋmɛi kɛ neck area. Mɛ yɛ Amoah, mɛ yɛ ni neighbor, kɛ wɔ temi noise no mɛ tei gbogbo outside mɛ hɛ no sit dze fɛɛ ground, onyemi le kɛ confuse, kɛ ni neck ehii no bebɛ. Enimɔ gbɛ o, nɔŋ eni fine o chale — gbɔmɔ no lɛ shikpɔŋ boa. Boa mi!
narrative_dagbani: N daa, ʒɛŋ kɔriŋ bee ti yidana ka bi diɣi Dichemso. Biɛribiya bihi yaŋ sɔŋ naa Owusu, nindaan nee bi diɣi nyɛŋ kpeŋ doo — bi gbaŋ o ŋun ni o kuri ʒɔɣu. Maa yɛlifu la Amoah, n gbɛli o ziɣim, ka di maŋ m ni di gɔli m taa pɛliɣu ka m yɛliɣ o chɛŋ bugim bugim ka o diɣi tama ka ŋun o yɛligu o kuri ʒɔɣu ka yɛligu daa o ti sɔɣim. Ka o ti soli la koo, naa kaa n gbaŋŋa la, nindaan din nya sɔŋ.
narrative_fante: Me pa wo kyew, biribi bone asi ha wɔ yɛn fie mu wɔ Dichemso. Mmarimanom bi bɔɔ Owusu, opanyin a ɔte kɔkɔɔ n'aseɛ wɔ entrance ho — wɔbubuiɛ no wɔ n'tiri ne kɔn ho. Me ne Amoah, me yɛ n'ɔmanfo, na sɛ mitiee ɔpompo no a, miriee pue abɔn huu no tete fam, n'adwene reyɛ no bɔɔ, na n'kɔn yɛ no yaw paa. Ɔnhunu hwee da, nanso ne ho nyɛ koraa — opanyin no hia mmoa.
narrative_gurene: N tɩɩma, bɩ yɛla kɛ naɣa daa wa tɩ yir ne ka Dichemso ka. Bibiisi dɩɩba kɛ wa gbana Owusu, nindaan o be ka yir nɛ ka yirɛ kɔŋa la — ba gbana o ŋun ne o kɔŋ zɔŋa. Mam yɩrɩ Amoah, mam Owusu ziem bee, ka m wʋrɩ yankari m zɔŋ pʋɣa, m bɔɔrɩ pɛlɛ ka m wa nɩ o be ŋmɛnɛ fam, o yam kɩ taŋ, ka o kɔŋ yɛligu na a yɛligu bi yɛ. O ko a nyɛ nɛ kɛ yɛ, la o bee dɩɩbi biim. Nindaan o tɩga sɔŋ — sɔŋ mam!

narrative_en: My sister Araba was involved in a road accident this afternoon on the Kumasi-Tamale road, somewhere close to Ejura. A vehicle hit the car she was traveling in and she knocked her head badly — her neck is also paining her a lot. She is conscious and talking, but she looks confused and there is some swelling around her head. Please, we need help because we are not sure if it is safe to move her.
narrative_ewe: Allo! Meɖekuku, kpe ŋunye! Nɔvinyɔnu ce Araba wɔ accident ɖe ɣeyiɣi sia ɖe Kumasi-Tamale ʋɔ dzi, ɖe Ejura kpɔkpɔe. Akɔ ɖeka dze akɔ si a le eme ƒu, eye ta wɔ ɣlim nyuie — ɖo me hã eyi nam nyuie. Eɖo edzi eye edze nu, gake enézí ɖe ame si ma ŋlɔ o. Ta ŋkɔme hã fɔ aɖeke. Meɖekuku, miɖi kpɔ, elabena mietsɔ wo la, míe nyá ble be xɔ va ŋu o. Frɛ ambulance miɖe, akpe!
narrative_ga: Chale, Araba, mi ŋmaa, accident bumo eni le afiɔ dzeng — Kumasi-Tamale kpee dzi, Ejura fa. Kaa bi bumo kaa bi nɔŋ ebi shiŋ, ebumo ŋmɛi gbɛɛ — ni hii ŋmɛi kɛ ni hii nyɛ shi gbɛɛ nɔŋ. Oni le kɛ oha okwɛɛ, shikpon onim le haŋ haŋ, kɛ ŋmɛi fa swelling le dɔ. Ofainɛ, yɛ hia bo — yɛ nyɛ shɔ ni safe nɔɔ okwɛɛ eŋmɛ le ami.
narrative_dagbani: N dagɔ Araba, o nyɛ accident daa daa zaŋ bi chaŋ, Kumasi-Tamale nyɛŋ zuɣu, Ejura kɔɣili. Kaɣa yini pɛ kaɣa waa o bɛ o chɛ zaŋ, ka o ŋun gbini daa yɛligu — o kpihi ka yɛligu daa ti. O ti soli, o bia o yɛlim, ama o ŋun ku maŋ yɛligu — ka nyɛ be wɔhim bɛ o ŋun kɔɣili. N daa, bori la sɔŋ, bala ti bi nyɛ o boli bo yaa kpihi, ka zaŋ o di ku nyɛ ti yɛli.
narrative_fante: Me nuabaa Araba hyɛɛ accident afi saa awia yi wɔ Kumasi-Tamale ɔkwan so, bebi a ɛbɛn Ejura. Kar bi bɔɔ kar a na ɔrekofa so, na ɔbuae ne tiri yiye — ne kɔn nso reye no yaw paa. Ɔte hɔ na ɔreka asɛm, nanso wohunu sɛ ɔsɛe na ne tiri ho ho nso ahyew. Me pa wo kyew, yehia mmoa efisɛ yɛnnim sɛ ɛho hia sɛ yɛsɛsa no anaa dabi.
narrative_gurene: Mam zo Araba bɔɔrɩ accident gaa yuu zanê, Kumasi-Tamale nyɛŋ zuɣu, Ejura ninsɩ. Kaɣa yenno suɣa kaɣa naa o bee o la, ka o ŋun bɔɔrɩ fɔɔ fɔɔ — o pore bee a yɛligu fɔɔ. O ko a nyɛ ne, ka o goama, ama o nyɛ la o yɛlɩ a kpɛŋ-kpɛŋ, ka a suɣʋm bee o ŋun ninsɩ. N tɩɩma, sɔŋ mam — bɩ yɛ bɩ nya sɩ o nuŋ nyɛ, ka bɩ sʋŋ bɩ nye de o.

narrative_en: My sister Ekua fell down badly here at the Takoradi market, please you have to send someone quickly. She slipped and hit her head on the ground, and now she is not talking and her neck looks very wrong. I am Asare, I was with her when it happened. She is breathing but she is not moving at all and there is blood coming from her head — it is serious, please hurry.
narrative_ewe: Allo! Meɖekuku, nɔ̃ nɔ̃! Nyɔŋkɔ nye Ekua ewu ƒu le Takoradi market si le afisia — meɖekuku, xɔ ame dzo akɔ fifia! Eɖo ƒu, ekpɔ ta ƒo ƒu ɖo ŋɔ, eye fifia, womegblɔ gbe o. Ɖo ƒo le eŋu, enɔa na nyuie o. Ŋkɔ nye Asare — me le eŋu ko egblẽ sia gbɛ̃. Edzi ƒu ame, gake emegã o, egagã. Dzi le eƒu ta dzi — nulia si ɖe nyuie o, meɖekuku, mia dzo fifia fifia!
narrative_ga: Hɛloo — Ofainɛ, boa mi! Nua nyɛ Ekua obumo wɔ Takoradi market ni, ofainɛ frɛ someone shi jɛ bi. Ofa kpee shi obumo, ŋmɛi obui ground — nɔ enimɔ gbɛ, enyemi le oo. Nɛ ŋɛ, onyɔŋ ni nɔ ehii — ni look very wrong chale. Me yɛ Asare, mɛ kɛ ɔ wɔ shia. Ɔhɔmɔ dɛ, nɔ ŋɛ ɔtɔ lɛ o — onyemi le. Shwe etsotso ŋmɛi ni — chale, ni serious. Ofainɛ — ambulance frɛ, shi jɛ fast fast!
narrative_dagbani: Allo! N daa, sɔŋmi ma! N pɔɣa Ekua gbini ka ziŋ yuu ka o ŋun kpɛ sabi Takoradi market pɛlim, n bɛ yɛ ni yi frɛ bia yini yi bi ka yee. O gbini ka o ŋun dɛɣima sabi, ka din ka o bɛ yɛ yɛli daa — o ŋoo kaa lahi nyɛ vo daa, o nyɛ kaa kpɛm. N yuli la Asare, m bɛ o chɛŋ din o gbini. O nyɛ o maa yɛla dii o kaa ti ti soli gbɔŋ, ka o bɛ gɔliɣi mi daa, ka ziim bee o ŋun pɛlim — ka din yɛri yɛri! N daa, yi bi ka yee!
narrative_fante: Me nuabaa Ekua tuu fam pintinn ha Takoradi market, me pa wo kyew, ɛsɛ sɛ wode onipa bi brɛ ha ntɛm ntɛm. Ɔhwee fam na ɔ buee ne tiri wɔ fam no so, na seesei ɔnnka asɛm biara na ne kɔn nso ɛnyɛ yiye — ɛte sɛ ɛnyɛ pɛ. Me na me din de Asare, mewɔ ne nkyɛn bere a ebi baa saa. Ɔhome dɛɛ na ɔhome, nanso ɔnnyɛ ntwetwe biara — ɔgyina hɔ pataa. Na mogya retu fii ne tiri mu — ɛyɛ den, ɛyɛ den papa. Me pa wo kyew, mpɛn ntɛm!
narrative_gurene: Yinɛ! N yir pɔɣa Ekua bɔɔrɩ feo ka o kpɛŋ fam tiŋa, Takoradi market nɛ, n tɩɩma frɛ nnɩ deo yɛ ka o wa beŋa. O nyɛka fam ka o ŋun bɔɔ tiŋa zɔŋa, ka saa o nan gɛsɛ ka o yɔrɛ nyɛla ka o bee gare. Mɩ nyɛ Asare, m bɩɩ o la bee la o. O nɔɔna bee o ka o nan firi yel la, o nan yisi yela ba — ziim bee o ŋun zɔŋa. N tɩɩma, yi yɛ kparbiisi — frɛ ambulance beŋa, nyɛla ka o yɛligu!

narrative_en: Please, I want to report something that happened here in Nima. My neighbor's son, Issah, was attacked by some boys this evening near the roadside — they beat him and he got a bad cut on his arm, and it's swollen now. Fuseini is my name, I'm the one calling. The boy is not unconscious or anything like that, but the wound is serious and his arm is paining him a lot. We need to know if we should take him somewhere or what we should do.
narrative_ewe: Meɖekuku, meɖi be matso nuŋɔŋlɔ ɖe ame siwo ŋu, abe nusiwo ƒu ame si le afim Nima. Ɖevi si le ŋutɔnyalawo ƒe vi, Issah, wódzí egblɔ egblɔ eŋu egbe fifia — amewo ŋɔ ame aɖewo yí dó egbé le ʋɔ ŋkɔe — wóta egblɔ, eye wó ɖe asi dzi tso dzi, eye asi ka bɔ egblɔ dele. Fuseini nye ŋkɔe, nye ŋutɔ wòfrɛ mi. Ɖevi la eɖo edzi — eɖo edzi nam kpɔkpɔ, eye eƒe ŋu xɔ asi o, gake nusɔsɔ la sɔ didi, eye asi la yi eyi nam ʋuʋu. Míaɖi si be mídé eme na agbé le miaɖe ɖe asiwo, abe nusiwo míawɔ na eŋu.
narrative_ga: Ofainɛ, mifɛ report bii shi ŋ dzɛ Nima. Mi mli blɛ bie dɛbi, Issah — boys bi tumi nɔ ni ehii ni kpee dzẽ yɛlɛ — wɔ bɔɔ ni nɔɔ, shikpon ni gbɔ bi ŋ kɔ gbeke, nɔ ni swollen le sane. Fuseini name bi, miye nii frɛ ŋ. Dɛbi yɛ enimɔ gbɛ o, kɛ wound bi yɛ serious, ni gbɔ hii ni lɛ paaa. Mifɛ shi bo — mitsɔ ni hospital, abi hɛɛ mishi bii?
narrative_dagbani: N daa, n bɛ yɛ ni n yɛla din kɔri ka o ti kɛ Nima. N doo biɛla, Issah, gbɛribu kasi o yɛssi aboŋ din be nyɛŋ doo — ka di saliya din kpɛ o nuu, ka o nuu sɔɣim daa. Fuseini n yuli, n nini be o frɛ yi. Bia o nyɛ o maa yɛla ka ka — o ti bɔri kuri ka — amma ziim ŋuni din kpɛ o nuu n'ka ka yɛligu daa o nuu pam. N bori yi n yɛl' maa ka dini kpɛ nyɛla ka o ti kɔ, wall' ni yɛ dini n di maa bi di.
narrative_fante: Please, I want to report something that happened here in Nima. My neighbor's son, Issah, was attacked by some boys this evening near the roadside — they beat him and he got a bad cut on his arm, and it's swollen now. Fuseini is my name, I'm the one calling. The boy is not unconscious or anything like that, but the wound is serious and his arm is paining him a lot. We need to know if we should take him somewhere or what we should do.
narrative_gurene: N tɩɩma, m bɛ yɛ kɛ n tuma yɛligu ning bi maaɣɩ ka Nima. M pɔɣɔ bia, Issah, biisi la a dɛ o gbɛ o zɔɔ bioŋ a Nima nyɛŋ see — ba gbɛɛ o la ka o nɔɔri ka nɔɔri ning bee o a o nɔɔri pʋɣʋ, ka nɔɔri ning a yam yam zɔɔŋ. M yʋʋrɩ la Fuseini, mam la b'a frɛ. Bia ko ka a nyɛ te, amaa nɔɔri la yɛligu wʋsɛ, ka o nɔɔri yɛligu o wʋsɛ wʋsɛ. Ti bɛ yɛ kɛ ti nyɛ a ti na ŋan ti kɛ o nyɛŋ teŋa kɛ a la bee ti na bɛ maaɣɩ.

narrative_en: Please, something happened on the Bawku road, close to Zebilla. A young man, Nii Kwei, was knocked down by a tipper truck and he fell badly on the road. He is not unconscious or anything like that, but his leg is looking very bad — maybe broken or seriously hurt, we are not sure. I am Akosua, I was passing there when it happened and some people have gathered around him. He needs help to come because he cannot stand on his own at all.
narrative_ewe:
Meɖekuku, nudzadzra ɖe Bawku ʋɔ dzi wòava, kplɔ̃ Zebilla. Ŋutsu ɖevi ɖeka, Nii Kwei, tipper truck dze eŋu o wu ƒu ʋɔ dzi vevie. Eɖo edzi — eƒu agbe, efɔ̃ o — gake afɔa nɔa vevie, eɖom be ɣleti vɔ, be efɔ ŋutɔ o kaka. Míaɖe si dzi gake mínyo tso daa o.

Nyem Akosua — m'le ʋɔ dzi ɖa la ŋu wòyia, míakpɔ nudzadzra ɔ. Amewo le eŋɔ si le fifia. Eɖi kpɔ ŋunye vevie esi ate ŋu vaƒo asiwo — emate ŋu ɖo afɔ dzi to ŋutɔ ŋu o kata. Meɖekuku, frɛ ambulance mi va kpɔ eɖe!
narrative_ga: Ofainɛ, something happen for Bawku road, near Zebilla. Ŋun baaba biɛ, Nii Kwei, tipper truck bu no, ebumo koko for kpee no. Enimɔ gbɛ biɛ o — oni le, onyemi le — but gboo no eni shiaŋ kɔkɔɔ — maybe fracture, abi ehii no wɔɔ, mitsɔ shɛkɛ o. Me na Akosua — me wɔ hɛ nɔ ehi no happen, na nii gbɛɛ bɔ ehe no shɔ. Boa no atswa obaa, ofainɛ — onyemi gbɛ eŋmɛ faa le o.
narrative_dagbani: N daa, ka yɛla kpɛŋ Bawku nyɛŋ so, Zebilla kpiŋ. Samli bila, Nii Kwei, tipper truck dɔ o, ka o gbini nyɛŋ so yɛligu yɛligu. O ti soli kɔ o nyɛ o maa yɛla kɔ — ɔɔ, o maa nyɛ o soŋ — ka o tiŋ naa yɛligu daa. Winimi o tiŋ kprɛ, ka winimi o tiŋ yɛligu pam — ti nyɛ yɛligu puŋ. N din nyɛ Akosua; nba m bɛ nyɛŋ so m bɛ kɔ ka yɛla kpɛŋ, ka nima dɔɔ doŋ bii din chɛ o kpeŋ. O bori la sɔŋ, bɔ o suu o ti raa nuu, o ti sɔŋ o nuu titi.
narrative_fante: Me pa wo kyew, biribi asi Bawku ɔkwan so, Zebilla ho faako. Ɔbarima ketewa bi, Nii Kwei, tipper truck bi bɔɔ no maa ɔtuu fam yiye wɔ ɔkwan so no so. Ɔnkolapuse anaa biribi saa ara — ɔte ase, na ɔhunu hwee — nanso ne nan no hyɛ hɔ na eye hu yiye — ebia fracture ne ho anaase ɛyɛ no yaw kɛse, yɛnnim nokwasem no daa. Me din de Akosua, na me rekɔ hɔ ara na na esii. Nnipa bi ahyia no ho, nanso ɔhia mmoa kɛse — ɔntumi agyina ne nan so koraa.
narrative_gurene: N tɩɩma, bɛ kɔŋ yɛ Bawku nyɛŋ so, Zebilla saɣim. Doa bia, Nii Kwei, tipper truck dɔɔ o, ka o kpɛŋ nyɛŋ so bãane. O ko a nyɛ yɛlɩ la, amaa o nʋŋ ka yɛligu noɔgo — wiɛla o nʋŋ bɔrɛ, ka bɛ mi sɔɔrɛ. Mam yɛ fo, m yɛ Akosua — m da be be ka bɛ kɔŋ yɛ, ka neba dɔɔ o saɣim zɔɔŋa. O tara sɔŋ ka o wa, bala o tɩɩŋa ka o zɔŋ a nɩ o tɩma.

narrative_en: My sister Abena was knocked by an OA taxi just outside our family house here in Navrongo. She is a young girl, maybe around sixteen. The bike hit her leg but she is okay, she is not unconscious or anything like that — just her leg is paining her small and there is a cut there. I am Araba, I am the one calling. Please we just want to know what we should do for her now.
narrative_ewe: Allo! Meɖekuku, kpe ɖe ŋunye! Nɔvinyɔ me Abena wò xɔ OA taxi ɖe asi le yɔ be woƒe ƒome aɖe si le Navrongo la ŋutɔ. Ɖevi nyɔnu enye, eƒe ƒe la ɖe ƒe wuiɖeka vɔ la ŋutɔ la ene. Akɔ la wu eƒe afɔ, gake eɖo edzi — eɖo edzi o taɔ, ele teƒe, esi o nuɖoɖo ɖe asi o. Afɔ ŋu ko eyi nam kpui, eye woƒu eƒe afɔ dɔ abe ɣlevia aɖe ene. Nyɔŋutɔ Araba nyem, ŋɔŋlɔ lɔm. Meɖekuku, kpɔ mi ɖe ŋuti — miwɔ ŋudi ka wòanɔ nam tso fifia?
narrative_ga: Hɛloo, ofainɛ boa mi — naanɔ mi Abena, OA taxi bumo no just outside yɛ family house shi le Navrongo. Oblɛ bi le, maybe around sixteen years. Kaa no bumo no gboo — gboo no hii no kakra, nɔ cut bi le ŋ — but enyemi le, enimɔ gbɛ o, onyemi le paa. Mi yɛ Araba, mii frɛ. Ofainɛ, yɛ pɛ sɛ yɛ nyɛ what we should do for no now.
narrative_dagbani: Allo, N daa. Yolgu maa, pio maa Abena, OA taxi gbɛ o Navrongo, ti yili kpiɣibu doo. O nyɛ biɛla, o zaguri pia ayɔbu bee. Motor — aaa, taxi — gbɛ o tiŋ, ama o bɛ kuli daa — o ti soli wula, o ti nyɛ yɛla wula — tiŋ doo ka yɛligu o daa chee, ka sibu bee o doo. Mi maa nyɛri Araba, n kuri shɛli. N daa, ti bori n nyɛ, ti bu ma doo bɛ ka o ni naa? Sɔŋ ti n nyɛ ka ti bu o bɛ.
narrative_fante: Me pa wo kyew, me nuabaa Abena — taxi bi bɔɔ no fɛ ha Navrongo, yɛn fie akyirikyiri no. Ɔbaa ketewa ni, nna enum ne nsia a etwa to no mu — edu nsia kɔ edu nson n'aborɔ mu. Motobaik no bɔɔ n'nan, nanso ɔho yɛ — ɔnkolapuse ɛnna ɔnhunu hwee — n'nan na ɛyɛ no yaw kakra, na wɔatwa no hɔ bi. Me din de Araba, na me na merefrɛ yi. Me pa wo kyew, yɛpɛ sɛ yɛhunu deɛ yɛbɛyɛ ma no seesei ara.
narrative_gurene: M sɔ Abena — OA taxi bɔɔrɩ o kʋŋ a yɛŋ nɩ ɩ yɩrɩ a yir poore Navrongo. O bi pɔɣa, a bɛ suɣiri ayima o lɛ nɩ bomo ayi nɩ ayi a bee. Kaɣa bɔɔrɩ o nɩ o nɔŋ, ama o nyɛ kãa — o ko a nyɛ daa yɛla, o nyɛ zee. A sɩŋa a yɛligu o nɔŋ la yɛ, ka ziim too o nɔŋ zɔɣɔ. Mam yɛ fo, m yɩrɩ Araba — mam nɩ frɛ fo. N tɩɩma — sɔŋ mam bɩ gʋbgʋ: bɩ yɩrɩ bɩ maal nɩ Abena sasa?

narrative_en: Please, my name is Ofori and I want to report something that happened to an old woman here near Savelugu, on the Tamale-Yendi road. She is called Naa Dedei, she is quite elderly, and she was doing some farm work by the roadside when something fell on her — I think it was from the cart she was loading. She is in pain around her chest and her back, but she is still conscious and talking, so it is not the worst, but she needs help. I am with her now and she cannot stand up properly.
narrative_ewe: Meɖekuku, ŋkɔnyé enye Ofori, eye meɖi gblɔ wo nu etso le afisia Savelugu dzi, le Tamale-Yendi ʋɔ dzi. Nyɔnu srɔ̃ si le afea ŋkɔ nye Naa Dedei — agbenyaga kpem. Ele ʋɔ ŋkɔ le adzeƒe aɖe wò wɔa dzewo ɣlɔ̃ akɔ si la ƒe ŋɔ dzi, eye nu aɖe wó do akɔ la dzi wo, eɖo aƒe na ewe — mebu be akɔ tɔtro la teƒe la dzi ame. Eyi nam le ɖevi ŋɔ kple eƒe ʋu megbenyawo dzi, gake eɖo edzi dodo, eye ele ŋu gblɔ nu — nenema koɖé, gake eɖi kpɔ nyui. Meŋle ƒu eŋu fifia, eye mate ŋu zu ɖe zi o. Meɖekuku, frɛ ambulance mi wò va kpɔ ewe akɔkɔe.
narrative_ga: Hɛloo, ofainɛ — mitsɔŋ Ofori, ni mifɛ report bɔɔ shi Savelugu kɛ, Tamale-Yendi kpee yi. Gbɔmɔ yɛ le shi, onitsɔŋ Naa Dedei — oni gbɔmɔ pɛpɛɛpɛ. Otsɔ farm work oshɛ kpee kɔ, ni bɔɔ yɛ bumo ŋ — mitsui cart bi ni otsɔ load oshɛ, ni bɔɔ kɛ ebumo ŋ. Ehii ŋ gbɔɔ kɛ okusum yi, shikpon mli — ni hii ŋ pɛɛ. Kɛɛ, oni le dɛɛ, ogbɛi le wɔ ni okasa le — eŋɛ ewie biaa, kɛɛ boa ni shi. Mihe ŋ kɔŋ sɔŋ yaɔ, kɛɛ oni tse ji shiɛŋŋ.
narrative_dagbani: Allo, N daa. M yɛligu din Ofori, ka n bɔri kɔligu shɛli din nini o kpɛma bi o la Naa Dedei, o bɛ Savelugu saɣim, Tamale-Yendi nyɛŋ daba. O kpɛma daa, ka o bɛ o farm tobu nyɛŋ ŋɔ, ka shɛli gbini o zuɣu — m tuma o kaaʼa shɛli din o bɛ ko-m-ko o la. Ka yɛligu bee o digindi ni o zuɣu nyɛŋ, amaa o ti soli daa — o nyɛ ni o yuɣu yɛla tuma. O bori sɔŋ. M bɛ o faan ka o ti zuɣu niŋ tobu daa. N daa, yi sɔŋ ma ka yi frɛ ambulance.
narrative_fante: Me pa wo kyew, me din de Ofori na mepɛ sɛ meka biribi a esii ɔpanyin bi ha fã Savelugu ho, wɔ Tamale-Yendi ɔkwan so. Wɔfrɛ no Naa Dedei, ɔyɛ panyin paa, na ɔreyɛ afuom adwuma fã ɔkwan mpoano no ho a biribi tuu no so — mete sɛ ɛfiri cart a ɔno de no so. Ɛyɛ no yaw wɔ ne bruwa ne ne akyi ho, nanso ɔte hɔ rekan, enti ɛnyɛ dɛ ɛboro saa, nanso ɔhia mmoa. Mɛka ne ho seesei yi na ɔntumi nsɔre yiye.
narrative_gurene: Please, my name is Ofori and I want to report something that happened to an old woman here near Savelugu, on the Tamale-Yendi road. She is called Naa Dedei, she is quite elderly, and she was doing some farm work by the roadside when something fell on her — I think it was from the cart she was loading. She is in pain around her chest and her back, but she is still conscious and talking, so it is not the worst, but she needs help. I am with her now and she cannot stand up properly.

narrative_en: My sister Afia was involved in a car accident not too far from our house here in Suame, Kumasi. She was coming back home when a vehicle hit her, but thank God it was not too serious. She has some pain around her stomach and chest area, like she got hit there, but she is awake and talking. I am Agyeman, I am with her now at the house. Please we just want to know what we should do for her.
narrative_ewe: Allo! Meɖekuku, kpe ɖe ŋunye! Nɔvinyɔnu ce Afia wɔ accident ɖe Suame la ŋu, Kumasi — mele afɔ kakaka aƒe maƒe ene. Ele ewɔ ƒu la nu, akɔ deka dze edzi le ʋɔ dzi, gake Mawu kɔ, nye nuɖoɖo o. Eyi nam ɖe abɔ kple ɖevi ŋɔ aƒa ŋu — ele be woyi dzo ɖe afɔaƒe la ŋu. Gake eɖo edzi, ele egbɔgblɔ. Nyem Agyeman, mele eŋu fifia ɖe aƒe. Meɖekuku, kpɔ ŋunye, ze ŋkɔ na miaƒe, na mia be mia ga wɔ he la nɛ tututu.
narrative_ga: Hɛloo, boa mi — naaŋ Afia obu accident kɛ kaa, ni fɛɛ yɛ shia kɛɛ, Suame, Kumasi. Ebi kɔ yaŋ ni kaa taa ni, kɛ shukuu Nyɔŋmɔ, ni eni ko serious wɔ. Kɛ ehii ni shi kɛ gbɔɔ, naŋ ekɛ taa ni wɔ gbei, kɛ oni le kɛ edaa. Miŋ Agyeman, mi le nɛɛ bii ko kɛ yaŋ shia. Ofainɛ — shi naa mɛ yɛ ni hɛ bɔɔ?
narrative_dagbani: Allo, N daa. Manbiɛla Afia o kpɛŋ accident — n yili kanna Suame, Kumasi. O ŋmani bee pam ni digindi yɛligu — ka yɛligu bee o bee wɛra. Ama Godna ka o nyɛ yɛligu wuhibu. O nyɛ bii, o goli, o yɛlda. Maa yuli Agyeman, m bɛ o chɛŋ ziŋ. N daa, yi naa yi yɛl maa ŋun o ka ti bɔri ti mala ka ti yi ti ŋmani o.
narrative_fante: Me nuabaa Afia hyɛɛ accident wɔ Suame, Kumasi hɔ — ɛnnyɛ kakra na ɛwɔ yɛn efie nkyɛn. Ɔresan aba fie a, kar bi bɔɔ no, nanso Onyame adom, ɛnyɛ den paa. Ɔyaw no wɔ ne yafunu ne ne bruwa ho — sɛ bɔɔ no wɔ hɔ — nanso ɔrenya n'aho na ɔrekan asɛm. Me din de Agyeman, na mewɔ ne nkyɛn ha wɔ fie. Me pa wo kyew, yɛpɛ sɛ yɛhunu nea yɛbɛyɛ ma no.
narrative_gurene: Yinɛ! M pɔɣa Afia, o da accident bi Suame, Kumasi, a ka ŋa yɛ tɩ yir. O da ba yiri la kaɣa da pɛl o, la a zuɣu Yinɛ, ka a ka yɛ tuonlana. O yɛligu a pʋʋrɛ mɩŋ la o gʋŋ, bɩ la o da pɛl biɛ, la o nyɛŋ a o goama. M yɛ Agyeman, m bee o saha yɛ yir. N tɩɩma, tɩ bɔri a bɩ nyɛ dɛ tɩ na maalɩ o la.

narrative_en: My name is Afia, I'm calling because there was an accident on the Tamale-Yendi road, close to Savelugu. A man called Appiah was in a trotro that was hit by an articulated truck, and he was thrown out. He has some cuts on his head and his neck is paining him, but he is awake and talking so it doesn't look too serious. We are by the roadside now and we need someone to come and check him properly.
narrative_ewe: Allo! Nyeŋɔ nye Afia. Mefrɛ emi le asi ŋu accident bubu si wòwɔ le Tamale-Yendi ʋɔ dzi, kplɔ̃ Savelugu hasɔ. Ŋutsu ɖeka, wofrɛ eŋu Appiah, eле trotro me, eye articulated truck ɖeka dze ɖe trotro ma, eye woŋlɔ Appiah fié afé etɔ. Afi bubuwo le eƒe ta dzi, eye eƒe ɖo yi eŋu, gake edzi edzi eɖo edzi o — le gbe xɔxɔ kple ɖoɖo wɔm, eye menya be ame nyui kaka o. Fifia miaƒo ʋɔ sisi, eye miedi be ame aƀɔ ava kpɔ eŋu nyuie.
narrative_ga: Mɛ din de Afia, mɛfrɛ shɛ accident bi nyɛ Tamale-Yendi road ŋ dɔŋ, Savelugu kɛ. Oba bi wɔ Appiah din, otseo trotro bi ŋ dɔŋ — articulated truck bi bɔ trotro ŋ, nɔ ebumo faaa outside. Wɔ ni lɛ nɔ shwe etsotso ŋ bi ni oŋmɛi ŋ, shi ni gbɔɔ — eŋmɛi ehii mli, kɛ ŋ dɔŋ naa ni neck ŋ ehii ŋ — shikpɔŋ ni enimɔ le, eshii le, oshishi le, chale, enɔ gbi lɛ kome loo. Yɛhɛɛ kpee ŋ dɔŋ naa hɛla yɛ, mɛshwɛ oba kɛ doctor bi aba lɛ check ɔ kɛɛ.
narrative_dagbani: Allo, m yɛligu Afia. N bɛ frɛ yɛ bɔŋ accident na daa zaa Tamale-Yendi nyɛŋ so, Savelugu saɣim. Nindoli yini, o yɛligu Appiah, o bɛ trotro pum, ka articulated truck na daa bu trotro na, ka o lahi o kpɛm pam nyɛŋ so. O ŋun so aboli zaa bee o, ka o ŋun tiŋ yɛligu n daa o. Amaa o nyɛ o maa jijiga n bɛ yɛl kuli, ka a yɛ be ku gbɛri ka yɛla na nyɛ be ka yɛlgu. Ti bɛ nyɛŋ doo ni zɔŋ, ka ti bori la nɔ yini n bi n na gʼo nyɛ viɛla.
narrative_fante: Me din de Afia. Merefrɛ wo efisɛ accident bi baa Tamale-Yendi kwan so, Savelugu nkyɛn. Ɔbarima bi a wɔfrɛ no Appiah naa ɔwɔ trotro mu a articulated truck bɔɔ mu, na wɔde no hwee fam. Ne tiri so wɔ cuts na ne kɔn yɛ no yaw, nanso ɔnhɔ na ɔrekan asɛm, enti ɛnyɛ hu paa. Yɛwɔ kwan nkyɛn seesei na yehia obi a obɛba nhwɛ no yiye.
narrative_gurene: My name is Afia, I'm calling because there was an accident on the Tamale-Yendi road, close to Savelugu. A man called Appiah was in a trotro that was hit by an articulated truck, and he was thrown out. He has some cuts on his head and his neck is paining him, but he is awake and talking so it doesn't look too serious. We are by the roadside now and we need someone to come and check him properly.

narrative_en: My brother Yaw was involved in a road accident on the Accra-Kumasi highway, somewhere around Nsawam. I am Baffour, his elder brother, and someone called me that a vehicle hit him from behind while he was crossing the road. He is a young man, maybe around nineteen or twenty years. From what I was told, he has serious pain in his chest and his side, and he cannot stand up straight, but he is conscious and talking. Please, we need help to get him to a hospital quickly.
narrative_ewe:
Allo! Meɖekuku, kpe ŋunye! Ƒo nye ŋkɔ nye Baffour, eye nɔvi ta Yaw wò xɔ nudzadzra le Accra-Kumasi ʋɔ dzi, ƒe Nsawam kɔ. Wofrɛm be akɔ ɖeka bu eŋu afi si eya ʋɔ da eye nudzadzra ma wɔ eŋu ɖe ega.

Yaw ɖevi ŋutsu — evelia be eƒe ƒeawo le ewo alo ewoɖeka hã kɔ. Gake ame si kpe ɖe eŋu ɖoa nam be eyi eŋu ŋutɔ ɖe eƒe ɖevi ŋɔ kple eƒe ŋɔ ŋkume ŋutɔ — eyi nam ŋutɔ. Emate ŋu zɔ ɖo ɖo o, gake eɖo edzi, egblɔgblɔ.

Meɖekuku! Meɖi kpɔ ŋunye. Woaƒo ambulance nami, abe wòaƒle eya na asiwo nyuie ɖe ɖoɖo ɖeka me. Dɔ si wòaƒle tso esia mi va egbɔ ɖoɖo, ko nyuie. Meɖekuku, djro nami vitsi!
narrative_ga: Hɛloo, ofainɛ, boa mi — accident abɔ me nua Yaw wɔ Accra-Kumasi highway mli, somewhere around Nsawam. Miinɛ Baffour, oni nua panɔ, eye ŋ obiara frɛ mi kɛ kaa bumo ŋ wɔ ni wↄ kɔ agbaa kpee wↄ. Ŋ yɛ oblɛ bi, approximately nineteen or twenty years. Wↄ kɛɛ mi kɛ ŋ gbɔɔ hii ŋ kɛkɛ, kɛ ŋ side naa hii ŋ, eye ŋ tii gbã straight o — ŋ titi straight o. Kɛ ŋ oni le, ŋ kasa bi le. Ofainɛ, yɛ hia help ni yɛ kɔ ŋ hospital fɛfɛɛfɛ — chale, frɛ ambulance, boa mi!
narrative_dagbani: N daa, m deem Yaw kpɛŋ accident din o bɛ nyɛŋ doo Accra-Kumasi highway ni, din bɛ Nsawam doŋ. Mma Baffour, o deem paɣa, n daa. Niiŋ biɛɣu m ni kaɣa din tuuni o zaa o bɛ nyɛŋ tɔɣim. O nyɛ doli bia, o yɛ shɛli pia-awɛi wala pia-pia lala. Din din m winni, o digindi ka yɛligu dani, ni o tiim maaŋ maaŋ, ka o bɛ tumi goli daa bɔribiɛɣu, ama o nyɛ soli, o bɛ yɛli. N daa, yi sɔŋ yi frɛ sɔŋ ma yi kpɛŋ o hospital ni niŋ yɛliŋ.
narrative_fante: Me pa wo kyew, me nuabarima Yaw ayi accident wɔ Accra-Kumasi highway no so, bɛn Nsawam hɔ. Mene Baffour, ɔne me nuabarima, na obi frɛɛ me sɛ kar bi bɔɔ no wɔ ne yɛnkɔ so bere a ɔrekɔ twa ɔkwan no. Ɔyɔnkofo, abɔfra no mu, bɛn mfe duekron anaa mfemenu so. Sɛ wɔkae kyerɛɛ me no, ɔhunu yaw kɛse wɔ ne bruwa ne ne nkyɛn, na ɔntumi nsɔre trɔtrɔɔ, nanso ɔte nkwa mu na ɔreka asɛm. Me pa wo kyew, yehia mmoa na yɛnkɔ de no kɔ hospital ntɛm ara.
narrative_gurene: Yinɛ! M pio Yaw bɔɔrɩ accident Accra-Kumasi nyɛŋ pʋɔ, Nsawam saɣim. Mam yɛ fo — mɩɩ la Baffour, o pio kɔrɔŋ. Ned fɔɔ bilim mam bɔ kaɣa yɩɩrɩ o poore pʋɔ a o bee pʋɔ nyɛŋ gɛŋɛ wɛ. O la biig, o mɩɩma zuɣ o la yenno la adaŋ ŋa naas. Wɩɩna ban bɔɔrɩ mam la bɔ ka yɛligu dɔɣɩ o timpeeni pʋɔ la o gbɔŋ pʋɔ, la ka o tɩ zɔɣ zɔɣɔ, la o ko nyɛ a o bee yɛla. N tɩɩma — sɔŋ mam a tɩ mɩ o wa hospital siiri siiri!

narrative_en: Please, I want to report something that happened here in Savelugu this evening. My neighbor, Fuseini — he is an old man — was coming back from the market when some men grabbed him and tried to take his money. They pushed him down and hit his arm when he tried to resist, and now the arm is swollen and he cannot lift it at all. He is in pain but he is conscious and talking. I am Abubakar, someone called me when it happened. Please someone should come and help him because he is old.
narrative_ewe: Meɖekuku, meɖi ɖe nudzadzra bubu siwo wòwɔ fifi afisia Savelugu. Aƒegbɔgblɔ̃ ŋutsu ŋunye, Fuseini — srɔ̃ nye — le agble do le lo akɔxɔme, gake ame bubuwo zã asi dzi eŋu la ewo. Wòtso eƒe ga aɖe ɖa, eye wòkpɔ eŋu ƒu, eye wòtsoo eƒe asi dzi gã hã le nugɔ la me, elabena ewòwo ɖe ame ŋu. Esia zu te, eƒe asi fã kpui kpui la, eye mate ŋu tsɔ asi la kpe ɖe anyiehe o. Eyi nam kpɔɖeŋu, gake eɖo edzi ale koe, eye edze gblɔgblɔ. Nyeŋutsu la nye Abubakar — ame ŋuti biame afoe nudzadzra la wòle wɔm la. Meɖekuku, mia ame aɖe ni va kpe ɖe eŋu, elabena srɔ̃ nye, eye elea dzetugbe gã ŋu.
narrative_ga: Ofainɛ, metsɔ bɔ report kɛ shi wɔ Savelugu gbɔŋ, yɛhii. Ni manɔ, Fuseini — gbɔmɔ ni oshwɛ — ɔyɛ nɔ market tɛɛ fee, kɛ nnɔ gbɛi akɛ ɔtsrɛ, ŋmɛi ni oo sane ɔsane ɔkɛ ɔhɛ ni ogbɛ oshwɛ. Ebumo, kɛ ebuu ŋ ni gbɔ tete ni ɔlɛ resist, kɛ yiɛ gbɔ omo swollen — ɔmiti kpoo shia o. Ehii mi gbɔ ŋ, nikɛ oni le, ɔkasa kasa. Me ni Abubakar — obi frɛ mi tete kɛ accident ŋ etso. Ofainɛ, obi ni ebɔ, shi kɛ boa ŋ — gbɔmɔ ni oshwɛ, chale.
narrative_dagbani: N daa, n bɛ yɛ ni n yɛl aabiɛ ti di paai ka o maa ti zaa Savelugu gbɛra kaa. N dobiili, Fuseini — o nye kpɛma — o beei ti gba zuɣu ka o yi wuntɔŋ waa, ka bihi ŋmariba nii o ka o bɔri o gbaŋa silimi. Ka bi dii o ka bi gbani o nuu waa ka o maa si bi kpem, ka o nuu maa kuri daa ka o maa tali a yuli biŋ. O nyɛ yɛligu daa amaa o nyɛ o palli ka o kuli yɛla. N tooi Abubakar, o yɛl ma ka din paai. N daa, dobiili ni bi ti yi n sɔŋ o ŋun, barka, o bi nye kpɛma daa.
narrative_fante: Me pa wo kyew, mepɛ sɛ meka biribi a asɪɛ ha wɔ Savelugu anɔpa yi. Me yɔnko, Fuseini — ɔyɛ opanyin — ɔreba fie fiɛ market no mu no, mmarima bi kyeree no bɛbrɛ na wɔpɛɛ sɛ wɔde ne sika gu wɔn nsam. Wɔpushirii no fam na wɔbuee ne nsa no mu bere a ɔpɛɛ sɛ ɔsɔre akyɛ wɔn, na afei ne nsa no ahyew na ɔntumi nnye no soro koraa. Ɛyɛ no yaw paa, nanso ɔteɛ ase na ɔrekasa. Mena Abubakar — obi frɛɛ me bere a asɪɛ no. Me pa wo kyew, obi mmra ha mmoa no, efisɛ ɔyɛ opanyin.
narrative_gurene: N tɩɩma, m bʋɔrɩ a tɩ yɛl bʋɔrɩ za a wa maŋa Savelugu yɛl nɛ a zaaŋ. M dua, Fuseini — o yɛ nindaan — o wa be daa pʋɣʋ sɔɔna, ka anɩ dagbɩna nɩ kɔ o kɔɔr, n sɔŋ n bɔ o sɔlɩŋ. Ba push o fam ka ba kpaa o nuɣu, bɩ o nɩ gɔl ba nɩ, ka dɩɣa o nuɣu nɛ a yeŋe ka o da tɩ tɔɣɩ o wa zɔm o. O yɛligu doo, ama o ko nyɛ ka o nɩ yɛl. Mam yɛ fo, Abubakar la mam — ŋun nɩ bɔɔl mam tɩ tɩ bɩ maa. N tɩɩma ŋun nɩ wa sɔŋ o, bɩ o yɛ nindaan doo.

narrative_en: Please, I want to report an accident that happened on the Cape Coast-Elmina road. A young man called Darkwa was knocked by an aboboyaa while he was crossing, and he fell down. I am Edem, I was standing nearby when it happened. He is up and talking, but his leg is paining him small and there is a cut on it. We need someone to come and check him because we are not sure if anything is broken.
narrative_ewe: Meɖekuku, meɖi gblɔ accident siwo wɔ ye Cape Coast-Elmina ʋɔ dzi fia. Ŋutsu aɖe ƒe ŋkɔ nye Darkwa — aboboyaa wo edze eŋu ɣeyiɣi me be eɖo ʋɔ dzi, eye edo ƒu. Nyem nye Edem, me le teƒe gbɔ ɣeyiɣi si nudzadzra la wɔ. Esia, eɖo edzi eye egblɔa ɣlɔ, gake afɔa eyi nam dzetsey eye nɔvi la le dzi eŋu. Meɖi ame aɖe va kpɔ eɣe, elabena míeme nyam hã be ɣleti vɔ la o abe mógbe o.
narrative_ga: Ofainɛ, mebii report accident bi wo Cape Coast-Elmina kpee dzɛ. Onukpa dɛbi bi wɔ frɛ Darkwa, aboboyaa bi ko ɔ wɔ ɔ kpee kɔ, ebumo fɛɛ. Miŋ nyɛ Edem — mɛhɛ ni shikpon wɔ baako wɔ ni ehii. Ɔni le kɛ ɔkɛ yɛ nii nɔŋ, kɛ gboo bi hii ɔ kakra, kɛ cut bi wɔ fɛɛ ni. Yɛhia onukpa bi wo bi na ɔbɛ check ɔ, bii yɛnɔŋ fracture bi wɔ fɛɛ abi daabi.
narrative_dagbani: N daa, m bɛ yɛ ni kɔligu accident ŋun be Cape Coast-Elmina nyɛŋ doo. Dindaan bila yɔligu Darkwa, aboboyaa nakʼo o nyɛŋ ŋɔ ka o gbini. Maa nyɛ Edem, m gbini tɔŋ mali bi kpe yɛlim naa. O kpɛŋ o chɛŋ kaabu, amaa o tiŋ yɛligu daa o gbali, ka tiebu bɛ o tiŋ doo. Ti bori la nɔŋ o ni pa o giri, doŋ ti niŋ ti ti nyɛ maa wula din yeli a ti nyɛ ti siɣim doo bi.
narrative_fante: Me pa wo kyew, mepɛ sɛ meka accident bi a ɛbaa Cape Coast-Elmina ɔkwan so. Obarima kakra bi a wɔfrɛ no Darkwa, aboboyaa bi bɔɔ no bere a ɔrekɔ atwam, na ɔtuu fam. Mene Edem, na megyina hɔ fitii ase bere a ɛbaa. Ɔsɔre agyina na ɔreka asɛm, nanso nan yɛ no yaw kakra, na ɛwɔ cut bi so. Yehia onipa bi sɛ ɔnna aba hwɛ no, efisɛ yennhu yiye sɛ biribi broken anaa dabi.
narrative_gurene: Please, I want to report an accident that happened on the Cape Coast-Elmina road. A young man called Darkwa was knocked by an aboboyaa while he was crossing, and he fell down. I am Edem, I was standing nearby when it happened. He is up and talking, but his leg is paining him small and there is a cut on it. We need someone to come and check him because we are not sure if anything is broken.

narrative_en: Please, something serious has happened here in Dansoman. My grandmother Efua, she fell down this morning and she is in a very bad condition — one of her legs is twisted in a way that doesn't look right at all, and she can't move it. She was trying to come down from the porch steps and she just slipped and came down hard. I'm Naa, I'm her granddaughter, and we are very scared because she is old and she is crying from the pain. Please we need help to come quickly.
narrative_ewe: Meɖekuku, nudzadzra gãtɔ aɖe wɔ ɖe afim fiam afi si le Dansoman. Nɔnɔm Efua, edo ƒu gbã ŋdi sia — afɔ aɖe le eŋu eƒe te ne abe o, eye mate ŋu yi le agbem o. Ele eme le steps si le ɣlɔ̃ŋɔ ŋu, eye gbo si ɖe si akɔ, ewu ƒu gãgã. Nyem nye Naa, menye eƒe tɔgbuiɖevi, eye miaƒo atikɔ kpuie abe o — esi ele srɔ̃ la, eye eyi nam gãgã, le eblɛm dzi. Meɖekuku, mia di kpɔ ŋunye, miava kaba kaba.
narrative_ga: Ofainɛ, situation kɛ serious bi tsɔɔ wɔ Dansoman ha. Naa mi nyɛ — mi maŋtsɛ Efua, ebumo wɔ nɛɛ gbɔɔ, naanɔ condition nyɛ bɔɔ hewalɛ — ŋ gboo bi kee fɔɔ ni eko naanɔ shi wiemɔ, ehii ŋ lɛ. Oshiŋ hii ni eba wɔ porch steps lɛ shi, nɔ eshi lɛɛ eko kɛ force. Mi nyɛ Naa, ŋ maŋtsɛ oblɛ, mɛni lɛ tsɔɔ shi agbe lɛ kɛ hɔ — gbɔmɔ nyɛ nɔ, shɛ ehi kpɛkpɛ. Ofainɛ, mɛhii boa — baa faafoɔ, chale, baa wiemɔ.
narrative_dagbani: N daa, kuli tɔɣim bee ŋun ka wula kul naa ka bee nyɛŋ ka n bi ŋmɛ ŋmin. Dansoman ŋɔ yɛ ka n yɛra gbɛra kuli dɔɣiri. M baŋa Efua, o gbini ka ŋmɛ wɔlim ŋɔ, ka o nyɛla ka tarribu bihi. O tiŋ doo ŋɔ kpɛla ka diyɛla n di kuli — o tiŋ doo ŋɔ nyɛla o maa yɛla, ka o bi zaa o tɔɣim. O bɛ yɛ n bɔri nya yɔpɔ ka maa nyɛla tubu dii ka o gbini pam. N yɛla Naa, n yɛ o bibia, ka ti gbɛra yɛli daa — bɔlibu bee ti, bala o kpɛma ka o yɛligu bee o pam, o ziim loo. N daa, sɔŋ ti, frɛ ambulance di ku ŋmɛ. Yi maa mani!
narrative_fante: Me pa wo kyew, biribi a ɛyɛ hu paa aba ha Dansoman. Me nana Efua, ɔtuu fam saa anɔpa yi, na ne ho nyinaa nni mu yiye koraa — ne nan baako mu aba twist a ɛnhwɛ yiye sei, na ɔntumi nsa no. Ɔreba fam steps so a na ɔsɔre, na ɔkɔɔ fam maakwaa. Me ne Naa, me yɛ ne nana ba, na yɛn ani agye hu paa efisɛ ɔyɛ opanyin, na ɛyɛ no yaw dɛɛ ɔsu. Me pa wo kyew, yɛhia mmoa — mmra ha ntɛm ntɛm.
narrative_gurene: Please, something serious has happened here in Dansoman. My grandmother Efua, she fell down this morning and she is in a very bad condition — one of her legs is twisted in a way that doesn't look right at all, and she can't move it. She was trying to come down from the porch steps and she just slipped and came down hard. I'm Naa, I'm her granddaughter, and we are very scared because she is old and she is crying from the pain. Please we need help to come quickly.

narrative_en: My daughter Abigail was on the Hohoe-Jasikan road when a trotro hit her from behind. She is a young girl, maybe seventeen. I am her mother, my name is Hadija. She has some pain around her neck and a small cut on her head, but she is awake and talking to us. We are with her now and she seems okay, but I just wanted someone to know what happened.
narrative_ewe: Allo! Meɖekuku, ŋɔ nami. Nyɔnuvi tɔ nye Abigail. Ewo le Hohoe-Jasikan ʋɔ dzi, gake trotro ɖeka sɔ eƒe megbe. Nyɔnuvi aɖe wò, ebe agble aɖeke eƒe ŋɔlogɔ la me — abe gaƒoƒo eve me katã ene ene ene la bubu. Nye nɔ, nye ŋkɔ nye Hadija. Eyi nam le eƒe ɖo ŋkɔme, eye ta ŋɔkeke aɖe le tso enu, gake eɖo edzi, eye ele nu gblɔgblɔm le míaƒe dome. Míale eŋu fifia, eye eye ebuna ɖe bubu, gake meɖi be ame aɖe ni nya nu si wò wɔ.
narrative_ga: Hɛloo, ofainɛ — dɛbi bii Abigail ne, ohe Hohoe-Jasikan kpee so, trotro bumo no shikpon fɛɛ. Oblɛ yɛ — maybe seventeen. Miyɛ ni naŋ, mi din yɛ Hadija. Ehii no ni ŋmɛi yaŋ akɛ shi akɛ, akɛ kɛ kɔ kɔ no ni ŋmɛi so — shwe kɛɛ pii o — eko. Onyemi le akɛ, ogbɛɛŋ kɛ wɔ. Mɛhe ni nɔ ni abuuji. Chale, miwɔ ke frɛ nɔ fee, wɔ nyɛ kɛ nyɛ shia ni eehe.
narrative_dagbani: Allo, n daa. M biɛla Abigail bɛ Hohoe-Jasikan nyɛŋ ŋɔ a, trotro daa na o kum kpiŋ zaabu. O bi biɛla, o pia ayɔpɔin doli. N nyɛ o ma, n yuli Hadija. O yɛligu daa din ŋun yuŋ kɔŋ, ka o ŋun ŋɔ kɔŋ gbini kpitali. Amaa o nyɛ juɣuri ka o gbandaa ma tarigi. Ti bɛ o kɔŋ sana. O nyɛ ka o ti daa yɛli ma. N bɛ n yɛla tiŋ yɛlibu dorim, n daa bori din ŋun nyɛ yɛli o baŋ nini ka a shɛli.
narrative_fante: Me ba Abigail na ɔrekɔ Hohoe-Jasikan road no so a trotro bi bɔɔ no fii n'akyi. Ɔyɛ abaa ketewa — saa mfe duason na nso ɛbɛyɛ. Meyɛ ne maame, me din de Hadija. Ɔwɔ yaw kakra wɔ ne kɔn ho, na wɔabu ne tiri so kakra, nanso ɔda nkwa mu na ɔreka asɛm kyerɛ yɛn. Yɛwɔ ne ho dada yi, na ɔbɛyɛ saa okay. Me pɛɛ sɛ obi te asɛm a aba yi ase.
narrative_gurene: M bia Abigail bɛ Hohoe-Jasikan nyɛŋ pʋɔ, ka trotro bɔ o poore. O yɛ bia pɔɔ, o wɔ yenno ayi nii baarɩ. Mam yɛ o ma, M yʋʋrɩ yɛ Hadija. O tɩŋa yɛligu o ŋun bɔɔtɩ, ka ziim pʋɔ yaa bee o ŋun zɔŋ, ama o nyɛ o nii, o gomm ka tɩŋa. Ti bɛ o kɔŋ saa, o nyɛ daanɩ, ama M yɩŋ sɔŋ bia mam yɛ ŋɔ yɛ n nyɛ bee wʋn bɛ daanɩ.

narrative_en: "Please, something bad happened and I need to report it. My friend Muntari, he was attacked on the Hohoe-Jasikan road, some people beat him. They hit him on the head and his neck is also paining him but he is still awake and talking, so I don't think it is too serious. I am Abubakar, I was with him when it happened. We need somebody to come and help us or tell us what to do because he is not feeling fine at all."
narrative_ewe: Meɖekuku, nuvo ɖeka wɔ, eye meɖi se kpɔ ŋunye na mi. Edɔlaƒe nye Muntari, woyi wo ŋu le Hohoe-Jasikan ʋɔ dzi, ame geɖe wo ta ƒe eŋu. Wogblɔ eƒe ta, eye eƒe ɖo hã eyi nam, gake eɖo edzi kple ele gblɔgblɔm, nenye esia tso nye ŋkume be edzi gã o. Ŋkonyala nye Abubakar, me le eŋu xexe le blibo sia me. Meɖi ame aɖe aʋo abu abe mia gɔme kpɔ mia abe — kaka woɖo aƒe gɔ amegbɔ namiwo be miaɖo nu aɖe ŋu abe o la, esi nyo o, o miaɖo aɖe ŋu abe, esi nyo o. Meɖo edzi aɖa nyo na eŋu o katã.
narrative_ga: Ofainɛ, chale, something bad happen na mitsɔ report. Ɛŋ friend mi Muntari, wɔbuɔ no wɔ Hohoe-Jasikan kpee la, nnɔɔ bi fii no pɔ. Wɔtɔ no ŋmɛi la, na ɛŋ neck nɔ ehii no — ŋmɛi kɛ neck ni hii no — kɛɛ onyemi le nɔɔ, ohii le nɔɔ, so mitsɔ nyɛ ni serious kɛkɛ o. Miŋ Abubakar, na me kɛ no wɔ ŋ shiaŋ lɛ wɔ ni happen. Chale, boa mi — yɛhia somebody fi bɛ boa mii, kaa ɛnyɛ o, mɔkɔ, shi yɛ fɛɛ ni yɛ mɛtɛ ni ye — ɛfee Muntari enimɔ fine o gbɛɛ.
narrative_dagbani: N daa, kuli gbiani ka paɣi ka n bɛ yɛ ni n zaŋ a tum n yɛl' yɛla yi. M toa Muntari, nib' kpaɣi o bɛ Hohoe-Jasikan nyɛŋ zuɣu, nib' nubi o. B' dini o ŋun zuɣu ka o nyaŋ nyaŋ ka o yɛligu daa o zuɣ' bila ni. Amaa o ti paɣi — o ti soli naa, o bɛ yɛla ka o gbiti yɛl'si, ka n nyɛ a be yɛla n kpe ti daa. Maa yɛla n yɛla N yuli Abubakar, n bɛ ka yɛla paɣa tuma o chɛ. Ti bori nid' n wa ti sɔŋ, bɔ' bɔri yi gari ti tum naa ti di ŋman bɛ ti bɛ yɛ' din ti bɔri n bua, bala o ti nyɛla yiyɔɣu daa.
narrative_fante: Me pa wo kyew, bɔne bi aba na mehia sɛ meka ho asɛm. Me adamfo Muntari, wɔbɔɔ no fɔ wɔ Hohoe-Jasikan ɔkwan so — nnipa bi bɔɔ no. Wɔbuaa no tiri so, na ne kɔn nso eye no yaw, nanso ɔda hɔ rekankan, ɔnte ase da. Enti menhunu sɛ asɛm yi yɛ den paa. Me din de Abubakar — na mewɔ ne nkyɛn bere a asɛm no baa. Yehia obi a ɔbɛba mmoa yɛn, anaasɛ wɔkae bɛkyerɛ yɛn deɛ yɛnyɛ, efisɛ ne ho nyɛ koraa.
narrative_gurene: N tɩɩma, bʋʋd ɓoro bee tɩ la, ka m sɔŋ n yɛl fo a zuɣu. M doa Muntari, nɩbɛ nɩm o zɔŋ Hohoe-Jasikan nyɛŋ zuɣu, nɩbɛ dɛm o. Ba gom o ŋun zuɣu, ka o gbɔrɔɔ poore yɛligu o la, ama o ko a nyɛ akɩ o gom yellim yellim, a la ka m mɩŋ o yɛligu ka bʋʋd pa yɛligu be. Mam nyɛ Abubakar, m bee o la a yelle bee tɩ. Tɩ sɔŋ nɩ bu wa sɔŋ tɩ, abisi yɛl tɩ bʋʋd tɩ na maalɩ, bɩ Muntari nɩ zɔm o pam la, o ka pɔ rɩ ɓoro.

narrative_en: Please, I want to report something that happened to me here in Axim. I am Felicia, and I fell down inside the house this morning — I was coming down from the stairs and my leg just slipped. It's my arm that got hurt, but it's not too serious, just some pain and a small swelling. I can still move it small small, so I don't think anything is broken.
narrative_ewe: Meɖekuku, meɖe nane wòava ŋunye fifi la kaɖe na wo afisia Axim. Nyem nye Felicia, eye megbɔ ƒu le ƒe me egɔme — mele ablɔ̃wo dzi detso gbɔ le nu siawo si, eye afɔnyem tsɔ lã loo. Asim kɔ si eyi nam, gake kame yɔ ɖe wu ɖe o — eyi nam susu o, eye eme fɛfɛɛ le ge ge loo. Mate ŋu yi kpe na esi susu susu, nenye ŋutinya mele ŋu bé ɣleti vɔ o.
narrative_ga: Ofainɛ, mɛhiɛ bɔɔ kɛ mɛ ka bɔɔ ni hɛ mi ni Axim. Mii nyɛ Felicia, kɛ mɛbumo shi yɛ ni shia jɔ — mɛhiɛ tsɔɔ stairs ji kɛ me gboo ji slip. Gbɔ ji ni hii mi, kɛ enyɛ serious raa — hii kakra wɔ ni, kɛ swelling kakra wɔ ɛ. Mɛtsɔ kɛ move ni kakra kakra, chale, mɛsɔ susu kɛ fracture bi enyɛ ni.
narrative_dagbani: N daa, n bɛ yɛ ni n yɛli ka n nindim yɛrila doo Axim ka. N yuli la Felicia, ka n kpɛŋ yili puŋ dini yɛli — n bɛla n zaɣa zaɣa mali yili zuɣu ka n tiŋ bɛla n gbini. N nuu la ka a yɛligu, ama a yɛligu bi zaachi daa, yɛligu bilbiɛɣu kali a tibili bila. N naani n nuu bɛla n yii a n yii, ka n gbinli la ka kuli bi pɛla yaga.
narrative_fante: Me pa wo kyew, mepɛ sɛ mebɔ report bɛ fa biribi a ɛsii me ha wɔ Axim. Me din de Felicia, na mɛtuu fam wɔ efie saa anɔpa yi — na meresiɛ firi stairs so na me nan kɔɔ slip. Ɛyɛ me nsa na ɛhwe ase, nanso ɛnyɛ den paa, swelling kakra bi nko ara na ɛho yaw me kakra. Metumi keka no kakra kakra daa, enti menhwɛ sɛ biribi broken wɔ mu.
narrative_gurene: N tɩɩma, mam yɛ sɔŋ mam n bɩ ka a be saha la ŋa Axim. Mam yɛ Felicia, ka m bɔɔrɩ ka m kpɛŋ fam yɩrɩ a yir sɔ bɩŋ — mam bɩ yiri stairs zugu fam ka m nʋɔrɛ gbɔrɔ. N nʋʋrɛ n yɛligu, ama a yɛligu ka kpɛŋ bilbiisi, yenno swelling bilbiisi bee o pʋɔ. Mam tʋma a nʋʋrɛ ka na bilbilim, a zaa mam ko nyɛ t'a wʋlɩ dii a.

narrative_en: My brother Kojo fell down this morning here in Wa Central and hurt his leg. It doesn't look too serious, he's walking small small but he's limping and saying it's paining him. I'm Amankwah, I'm the one calling because he doesn't want to go to the hospital but I think they should check it well. Please, can someone advise us on what to do?
narrative_ewe: Allo, meɖekuku. Nɔvinyɔ Kojo wò edo ƒu egɔme fé ŋdi sia Wa Central tso, kpe eƒu afɔ. Mele nyanya vevié o — ele ɖe ɖrɔ̃ɖrɔ̃ la, gake ele yi zãzã zãzã, eye ele keke la, le gblɔm be afɔa yi enam. Nyem Amankwah, nye de ŋuti ƒo fon sia, elabena nɔvim Kojo megbe yi asiwo o, gake midze nu be wòa kpɔ afɔa ŋutɔ nyuie. Meɖekuku, ame aɖe ate ŋu kpe ɖe ŋumitɔ be míawɔ nane?
narrative_ga: Hɛloo, ofainɛ — mi yɛ Amankwah, mífrɛ mi loo. Mi nuŋ Kojo ebumo bii bɔɔ yaŋ ha Wa Central, ne egbe gboo ehii nɔ. Enilɔ serious pɛɛ o — oshie ke oshie kɔ, shi elonge, kɛ oshiɛ gboo ehii nɔ dɔŋ. Nɔ ŋɛ, ɔnyɛ ni kɔ hospital o — enye ni kɔ o — kɛ mii shishi ke hospital ni hɛ nɔ well. Chale, ofainɛ — obiɔŋ ni tumi advise mi shikpon? Yɛ ni bɔ nii? Mínaa nɔ dɔŋ.
narrative_dagbani: N daa, ma yɛltɔɣa Kojo gbini falaa o wuntira yi Wa Central, ka o tiŋ yɛligu. Ka a nyɛ ka a yɛligu su daa gbɛ diri, ka o tiŋŋi bee tuma kɔbiga kɔbiga amba, amba o bɛri o tiŋ yɛligu. N nyɛla Amankwah, naŋ bɛ fɔŋ fɔni, bii ka Kojo o yɛli waa ka o su bori ka o di ka o yi hospital. Amba n nyɛ ka o ti kɛ sɔŋni ka ban nii o tiŋ niŋsim. N daa, waa daa pam la kana di yi ti tuma niŋdi?
narrative_fante: Me nuabarima Kojo tuu fam saa anɔpa yi ha Wa Central mu na ɔkae sɛ ɔnan no yɛ no yaw. Ɛnyɛ kɛse papa — ɔnantew tiaa tiaa nanso ɔteetee na ɔkae sɛ eye no yaw paa. Me din de Amankwah, me na mefrɛ ɛfiri sɛ ɔno ankasa ɔmpɛ sɛ ɔkɔ hospital, nanso me suahu sɛ ɛsɛ sɛ wɔhwɛ nan no yiye. Me pa wo kyew, obi betumi akyere yɛn sɛ yɛnyɛ dɛn?
narrative_gurene: M pio Kojo bɔɔrɩ fam a zaaŋ soba Wa Central, ka o nɩ o nɩŋ yɛligu. Ka ka yɛligu wʋlʋ ŋwaanɩ — o nɩ kɔŋ la, ka ka yɛligu pala ka o guri ka a yɛligu o. N yɛtɔ Amankwah, mɛ na frɛ bɩ o ka o daa yɛl ka o ta kɔ hospital, ka m nyɛ sɩd ka ba na en o tɩtɩ. N tɩɩma, daa la sɔŋ tɩ bee bɩ tɩ na maalɩ da?

narrative_en: "Please, something happened on the Hohoe-Jasikan road small time ago. A young boy called Selorm, he was on a pragyia and it overturned on the road there. I am Sulemana, I was passing by when it happened. The boy is conscious and he is talking, but he is complaining of pain in his chest and stomach area, like around his body middle. It doesn't look too serious but we think somebody should come and check him."
narrative_ewe:
Meɖekuku, nudzadzra ɖeka wɔ Hohoe-Jasikan ʋɔ dzi enumake. Ɖevi ŋutsu ɖeka, woɖe ŋkɔ le Selorm, ewo pragyia dzi, eye pragyia la wɔ tɔtrɔ le ʋɔ dzi ɖe eŋu. Nye ŋkɔ nye Sulemana — medo ʋɔ dzi le gbe sia gbe sia nu wɔm abe ná.

Ɖevi la eɖo edzi, egblɔ gɔme le etso — gake eyi nam le ɖevi ŋɔ kple abɔ ƒe xɔxɔe, abe ame ŋutifafa ƒe ƒuƒoƒo ene. Mele nɛ le dzidzɔ dzi o, gake mésɔ mianɔ ame aɖeke ni va di ɖe eŋu akpɔ nu.
narrative_ga: Ofainɛ, accident bi etɔɔ Hohoe-Jasikan road so kakra kakra agooo. Oblɛ bi, wɔfrɛ ni Selorm, ɔwɔ pragyia so, pragyia ɛtɔ kpee so ŋ hɛ. Mii yɛ Sulemana — mɛhɛ kpee ŋ ni etɔɔ ɔhɛ. Oblɛ no oni le, ɔkasa kasa, shi sane — kɛ ni hii ni gbɔɔ kɛ shi area, like wɔ body middle ŋ hɛ. Enyɛ tɛtɛ dɔŋ, kɛ yɛ suibi ni doctor bi ni obi bɛba check ni ɔhɛ.
narrative_dagbani: N daa, accident kɔhiri Hohoe-Jasikan nyɛŋ zuɣu ka yɛ ka gbɛ. Biɛla do, bi yiri o ni Selorm, o naa o nya pragyia so, ka pragyia no gbini o so nyɛŋ zuɣu. Mi yɛligu Sulemana, n naa n gbɛ ka mali ka a kɔhiri. Biɛla no o nyɛ o maa yɛla, o guri kuli a yɛligu, ama o bɛri ka yɛligu n bee o digindi ni o pam chɛɣu, kaɣa o yɛla doo zuɣu. Ka yɛla pa gban daa so, ama ti ŋɔ ni dɔɣite do ni bi kɔ ni o gbɛn.
narrative_fante: "Please, something happened on the Hohoe-Jasikan road small time ago. A young boy called Selorm, he was on a pragyia and it overturned on the road there. I am Sulemana, I was passing by when it happened. The boy is conscious and he is talking, but he is complaining of pain in his chest and stomach area, like around his body middle. It doesn't look too serious but we think somebody should come and check him."
narrative_gurene: N tɩɩma, bʋʋrɛ bi tɩŋa Hohoe-Jasikan nyɛŋ zuɣu yɛlim yɛla. Bia wʋlʋm bi pɛlɩ Selorm, o nɩ motor — pragyia — zuɣu, ka pragyia no kpɛŋ fam nyɛŋ zɔŋ. Mam yɩɩra Sulemana, m nɩ nyɛŋ zuɣu m gaa a lɛbɩ yɛla no tɩŋa. Bia no ko a nyɛ wʋ — o yɛla, o zeɣu wʋ — ama o pɔɣɩm ka a yɛligu o pʋʋrɛ ŋa, zɔŋ o tɩŋ azeɣa sɔɔ. A ko yɛlɩ wʋlɩ baa, ama ti miɩ nɩ ned pɛɛlɩ waa a nyɛ o.

narrative_en: Please, I want to report something that happened here in Dormaa Ahenkro today. My friend Nhyira got into a dispute with a man near the market over payment for her goods, and he slapped her on the side of the head and pushed her. She didn't fall badly but her neck is sore and she's saying her head is pounding. Muntari is my name, I was there when it happened. We want to report it properly because it was unprovoked and she is not feeling well at all.
narrative_ewe: Meɖekuku, meɖo gbɔ be woaŋlɔ nudzodzro si wɔa afisia Dormaa Ahenkro egbea. Ƒometɔ nyem Nhyira ko gbɔ ŋuti xɔ dɔwɔnu ŋutɔ kple ŋutsu ɖeka dzi le luvo ŋkɔ li, eye woƒo asi le adefufu ŋu — nuŋɔŋlɔ la ɖo agbɛ dzi gɔme. Ewu ƒu kakaka o, gake ɖone la yi ŋunye na eɖe, eye edzo be tae yi ŋunye na eɖe kpɔɖenɔ. Muntari nye ŋkɔm, nam le eme ɣi sia ɣi. Meɖo be wòaŋlɔ nyawo nyuie nyuie abe wòaƒoa, abe wòaƒo akpa o na ame aɖeke, eye Nhyira le ɖagbe didi o egbea.
narrative_ga: Ofainɛ, metsɔ bɔ report shi yɛ kɛ ŋ le Dormaa Ahenkro lɛ. Chale, mi tsɔɔ Nhyira ko dispute kɛ wɔ gbɔmɔ ɛfɛ market lɛ kɔ — shia wɔ ko argue kɛ payment shi wɔ goods lɛ fees, na wɔ gbɔmɔ lɛ hyɛɛ no slap wɔ ŋmɛi shishi na opush no. Ɔbumo bɔɔ o, amɛ wɔ neck lɛ hii ŋ, na ɔshɛɛ no wɔ ŋmɛi lɛ pound ŋ pɛpɛ. Mi din na Muntari — mɛ le shia wɔɔ shi yɛ waa. Yɛtsɔ bɔ report properly because wɔ gbɔmɔ lɛ yɛɛ no fɛɛ — no provoke o — na Nhyira enyemi fio o.
narrative_dagbani: N daa, n bɛ yɛ ni n yɛli ŋun yɛligu din kɔri ní kɔ Dormaa Ahenkro yɛ. N dobitobu Nhyira ni nindoli ayi daa kɔri kasɛbu kasɛbu ka suɣuri lɔɣu la kɔri, ka o bɔri o zuɣu Nhyira ni biigu bugum din o di daa, ka naawuni bugum din gbani o. O ti kpɛŋ paɣɩŋ kana, amaa o nyɔrisi bee o, ka o yɛlda ka o ŋun yɛligu daa. Miri yɛligu din n yuli, din n yuuni Muntari — n bɛ kɔri zaa yin daa. Ti bɛ yɛ ni ti kpe ti report ka yɛltɔɣa daa ka o ti kuli ka kɔri o zug din yɛligu ka o bɛri tɔɣu.
narrative_fante: Me pa wo kyew, mepɛ sɛ meka bɔne bi a ɛsii ha wɔ Dormaa Ahenkro ɛnnɛ. Me adamfo Nhyira hyiaa ntɔkwa bi wɔ ɔbarima bi ho bɛn market no ho — ɛfa sika a ɔkaa ho ne n'adewa ho. Ɔbarima no buae no na ɔpeem no. Ɔntuu fam dennen, nanso ne kɔn yɛ no yaw, na ɔreka sɛ ne tiri reyɛ no pɛɛpɛɛpɛ. Me din de Muntari — na mewɔ hɔ sɛ ɛbaa saa. Yɛpɛ sɛ yɛbɔ report no yiye, efisɛ ɔbarima no na ɔfii ase — Nhyira anka ɔnyɛ no hwee. Na ne ho nso nhia no koraa saa ɛnnɛ.
narrative_gurene: N tɩɩma, m nongo yiri a bee Dormaa Ahenkro nin daa la. M doa Nhyira bɔ la ni kaɣa la palim zɔŋ ka o bee niri yenno din be tɔlim laaɣa zaa, o yɛligu koom din bee a nuo, ka niri la yiri o nɩŋ a ŋun zɔŋ ka o tʋʋs o. O ko bɔɔrɩ bɩ ka yɛligu, ama o gbooŋa ka yɛligu, ka o yeta a ŋun bee o yɛligu wʋlwʋl. Muntari la m yɛlɩm, mam bee be nina a bɛ bee o la tuma la. Tɩ nongo be nongo a saɣiŋ, bala niri la bɔ tuma a kʋɔrɩ wʋlwʋl ka Nhyira ko wʋlwʋl la yɛ.

narrative_en: Please, something happened here in Dansoman not too long ago. My neighbor Araba was crossing the road near the market when a tipper truck hit her and she fell badly. Her leg is swollen and she can't put any weight on it at all, but she is awake and talking. I am Vida, I am with her now and we need someone to help us because we are not sure if the bone is broken or what.
narrative_ewe: Meɖekuku, nudzadzra ɖe afɔ sia ɖe Dansoman si megbo o. Ɖoɖo manɔ aɖe. Agbɔgblɔ ŋutsu nyɔnu Araba nɔ ɖe ʋɔ gbɔ na xɔ agble si le ʋɔ si kple ze si gɔme le fifia, eye tipper truck ɖeka ƒo eɖe eye ewu ƒu vuvɔ̃. Eƒe afɔ blibo le edzia, eye mate ŋu dze afɔ edzi o. Gake eɖo edzi eye edze nu le gblɔm. Meyɔ Vida, meɖe eŋu fifia, eye miɖi kpe ɖe ŋumitɔ aɖe — mekpɔ ble be ɣleti vɔ o, kaka ame ɖe na miaɖe nyuie.
narrative_ga: Ofainɛ, bɔɔ ebaa Dansoman ha, enyɔ shi ye. Mi jimanɔ Araba, otsɛ kpee kɛ market fɛɛ bii, tipper truck bi hɛ eni, ebumo baafu. Eni gboo ya mɔɔ, ni hii eni kɛ enyɛ bi tee gboo ani shɛɛ o — kɛ enyɛ bi tɛɛ eni gboo ani o. Kɛ eni oni le, oshɛɛ okwɛ. Miiyɛ Vida, mibɔ eni ha shiwo, yɛ lɛ boa — yɛ nyɛ nyɛ sabe sɔ bone bi wɔ fracture kɛ enyɛ o. Ofainɛ, frɛ ambulance maa yɛ ha.
narrative_dagbani: N daa, saa biɛla chɔŋ ka a bee ka a ti ka yi kpɛŋ ka yi dee bee ka yi naa Dansoman ni. N yulana Araba o bee o di nyɛŋ kpamli maha bo yua ni ka tipper truck yuli o ka o gbini yɛligu. O tiŋ gbɔɣi ka o pa ŋani tiŋ tɔŋ o ni, ama o nyɛ o yɛla ka o di yɛla. Maa Mali yɛli, n nyɛŋ din daa Vida, n bɛ o yulana ni saa, ka tɔɣim bɔri sɔŋm ka yi pa ŋani gbɛya wuni ka yi naa bɔri yɛla o tiŋ chɔŋ kpɛŋ daa bɔ, bia ti ku ŋani yɛla.
narrative_fante: Me pa wo kyew, biribi si ha wɔ Dansoman yi seesei ara. Me yɔnko Araba retu ɔkwan kɔ abofu no ho na tipper truck bi bɔɔ no ma ɔtuu fam paa. Ne nan ho ahyɛ mu na ɔntumi nnante so koraa, nanso ɔda hɔ reкasa na n'ani da hɔ. Me din de Vida, mɛ ne ho ha seesei, na yehia boa enti sɛ bone broken anaa dɛn no, yɛnnim.
narrative_gurene: N tɩɩma, zɔa bee sɩ kɛ ka Dansoman la, ka daa ta faaroŋ. M yirpɔk Araba o daa nɔ nyɛŋ saɣim Dansoman lɔɣɔ ka tipper truck gbɩɩ o, ka o bɔɔrɩ zaabu. O nɔɔrɛ yɛligu ka o pa tol o nɔɔrɛ zug, la o ko beem ka o gom. Mam yɛ fo, N yɛ Vida, ka m be o saɣim la ka ti daa yi bia ka o sɔŋ ti, bala ti nan nyɛ ŋmɛɛ sɩ o faabu bika daa gol bɩ, bi o zaa bee.

narrative_en: Please, something very serious has happened here in Sunyani and I need help urgently. My neighbor's son, Appiah, a young boy, fell into the water and he was under for a long time before we could pull him out. When we got him out he was not moving well and his chest and belly area looked swollen and strange, like something is wrong inside. Appiah is breathing but it is very weak and he is not responding properly when we call his name. Please send help quickly, his condition is very bad — I am Maame and I am here with him now waiting.
narrative_ewe: Meɖekuku, nudzadzra gãtɔ aɖe wɔe afi sia Sunyani, eye meɖi kpɔ ŋunye enumake. Ɖevi ŋutɔ si le ŋutikɔ ŋunye ɖe, Appiah, ɖevi ƒe atike, edo ƒu atsi me, eye enyo ame dsi le atsi me akpa didi hafi míakpɔ ɖe asi le eŋu. Mgble amexe le eŋu egbɔ o, eye ele nuɖuɖu nyuie me. Gake Appiah eƒu agbe — gake ƒuƒu sia tsɔtsɔ aɖe, eye ŋutikɔ eŋlɔ ŋutikɔ eŋlɔ o nyuie. Appiah eƒu agbe la nyuie, eƒu agbe tsɔtsɔ kaba, eye egblɔ o nyuie egbe mɔ la dzi míefrɛ eŋkɔ. Meɖekuku, zã kpɔ ŋunye habɔbɔ — ɖevi ŋkeke le vɔvɔ̃ gã. Nye ŋkɔ nye Maame, eye mele afi sia, meli eŋu le egbe ko, mele ɖo le gbe.
narrative_ga: Ofainɛ, bɔɔ kɛ ni shi kɛ la Sunyani ha, mi hii bo mi bɔɔ. Minute dɛbi — ŋshɔŋ dzɛŋ — wɔn yɛ Appiah, obumo shi nshi, yɛ oshi le tsɔ hii afɔ. Mɛhe mii shɔ naa oshi kɛ tɛŋ la, mii nii ye efi nshi. Woyɛ efi nshi shi, eni mɔ gbɛ pɛpɛɛpɛ — enyemi le yɛ, enimɔ gbɛ kɛɛ. Appiah ehɔmɔ le, la hɔmɔ ko niɛŋ, enimɔ tɛŋɛŋ — wonyɛ eyi Appiah, enimɔ fɛɛ. Shi ni gbɔɔ ni — shi ni gbɔɔ ni lɛ nmi le kɛɛ, lɛ shɔŋ lɛ, chale, ehii mi. Ofainɛ frɛ ambulance mli mli — oblɛ yi lɛ ni shi tɛŋ. Mi yɛ Maame, mi le ha fɛ Appiah ha, mɛmi ye wɔn bo. Mli mli, bo mi!
narrative_dagbani: N daa, ka di kuli yɛligu tim ka o di paɣa daa Sunyani, n bori sɔŋ daa ka tɔɣim. N nirim bia, o din yuli o Appiah, biɛla, o gbini kom pum, ka o daa be pum bɛ wɔɣo a ka ti rɔ o bɔɣi. Ka ti bɔɣi o wɔɣo, o mɔɣli ka ka nɛɛ, ka o digindi ni o pam daa yɛligu ni ba bɔlim yuli yuli, ka nyɛ ka ka di nɔɣim pum o. Appiah yiɣim nyɛm, ama o nyɛm kuli sɔɣiŋ ka daa, ka ka o din n yuli o yuli ka o nyɛ ni ka nɛɛ yɛligu. N daa, yi frɛ sɔŋ tɔɣim, o ti nɔɣim yɛligu tim — maa yuli m Maame ka m be ka o ni daa, ka n gɔli be n daa.
narrative_fante: Me pa wo kyew, biribi tɛɛ yaw paa ha Sunyani na mehia mmoa ntɛm ara. Me hɔho ba, Appiah, abofra ketewa, ɔtuu nsuo mu na ɔtena ase akye ansa na yɛatwi no aba. Sɛ yɛde no aba a, ɔrenante yiye na ne bruwa ne ne yafunu hɔ yɛɛ hunu sɛ ɛyɛ hu — sɛ biribi yɛ no den fam mu. Appiah rehome nanso ne home yɛ mmerɛw paa, na sɛ yɛfrɛ ne din a, ɔnhunu hwee yiye. Me pa wo kyew, sɛnd help ntɛm ara — ne state yɛ bɔne paa. Mene Maame na mewɔ ha ne no bɛkɔɔ ara dɛ yɛtwɛn mmoa.
narrative_gurene: N tɩɩma, yɛla dɔɣɩm bɔɔrɩ ka wa Sunyani kɔŋ ka m tɔɣɩ sɔŋ wʋlɔwʋlɔ. M yama bia, Appiah, bi yĩrɩ, o bɔɔrɩ nzuɣu mim pʋɔ ka o nɩ pɔɔrɩ be a ka tɩ pʋɔɔrɩ o bɔɔrɩ. Ka tɩ bɔɔrɩ o bɔɔrɩ sɩŋa o n kɩŋ n tɩgɩm dɔm ka o pʋʋrɛ nɛ o pɔɔrɛ tɩɩma o nyɔɣɩ sʋŋ ka yɛla n nyɛ ka sɩ bɩ lee o pʋɔ. Appiah mirim bee o, la wʋlɔ la wʋlɔ, ka tɩ bɔɔ o yɩɩrɩ o n yeli tɩ dɔm. N tɩɩma, sɔŋ mam wʋlɔwʋlɔ, frɛ ambulance — o yɛla bɛ dɔm. Mɩ yɩɩrɩ Maame, ka m nɩ be o kɔŋ ka m sɩɩ fo.

narrative_en: Please, something very bad has happened to a small girl here in Bawku. My neighbor's daughter, Adjoa, she fell down from a big mango tree in front of the house, and the way she landed, ehhh, it was not good at all. Her arm is twisted in a very wrong way and she is crying so much and cannot move it. I am Emefa, I live just next door, and the mother is not around so I am the one calling. Please we need help fast because the child is in a lot of pain.
narrative_ewe: Meɖekuku, nudzadzra gã aɖe wɔ ɖe ɖevi nyɔnu aɖe ŋu fifi la afi ŋutɔ Bawku. Agbɔŋutsu si le ŋunye gã la ɖevi nyɔnu, Adjoa, edo ƒu afi gã mango zu si le ʋɔ ŋgɔ la dzi, eye nɛ ado ƒu gɔme la, ehhh, esia mewɔ nyuie o le ŋutɔ. Eƒe asi la twi ŋu le ʋɔ vovovo me, eye eyi nam gã kpem eye mate ŋu zɔ asi la o. Nye ŋutɔ nye Emefa, mele ŋkɔe ɖe eƒe ʋu dzi, eye eƒe nɔvi nyɔnu la mele afisia o, nenye mi fia nye le kɔnuwo wɔm. Meɖekuku, miaɖi kpɔ ŋunye fĩfĩ, ɖevi la yi ŋu le eyi ŋue gã kpem.
narrative_ga: Ofainɛ, bɔɔ shi niɔ amɛ kɛkɛ le Bawku. Emi neighbor dɛbi, Adjoa, obumo fɛɛ mango tree kɛ bo ofanɔ ni eshiɛ ha, ehhh, oshi nyɛ kɛkɛ o. Ɔni gbɔ twisi kɛɛ wrong way kɛkɛ, na oni hii shi kɛkɛ, na ɔtiti move εi o. Mii nye Emefa, mitsɛ next door, na ɔni maŋ le ha o, emi ŋ ye ni frɛ. Ofainɛ, yɛhia help fast chale, dɛbi ni hii shi kɛkɛ.
narrative_dagbani: N daa, ka yɛligu pam bee din bi polinli ka bia din bɛ Bawku. M pɛlim biɛla, o nyɛŋ din bee Adjoa, o gbini o nyɛŋ mango tiŋa din bɛ yili nyɛŋ, ka o kpɛŋ din... ehhh, o kpɛŋ ka da miri. O nuu yiɣi ka sɔɣim ka da miri, ka o yɛli daa, ka o ti tumi o nuu. N yuli bee Emefa, m bɛ o pɛlim yili kobiliga, ka o ma da bɛ kɔ, n'a ka n frɛ dimi. N daa, yi maa sɔŋ yi tali ka bia ka yɛligu pam bee o daa.
narrative_fante: Me pa wo kyew, biribi bɔne paa aka abofra ketewa bi ha Bawku. Me hɔho ba, Adjoa, ɔtuu fam fi mango pon kesee bi so a ɛda dan no anim, na ɛkaa no no, ehhh, ɛnyɛ yiye koraa. Nʼnsa no kɔɔ ɔkwan bɔne bi so na ɔsu reto na ɔntumi nni mu. Me ne Emefa, mete hɔho fam ara, na nʼna nni ha, enti me na mefrɛ. Me pa wo kyew, yehia mmoa ntɛm paa efisɛ abofra no yaw redi no yiye ara.
narrative_gurene: N tɩɩma, sombo wʋlɔ ka n yɛligu bee ka n maalɩ yɛ a bee a Bawku. M cɔɔnɔ bia biye, Adjoa, o kpɛŋ fam bi mango yiri a kɔŋ deo tee, la ani o kpɛŋ deo pʋɔ — yinɛ! A ka ŋwanɩ paɣa. O nɔɔ boa wa ɣɔɔ soɣɔ la ka o bɛ tol a yɛligu, la o wʋɩ wʋɩ la o bɛ tɩ tʋr a nɔɔ. Mɛ yɛ Emefa, m bɛɛ kɔŋ a zie, la o ma bɛ bee fɔ, a le mɛ ka m tuma a wɩ a yɛ. N tɩɩma, sɔŋ mam kãa! Bia a yɛligu wʋlɔ, la ti tɩɩ sɔŋ sɛba!

narrative_en: My name is Ramatu, I'm calling about something that happened on the Tamale-Yendi road, close to Savelugu. A young man called Asare was helping offload sacks of grain from a truck by the roadside when one of the sacks came down sideways and hit him hard across his chest. He's standing and talking but he's saying his ribs are sore and breathing is a bit uncomfortable. I stopped when I saw them by the road and they asked me to report it.
narrative_ewe: Allo, yèɖem nye ŋkɔ nye Ramatu. Mefrɛ mi afi nɔŋu nu siwo wòwɔ ɖo ʋɔ si le Tamale kple Yendi tso — afi si gbɔ Savelugu. Ɣevɔɣi ɖeka, ŋutsu ƒe ɖevi, ŋkɔe nye Asare. Enya akɔ ŋutsu siwo tso agble ŋkume ɖa ɖe ʋɔ dzi, eyi nɔ kpe ɖe wɔ nu — eƒo akuʋa ƒe xexlẽ kple amesi bubu ɖe akɔ dzi. Nɔŋu nu bubu de la, xexlẽ ɖeka vɔ, ewu edzi, edu ŋgɔ, eko Asare ta ɖe dzi ŋutɔ, edu ɖe agɔ, eyɔ asi kple agɔ. Asare le egɔme tete le fe, enya eɖo edzi eye ele ɖuɖɔ kple ame. Gake eɖe abe n'elo egɔme ƒe egblo ame akɔ dzi ŋutɔ eye eɖe abe n'e mate ŋu ƒu agbe vevie o — esi ele ƒu agbe la, eyi nam. Mede wo ŋgɔ ɖe le ʋɔ dzi yɔ, wo kpɔ ŋunye gbɔ eye wogblɔ m be madzro kpe ɖe me ŋuwo. Meɖekuku, frɛ ambulance wo zɔzɔ, yɔ wo dze akɔ ɖe.
narrative_ga: Hɛloo, me din bi Ramatu. Mifra shikpon kɛ bɔɔ bi nɛ eko Tamale-Yendi kpee ji, kɔŋ Savelugu fee fee. Obaabi dɛ Asare — obi nyɛ oblɛ bi — ɛɛ, onyɛ wɔ ji kaa gbɔɔ ji shwee grain sacks tsoo truck ji lɛ kɛ kpee tɛɛ, na sack kɛɛ ebumo ejeŋ oye — eeye, ewui gbɔɔ ji fee fee kɛ force. Chale, gbɔɔ lɛ bi nɛ hihi ni. Kɛɛ oni le, onyɛ wɔ shikpɔŋ oo, kɛɛ obi yɛ ni ribs ji hiihii ni oo, kɛ hɔmɔ lɛ ayɔ ni bi oyaŋ wɔ yɛ. Mɛbo wɔ kpee tɛɛ na manya ni wɔ niŋ, na wɔ bii mi mafrɛ frɛ kɛ mɛshiishii. Ofainɛ, frɛ ambulance yɛ wɔ fee fee.
narrative_dagbani: Allo, m yɛligu din Ramatu. N bɛ yu yɛli zaɣa bee din kɔli Tamale-Yendi nyɛŋ, Savelugu doo. Daan wuntɔ yiŋ bʼo yɛligu din Asare — o bɛ sɔŋ kaɣa ŋun mali kɔnkɔɣa bee din bɛ nyɛŋ daa. O bɛ ti yɛli tuma do shɛli yɛligu, doli kɔnkɔɣa yini gbɛri bɔri o digindi kaba daa. O ti zoli ni o bɛ yɛla, amaa o bɛ yɛli ŋun din o saɣim bila bɛ o dɔɣim ka o digindi yɛligu daa, ni o yɛŋ tooi kaba. N gbɛli n yɛl zaɣa ka n tii o chɛɣi nyɛŋ daa, ka din bɛ kɔli nima sɔŋ mi n ti yɛli zaɣa bee din. N daa, frɛ ambulance ni yi sɔŋ o, n bori la.
narrative_fante: Me din de Ramatu, merefrɛ wo ho fa biribi a esiie wɔ Tamale-Yendi ɔkwan so, kɔse Savelugu. Aberante bi a wɔfrɛ no Asare no, ɔreboa ma wɔnhyia gunia aduane fii truck bi so wɔ ɔkwan nkyɛn no, na gunia baako baa ne ho firi nfa mu ma ɛhyɛɛ no yiye wɔ ne bruwa so. Ɔgyina hɔ na ɔrekasa, nanso ɔkae sɛ ne sɛn mu yɛ no yaw na home nso yɛ no den kakra. Megyinaa hɔ bere a mahunu wɔn wɔ ɔkwan nkyɛn no, na wɔsrɛɛ me sɛ memmra abɛbɔ ho asɛm no.
narrative_gurene: My name is Ramatu, I'm calling about something that happened on the Tamale-Yendi road, close to Savelugu. A young man called Asare was helping offload sacks of grain from a truck by the roadside when one of the sacks came down sideways and hit him hard across his chest. He's standing and talking but he's saying his ribs are sore and breathing is a bit uncomfortable. I stopped when I saw them by the road and they asked me to report it.

narrative_en: Please, I want to report an accident that happened here in Cape Coast. My neighbor's daughter, Afia, she was knocked by an OA taxi on the road, just near the market side. She is a young girl, maybe around seventeen or so. The knock was not too serious — she has some pain around her neck and a small cut on her head, but she is awake and talking. I am Alhassan, I am the one calling because her mother is with her now and they are trying to get a taxi to the clinic.
narrative_ewe: Meɖekuku, megblɔ accident ŋu na mi si wɔ ɖe afisia ɣe Cape Coast. Ɖevi nyɔnu si le ƒe aƒe kom, Afia, OA taxi tso akɔ nu ɖo wo dzi le ʋɔ dzi, ɖe market fifia tso gbo. Nyɔnu vivi ɖe eya — ahe ŋu le lagbe sia lagbe wo bena eƒe ƒuƒoƒo ɖe ene ɛ̃ ga ɖe esia ɛ̃ ɛne. Akɔ gbã la manyɔ nuto o — eyi ŋunye ɖe eƒe ɖo gɔme, eye wɔ̃ ta dzi ƒe nɛ kpikpi, gake eɖo edzi eye ele ðuwɔwɔ. Nyɔŋutsu Alhassan nye ŋutsu si le frɛ la — esi eƒe nɔ le egɔme fifia eye woɖi akɔ xɔ de wo clinic.
narrative_ga: Ofainɛ, accident bi ehii ŋ yɛ Cape Coast ha. Mi hɛ mli dzɛ mli oblɛ, Afia — kaa OA taxi bi ebua ŋ kpee ni, kpakpa market kɛ. Oblɛ bi ye dɛbi kɛ, ehɛ seventeen oshiŋ oshiŋ. Knock bi nyɛ strong paa o — ŋmɛi ni kɔŋ kɔŋ ehii ŋ kɛ, shwe kakra etsotso ŋmɛi ni bɔɔ, naŋ Afia oni le kɛ oye kasa. Miŋ yɛ Alhassan — mifɛ frɛ shikpon ei ŋ ei ŋmɛ yɛ Afia maŋ kɛ wɔn shikpɔŋ ha bi, naŋ wɔŋ hei kaa bi wɔ tɔ clinic kɛ.
narrative_dagbani: N daa, n bɛ yɛ n gɔliŋ accident din kɛ ka kɔ Cape Coast ŋɔ. N jirli biɛla, Afia, OA taxi di o kpɛŋ nyɛŋ zuɣu, sɔɣim baga bi zuɣu ŋɔ lala. O bɛ nya biɛla, o tooi ka o tɔɣibu yini ku ata ata la. Knock ɔ ku gbiniri sana — ka yɛligu bee o gundɔɣu zuɣu, ka o ŋun gbaŋ biŋ kpɛŋ, ama o nyɛ o maa yɛla la ka o mɔɣim. Maa Alhassan, ma na frɛ ka o ma bɛ ni o yɔɣim, ka bɛ bɛ yɛ taxi sɔŋ bɛ kɔ clinic la.
narrative_fante: Me pa wo kyew, mepɛ sɛ meka accident bi a ɛsii afie ha Cape Coast. Me hɔho ba, Afia, OA taxi bi bɔɔ no wɔ ɔkwan so, bebiara a ɛbɛn market no ho. Ɔyɛ abaa ketewa, ebia ɔdi mfe aduasa nson ansa. Knock no anka yen so koraa — ɛyɛ no yaw a ɛda ne kɔn ho, na ne tiri so wɔ ntasie ketewa bi, nanso ɔnyina hɔ na ɔrekan. Me ne Alhassan, mene me na mefrɛ, efisɛ ne maame na ɔwɔ ne ho seesei na wɔreyɛ hwee sɛ wɔbɛnya taxi akɔ clinic.
narrative_gurene: Please, I want to report an accident that happened here in Cape Coast. My neighbor's daughter, Afia, she was knocked by an OA taxi on the road, just near the market side. She is a young girl, maybe around seventeen or so. The knock was not too serious — she has some pain around her neck and a small cut on her head, but she is awake and talking. I am Alhassan, I am the one calling because her mother is with her now and they are trying to get a taxi to the clinic.

narrative_en: My brother Asante was knocked down by a car here in Dormaa Ahenkro, just near the main road, and it is very serious. He is a young guy, maybe around nineteen or twenty, and his leg is very badly hurt — it looks broken and there is a lot of bleeding. I am Nii, I am standing with him right now and people have gathered but nobody knows what to do. Please we need help to come fast because he cannot move at all and he is in a lot of pain.
narrative_ewe: Allo! Meɖekuku, kpe ɖe ŋunye! Nɔvi nyem Asante, akɔ ɖe eŋu fiafia afisia Dormaa Ahenkro, ɖe ɖaŋu ʋɔ ʋawo si. Nu si wɔ la, ekpɔe didi — eveve hã. Eya nye ŋutsu ƒe dzɔdzɔ ɖeka, abe eƒe ƒe ewo kple ɖeka abe eve énye eƒe ƒe. Eƒe afɔ la yɔ didi — ekpɔ abe ɣleti vɔ la, eye dzi le etsotso vevie hã esia dzi. Nyem la nye Nii — mele eŋu ŋu fifia, eye amewo xɔ agbagbee ɖe afisia, gake ame aɖeke nya nu si wòanɔ va wɔ o. Meɖekuku, kpe djro ɖe ŋutsɔ mia xoxo, kakaka! Emate ŋu ɖu agɔ aɖeke o, eye eyi nam vevie. Frɛ ambulance va mia afisia le gbãgbã!
narrative_ga:
Hɛloo, boa mi! Boa mi chale, ni serious paa!

Nua mi Asante, kaa bumo ŋ shi kpee ha Dormaa Ahenkro, close to main road no. Ehii ni serious paa chale. Ɔnɔ young guy, maybe nineteen or twenty, na gboo ŋ ehii ŋ kɛkɛ — enɔ fracture paa, na shwe etsotso ŋ pajojo.

Mii Nii, mitsɔ ŋ ji joo, na niibi dumo ha, bɔ eŋmɔ nyɛ ni boa ŋ. Ofainɛ, boa mi swift paa! Ɔnɔ tɔŋ gboo ŋ shɔ o — ni ehii ŋ kɛkɛ chale. Frɛ ambulance shi ji joo! Boa mi!
narrative_dagbani: Allo! N daa, sɔŋ mi! M doo Asante, kaɣa gbɛ o ka o gbini ka o kpɛŋ ka o nyɛŋ nni, Dormaa Ahenkro, nyɛŋ kpɛŋ nni. Ka yɛligu ti daa. O yaa biɛla, o yɛli pia ni awɛi anaa pia ni ayi baŋŋa. O tiŋ kpɛrɛ ti daa — ka o tiŋ kpɛni, ka o ziim ɣeya paɣa. N yaa Nii, m naa o niŋ ka nisimba ti kɔŋ ka pɔɣi kaa daa, amma anibi kaɣa n baŋ bɛ be ti timbu. N daa, sɔŋ ti pam, yi wuhi ka yi tɔŋ ka yi di — bala o viɛ viɛ — bala o ti soli ka o ti tumi nyu baŋ, ka yɛligu daa o. N daa!
narrative_fante: My brother Asante was knocked down by a car here in Dormaa Ahenkro, just near the main road, and it is very serious. He is a young guy, maybe around nineteen or twenty, and his leg is very badly hurt — it looks broken and there is a lot of bleeding. I am Nii, I am standing with him right now and people have gathered but nobody knows what to do. Please we need help to come fast because he cannot move at all and he is in a lot of pain.
narrative_gurene: My brother Asante was knocked down by a car here in Dormaa Ahenkro, just near the main road, and it is very serious. He is a young guy, maybe around nineteen or twenty, and his leg is very badly hurt — it looks broken and there is a lot of bleeding. I am Nii, I am standing with him right now and people have gathered but nobody knows what to do. Please we need help to come fast because he cannot move at all and he is in a lot of pain.

narrative_en: My son Ofori, he got burned this afternoon at our family compound here in Tamale. He was near the cooking fire and some hot water splashed on his chest and belly area — not too serious, just the skin is red and a little sore. I am Kofi, I am his father. The boy is moving around fine and not crying too much anymore, but I wanted to call and ask if we need to bring him somewhere or if we can treat it at home.
narrative_ewe: Allo! Meɖekuku, kpe ɖe ŋunye. Nyeŋutsu Kofi — meƒe ɖevi Ofori, eɖo dɔ ɖevi eve ŋgɔ afisiafi egbe fiɖefia. Miele afɔme le ŋutifafa Tamale. Ɖevi la le azɔ si woa ŋku dɔ dɔwɔnu ŋgɔ, eyata tsitsa dzɔdzɔ vɔɔ bu ko le eƒe ɖevi ŋɔ kple eƒe abɔ ŋgɔ — yeyeye ye dzetɔ. Asi wu ɖee gã o — aɖu ŋutɔ ko vɔvɔ dzi, eye egbea dɔ ka. Ɖevi la tsɔ ne yi le wo ko, eɖo wu o megbe le va yi o, eye egblɔ gã o le megbe. Gake nye ŋutɔ, medi bu se be mele ŋu yi wo Ofori asiwo na o, ke mate ŋu kpɔ ko le ŋutifafa nye? Akpe na wo, meɖekuku dze nami ŋu.
narrative_ga: Hɛloo, ofainɛ. Me din ne Kofi — mɛ bi Ofori, obuɔmo ni afternoon afei, yɛni compound shi kɛ Tamale. Ɔhii kɛ cooking fire — hot water etɔtɔ ŋ, obuɔmo ŋ gbɔɔ kɛ shi area. Enye serious paaa — skin ŋ eji pɛpɛ red, kɛ ni hii kakra. Dɛbi no oyemi le, ɔtsɔɔmɔ fine, kɛ ɔyi too too ŋ tso. Mewɔ lɛ mɛkɛ frɛ kɛ naŋ — yɛni bring ŋ hospital, abi yɛtumi treat ŋ yɛni fie? Boa mi, chale.
narrative_dagbani: Allo, N daa. M biɛla Ofori, o ku din gbɔŋ o bugum yɛsili din zaŋ Tamale, ti yili kɔŋ. O bɛ na kuka bugim saɣim, ka zaɣim biɛŋ gbɔni o digindi ni o pam saɣim — ka zaɣim gbaa biɛri daa, ka o zuɣu nyɛla o kɔbi bɔhim ti bɔhim biɛŋ n ka yɛligu biɛŋ. Mali yɛla, N nyɛ Kofi, m nyɛ o ba. Ka biɛla zuu ni zaŋ a yɛsili o nyɔɣu daa, ka o ti bɔ zaŋ ka ka yɛligu saɣim. Amo n frɛ yɛ sɔŋ maa — ti bori sɔŋ ti nyɛ ti di ti bia kpe nyɛla yili, awoɣo ti le daa ti ti nyɛ ni a ma o yili saɣim kɔŋ?
narrative_fante: Maaha. Me din de Kofi, me na Ofori maame. Ofori yɛ me ba. Ɔhyee owigyinae ha Tamale, yɛn efie mu. Ɔbɛn egya na nsu hyew bi guee no so — ɛkaa ne bruwa ne ne yafunu — nanso ɛnyɛ den paa. Ne ho nhyew koraa; ne honam mu din nko ara na ɛyɛ koraa nso ɛkyɛw kakra. Abofra no retwa ase yiye na ɔnsu bio saa. Nanso mepaa wo kyew, mebo wo frɛ sɛ menhunu sɛ yɛde no kɔ hospital anaa yɛbɛtumi abo no aduro ha fie ara?
narrative_gurene: M bia Ofori, o sɛba a yɛligu wɛ ana zaaŋ kɔ ka a te yir chɛɛ Tamale. O beeni zom ŋɔɔbu ziinli tɛɛ ka ko-tuuri fʋɔɔ o pogorigo ne o pʋʋrɛ zie — ka gbeŋ-gbeŋ, bii a nɩfaa bee gʋsɩ-gʋsɩ la, ne a yɛligu bibiisi. M yɛ Kofi, m yɛ o ba. Bia nɔ ko kpaa ne a bʋɔrɩ-bʋɔrɩ yiri ka o bɛ yoli peeni la ka bee. La m nɛɛ sɔŋ ka m frɛ mam yɩɩrɩ ka m bɩɩsɩ — bɩ o yɛ tʋma ka ti kaa o hospital, bii ka ti tɩɩ yɛ o tɩʋ-nɩŋ yɛla yɩɩrɩ yir?

narrative_en: Please, I'm calling to report something that happened here in Nalerigu. My name is Mensah, and I'm a neighbor to the family. Their daughter Elinam, she's a young girl, she fell down and hurt her arm or leg — I'm not sure exactly which one, but she was holding it and crying a little. It doesn't look too serious, no big wound or anything like that, but the parents wanted someone to know just in case. She seems okay now, just shaken up.
narrative_ewe: Meɖekuku, mefrɛ ɖe mi le Nalerigu. Nyem nye Mensah, eye menyɔ nutɔawo dɔwɔlawo. Ɖevia sia, Elinam, nyɔnu ɖevi — edo ƒu eye eyi nam — asi alo afɔ, me nyá nko ko o, gake na asi dzi eye egble kakra. Emegble vɔ kakra o — duƒuƒu le eme o, kuku alo nusiame bubuawo me le eme o — gake to ƒo awo yɔna ame be womadze ŋu ɖe wu. Fifi sia la, ele nyui, gake etsi kakra.
narrative_ga: Ofainɛ, mifra lɛ shikpon kɛ report bɔɔ lɛ ni esii fɛɛ ha Nalerigu. Mitsɔŋ Mensah, mibɔ neighbor ni ofoi mɔkɔ. Wɔn dɛbi ba lɛ, Elinam, obumo naagbee — oblɛ lɛ — oshɛ egbɔ kɛ gboo lɛ ehii nɔ — mishi nyɛ kɛkɛ which one, kɛ oshɛ etam ni ocry kakra. Enɔ naa serious denn, wound big bi mli o, kɛ bɔɔ shishi ba lɛ pɛɛ wɔn baa report just in case. Oni le kɛɛ naa, kɛ oshɛ etɛŋɛ kakra.
narrative_dagbani: N daa, m frɛ ka m nyɛ n yɛl' yɛliya din kuli maa Nalerigu. M yuli di Mensah, ka m gbɔŋ ni kpɛma bi ni o dabi. O biɛla Elinam — o bi biɛla daa — o gbini ka o nuu biɛla kana o tiŋ yɛligu, m ti gbɛ la sɔŋ n nyɛ ka din yɛligu, ama o tɔɣi ka o ŋm pam viɛla. Ka ziim kuli biɛli, ka vuli kali biɛli — ka yɛla kuli o daa laa bɛ vɛla timaha. Ama o bɔri ka nindiya nyɛ n sɔŋ, zaɣi yɛla be yɛ puɣibu. O laa laarim ka yɛla ŋani, ama o pam kali kpayiɣu.
narrative_fante: Me pa wo kyew, merefrɛ sɛ merebu nsɛm bi a ɛsii ha wɔ Nalerigu. Me din de Mensah, na meyɛ won hɔhoɔ wɔ efie no ho. Wɔn ba baa no, Elinam, ɔyɛ abofra ketewa — ɔtuu fam na ɔkaa ne nsa anaa ne nan — mintumi nhu nokore nkyerɛ no ara, na na ɔrekyekyere mu na ɔresu kakra. Ɛnyɛ hu papa — nsono kɛsɛ anaa biribiara te saa nni ho — na agya no ne na no pɛɛ sɛ obi nim, eye me yaw ase sɛ hwee bi bɛba. Seesei, ɔte ase yiye, na ɔnam so, ɛnso ɔbɔ n'adwene kakra.
narrative_gurene: N tɩɩma, m frɛ la a nyɛ dɔɣɩm bee Nalerigu. M yulɩ la Mensah, a m la yiri bee dɔɣɩm nɛ. O biɛ bɩɩsɩ, Elinam, o bɔɔrɩ fam, o pɔgɛ bɩɩrɛ bee o nɩŋ anɛ bɩŋ — m ko nyɛ sɔŋ a n yɛ gbaŋ, la o sɔŋ o nɛ a o sʋɣɩm. Ka zɔma la bɩ yɛligu wʋntɔ wʋntɔ — ziim ko bee o, bɔŋ la bɩ kɔ — la o ba-nɛ daa nyɛ bee wʋlʋm la ŋman o nyɛ. A daa la o kɩta, o ya la zoŋ, la o sɔŋtɩma bi dɔ wʋntɔ.

narrative_en: My grandfather, Nii Laryea, got burned this morning here in Zebilla. He was trying to warm himself near the fire and his cloth caught the flame, and it burned him on his chest and belly area. Felicia is my name, I'm the one calling. He's an old man so we are very worried, but the burning is not too deep — the skin is red and blistering in some places. Please we need to know what to do for him before we can get him to a place for treatment.
narrative_ewe: Allo, meɖekuku kpe ɖe ŋunye. Nyeŋutɔ, Nii Laryea, wɔ dzɔ ŋgɔ egbe afisia Zebilla. Ezo zo fii le dzɔ dzi be eaɖo edzi, eye eƒe nɔvi aɖe ŋgɔ vɔ dzɔ, eye dzɔ yia no ɖevi ŋɔ kple abɔ dzi. Nye ŋkɔ nye Felicia, eye nye na frɛ ɛ. Srɔ̃ wònyé, hã eya tso abe miaɖi aƒɔ ŋkɔŋlɔ nyuie aɖe ene. Gake nu si dzɔ ɖo eŋu hafi egblo dzoƒe o — adi le etsotso dzi, eye xevi aɖe le ɖevi ŋɔ kple abɔ dzi, eye aɖe le yi bubi bubi. Meɖekuku kpɔ mi mia, dze mia ŋu gblɔ mia be miaƒle aɖanu aleke mi atsɔ ame sɔ nu na no ɖeva si mia ate ŋu de no akɔ asi míado no asiwo.
narrative_ga: Hɛloo, ofainɛ boa mi — grandfather mi, Nii Laryea, fire kɛ no ŋ saa ŋ tɔɔ. Ŋ bie ɛ, ɔhii ŋ kɛ fire hiɛ ŋ Zebilla. Ɔyɛ ŋ ji ŋ dzɛɛ fire kɛtɛ nɔ, na ŋ ntɛɛ kɛ flame, ehi ŋ gbɔɔ kɛ shi ji. Mi yɛ Felicia, mii frɛ ŋ. Ɔ yɛ gbɔmɔ, ɛnɛɛ ni mii tsui ŋ naanaa — ɛŋkɛ kɛ fire hiɛ ŋ ɛnaanaa, skin etsɔɔ ŋ, kɛ blisters le hiɛ ŋ. Ofainɛ — ni mii bi, hana mɛɛ yɛ nɔ bo ŋ baahi, ebi mɛɛ kɛ ŋ kɛ hospital?
narrative_dagbani: Allo, N daa. M baba kpɛma, Nii Laryea, ka bugum di o gbɛya, ka o kɔbiɣu — Zebilla. O daa bɛ yɛ ni ka o si o yuli bugum daɣili, ka bugum nyu o daɣiŋ, ka o daɣiŋ gba o digindi ni o pam. Maa bi nyɛ Felicia, maa ni frɛ di wula. O nyɛ nindaan, o be so ka tɔbu bɛ n zuɣu — ka ti bɛ mi n pam. Ama bugum bi kpɛ n nyunli — o yuli bee biɛga ni nyɛ lɔɣi shɛŋa yɛlim bɔɣu nyɛya. N daa, ti bori nyɛ ti kara o di kara a, sɔŋ yi ti nyɛ ti ka ti bɔɣu o, ka ti kara a, ka ka ti zaa kara o ka ti kpɛ nyɛla yili.
narrative_fante: Me papa panyin, Nii Laryea, oguu ogya saa anɔpa yi ha wɔ Zebilla. Ɔreyɛ ho dɛm wɔ ogya ho na ne ntama hyew, na ogya no hyew no wɔ ne bruwa ne ne yafunu ho. Felicia na wɔfrɛ me, na mene na mrefrɛ yi. Ɔyɛ opanyin, enti yɛsuro pii, nanso hyew no nni mu denden — ne ho na ɛyɛ hɔnam no akɔkɔ na ɛreyɛ mmɔ mmɔ wɔ baabiara baabiara. Me pa wo kyew, yehia sɛ yɛnhunu nea yɛbɛyɛ ma no ansa na yɛde no akɔ hospital.
narrative_gurene: M baaba paɣa, Nii Laryea, da zɔ ŋ wʋlʋm yɛl o yɛnda Zebilla. O da ŋ kpɛŋ koom bɩna zɔ la, la o pɔɣɩ da kpa mɔɣa, la o da zɔ o tɩɩŋa la o pʋʋrɛ zie. Felicia la m yuuri, mam la ŋ frɛ. O yɛ nindaan, la ti yɛligu a bee o nɩŋa, la ka zɔ bʋɔrɛ tɛɛ — o pɔɣɩ yɛligu la nyɛsa zie, la wa bɔɔbɔɔ ŋa kpɛna. N tɩɩma, ti yɛ bɩla ŋ nyɛ bʋɔ ti bee o mɛɛ ti nan kɛ o kɛ yɛla yir.

narrative_en: Please, I need to report something that happened here in Cape Coast. My nephew Boateng — he's around nineteen — was trying to cross one of the drainage channels by the roadside that was flooded from the rains, and he slipped and fell in. Some people nearby pulled him out quickly, thank God. He's conscious and talking, but his arm got hurt when they were dragging him out — it's swollen and paining him. Alhassan here, I'm with him now, can someone come and check him properly?
narrative_ewe: Meɖekuku, meɖi na woŋu bena nuɖoɖo aɖe wɔ ɖe afisia ɖe Cape Coast. Ɖevi la ŋunye, Boateng — eƒe ƒe le du ewo kple ɛ̃ gbɔ — eɖi ʋɔ ƒo zu ʋuƒoƒo si le ʋɔgble dzi, ɣlɔ̃ wui abe dɔwɔwɔ aɖe ene, gake elabɔ elabe wui ɖo ɖe zɔzɔ gbɔ ƒu ame si le ʋusifu dzi. Amewo le ŋgɔ wɔ gbe deka de enu, wude de enu kpakpa, akpe na Mawu. Eɖo edzi kple egblɔ nu anyi, gake eƒe asi xɔ dzɔdzɔ eye eyi nam ŋutɔ wɔwɔ tso ʋɔzɔzɔ gbɔ — egorgor nyuie loo, eye eyi nam. Ŋutsu ŋunye Alhassan wɔ ŋu sia, me le eŋɔ fifia, ŋutinya le ame aɖe ate ŋu ayi dzidzi akpɔ eɖeke nyuie?
narrative_ga: Ofainɛ, mitsɔ mli report bɔ yɛ Cape Coast. Ŋ nephew Boateng — nineteen years oko wɔ — otsɔ obua kɛ cross drainage channel bi fɛ kpee fɛ ŋ, shikpon ni flood fɛ rain fɛ tɔɔ, ne oshuu ne ebumo bo ŋ shi. Nnɛ wɔ bi fɛ ŋ gborɔ ni kɛ twe no mli akpɛɛ, Nyɔŋmɔ akɛ enyɛ. Oni le, ofo kɛ yɛnii, naa ŋ gbɔ ni hurt wɔ bii ni wɔ tsɔ twe no mli — ni blɔɔm ebe ne ehii no. Me nye Alhassan, mele kɛ ŋ sɔŋ, ŋmɛnɛ obi bi frɛ obua ne check no kakra agbaa?
narrative_dagbani: N daa, n bɛ yɛ ni sɔŋ maa kɔŋ mana bi nyɛya din be Cape Coast. M pam biɛla Boateng — o nyɛ be sheni pia awɛi lan be — o pali nyɛŋ doo ziŋ mali, kuli din bu sheli zaa nyɛŋ din kɔ wɔɣiya ka zaɣa dali, ka o gbini ka o kpɛŋ be ziŋ ni. Ka niba nsi dani bin o yigibu pam pam, Naawuni puŋŋa. O nyɛ o nyɛ wunyɛɣili yela — o maa gbɛya ni o maa yɛla — amma o nuu yɛligu daa o wubu ni — o nuu bu o be a kʼo nuu zɛribu ka o yɛligu. N nyɛ Alhassan — m be o sibu. Dimi suɣulo, ŋun doo bi waɣi ban bi nyɛ o yela ŋmiŋ?
narrative_fante: Me pa wo kyew, mepɛ sɛ meka biribi a ɛsii ha Cape Coast. Me nuabarima ba Boateng — ɔwɔ afeɔ dunsia ne afeɔ baako nkyɛn — ɔpɛɛ sɛ ɔtwa ɔkwan so nsuo a ɛhyɛ ma ɔkwankyɛn no mu, na ɔsee ho maa ɔtuu mu. Nnipa bi a wɔwɔ ho no toɔ no firii mu ntɛm, Nyame da ase. Ɔte ase na ɔkasa, nanso ne nsa hurt no bɔɔ wɔ bere a wɔde no firi mu no — eswɛ na eye no yaw. Me ne Alhassan, mɛ ne no ha seesei, obi bɛtumi aba na wahwɛ no yiye?
narrative_gurene: N tɩɩma, m sɔŋ mam kɛ m gɛŋ yɛ yɛla bi tuma Cape Coast. M deem bia Boateng — o bee yenno nɔ ayobu aba nɔ piig — o bɔɔrɩ a nyɛ, o bɔɔrɩ kpɛŋ fam kʋŋ biig yɩɩgɩ bi nyɛŋ nɛ, biig bi taa pɔɔrɩ zaaŋ za ziim yɔɔŋ. Nɩba tɩtim bi tuma o la, bi boe o yɛligu, a zuɣu Yinɛ. O ko a nyɛ al' ka yaa — o yaa la o gɛŋ yɛ — o puhirɩ, yaa yɛligu sɔŋ. Bɔ o nʋŋ ŋa bɔɔrɩ a yɛligu la, a piirim, ka yɛligu sɔŋ o nʋŋ nɛ a zibeya. N yɛ Alhassan, m bee o la ama. N tɩɩma, ŋɔ naam la mam, so taa wa a nyɛ o yɛla pam?

narrative_en: Please, I want to report something that happened on the Cape Coast-Elmina road. A woman called Memunatu was riding a bicycle on the side of the road when a vehicle coming from behind clipped her and she fell off badly. She landed on her arm — it's swollen now and she cannot lift it at all. Baffour is my name, I was coming from the other direction and I saw the whole thing. She needs help to come quickly because the pain is quite serious.
