narrative_ewe: Meɖekuku, meyi kɔ nuxɔxɔ siwo wu ƒo ʋɔ si le Cape Coast kple Elmina dzɔ. Nyɔnu ɖeka, woɖe eŋu ɖe Memunatu, eɖo bicycle le ʋɔ ta ŋɔ la, akɔ ɖeka si yi ɣeyiɣi le eŋɔ yi la ƒo eŋu ta eƒe afɔ ɖeka si, ewu ƒu tɔtrɔ. Edu ɖe eƒe asi dzi — asi la su fifia, eye mate ŋu tsɔe ɣa ɣa dzi o. Baffour wòɖe nyranye, meva ʋɔ aɖe si kple ʋɔ la na, mekpɔ nuxɔxɔ la katã. Meɖi kpɔ ŋunye na ame la—woawɔ alesi woava ziŋ, elabena eyi ŋu nam nyuie.
narrative_ga: Ofainɛ, mewɔ bii report kɛ oshɔ Cape Coast-Elmina road ŋ. Ojugbaŋŋ bi, wɔfrɛ wo Memunatu, ɔsɔŋ bicycle kɛ kpee fɛɛ ŋ, nɛ kaa bi wɔ ji ebuɛ etsɔɔ eha ehii wo gbɔ ŋmɛ, nɛ obumo kɛ enyɛ nyiɛ. Obumo gbɔ ŋ — gbɔ he mɔ aweni ko, nɛ etsi gbɔ he fɛɛ o. Mii nyɛ Baffour, mibuɛ etsɔɔ mɛi kpee ŋ, nɛ mi hɛɛ gbɔɔ oshi nyɔŋ. Ofainɛ, boa mi frɛ ambulance shi faa enyɛ nyiɛ — ehii wo kɛ enɔ nyɛ serious pɛɛ.
narrative_dagbani: N daa, n bɛ yɛ ni kɔɔ n yɛlɣi wula din kpɛŋ Cape Coast-Elmina nyɛŋ zuɣu. Pog yʼa din be nyɛ Memunatu, o bɛ la cycling kaɣa biɛla nyɛŋ doli zuɣu, kaɣa wʼa tooi o doli puŋ waɣibɔɣu zaɣa o, ka o gbini yʼa dɛɛdɛɛ. O kpɛŋ o nuu zuɣu, ka o nuu ŋɔ bee ŋɔ yɛligu ka ŋɔ buri tiŋɣa la. O tiri o nuu ŋɔ, wula daa tuma puŋ. N yuli nyɛ Baffour, n naa be la shɛli doli puŋ ka n nyɛ wula ŋɔ bɛɛ. Ka yɛligu daa o yɛligu, ka sɔŋ ŋɔ bɛ la n daa tuma la. Yi zaa frɛ ka yi wa ka yi sɔŋ o.
narrative_fante: Me pa wo kyew, mepɛ sɛ meka biribi a ɛba sii Cape Coast-Elmina ɔkwan so. Obaa bi a wɔfrɛ no Memunatu, ɔresiw bicycle ɔkwan fa so, na kar bi a ɛfi n'akyi baa too no, na ɔtuu fam den. Ɔhwe ɔkye no nsa so — nsa no aho huu wɔ hɔ ara anaa, na ɔntumi nyɛ no na ɔsɔ no sɛn koraa. Me din de Baffour — mefiri ɔkwan foforɔ fa reba, na mhunuu gye nyinaa mara. Ɔhia mmoa a ɛmba ntɛm, efisɛ yaw no yɛ den paa.
narrative_gurene: Please, I want to report something that happened on the Cape Coast-Elmina road. A woman called Memunatu was riding a bicycle on the side of the road when a vehicle coming from behind clipped her and she fell off badly. She landed on her arm — it's swollen now and she cannot lift it at all. Baffour is my name, I was coming from the other direction and I saw the whole thing. She needs help to come quickly because the pain is quite serious.

narrative_en: Please, something happened at a house here in Ashiaman and I want to report it. My neighbor Abubakar was attacked by some men, and they beat him badly on his arm — it's swollen and he can't move it properly. I'm Mensah, I live close by and I saw part of what happened. He is in pain but he is still conscious and talking, so it's not like he fainted or anything. We need someone to come and help him or tell us what to do.
narrative_ewe: Meɖekuku, nudzadzra ɖe le Ashiaman fifia le afe ɖeka me, eye medi na mi ɖe. Agbɔtɔ ame Abubakar, amewo wu agbe nɛ, wo tɔ̃ egbe dzi nam, asi ɣe le woƒu wu — asi ɣe vɔ, eye mate ŋu tsɔ asi ɣe wɔ nku o. Nyem Mensah, mele agbɔ be la ŋu, eye mekpɔ nusiwo wɔa la ŋutɔ. Eyi nam egbe dzi, gake eɖo edzi na xexe, ele xɔxɔ kple nu gblɔgblɔ — edo edzi o alo nuɖe leɖe la nyem o. Medi kpɔ ame be wade ava kpe ɖe ŋune, alo mi na mi nuti be mado ŋu wɔ ke afi.
narrative_ga: Ofainɛ, bɔɔ biɔ akɛ Ashiaman hɛɛ yɛ kɛ mɛ fɛ report ŋmɛi. Mi jɛkɛ Abubakar — mɔ nɔŋmɔ — nɔkɔ nibii ekɛ enyɛ ŋ lɛ, mli ebuɔ ni shi enyɛ gbɔ wɔ — gbɔ lɛɔ ebuu kɛ onyɛɛ tumi bɔ shi gbɔŋ. Mɛhɛ Mensah, mɛ te shia yɛɛ kɛkɛ, kɛ mɛhɛ akɛ bɔɔ nɛ bi ekɛɛ shiaŋ. Ehii ni shi gbɔ wɔ lɛ, shikɛ oni le kɛ ekasa bɔɔ — enyɛ akɛ obumo gbɛ oo. Chale, yɛ hiɛ mɔ ni bɛ yɛ hɛɛ boa ni, ɛnɛ yɛ bɛ nu akɛ yɛ nɛ ni ye.
narrative_dagbani: N daa, nsibu yɛli kɔri Ashiaman ka n bɛ yɛ ni n gɔli yi. M sheliŋ Abubakar, kpɛma nibɛ nyɛŋa yɛli o zuɣu, ka bi dɔya o nuu ka o nuu gbɔŋa — o nuu koli ka o nan tumi o nuu noli daabiɛ. Mia Mensah, n daɣi bini ka n nini ban nyɛŋɛ pɛlim. O yɛligu daa ka o guri o mɔɣu noli, o ti gbɛni wula, o guri di n yɛli. N bori la dobiɛ taɣi bini ni ka o sɔŋ, biela ni yi goli amaa yi nɔŋ ti bɔri o ni ti mala dini.
narrative_fante: Me pa wo kyew, biribi asi fie bi ha Ashiaman na mepɛ sɛ meka ho asɛm. Me hɔho Abubakar — nnipa bi too no so hyeɛ, na wɔbubuiɛ no yiye no nsa so — nsa no aho hyew na ɔntumi nnyi ho mfaase yiye. Me din de Mensah, mete beaebi bebree ho, na mihunuiɛ biribi a esii no. Ɛyɛ no yaw, nanso ɔnte ase korapɔ — ɔkasa na ɔhunu ne ho — ɛnyɛ sɛ okolapuse anaa biribi te saa. Yehia obiara a obɛba aboa no, anaasɛ wɔmfa yɛn ade bɛkyerɛ yɛn sɛ yɛnyɛ dɛn.
narrative_gurene: Please, something happened at a house here in Ashiaman and I want to report it. My neighbor Abubakar was attacked by some men, and they beat him badly on his arm — it's swollen and he can't move it properly. I'm Mensah, I live close by and I saw part of what happened. He is in pain but he is still conscious and talking, so it's not like he fainted or anything. We need someone to come and help him or tell us what to do.

narrative_en: Please, something happened here in Goaso and I want to report it. My name is Ofori, I'm calling about a woman called Naa Dedei — she was knocked by an aboboyaa on the road and she fell down badly. She is holding her neck and complaining that her head is paining her, but she is conscious and talking small. It's not too serious but she needs help because she cannot move her neck well.
narrative_ewe: Meɖekuku, nuɖoɖo aɖe wɔ ɖe afisia Goaso, eye medi na mi be woŋlɔ. Ŋkɔnyé nye Ofori. Medze ɖo ame aɖe ŋu, nyɔnu si wɔ ŋkɔ be Naa Dedei — aboboyaa aɖe du eŋu le ʋɔ dzi eye edo ƒu dukɔ. Ele eɖo ŋɔ nu eye edze ɖo be etaɖi yi eyi nam, gake eɖo edzi eye ele gbɛ̃gbɛ̃ kpɛ kpɛ. Medoa nyo kpe hã, gake eɖi kpɔ ŋunye, esi eɖe ŋu mete ŋu ƒle eɖo ŋɔ nyo o.
narrative_ga: Ofainɛ, something happen wɔ Goaso ha, mi nyɛ report. Mi din nyɛ Ofori — mi frɛ frɛ ni shia ni oblɛ Naa Dedei. Aboboyaa buɔ mo wɔ kpee, ebumo gboi. Oni le, eŋmɔ kɛ eheemɔ ŋmɛi ni hii mo, kɛ etsɔɔ small small. Enyɛ serious kɛkɛ o, kɛ chale, omo kpaa boa — enyɛ able ni faa ŋ kɔlɔ well o.
narrative_dagbani: N daa, bɛ kɔri saha din bee Goaso, ka n bɛ yɛ n yɛl' yin. N tɔɣa yɛligu nyɛ Ofori. N bɛ zɔŋ dii bee pɔɣu din bee Naa Dedei — aboboyaa daa kum o nyɛŋ zuɣu, ka o gbini dii. O bɛ tiŋ o kpɛrgu, ka o bɛ yɛ o ŋun ka yɛligu bee o — amma o ti soli naɣa, o bɛ yɛligu din. Ka bɔɣu daa taa nyɛ k'a ka o bori sɔŋ, bala ka o ŋa ti tul' o kpɛrgu viɛla.
narrative_fante: Me pa wo kyew, biribi asi ha wɔ Goaso na mepɛ se merekyerɛ no. Me din de Ofori, na merɛfrɛ fa ɔbaa bi a wɔfrɛ no Naa Dedei — aboboyaa bɔɔ no wɔ ɔkwan so na ɔtuu fam paa. Ɔrekyere ne kɔn na ɔreka se ne tiri yɛ no yaw, nanso ɔte hɔ rekyerɛkyerɛ kakra. Ɛnyɛ den paa nanso ɔhia mmoa efisɛ ɔntumi ntu ne kɔn yiye.
narrative_gurene: N tɩɩma, bɛ yɛla yɩɩ ka a bee kɔ Goaso la, a be gerima o. M yɛɔr la Ofori — m frɛ kɔ pɔɣa a la Naa Dedei yɛla zuɣu. Aboboyaa bɔ o Naa Dedei nyɛŋ poore, ka o kpɛŋ fam yaga yaga. O beega a nimbiɛɣa kɔ ka o geri ka a ŋun yɛligu, ama o ko nyɛ la — o kpɩɩma pɩɩna pɩɩna. Ka a yɛla bee yaga yaga biisi, ama o sɔŋ keŋ, bɩ yɛla la o bee a nimbiɛɣa, o da tɩŋa a yɛligu zɔŋ la.

narrative_en: My son Amoah was helping me with some work around the house here in Yendi, and he got a small cut on his arm from one of the tools we were using. It's not too deep, just a little wound, but I am Edem and I wanted to make sure I report it properly. He is okay, just a bit scared, but the bleeding has slowed down already.
narrative_ewe: Allo! Nyeŋu nye Edem. Yɔm nye le Yendi. Ɖevi nye Amoah le kpɔ ɖe ŋunye ƒe vɔvɔ̃ wɔwɔ le ŋutɔ ŋutɔ le afe, eye akɔbɔa ƒe dɔwɔnu ɖeka vɔ asi ŋu susu — asi vɔwɔ yi ɖe eŋu. Asi gbagbã o, abe nudzã ŋutsu aɖe ko ene, eeyi nam be ne edzo nyi fe kakra. Amoah le teƒe ko, gake egu ŋgɔ susu. Dzi tso etsotso na le le abe xona la, eye dzi eda do kakra enumake. Meɖi ƒo nuto nawo be nànya, abe metoa asi nane nyuie o.
narrative_ga: Chale, dɛbi bi Amoah mi bo mi work around the house wɔ Yendi, na gbɔ bi ehii — tool wɔ yɛ use ooo, eshwe ni obumo small cut. Enye deep paaa, wound kakra pɛɛ, boa shwe etsotso wɔ ni slow down already. Eoni le, enye wahala paaa, scared kakra pɛɛ. Mi din Edem — mi hɛ report am properly ni. Onyɛ ko bad paaa, boa Amoah oshi le.
narrative_dagbani: N bi Amoah o bi maa sɔŋ maa tuma ka yili kɔɣibu, ka kpɛŋ yɛŋ Yendi. Ka tuma yɛla dabɔɣu yini bee o nuu, ka gbii o nuu la biɛla yini. Ka ka daɣibu ka ziŋ daa la — ka yɛligu biɛla, ka nyɛ suɣulo. Ka m yuli la Edem, ka n daa fɛŋ ka n yɛl' yɛla ni. O bɛ la zuɣu, ka daa zɔŋ biɛla, ama o ziim ka bee o gbɛ la mali.
narrative_fante: Me ba Amoah na ɔre boa me dwuma bi ha fie ha Yendi, na nkwadaa ntea bi mu, ɔnya ntea ketewa bi wɔ ne nsa so fi nneɛma a yɛde yɛ adwuma no mu. Enye deep papa, wound ketewa pɛ na ɛho, nanso me ne Edem, na mepɛe sɛ mɛka ho asɛm kakra. Ɔho yɛ, nkwadaa bi hu suro ketewa pɛ, nanso mogya a ɛretu no asa redwo ase kakra seesei.
narrative_gurene: My son Amoah was helping me with some work around the house here in Yendi, and he got a small cut on his arm from one of the tools we were using. It's not too deep, just a little wound, but I am Edem and I wanted to make sure I report it properly. He is okay, just a bit scared, but the bleeding has slowed down already.

narrative_en: Please, I am calling because my nephew Muntari has been burned and I am very worried about him. We were near Kintampo, along the road going towards Kumasi, when it happened — he was helping with something and fire caught his body, the chest and stomach area. He is a young boy, maybe sixteen years old, and the skin there is looking very bad, red and blistering, but he is still awake and talking, thank God. I am Esi, his auntie, and I am with him now but we need to know what to do before we can reach a hospital.
narrative_ewe: Meɖekuku, mekɔ ɖe enu abe ɖevi aɖe le ŋunye, Muntari, wòxɔ tsi dzidzi, eye makpɔ egɔme le ŋunye nyuie o. Míele Kintampo kɔ, ɖe Ʋɔ si doa Kumasi ŋu ƒe ɖim, ko wò xɔe — ele nu dɔwɔwɔ me ko agbe ƒu esi, ɖevi ŋɔ kple abɔ ƒe ƒu ŋɔ. Nye ŋutɔ nyɔnu ɖevi, ale ko ewo ƒe eve, eye le ta ŋɔ ɔ, ɣesi wòɖo dzi dɔ le nyɔnui nyuie o — ele vɔvɔ, eye le ŋkume ɖe ŋkume le fiam, gake elé edzi eye ele ŋutsu, akpe na Mawu. Nye nye Esi, dzrɔfa ŋutɔ, eye mele eŋu fifia, gake mīɖi nyanya be mídé ŋu dɔ afi si, kodoo míate ŋu yi Asiwo.
narrative_ga: Ofainɛ, mibɔ call ni Muntari, mi nephew, fire ehii no — mi nim worry paa ni oshie. Yɛ bii Kintampo kɛ, kpee shi nɔŋ Kumasi fee, na ehia — obe help kɛ shishi nɔŋ, na fire ekɔ ono gbɔɔ kɛ shi shi. Oblɛ dɛbi, years shia sixteen, na skin ni gbɔɔ kɛ shi — enɔŋ fɛɛ paa, red kɛ blisters eba, chale enɔŋ fɛɛ paa. Mpo Nyɔŋmɔ shia, oni le, ona kasa. Mii Esi, oni auntie, mibii oshie nɔŋ, mpo yɛ hia sabi shishi to do before yɛ tumi faa hospital.
narrative_dagbani: Allo, N daa — m bɛ yɛ ni i nyɛŋ maa ni. M wuhiri ka m dau biɛla Muntari ziim kpɛŋ o, ka m nyɛla ka m zoli wuhibu paɣa o zuɣu. Ti bɛ Kintampo doo, nyɛŋ o bee Kumasi ŋan, ka ka kpɛm — o bɛ di gba kuli ka bugum kpɛŋ o yuɣa, o digindi ni o pam guɣu. O nyɛla biɛla, laɣim o pia ni ayɔbu yɔɣu, ka o yuli doo ka ŋun kpɛrɛ paɣa — ka o kukuri nyɛ nyiɛli ni ka bublim bee o. Amaa Allah puŋŋa, o nyɛ diɣim ni ka o bɛ yɛlimu. Miya nyɛla Esi, o amɔya, ka m bɛ o wɔri diɣa. Ti bori yɛla wula ti ni di tuma, ti zi taa hospital ka n taa.
narrative_fante: Me pa wo kyew, merɛfrɛ efisɛ me wɔfa ba Muntari wɔ mu fire a ɛhyew no, na me werɛ ahow paa ne ho. Na yɛwɔ Kintampo ho, wɔ ɔkwan a ɛkɔ Kumasi no ho, na ɛbaa saa — na ɔreboaboa biribi na fire frɛɛ ne honam, ne bruwa ne ne yafunu ho. Ɔyɛ abofra kumaa, ebia mfeɛ du nsia na wɔwɔ no, na ne honam wɔ hɔ yɛ hu paa — ɛyɛ kɔkɔɔ na bubble retutu sɔ — nanso ɔda ase na ɔreka asɛm, Onyankopɔn ase. Me na me yɛ Esi, ne auntie, na mewɔ ne ho seisei, nanso yɛhia sɛ yɛnnim deɛ yɛbɛyɛ ansa na yɛnkɔduru hospital.
narrative_gurene: N tɩɩma, m frɛ fo ber m deem bibia Muntari bɔɔrɩ zɔŋ a ŋmɛɛ, la m gelim o yɛligu pɛɛlɩ. Ti bɛ Kintampo saakʋ, nyɛŋ nɩ bɛ kʋɔrɔ Kumasi, la o bɛ sɔŋ zɔŋ wʋntɔ, la fiiri kpa o ninsaali zuɣu — o kɔbga la o pʋʋrɛ zɔŋ kpɛɛŋa. O yɛ bibia wʋntɔ, o yɛ be yenno nabiisi la ber, la o ninsaali deem zi yɛligu nɩ, o ŋmea la o bʋɣɩ yɛligu. Ama Yinɛ zuɣu, o ko nyɛ — o zuri la o yɛla. M yɛ Esi, o auntie, la m bɛ o saakʋ nɩ. Ama ti daɣɩ bɛ ŋwani n tɩɩma sɔŋ ti, la ti kɛ nyɛ hospital tɛ, ti la lɛ bɛ ŋwani ti tʋma.

narrative_en: My name is Dzifa, I'm calling about a young girl I know here in Cape Coast. Naa works at a tailoring shop and she was using the sewing machine when her hand slipped and the needle went through her finger. There is bleeding but it's small, and she's conscious, just shaking a little from the fright. We've put a cloth over it but we want to know if she needs to come somewhere for it to be properly cleaned and dressed because the wound was quite deep.
narrative_ewe: Allo, yèɖom Dzifa. Mefrɛ wo ɖe adzaŋu nyɔnu ɖevi aɖe si menya ɖe Cape Coast. Naa wɔa adababi ƒe dɔ, eye elea sewing machine dzi wɔwɔ dɔ la ɣawo, asi le efisi asi dida, ta needle ɖa ƒu eƒe nu aɖewo ŋu. Dzi le etsotso, gake aɖeaɖe koe, eye eɖo edzi — ame o, gake etututu dzetsetse le agonyagbɔ ŋu. Míakpɔ nu si tso ɖuɖu ɖe edzi, gake míeɖi nyé se ŋu be eɖo asiwo ɖe wɔwɔ la nyuitɔ la wu, esi ele tso dɔ la kpɔkpɔ kple ŋlɔŋlɔ ŋu, efesia nugbogbɔ la le tetɔ.
narrative_ga: Hɛloo, me din bi Dzifa. Mifre lɛ akɛ oblɛ bi yɛ ni Cape Coast. Naa yɛ tailor shop bi ni, eye wɔ sewing machine ni wɔ bɛ hewɔ, ne gbɔ blɛɛ shi sewing machine lɛ ni, needle kɛ gbɔ-nyɔŋ bi gbɛ etso. Shwe etsotso, enyɛ bebree o — enyɛ kakra, eye Naa oni le, enyɛ shishi loo, ŋma shishi dani kakra lɛ akɛ ofiɔ. Yɛde cloth bi atɔ ni so, nye yɛpɛ ni yɛhũ sɛ ɔhia oba hospital anaa clinic bi ni, na wɔ clean wɔ dress wound lɛ yɛyɛ, akɛ wound lɛ ko fɛɛ.
narrative_dagbani: Allo, m yuli di Dzifa. N bɛ yɛ ni ka Cape Coast ká bia puɣa din m si o yɛlim. O yuli di Naa, o bɛ tailoring shop kɔŋ, ka o bɛ sewing machine tobu ka o nuu yɛliɣi, ka needle kɔŋ o nuu kɔligu zɔŋ. Ziim bee o, ama o ziim ɣeya paɣa daa — ka o nyɛ yɛla, o ti soli daa, ama ka o yɛri yɛri bi daa bɛ dɔɣim din o soli. Ti gbaai tɛmpiri bini zɔm dali, ama ti bori ka ti nyɛm: a yɛla yɛ yɛliɣi zaɣim naa Naa yɛ kɔŋ nyɛla yili bɔ, bo ka o dɔɣim din pam yɛliŋ yɛ sihi naa zoli, barka ka kɔligu ŋun ni zaɣa?
narrative_fante: Me din de Dzifa. Merɛfrɛ fa saa abofra baa bi a menim no ho asɛm, wɔ Cape Coast ha. Naa yɛ adwumayɛfo wɔ tailoring shop bi mu, na ɔreyɛ adwuma wɔ sewing machine so no, na ne nsa sɔre ma needle no tuu ne nsateaa mu. Mogya retu kakra, na ɔte ne ho, ɔsɔre sɔre kakra ara na ɛyɛ suro na eyi enti. Yɛde ntoma atwe so dada, nanso yɛpɛ sɛ yɛnim sɛ ohia sɛ ɔmbɛ baabi sɛ wɔde yiye nhoro no na wɔato so hyira no, ɛfiri sɛ akɔ mu dɛn ara.
narrative_gurene: M yʋʋrɩ la Dzifa. M frɛ bee bia pɔɣa bi yaa fo fo la m nyɛ a, Cape Coast. Naa la o bɛ yela yir ka o sɔŋ daʋʋra yela yir — o bɛ a sɔnda bugiri la o nɩ kpɛŋ ŋmaŋa, ka o nɔŋ biɛ ka ŋmaŋa kpɩ o nɔɔ nue. Ziim bee o, la ka yɛligu bɩ yɛla, la o ŋwani, ka o yɛ o ko wʋlʋŋ daa nɩ ka o bʋɔra biɛ. Ti kʋɩ yɛla dɔɔ o nɔŋ zuɣu, la ti daa nyɛ a o sɔŋ bɛ hospital? A yɛla la dɔɔ, ka ti daa dɩɩ a sɔɔna la o nɔŋ yɛla kpe ka o ŋmaŋ sɩɩ.

narrative_en: Please, I want to report an accident that happened here in Suhum. My neighbor Asante, he is an old man, he was knocked by an aboboyaa on the road this afternoon. The aboboyaa hit him on his side, around his chest and stomach area, and he fell down hard. Ramatu is my name, I am the one calling — I was nearby when it happened and I helped him to the roadside. He is conscious and talking but he is in pain, not too serious but he needs to be seen by a doctor.
narrative_ewe: Meɖekuku, meɖi ɖe nudzadzra bubu kple míaƒe ŋu eye míle Suhum afisia. Ametata ŋunye Asante, srɔ̃ wòle, aboboyaa ƒe ŋu wu eɖe ƒi Ʋɔ dzi egbe ŋdi sia. Aboboyaa ɖe wu eƒe ɣeyiɣi si — le eƒe ɖevi ŋɔ kple abɔ ƒuƒlu — eye ewu ƒu dɔdɔ vevie. Ramatu wòle ŋkonye, nye matrɔ ɖe phone la dzi — nele gã si nu le ele gome eye makpe ɖe eŋu ɖe Ʋɔ dzidzi. Esia, eɖo edzi eye egblɔ gbɔ, gake eyi nam; asi me vevie hã ao, gake eɖi edzi dɔkɔta ƒe ŋu.
narrative_ga: Ofainɛ, mɛ shɛ accident report mli — accident bi ewɔ afɛ ha Suhum. Mɛ jiɔ Asante, gbɔmɔ bi, aboboyaa bi ekɔ ehi ŋmɛi kpee shɛ ni hewalɛ. Aboboyaa bi ebii ni gbɔɔ mli kɛ shi nɔɔ, ni ebumo kasɛkasɛ fɛɛ. Ramatu ŋ dzii — mɛ ŋmɛɛ frɛ frɛ — mɛ bɛ ni shia le ahe ni ebumo naa, mɛ kɛ bo ni jɛkɛ kpee nɔɔ. Enyɛ gbɔmɔ ni oni le kɛ ebaa yɛ yɛ, ŋ bɔ ehii ni, noko enyɛ kɛ atɔɔ wo ji, kɛ otsɛ doctor bi hu ni.
narrative_dagbani: N daa, n bɛ yɛ ni n tirimi accident din kuli ka ŋa Suhum. N zɔŋ Asante, o nya nindaan, aboboyaa din na o zaŋ o nyɛŋ ni wula ni. Aboboyaa din na o zaŋ o soli ŋa, o digindi ni o pam pali, ka o gbini daa yɛligu. Ramatu m yuli, maa n tuma fɔni bi — n bɛ ni o lala ka o kuli ka n sɔŋ o ka n kpari o nyɛŋ kpara. O nyɛ o maa la ka o gbiri yɛli, amaa ka yɛligu bee o — ka kpɛŋ-kpɛŋ daa, amaa o bori dɔɣite n na o.
narrative_fante: Me pa wo kyew, mepɛ sɛ meka accident bi a ɛsii ha Suhum. Me ntiboa Asante, ɔyɛ opanyin, aboboyaa bi bɔɔ no wɔ ɔkwan so akyirikyiri yi. Aboboyaa no bɔɔ no wɔ n'afa so, bɛn ne bruwa ne yafunu ho, na ɔtuu fam pintinn. Ramatu na ɔfrɛ me — mena na mifrɛ — na mewɔ hɔ ara bere a ɛsii, na mede no kɔɔ ɔkwan mpoano. Ɔnim ne ho, na ɔrekasa, nanso ɛyɛ no yaw. Ennyɛ den koraa, nanso ɔhia sɛ oduruyɛfo bi nhwɛ no.
narrative_gurene: N tɩɩma, m bɛ gʋŋ accident yɛligu a zaa ka o be Suhum. M zoa Asante, o yɛ nindaan, aboboyaa buɣɩ o Nyɛŋ zʋŋa yɛnto. Aboboyaa buɣɩ o a tɔɔrɩ, o kɔɔn doo ŋ pʋʋrɛ wʋnto, ka o kpɛŋ fam nɛɛ. Ramatu yɛ m yɛbri — mam la frɛ dɩɩ. M be o zoa bɩ yɛligu ka o bɔɔrɩ, ka m sɔŋ o nyɛŋ nɔɔrɩ. O be a nyɛ la ka o gom, a yɛligu bee o, n ga yɛ kpɛm pɔɔ, la o sɔɔg a naalʋŋ la.

narrative_en: Please, I want to report something that happened to an old man here in Yendi. My neighbor Asare, he is quite old, someone came and beat him at his home — they hit his arm very badly and it is swollen now. I am Owusu, I live close to him, and when I heard the noise I came out and saw what had happened. He is not in danger of dying but the arm is paining him a lot and he cannot lift it. We need someone to come and help him or tell us what to do.
narrative_ewe: Meɖekuku, meɖi se nane wòwɔ ɖe srɔ̃ aɖe ŋu afisia Yendi. Agbemetɔ nye ŋutsu Asare — srɔ̃ hã, ame aɖe wòyi eƒe ƒe dzi, woyi na enu, wowu eƒe asi ƒu vevie — asi la xɔ tsi esia de ɖe kpɔɖeŋu. Yèna nye Owusu, mele eƒe dɔwɔƒe kpɔ, eye ŋkeke si medzi nu kple egbo la, mɛɖo nya ɖekema, mava kpɔ nuŋlɔŋlɔ si wɔwɔ. Efe agbe meli egblẽ o, gake eyi nam vevie eƒe asi ŋu, eye mate ŋu trɔ asi la o. Meɖi ɖe wodze abe ame aɖe aɖe na aɖee na edzi abe, alo ne nusi miewɔ na ene. Meɖekuku, kpe ɖe ŋunye.
narrative_ga: Ofainɛ, chale, mɛ bɛ report bɔ ni ehii kroŋ kɛ gbɔmɔ kɛ Yendi. Emi naaborɔ Asare — oye gbɔmɔ pɛpɛɛpɛ — onipa bi bɛ dɛ obuɔ ni oshɔ le ɔman — wɔbuaa ne gbɔ yɛyɛ hu, na tsɛke ne gbɔ fɛfɛɛfɛ noko le swollen. Miiye Owusu, mɛ tso ne shishi, na mgbe mɛyɛɛ ewiemfo no, mɛfiri hɔ bɛhuu deɛ ebeɛ. Ɔnhwɛ sɛ ɔbɛwu, na ne gbɔ no ehii ne pɛpɛɛpɛ yɛyɛ — ɔntumi ɔnsane ne gbɔ o. Ofainɛ, yɛhia onipa bi bɛba boa no, anaa mɔ kɛ yɛ ni shia kpee lɛ dɔɔŋ.
narrative_dagbani: N daa, n bɛ yɛ ni n yɛl'a kuri din maa kpɛm Yendi. N dobiila Asare, o kpɛma daa, ŋun daa di o yili ka o zɔri o nuu daabiɛgu — o nuu maa yɛligu o daa ka o kpɛŋ maŋ. Mi yɛligu Owusu, n daa bi o dobu, ka n maa gbɛribu din daa kpɛm maa, n yigi ka n nya o. O ti mali kuli soli, amaa o nuu maa yɛligu o daa daabiɛgu, ka o ti palli ti diri o nuu. N bori la ŋun daa di ka yi sɔŋ o, zaa yi nyi din n maa malli ka tuma yi ti yɛl'o maa ka wula ka bii o.
narrative_fante: Me pa wo kyew, mepɛ sɛ meka asɛm bi a asii opanyin bi ha Yendi. Me ntiboa Asare, ɔyɛ opanyin paa, ɔbaabi bɛbɔ no wɔ ne fi — wɔbɔɔ ne nsa yiye, na ɛnyini dennen seesei. Me din de Owusu, mete ne nkyɛn, na bere a metee dede no, mepuee baa pɛ asɛm a asii no. Ɔnhwehwɛ owu, nanso ne nsa no yɛ no yaw paa, na ɔntumi nyi no soro. Yɛhia sɛ obi bɛba mmoa no, anaasɛ wɔka kyerɛ yɛn sɛ yɛnyɛ dɛn.
narrative_gurene: N tɩɩma, m bɛ yɛla biɛla a saa yɛligu Yendi. M yaba Asare — o nye nindaan — ŋun nyɛɛ o kɔɔ o yir bee o, o gɔli o nↄↄrɛ wʋntɩ tɩɩma, a yɩɩra o pɔɔrɩ sɔŋa. Mam yɛ fo, m nyɛɛ Owusu, m da o yaba. M nↄↄ bala a yɛligu a, m yi a m nyɛ a saa yɛligu. O ko a ka fa kpɛ, la o nↄↄrɛ yɛligu o wʋntɩ tɩɩma — o ka tɔɔ o nↄↄrɛ sori fam. Sɔŋ mam — frɛ ŋun na wa sɔŋ o, kaa yɛ ama mɩna a yɛla a nɩ ti bɩ maal.

narrative_en: My name is Dela, I'm calling to report an accident that happened here in Cape Coast. A young man called Kpodo was hit by a trotro on the road not too long ago — he looks like he's maybe in his late teens or early twenties. His leg is hurt badly, there's some swelling and he said it's paining him a lot, but he's awake and talking. We managed to get him to sit by the side of the road and someone is with him now. Please we need help to know what to do next or if he should go to the hospital.
narrative_ewe: Allo, yɔ nyem Dela. Mefrɛ mi be meɖe nudzadzra si wɔ ɖem afisia Cape Coast tso ŋu na mi. Ɖevi ŋutsu aɖe si wɔɖo be Kpodo, akɔ — trotro — ŋɔ eƒe afɔ le ʋɔ dzi gbã me — eyata wòle akpa si le ŋgɔ. Ele nɛ abe ɖevi si kpɔ ɛfo azãtɔ si le xomevi la nem — ebe akpe eve wòle kɔ, ale abe ewe ɖe, kaka abe wɔ̃ aɖe ene ɖe ewo habia. Eƒe afɔ la yiiyi didi — etso fo kpɔ̃, eye ɛ̃ be eyi nam didi. Gake eɖo edzi eye etso gblɔgblɔ le. Míe kpe eŋu wòdzo ɖo ʋɔ ŋkɔ la ta, eye ame aɖe le eŋu le fifia. Meɖekuku, kpe ɖe ŋumíe — míe di be míaŋlɔ nu sia ŋu de, kaba abe ŋutsu la nyé be wòyi asiwo o. Kpɔ ŋumíe!
narrative_ga: Hɛloo, mi nyɛ Dela. Mibɔ frɛ frɛ ji accident bi shi Cape Coast ni ehia. Okpanyo bi, wɔfrɛ no Kpodo, trotro bi bumo no kpee ji — enimɔ le, oni le, ebe kasa, nibii. Ehii no shi no gboo — gboo ni eswia, yɛ eno kɛ ehii no teee. Eshi no, enimɔ gbɛ o — oni le, ebe kasa shi. Yɛ kɛ bo no faa kpee agbaa ji, nɔŋ bi le shiaa ne no. Ofainɛ, boa mi — nɔŋ yɛ nyɛ tsɔŋ baŋ? Eblɛ no naŋ hospital, wala? Chale, yɛ hia help teee.
narrative_dagbani: Allo, m yɛligu Dela, m frɛ n kɔri accident din naɣi ka ti Cape Coast. Saɣim biɛla, o yɛligu Kpodo, trotro din daa o nyɛŋ zuɣu saa dini kuli — o nyɛ ka o bɛ doli wuhibiɛla yaa ayi pia biɛŋ laliŋ puuni. O tiŋ daa o yɛligu n'a ka o daa ka o tiŋ paɣim, ka o yɛla ka ka yɛligu o yɛligu sam pam — ŋun n'o maa soli ka o kuli lee zaɣi yɛla. Ti naɣi ka o chɔɣi nyɛŋ ninkpɛŋ, ka nindabi yiŋ o yɛla bɛ o ni saa. N daa, ti bori sɔŋ n nyɛ bɔri so ka ti bɔri ti din bɛ n bɔri naɣi, yaa ka ti musi o kɔ nyɛla yili.
narrative_fante: Me din de Dela, merefrɛ sɛ merebɔ accident bi ho amaneɛ a ɛsii ha Cape Coast. Aberante bi a wɔfrɛ no Kpodo — ɔte sɛ ɔwɔ n'afe aduonu ho anaa aduonu mmienu ho — trotro bi bɔɔ no wɔ ɔkwan so, ɛnkyɛ ara. N'nan yɛ no yaw papa — ɛhoo no na ɔka sɛ ɛyɛ no yaw paa — nanso ɔrenyɛ kolapuse; ɔda ne ho no na ɔrekan asɛm. Yɛatumi ama no atena ɔkwan ho na obi wɔ ne nkyɛn seesei. Me pa wo kyew, yehia mmoa sɛ yɛnhunu deɛ yɛbɛyɛ isin anaa sɛ ɔkɔ hospital.
narrative_gurene: M yɛtɔ la Dela. M bee frɛ fo tɩ m gori accident nɩ be la Cape Coast kɔ. Saan biisi la, bia din bee nyɛŋ zug la, Kpodo, trotro bɔ o la nyɛŋ kɔ — o nyɛ la wa o bee pɛlɛŋ la bia a ta ayɩɣa la wɔ, wɩɩsɩ, o ta bee twenty kal la. O nɩŋ yɛligu o ka ka, a bee pʋŋ la, la o yɛlɩ tɩ ka yɛligu o ka ka zʋŋ. Ama o ko nyɛ o, o bee yɛligu la. Ti sɔŋ o tɩ o zɩ nyɛŋ bʋɣʋ, la nia la nɩŋ bɛlɩ o be la. N tɩɩma, yi mam tɩ ti nyɛ bɛ ti ta la tɔŋ buŋ, wɩɩsɩ, o sɔŋ bia tɩ o kɔ hospital?

narrative_en: Please, I am calling to report something that happened here at the Takoradi market area. My name is Hamza, and my young brother Yakubu — he slipped and fell down near one of the market stalls. He hit his head on the ground but he is okay, he is conscious and just complaining of small pain at the back of his neck. It is not serious but we just wanted to report it properly.
narrative_ewe: Meɖekuku, mefɔ wɔ ɖe nuɖoɖo siɖe abe nuɖɔɖɔ wɔɔ ne — nu siwo wu ɖe afim Takoradi market ŋu. Nyeŋutsu ŋkɔ nye Hamza, eye nɔvitɔ ɖevitɔ ce — Yakubu — eya wo, ewu ƒu ɖem fɔ market stalls aɖe ŋɔli. Eta dzo ƒu ƒu ŋuɖoɖo dzi gake ele nyuie, eɖo edzi, exa gake ɖo ŋɔli aɖe le edzi eyi nam ɖe xexeame. Esia sɔme gaã o, gake miaƒe ɖu nyuie be mia wɔ nuɖoɖo titina.
narrative_ga: Ofainɛ, mibɔ phone ni hewalɛ ɛyɛ shi Takoradi market area. Me din bi Hamza, na me nua oblɛ Yakubu — obumo kɛ ejɛ market stall fɛɛ. Obi ŋmɛi fɛɛ ground la, nɔŋ oshi wo oni le — enimɔ gbɛ o. Ni hii mli kɛkɛ mli ŋmɛi akɔ shi, nɔŋ ɛnyɛ serious. Chale, yɛbɔ phone jebuɔ ni hewalɛ shikpon.
narrative_dagbani: N daa, m frɛ yɛlgu kamba maa bɛ saha yi. M yuli di Hamza, ka m nua biɛla Yakubu — o kpɛŋ gbini kɔŋ Takoradi market dooi kɔŋ kaɣa yili dee kɔŋ. O ŋun gbini saa ni, ama o bɛ ti soli — o nyɛ o yɛla, ka o gbini yɛlga o ŋun kpɛŋŋa zɔliŋ yɛligu biɛla pam. Ka yɛligu bi yɛla ka ka daa, ama ti bɔri la a din ti yɛlim be ni doo.
narrative_fante: Me pa wo kyew, merefrɛ sɛ mɛkaa biribi a asi ha wɔ Takoradi market area hɔ. Me din de Hamza, na me nua ketewa Yakubu — ɔhwee fam kɔkyeree stall bi ho wɔ market mu. Ɔbuaa ne tiri wɔ fam no so, nanso ne ho yɛ, ɔnim ne ho, na ɔkaa sɛ eye no yaw kakra wɔ ne kɔn akyi. Ɛnyɛ kɛse, nanso yɛpɛe sɛ yɛka no yiye.
narrative_gurene: N tɩɩma, m frɛ fo se nɩ m gʋa bi yɛligu ka bɩ sɔɣa Takoradi market pʋɔ. M yɛlɩ Hamza, la m doa biɛla Yakubu — o sɔbrɩ o gbɛɛ la o kpɛŋ fam wa kɔŋ market yir biɛla pʋɔ. O ŋun dɩŋa nunni fam bɛ o ka bɛri zugu — o ko a nyɛ waa, o nyɛ la bam, a yɛligu biɛla tɔ o ŋun la o poore nyɛŋ pʋɔ. Ka ka yɛligu bebera, bɛ ti yɛlɩ se m da frɛ fo la a bʋɣɩ yɛlɩ bɔbɔrɛ.

narrative_en: Please, I want to report an accident that happened here in Navrongo. My name is Muntari, and I'm calling about my neighbor Asare — he was knocked by a shared taxi on the road not too long ago. He's okay, he's not in a serious condition, but his leg got hurt and he's feeling some pain there. We think maybe it's just a cut or bruise, nothing broken, but we want to be sure he gets checked properly.
narrative_ewe: Meɖekuku, meɖi ɖe nudzadzra si woa ɖe afisia Navrongo. Ŋkɔnyem enye Muntari, eye mefrɛ mi le ŋkɔ ƒe dzidzidzi ƒe dɔ wɔwɔ ŋu — ameƒoa nye Asare. Shared taxi ɖe ʋɔ dzi tia eɖe abe ɣeyiɣi ma ɖe sia o. Ele nyuie, evɔ o, gake eƒe afɔ xɔ aɖe, eye eyi nam le afi ɔ dzi. Mia susu abe enye aɖaŋu kaka o, nyawo le ɣleti vɔ o — gake miɖi be wòanɔ nɔ la sɔ axa ezi, eye woana bu eƒe ŋu le asiwo.
narrative_ga: Ofainɛ, mifɛ report accident bi ŋ happen fɛ Navrongo. Mii yɛ Muntari, mi frɛ frɛ ŋ mi neighbor Asare — shared taxi bi knock no kpee ŋ, enɔ bi o. Onyimi le, eni serious o, gboo bi hurt no ni ehii no fɛ he. Yɛ lɛlɛ ni maybe cut anaa bruise no, nothing broken, gbɛɛ yɛfɛ make sure ni doctor check no well well.
narrative_dagbani: N daa, n bɛ yɛ ni m frɛ ŋ kuri accident din kpɛŋ Navrongo. N yuli di Muntari, ka m bɛ yiri n dagɣibu Asare yɛla — kaɣa din bɛ ti gbani wuhibi gbini o Navrongo nyɛŋ din naa. O bɛ ŋaan, o nyɛ yɛlim yɛla mɔɣɔ, ama o tiŋ yɛligu o kura, ka yɛligu bi zaa o zuɣu. Ti kuri be di yɛla ŋani kaɣa gbini so kana yɛligu kana tiin gbini bɛ, ka bia di kani, ama ti bɔri la Asare ni o nyɛ dɔɣite mali, ka o di zaa be o yɛla.
narrative_fante: Please, I want to report an accident that happened here in Navrongo. My name is Muntari, and I'm calling about my neighbor Asare — he was knocked by a shared taxi on the road not too long ago. He's okay, he's not in a serious condition, but his leg got hurt and he's feeling some pain there. We think maybe it's just a cut or bruise, nothing broken, but we want to be sure he gets checked properly.
narrative_gurene: N tɩɩma, mam yɛlɩ accident sɩŋ ka ba maŋ ka o sɩɩ Navrongo no. M yʋɔrɩ Muntari, ka mam frɛ a zuɣu m jiig Asare — shared taxi bɔɔrɩ o nyɛŋ zug ka o kpɛŋ fam ka ka yɛ pɛlɩ la. O be, o ka bɛri yɛligu wʋbɩŋ, bɛ o nɩŋmɩn yɛligu ka a yɛligu ka o be dɔɔ. Tɩ nyɛ ka bala ka o kpɩɛrɩ baa yɛligu, ka ka bɩ dɔbɩ ka ka maa yɛlɩ nyɛ ka o kpɩɛrɩ baa kpɛɛm la — bɛ tɩ yɛ sɔŋɔ ka o kɔ yɛla yir mɔɔ ka ba nyɛ o zug nɩŋ nɩŋ.

narrative_en: Please, I am calling to report an accident that happened here in Hohoe. My sister Maame, she is a young girl, she was knocked by an articulated truck on the road and she fell very hard. The main place she got hurt is her neck and her head — she is conscious but she is complaining of pain and she cannot move her neck well. Kweku here, I am the one with her now and we need help because we are afraid to move her.
narrative_ewe: Meɖekuku, mefrɛ mi be medzea nudzadzra si wɔa afisia Hohoe la nu. Nɔvinyɔnu ce Maame, ɖevi nyɔnu wɔɛ, akɔ gãtɔ ɖeka dɔa eŋu ʋɔ dzi, eye edo ƒu tegbee. Ƒome si eyi eŋu wu ɖe vuvɔ la enye ɖo kple ta — eɖo edzi, gake egblɔa be eyi nam ɖo kple ta, eye eme le be eda ɖo nyuie o. Kweku wɔm, míenye ame si le eŋu fifia, eye miɖi kpɔ ŋunye ɖe gbɔ, efɔ be míegbɔ eɖi akpa eƒe ŋu, mete ŋu wɔe o, efɔ be mídro eŋu kpɔ.
narrative_ga: Ofainɛ, mitsɔ phone frɛ frɛ shikpon accident bi bɔ ha Hohoe. Yɛ naaŋ Maame — oblɛ bi — articulated truck bi bɔ mo ni ekɛ kpee nuŋ, ebumo ko yɛ hewalɛ. Ni hii ji wɔ nyɛ ŋmɛi kɛ ŋmɛi gbɔɔ fɛɛ — oni le, kɛ enyɛ mli ehii ni, kɛ enyami ŋmɛi fɛɛ o. Kweku na mle ni, mitsɔ ni wɔ kɛ yɛ. Boa mi, chale — yɛ tsee ni yɛ nni etse eni bumo o, enyɛ shia.
narrative_dagbani: N daa, m frɛ yi ka m wiligi accident din kpɛŋ ka din kuli ka Hohoe. M poa Maame, o nyɛ biɛla, articulated truck gbuni o nyɛŋ zuɣu ka o gbini ka yɛligu pam. Baŋ din ka o yɛligu pam bɛ o kpɛŋ ni ni o ŋun — o ŋani ka ti di soli bia, amo o zua ka yɛligu bee o ka o na wuhiri o kpɛŋ ŋan. Kweku n bɛ ka n yini o diŋ, ka ti bori sɔŋ pam barka ka ti suri ka n wuhi o, barka ti suri tiri o ka ti bori yi sɔŋ mia.
narrative_fante: Me pa wo kyew, merefrɛ sɛ mebɛka accident bi a ɛsii ha wɔ Hohoe. Me nuabaa Maame, ɔyɛ abofra bea, articulated truck bi bɔɔ no wɔ ɔkwan so na ɔtuu fam yiye. Baabi a ɛyɛ no yaw pa ara ne ne kɔn ne ne tiri — ɔhunu hwee, nanso ɔreka sɛ ɛyɛ no yaw na ɔntumi nnyin ne kɔn yiye. Kweku na mewɔ ha, mene no wɔ ha seesei na yehia mmoa efisɛ yɛsuro sɛ yɛbɛyi no.
narrative_gurene: N tɩɩma, m frɛ sɔŋ a kom accident be la Hohoe ŋa. M mog Maame, o bee bia pɔɣa, articulated truck bugi o nyɛŋ zuɣu la a o bɔɔrɩ ka yɛligu pɛɛli. Ɛ yɛligu dɩŋɩ ka bee o nɔɔrɛ ne o ŋun — o ko a nyɛ daa, la o yɛligu ka o nan tʋʋmɩ o nɔɔrɛ mɩŋ. Kweku mɩŋ m bee o ŋa la, ka ti daɣɩ sɔŋ bɩ ti nyɛ ti gɔri o, ka ti zɔɣɩ.

narrative_en: Please, I want to report something that happened to a young boy near Mankessim, on the Kumasi-Cape Coast road. My name is Vida, and the boy's name is Abubakar — he's maybe fifteen or sixteen years old. He fell into some water by the roadside, I think it was a drainage channel or something like that, and he went under for a short time before some people nearby pulled him out. He is breathing fine now, thank God, but his arm got hurt when they were pulling him — it's swollen and he's holding it in a funny way. We just want someone to come and check him properly because he's still shaken up.
narrative_ewe: Meɖekuku, meɖi na wo se nu siwo wò va tso Mankessim, ƒe ŋkɔ Kumasi-Cape Coast ʋɔ dzi. Nyeŋɔŋlɔ nye Vida, eye ɖevi ŋɔŋlɔ nye Abubakar — eya le ƒe ewo vɔ ɛ̃ alo esia ene ƒe ame ɖeka. Ewu ƒu aʋa abe dɔwɔƒe tso ʋɔ ta ɔ, meɖo nu be dɔwɔƒe ɔ nye drainage channel o alo nubɔbɔ alo nutiŋu akɔe, eye ewui ɖe eyome fifia ɖeka eye ameawo siwo le ŋkɔe tsoe yi eƒe. Eƒu agbe nyuie fifia — akpe na Mawu — gake eƒe asi vɔ ame ɖeka gake ame siwo tsoe yi, eye asi sia dze nyigba te eye edze eyi nam teŋ. Mado nu be ame ɖeka ni wa tsi kpɔ eye ni wa bɔ eŋu nyuie, elabena evi si le ŋkɔe nye Abubakar la ɖeka wɔ fifi siwo dede.
narrative_ga: Ofainɛ, me pɛ sane ke enyɛ bii sane eba — oblɛ baako eko Mankessim kɛ, kpee Kumasi-Cape Coast kpee lɛ shishi. Mii dzin Vida, kɛ oblɛ lɛ dzin Abubakar — eni akɛ fifteen abi sixteen years old. Obumo ni wɔɔ kɛ kpee shishi lɛ — mishi ke edaa drainage channel abi sane biaa bɛyɛ hɛɛ — okɛ aba faako before ni wɔlɔi gbɛɛ lɛ tsɔɔ ooo. Oni hɔmɔ le nɛ, Nyɔŋmɔ na enyɛ shia, kɛ ni wɔɔ tsɔɔ ee, ogbɔ lɛ hii — esumo kɛ eni gbaa ni obɛ too ee, ni eyii le biaa ehii. Yɛ pɛ ke doctor biaa eba kɛ check o pɛpɛɛpɛ — oblɛ lɛ shakeshake kɔmɔ lee nɛ.
narrative_dagbani: N daa, n bɛ yɛ ni n saɣim di kuli o nyɛ biɛla din gbini ka o nyɛ Mankessim poo, Kumasi naa Cape Coast nyɛŋ din doli ni. N yuli la Vida, ka biɛla yuli la Abubakar — o kuri shɛli pia anu wɔli kuri pia ayɔbu. O gbini daa nyɛŋ doŋ ni, boli tim pam wula o gbɔŋ, m niŋ maa n nyɛ o gbini ka mɔɣli niŋ ka o kpeŋ bɔŋbɔŋ. Daba doɣi o daa bɛ chaŋ o bɔri ka o puli. O puli pam daa, Naawuni tuma, amma o nuu nyɛ yɛligu daa din daa o biɛm o — ka o nuu gbɔlim daa ka o nuu kiri a zaɣa yɛla. Naa, ti bori dɔɣite ni o naa ti pam bɔ o sahimsi, bɛ nyɛ o daa dani ni ka nyɛligu bee o maa.
narrative_fante: Me pa wo kyew, mepɛ sɛ meka biribi a ɛsii abofra ketewa bi ho fa Mankessim ho, Kumasi-Cape Coast road so. Me din de Vida, na abofra no din de Abubakar — ɔwɔ mfe edu-enum anaasɛ edu-nsia enti. Ɔtoo nsuo mu a ɛwɔ ɔkwan ho — me susu sɛ drainage channel bi ne no, na ɔkɔɔ fam ntem no ansa na nnipa bi a wɔwɔ ho kɔtaa no. Ɔrehome yiye nnɛ, Onyame ase, nanso nsa a wɔtaa no no mu no, ɛyɛ no yaw — ɛhyew na ɔkyere no strange. Yɛpɛ sɛ obi bɛba na ɔnhwɛ no yiye, efisɛ ɔkyɛn ho daa ara.
narrative_gurene: N tɩɩma, m pɛ kɛ m gom ade bee sɔŋa be wa tɩɩ bi bia yɔɣɔ Mankessim, Kumasi ne Cape Coast nyɛŋ zug. M yɛtɔ la Vida, la bi bia yɛtɔ la Abubakar — o daa be mɩŋa fifteen, sixteen lala. O bɔɔrɩ nzu ayi be be nyɛŋ nɔɔrɛ, m lɛ kɛ drainage yiri la, la o kɛnɛ a siga nzu pʋɔ ade fʋfʋ yɔɣɔ yɛn nɩba sɔŋ o yigi. O zee la ka o sii yɛligu, Yinɛ zuɣu, la o nua wa yɛligu a sɔŋ o bee nɩ — o pʋɔ la buŋa la o tʋŋ o nua ayi zam-zam. Tɩ pɛ la kɛ nɩba wa nɔɔrɛ o nɩŋma sɔŋ, bala o pɔ bee yɛligu yɛtɔ.

narrative_en: Please, something happened on the Takoradi-Cape Coast highway, close to Anomabo. A young man called Selorm was knocked down by a car — I think he's around eighteen or so. He hit his head and neck badly on the road when he fell, and he was bleeding from his head but he was still breathing and talking small. I'm Akosua, I was passing by when it happened and some people have gathered around him now. We need help to come quickly because the boy needs to be seen by a doctor.
narrative_ewe: Meɖekuku, nudzadzra etso ƒu Takoradi-Cape Coast ʋɔ dzi, kplɔ̃ Anomabo si. Ɖevi ŋutsu aɖe be Selorm wɔe nya woɖo akɔ dom — medo ba ko be eƒe xexlẽme le agerĩ ewo ƒe nyuie me. Esi ewu ƒu, eta kple eƒe ɖo ɖo ʋɔ dzi gblɔgblɔ, eye dzi le etsotso eƒe ta ŋu — gake edzi ƒu ame eye elelea gble gble aɖe. Nyeɖe Akosua, neo enye, ma fɔe ŋu le ʋɔ dzi esi nudzadzra la tso ƒu, eye amewo du ɖe eŋu fifia. Meɖekuku, kpe ɖe ŋumitɔ la ŋu enum, elabena ɖevi la hia dokita aɖe ana eŋu bena wòkpɔ e.
narrative_ga: Ofainɛ, something happen for Takoradi-Cape Coast highway, close to Anomabo. Obi kpɔɔ biaa wei, Selorm, kaa sumo ŋ — mi lɛ eni years eighteen or so. Ŋmɛi ne ŋmɛi-bii fɛɛ ehii mi nu kpee year obumo, ne shwe etsotso ŋmɛi pɛ, shikpon eni hɔmɔ ŋ ne ebi kasa kɛkɛ. Mii nye Akosua — mi lɛ mi ye dzii ŋ, accidents happen, ne nɔŋ biikpɔɔ fei kuu ni le ŋ akɛ. Boa mi! Yɛ jɔgbaŋŋ mli nu, oblɛ wei hia doctor ko ka.
narrative_dagbani: N daa, shɛli kɔ nyɛ Takoradi-Cape Coast nyɛŋ zuɣu, Anomabo kɔŋ. Doli biɛla din bɔri Selorm — n nyɛri o bɛ saa piaha ayi mɔ ani ni — kaɣa daa yɛli o. O daa yuli o ŋun ni o gbolo Takoradi-Cape Coast nyɛŋ zuɣu wula, ka ziim daa bee o ŋun, amaa o daa di-ŋmali ka o daa yɛla yela biibi. N yuli Akosua — maa daa nyɛ pam ni shɛli kɔ nyɛ. Nima bɛ, ninbi daa pam o puu la. N bɛ yɛ ni, ti bori sɔŋ din wa yaga yaga, k'o zaŋ o wa dɔɣite dindana.
narrative_fante: Me pa wo kyew, biribi abu so wɔ Takoradi-Cape Coast highway no so, bɛn Anomabo. Ɔbarima ɔfoforo bi a wɔfrɛ no Selorm — metwe no sɛ ɔwɔ ahennum mmienu saa ana — kar bi bɔɔ no maa ɔtuu fam. Ɔtuu no wɔ ɔkwan so a n'akyi yɛɛ den — ɔhuruu ne tiri ne ne kɔn wɔ fam no so yiye, na mogya retetu firi ne tiri mu, nanso ɔne oteam na ohome daa. Me de, me din de Akosua — na mepɛ na mimfa kwan no so a na aba na ahu biribi abu so. Nnipa bi ahyia ne ho seesei. Me pa wo kyew, mmoa bi mmmrɛ mmra ha na mmoa — ɔbarima no nohia oduruyɛfo ahu no ara oyie.
narrative_gurene: N tɩɩma, somɩ yɛɛ Takoradi-Cape Coast nyɛŋ pʋɔ, Anomabo yɔɔrɛ. Bia bi yɩɩrɛ Selorm — m tɩɩ o yɛlɩɩ yenno asɔɔna-nɛɛ boro — kaɣa bɔ o yɛligu. O kpɛŋ fam, o ŋun nɛ o koore bɔ nyɛŋ ka yɛligu. Ziim bee o ŋun pʋɔ, amaa o bee nyɛ tɩɩ tɩɩ la ka o goam mɔɔ. Mɩ yɩɩrɛ Akosua — m laa be nyɛŋ pʋɔ la a yɛɛ. Nɩbɛ la be o yɔɔrɛ sʋɔ. N tɩɩma, sɔŋ la wa nɩɛra nɩɛra, bʋɔr bia fɛ doktɔr nyɛ o.

narrative_en: My name is Maame, and I'm calling to report something very serious that happened to an old man near Kintampo, along the road going to Tamale. His name is Wiredu, and some people attacked him — they beat him very badly on his head and his neck. He is an elderly man and he cannot stand up, he's just lying there and there is blood coming from his head. Please, the situation is very bad, he needs help quickly before something worse happens to him.
narrative_ewe: Allo! Nyeŋɔŋlɔ nye Maame, eye mefrɛ mi ɖe nudzadzra bubu sia dzi si wɔ ɖe srɔ̃ ɖeka ŋu kple Kintampo xɔme, le ʋɔ si yi Tamale dzi. Eŋɔŋlɔ nye Wiredu, eye amesiwo wɔ dzo ame ƒe nu — wokpɔ yi egɔme vovovo le eƒe ta kple eƒe ɖo dzi. Srɔ̃ si me le gome o — ŋutsu agbenyaga ne, eye mate ŋu zu ɖo o. Ele ɖe anyigba dzi ko, eye dzi le etsotso le eƒe ta gome. Meɖekuku, nusi le gome le vevie kaka — eɖi kpɔ ŋunye akpɔkpɔe, eye newoɖe nu vɔ̃ ɖevo me ŋuwò le eŋu. Meɖekuku, wò akɔ deko!
narrative_ga: Hɛloo, yɛ dzɛ mi nyɛ Maame. Mifra frɛ oshɔ ni mli eko tɛ — ni ehii gbɔmɔ bi okɛ Kintampo, akɛ kpee ni eko Tamale. Eŋ dzɛ Wiredu. Nibii eko, nitsɔ ni amli gbeke — enwɔ ni ehii lɛ gbɔɔ — enwɔ ŋmɛi ni gbeke, enwɔ eŋ gbeke aswaa. Gbɔmɔ lɛ, enyɛ mi — oni bɔ wɔ shia lɛ ŋ, ebemɔ le kɛ ebumo akɛ shia lɛ. Shwe etsotso ŋmɛi ni — shwe etsotso ni gbeke. Ofainɛ, ni hii lɛ ehii gbɛ — onyɛ mi ehii o, oboa ni mli eko kɛkɛ wɔ pɛ, tsɔ ni amli bo eko ni ni gbemɔ gbɛɔ.
narrative_dagbani: Allo, m yuli di Maame. N bɛ yɛ ni, m bɛ frɛ yi ka n naan yɛli yi doo nyɛla wuhigu tɔɣa yɛla din kɔŋ Kintampo saɣim, Tamale nyɛŋ dooi. Nindaan bi yuli di Wiredu. Niriba doro sɔŋ o mali — o ti gbuni o wuhibu daa, o ŋun ni o koŋ. O kpɛma daa, ka o paa zoli ka o ti soli, o bɛ daa yɛŋ o ŋun saɣim, ka ziim bee o ŋun. N daa, yɛla ni ka o daa, o bori sɔŋ ka o zaɣim, ni yɛla dabli ŋun ti kɔŋ o. Sɔŋ maa — sɔŋ Wiredu zaɣim!
narrative_fante: Me din de Maame, na merefrɛ mo sɛ ɛba bɛn a ɛyɛ den paa na ɛsii opanyin bi ho fa Kintampo ho, wɔ ɔkwan a ɛkɔ Tamale no so. Ɔdin de Wiredu, na nnipa bi bɔɔ no — wɔbubuɔ no yiye yiye wɔ ne tiri ne ne kɔn so. Ɔyɛ opanyin, na ɔntumi agyina ase, ɔda fam pɛpɛɛpɛ na mogya retu firi ne tiri mu. Me pa wo kyew, ɛberɛ no yɛ den paa — ɔhia mmoa ntɛm ntɛm ansa na biribi a ɛyɛ dɛ hunu no.
narrative_gurene: M yɔɔrɩ Maame, mam frɛ fo a yɛlɩ fo ninsaal yɛla, yɛla wʋlʋm daa wʋl. Nindaan yenno o bee Kintampo tiŋa, nyɛŋ nɩ bee Tamale. O yɔɔrɩ Wiredu. Ninsaalbɛ daa dɛmɩ o, o bugsɩ o wʋlʋm — ba daa zɩŋɩ o ŋun nɩ o goŋ nɩ yɛligu yɛligu. O yɛ nindaan, o nan tʋmɩ a zɩ, o kpɛŋ fam yɔɔrɛ, ka ziim bee o ŋun. N tɩɩma, yɛla daa wʋlʋm yɛligu, sɔŋ o yɛŋ, ka yɛla bɛ yɛlɩ pam yɛ o zuɣu.

narrative_en: My name is Fuseini, please I want to report an accident that happened on the Takoradi-Cape Coast highway, close to Anomabo. An old man, Owusu, was knocked by a vehicle and he fell badly on the road. It looks like his leg or arm got hurt because he cannot move it well and he is in a lot of pain, but he is still conscious and talking small. We have moved him off the road and some people are with him now, but he needs help to come quickly.
narrative_ewe:
Allo, yèzɔ nyem. Nyem ŋkɔ nye Fuseini. Meɖekuku, medzro be maŋlɔ nudzadzra si wòwɔ le Takoradi kple Cape Coast ʋɔ dzi, gbɔ Anomabo. Srɔ̃ ɖeka, Owusu, akɔ ɖeka ŋlɔ eŋu, eye ewu ƒu gbãgbã le ʋɔ dzi. Eya ta, afɔ aɖe asi aɖe le eŋu, ebe mate ŋu ɖe wo dzi nyuie o, eye eyi nam nyunyuie. Gake eɖo edzi eye edzra gɔme kakaka.

Míaɖe eŋu ʋɔ ŋgɔ kpakple ame vɔ̃wò le eŋɔ fifia, gake eɖi kpe ɖe ŋunye ko, eye eɖi be woava vevie. Meɖekuku, frɛ ambulance to be woava gbɔ Anomabo ko. Akpe.
narrative_ga: Hɛloo, mi nyɛ Fuseini. Ofainɛ, accident bi hɛɛ Takoradi-Cape Coast highway shi, Anomabo kɛ shi. Gbɔmɔ bi, Owusu, kaa bi bumo no kɛ ebumo wɔ kpee shi kojojo. Eni tse shɛkɛ gboo anɔ gbɔ bi hii no, efee eniyɛ enihe ŋ wɔ nɔ fɛɛ — ni hii no naŋŋ. Owo le kɛ etse kɛ etsɔɔ yɛɛmi oblɛ nɔ, kɛ ebɔ ehii no kojojo, kɛ oniŋ le kɛ etse wɔ kɛ etsɔɔ yɛɛmi. Mɛ gbɛ no kpee shi, kɛ nii bi bɛ le kɛ no ni, shikpon. Chale, boa mi — ambulance nyɛ tɛ aba ntɛɛ. Ofainɛ!
narrative_dagbani: Allo, m yuli di Fuseini. N daa, m bɛ yɛ ni kɔli accident din be te Takoradi-Cape Coast nyɛŋ so, Anomabo kɔɣili. Nindaan baŋ, Owusu, kaɣa yiɣi o, ka o gbini nyɛŋ so ka yɛligu o daa. A miri be so o tiŋ naɣili o nuu — o nan kpɛŋi bɛ ŋani, ka yɛligu o daa paɣa. Ama o nyɛ o mi, o goliŋ kɔm kɔm. Ti kpɛŋ o nyɛŋ kpeŋ, ka nima baŋ be n'o niŋ dali. Ama o bori sɔŋ, ni yi bi yi n zaa pam. Yi frɛ ambulance ni bi ka yɛligu o yi daa.
narrative_fante: Me din de Fuseini, me pa wo kyew, mepɛ sɛ meka accident bi a ɛsii Takoradi-Cape Coast highway no so, Anomabo nkyɛn. Opanyin bi, Owusu, kar bi bɔɔ no na ɔtuu fam yiye wɔ ɔkwan no so. Ɛte sɛ ne nan anaa ne nsa hyɛ no yaw, efisɛ mintumi nsa no yiye, na ɛyɛ no yaw paa — nanso ɔda hɔ na ɔkasa kakra. Yɛadeane no fii ɔkwan no so, na nnipa bi wɔ ne ho seesei, nanso ɔhia mmoa — me pa wo kyew, mmoa no mprempren mmra!
narrative_gurene: M yɛli la Fuseini, n tɩɩma, m bɛ yɛla accident nɩ da wa yɛ Takoradi-Cape Coast highway zuɣu, Anomabo saɣim. Nindaan bi, Owusu, kaɣa daa nɩ o, o bɔɔrɩ fam yɛlibu nyɛŋ zuɣu. A nyɛ la a gɔ tɩŋa bee o nʋŋ yɛligu, bʋŋ o da tɩ tii dɩ o yɛligu ka o ka yɛligu wʋlɩ, la o ko nyɛ la o yɛla kpeŋ. Ti daa o nyɛŋ zuɣu yi, nɩ nɩbɛ be o zɔŋ sɔŋɔ, la o sɔŋ bɛ waab nɛ. N tɩɩma, frɛ sɔŋ o wa nɛ tɩɩn!

narrative_en: My son Kofi was involved in an accident on the Takoradi-Cape Coast highway, close to Anomabo. I am his mother, my name is Elinam. A vehicle hit him while he was by the roadside — he is a young boy, maybe sixteen — and he fell and hurt his side, around his chest and stomach area. He is not in a serious condition, he is conscious and walking, but he is in some pain so I wanted to report it. We are still near the scene now.
narrative_ewe: Allo! Meɖekuku, kpe ɖe ŋunye. Yomevi ce Kofi wɔ accident Takoradi-Cape Coast ʋɔ dzi, ko Anomabo dzi. Nye nye ame si du no, ŋkɔ ce nye Elinam. Akɔ ɖeka du we, eƒu ʋɔ te — ɖevi kpɔɖeŋu, efɔ ƒe aɖe ewo bé esia hafi — ewo ƒu, eye eyi ŋu ƒe dzi la gbã, ʋɔ ɖe ɖevi ŋɔ kple abɔ ŋutame ɔ. Akpa nyuie ɔ, eɖo edzi, le zãdzã fafa ɔ, gake eyi ŋu kakaka, eye nye be manye nu na mi abe nudzadzra sia fia ene. Míele scene la te ko fifia.
narrative_ga: Hɛloo, ofainɛ boa mi. Mii bii Kofi na accident ehii ŋ wɔ Takoradi-Cape Coast highway kɛ Anomabo faahe. Mii yɛ ni naŋ, ni name yɛ Elinam. Kaa bi hii ŋ wɔ kpee faahe — oba yɛ dɛbi, maybe sixteen years — ebumo kɛ ni gbɔɔ kɛ ni shi faahe ehii ŋ. Enyɛ serious condition o, oni le kɛ ehɔ shiŋŋ, jɛi ehii ni kakra. Maa jɛ frɛ report la. Yɛ tɔŋ wɔ accident place ɛnnɛ.
narrative_dagbani: Allo, N daa. M bia Kofi, o kpɛŋ accident Takoradi-Cape Coast nyɛŋ ti, Anomabo cɔɣu. Mba nya o ma, m yuli di Elinam. Kaɣa nyɛma o, o gbini nyɛŋ nuu — o ti bia daa, o sheŋ pia ayɔbu so. O kpɛŋ ka o tiŋ gbini, o digindi ni o pam cɔɣu yɛligu o. O nyɛ o di ninkpen yɛla, o ti nyɛ o maa so — o soli ka o nyɛ lee nyɛŋ — ŋun ka yɛligu bee o ka m bori la ŋun yɛla la a miri. Ti kpɛŋ accident sɔɣim tiri.
narrative_fante: Me ba Kofi hyɛɛ accident wɔ Takoradi-Cape Coast highway no so, kɔse Anomabo ho. Mene ne maame, me din de Elinam. Kar bi bɔɔ no bere a ɔne ɔkwan no ho — ɔyɛ abofra, ebia ɔwɔ sixteen — na ɔtuu fam na ɔyɛɛ ne nkyɛn yaw, bɛn ne bruwa ne yafunu no ho no. Ɔnni yaw kɛse papa — ɔhunu n'asɛm na ɔtumi nante, nanso ɛyɛ ne yaw kakra, na ɛno nti mepɛɛ sɛ mɛbɔ report. Yɛnnyɛ dinn wɔ accident no ho ara.
narrative_gurene: My son Kofi was involved in an accident on the Takoradi-Cape Coast highway, close to Anomabo. I am his mother, my name is Elinam. A vehicle hit him while he was by the roadside — he is a young boy, maybe sixteen — and he fell and hurt his side, around his chest and stomach area. He is not in a serious condition, he is conscious and walking, but he is in some pain so I wanted to report it. We are still near the scene now.

narrative_en: Please, something happened here in Techiman and I need help. My name is Efua, and I'm calling about my younger brother Yaw — he fell this afternoon inside the house and hit his head on the floor very hard. He is conscious but he is complaining that his neck is paining him and he looks a bit confused. We are worried because he is not himself right now.
narrative_ewe: Meɖekuku, nuɖoɖo aɖe wɔ ɖe afim le Techiman si, eye meɖi kpɔ ŋunye. Ŋkɔnyé nye Efua, eye mefrɛ wò hã na ŋutsu ƒe ŋkɔ si nye Yaw — edowu ƒu egɔme ŋdi ɖem le ʋu me, eye eta wuɖo ƒu anyigba dzi gã aɖeke. Eɖo edzi, gake ele ɖɔm be eɖone yi nam, eye enɔ la, ele ŋu aɖe — ame mele eme ŋutɔ o. Míele dzodzo dzi hã, ɛ̃e nye xexeme o ɖi dzidzime.
narrative_ga: Ofainɛ, bɔ eshɔ ŋ Techiman yi mli, mi hia bo. Mi nyɛŋ Efua, mi frɛ frɛ ɔfaatɛ mi Yaw tɔŋ — ebumo kpee ni dzɔ ŋ oshishi yi mli, yɛ ebi ŋmɛi wumo shi tɛtɛ. Oni le, shikpon ehi ehii mi gbɔkɛŋ, yɛ enɔkɛ confuse kɛ. Miiŋ hia ni bi oshishi kɛ akpɛ, chale — ni nyɛ Yaw onyɛ onii kɛ le wɔ.
narrative_dagbani: N daa, bi yɛla paɣa ka ŋmani Techiman ka n bori sɔŋ. N yuli la Efua, ka n frɛ ka n yɛl' ɔ n danŋmɛ Yaw niŋ — o gbini zaŋ ŋɔ nyɛŋ daa yili shɛli, ka o ŋun kuligi kaba pam tuma. O nyɛ o yɛla diɣim, ama o yɛlda ka o kpalakulɣu yɛligu n daa ka o yeli ka ka yɛligu daa. O nyɛ o daa ŋɔ yɛlna gbɔŋ yɛla, naa o ti be ni o zim yɛla maa shɛli bɔ. Ti daa boŋ, bala o ti nyɛ ni o yɛla ka o be shɛli zaŋ ɔ.
narrative_fante: Me pa wo kyew, biribi asi ha wɔ Techiman na mehia mmoa. Me din de Efua, na merɛfrɛ efa me nuabarima kumaa Yaw ho — ɔtuu fam adekyee yi wɔ efie mu na ɔbuae ne tiri wɔ fam mu yie paa. Ɔda hɔ na ɔhunu hwee, nanso ɔreka sɛ ne kɔn yɛ no yaw, na wahunu sɛ ɔsɛe kakra. Yɛsuro efisɛ ɔnnyɛ ne ho ankasa seesei.
narrative_gurene: Please, something happened here in Techiman and I need help. My name is Efua, and I'm calling about my younger brother Yaw — he fell this afternoon inside the house and hit his head on the floor very hard. He is conscious but he is complaining that his neck is paining him and he looks a bit confused. We are worried because he is not himself right now.

narrative_en: Please, I want to report something that happened to my son Kpodo here in our compound in Bolgatanga. The boy was climbing — I don't know what he was trying to do up there — and he fell down and landed badly on his arm. It is swollen now and he is saying it is paining him a lot, but he is still conscious and talking. He is a young boy, maybe fifteen, and I am worried because the swelling is not going down. Please can someone come and help us or tell me what to do?
narrative_ewe: Meɖekuku, meyi gbɔ nu siwo wòwɔ ɖe ŋunye ɖevi ce Kpodo ŋu afisia ŋu le yɔme le Bolgatanga. Ɖevi la ɖo wuiui — mese ɖe wo ŋu o, ena wɔ aɖe le ƒu — eye ewu ƒu akpo yi afɔ dzi, eye asi tso ŋɔnyime. Kpɔ afisi le eŋu la, ekpe eye Kpodo gblɔ be eyi nam nuto, gake eɖo edzi eye ede kpekpe. Ɖevi la nye ɖevi didi — gake nye ŋɔ eƒe ƒe la, ele ewo ƒe aɖe hã — eye mehã ŋu o ɖe epeme, elabena kpe la meye kpɔ o. Meɖekuku, ame aɖe ate ŋu ava kpɔ mía, aɖe ate ŋu gblɔ nam nusi na wɔm?
narrative_ga: Ofainɛ, mɛ pɛ kɛ mɛ tɛ oshiɛ bi shi dɛbi bi Kpodo yɛ, ŋ yɛ wɔ yɛ fie shi kɛ Bolgatanga. Oblɛ no ebumo — minti shi ni ebɛ pɛ kɛ etsɔ ebɔ jɛ hɛɛ — ebumo faa lɛ akɛ gbɔ yɛ. Gbɔ no ekpɛkpɛ nɔ yɛ, akɛ ɔyɛ ni ehii ni hii wɔ lɛkɛ, kɛ ɔni le — oni le akɛ okasa. Oblɛ yɛ dɛbi kɛkɛ, ŋ tɔŋ mli bii ni kɔŋ shie fifteen, akɛ shwe mli lo mi ni gbɔ no ekpɛkpɛ — ekpɛkpɛ no enyɛ le o. Ofainɛ, obibi osa boa mi wɔ, kana boa mi? Kana tɛɛ mi ni mɛ yɛ shi?
narrative_dagbani: N daa, n bɛ yɛ ni. N bori n yɛl' ŋun daa shɛli bee n bia Kpodo da kɔŋ ŋa ka o ti n doli wuni ka o gbini n dini o nuu zɔŋ. Ka n yiri n kpaabu Bolgatanga ŋa ŋun na n be. Bia yaba daa n bɛ ŋɔŋɔ bee bɛ na yɛ o be yibu ŋa koli — n bi laɣim o daŋ o nii bori naɣili — ka o gbini ka o nuu kpali ka pɛ yɛligu. O nuu kɔhim ka daa kɔhiŋ n gbima, ka o yɛlim o yɛligu daa ziŋ, ka o be yɛl'ma ka o ti soli kali. O bi kpɛma — o be zoli doɣili pia anu wɔli — ka n mi ka shɛli n daa o nuu kɔhiŋ be gbanim ka be mini. N daa, ŋun be yɛ sɔŋmi ya bɛ raa ni, o wa sɔŋmi, di kali yɛlim maa ŋa n bori naɣili?
narrative_fante: Please, I want to report something that happened to my son Kpodo here in our compound in Bolgatanga. The boy was climbing — I don't know what he was trying to do up there — and he fell down and landed badly on his arm. It is swollen now and he is saying it is paining him a lot, but he is still conscious and talking. He is a young boy, maybe fifteen, and I am worried because the swelling is not going down. Please can someone come and help us or tell me what to do?
narrative_gurene: Please, I want to report something that happened to my son Kpodo here in our compound in Bolgatanga. The boy was climbing — I don't know what he was trying to do up there — and he fell down and landed badly on his arm. It is swollen now and he is saying it is paining him a lot, but he is still conscious and talking. He is a young boy, maybe fifteen, and I am worried because the swelling is not going down. Please can someone come and help us or tell me what to do?

narrative_en: Please, I want to report something that happened to an old man here in Techiman. My neighbor Mensah, he is quite old, was doing some work — I think he was cutting something or doing some kind of farm work — and he hurt his arm or maybe his hand, I didn't see exactly. Amoah is my name, I'm the one calling. It doesn't look too serious, the cut is small and he is walking fine, but because of his age we thought it's better we call. He is resting now but we want to know if he needs to come in or if we can just clean it at home.
narrative_ewe: Meɖekuku, meɖo gbɔ aɖe na mi — nudzadzra aɖe wɔ agbenyaga aɖe afisia Techiman. Ɛ̃gbɔ ŋutsu Mensah, srɔ̃ aɖe, nɔnɔ wu. Ele wɔwɔ dɔ aɖe — makatsɔ be egblẽ nuviwo aɖe alo ele ƒuƒu dɔ agbe me — eye wonya asi alo nunye ŋu. Mekpɔ nane nyuitɔ o. Ŋkɔnya Amoah, nye ŋutɔ wɔm frɛfrɛ sia. Nusidzɔdzɔa ne la, enyo bu tsitsi — gblẽgblẽa keteke, eye ele ɖo ƒo le agbeɖeɖe me. Gake abe woɖoa na ŋutsunyawo la nàkpɔ — woyɔ be mie kae asiwo la alo miateŋu ɖe ʋuiʋui ŋu wɔ dɔwo la le ŋkeme. Esia wɔ nu eye mienɔ ŋkuŋlɔ. Meɖekuku, kpɔ nami be ekae asiwo aleke?
narrative_ga: Ofainɛ, mɛ bɛ report bɔɔ shi ni wɔ ye Techiman ni. Neighbor wɔ mi, Mensah, gbɔmɔ bi ŋ — oye work bi, mɛ suɔ ni oshwi bɔɔ bi kɛ oye farm work bi — nɛ ogbɔ ehii, gbɔ wɔ ŋ kɛ gbɔ wɔ ŋ kɔŋ, mɛ nyɛ exactly o. Amoah wɔ din wɔ mi, míí frɛ. Cut ŋ enɔ serious nɔ o — cut bi kpakpa ŋ, nɛ okpe shwaa hɛɛ. Kɛ shikpon gbɔmɔ, wɔ yɛ ni ɛsɛ wɔ frɛ. Oni le nɛ orɛst shwaa, kɛ wɔ pɛɛ nyɛ: ɛsɛ oba hospital bi, kɛ wɔ tumi clean ŋ wɔ fi kɛ?
narrative_dagbani: N daa, n bɔri n yɛla ŋun yɛligu bi mani ni Techiman. N yigɔ Mensah, o yɛ kpɛma daa, o bɛ tuma bɛ — n nyɛ bee o bɛ daa zaŋ kpaabu wɔbu bee o bɛ ŋmɛra tuma bɛ — ka o nuu wɔbi, bee o nuu kpɛŋ, n da nyɛ o tim tim. Amoah n yuli, m yɛ o maŋ bɛ tiri. Yɛligu bee n nyɛ la ka o ti yɛ gbiri ka daa — nawuya biɛla daa, ka o daa yɛ nyɛŋla nyɛŋ bɛ sɔŋ. Amaa ka o yɛ kpɛma, ti nyɛ ti bɔri la n bɔŋ ka ti fɛli. O bɛ naŋ naa la di zaa. Amaa ti bori n ŋmani — o daa nyɛ ka o bi naa nyɛla yili, bee ka ti daa wari o yiri?
narrative_fante: Me pa wo kyew, mepɛ sɛ meka biribi a asi ha wɔ Techiman. Me yɔnko Mensah, ɔyɛ opanyin paa, ɔreye adwuma bi — mese ɔretu biribi anaa ɔreyɛ farm work bi — na ɔhaw ne nsa, anaa ne nsa kɛse, minhu no pefee. Amoah na wɔfrɛ me, me na merefrɛ yi. Ɛnyɛ hu paa — the cut yɛ ketewa na ɔntumi nante yiye — nanso efisɛ ne nyin, yɛdwene sɛ ɛyɛ sɛ yɛfrɛ. Ɔreste seesei nanso yɛpɛ sɛ yɛhunu sɛ ɛsɛ sɛ ɔba hospital, anaa yɛbɛtumi clean no wɔ fie ara?
narrative_gurene: N tɩɩma, m pɛ yɛla kʋɔrɩ yɛla bi nɩ o da kɔŋ ʋ nindaan la Techiman. M yɩrna Mensah, o kpɛŋ o yɛligu, o bee o tɔŋ — m lɛɛrɩ o bee bɔbɔ nɩ o yɛɛ kpaam kɔŋ yɛɛ, bee o bee sɔŋ sɔŋ puuni — la o yɛli o nuɔrɩ bee o nʋɔrɩ bila, m nan nyɛ a ziig la. M yɩrɩ Amoah, mɛ la nɩ m frɛ ɔ. Ka a nyɛ la ka ŋ yɛligu ka bɛ ka yɔɔ mɔɔrɩ — kpem la ka biig, la o ŋmari nyɛŋ nyɛŋ ka bɛ yɛligu. Ama ka o kpɛŋ la, tɩ lɛɛrɩ ka tɩ frɛ. O kʋɔrɩ la a be, ama tɩ pɛ nyɛ ka o sɔŋ o de hospital bee tɩ tɩɩ o kpem la a yir?

narrative_en: My son Mawuli was knocked by a pragyia this afternoon on the Hohoe-Jasikan road. The bike hit him and he fell, and it looks like his arm got hurt — he's crying and holding it but he can move his fingers small. I am Boateng, I'm his father. Please we need someone to come and check him because even though he seems okay, I don't want to take chances with a child.
narrative_ewe: Allo! Meɖekuku, kpe ɖe ŋunye! Ŋutsu ɖeviam Mawuli — pragyia xɔe ɖe Hohoe-Jasikan ʋɔ dzi gbã fifia egbe. Motor xɔe, edo ƒu, eye asi ŋutɔ la, vide nye ale ko — ele gbe ŋu le la dzi eye etɔ̃ asi ŋutɔa, gake ale koe ate ŋu ŋlɔ nuwówo ɖe nusiwo le asi ŋutɔ la nu. Nyem Boateng, menya tɔ. Meɖekuku, midzro ame aɖe ava kpɔe, ava bia le eŋu — ɖevi la vide nye ale ko le gbe, gake maɖi be mawɔ nudzadzra o, ɖevi ko nye. Meɖekuku, frɛ ame aɖe va kpɔe fifia!
narrative_ga: Hɛloo, boa mi! Dɛbi bi — Mawuli, mɛ ba — pragyia bumo no afternoon yi Hohoe-Jasikan kpee dzɛ. Motor nɔ bumo no, ɛbumo fɛɛ, na ni hii ŋ gbɔ — ɔhii na ɔshɛ gbɔ no, kɛ onyemi le, fingers nɔ tumi yio small small. Miinɔ Boateng, mɛ si ŋ papa. Ofainɛ, yɛ frɛ atsɛ obi mli hɛ bi, ebi check no — nawotso oblɛ oni le, mi nyɛ hu atsɛ yɛ bɔ chances dɛbi dzɛ. Boa mi chale, frɛ ambulance bi mli shi!
narrative_dagbani: N bia Mawuli, aboboyaa bi o Hohoe-Jasikan nyɛŋ zuɣu yindi bi. Aboboyaa bi o, o gbini, ka o nuu yɛligu — o yɛya ka o di o nuu, ama o zɔŋ o nuu nyɔɣim nyu biɛla. Mii Boateng, m bɛ o baaba. N daa, ti bori la o gbɛŋ dɔɣite wa nini ka o na o nya, bimba din o yɛri ni o yɛli mali, m ti bori ka dɔɣite wa nini ti nyɛ o, ka biɛla nyɛla, n ti lana ka kuli kuli.
narrative_fante: Maaha. Me ba Mawuli, pragyia bi bɔɔ no ennora awia so wɔ Hohoe-Jasikan ɔkwan so. Bike no bɔɔ no na ɔtuu fam, na eye sɛ ne nsa hurt — ɔresu na ɔrekyekyere no, nanso ɔtumi sesa ne nsateaa kakra. Me ne Boateng, me ne ne agya. Me pa wo kyew, yehia obi a ɔbɛba hɔ na ɔhwɛ no, efisɛ ɛwom sɛ eye sɛ ɔho yɛ no, nanso abofra ho asɛm, mmpɛ sɛ meto chance.
narrative_gurene: Mam bia Mawuli, pragyia sɔ o daa yɛl nɩ Hohoe-Jasikan nyɛŋ zuɣu. Motor sɔ o, n'o bɔɔrɩ fam, ka a nyɛ o nuu yɛligu — o gɔŋa n'o tɔɣɩ o nuu, ama o tɩɩ a nuu ninsaasi bee o ka a nyɛ. Mam yɛ fo, ti daɣɩ ŋun o wa nyɛ o, bʋɣʋ o bee ka nɩ, m na yɛl tɩ na kpɛ bia yɛl ma. M yɛ Boateng, m yɛ o ba. Bia yɛl ka a mɔɣɩ maam — n tɩɩma, sɔŋ mam wa nyɛ o.

narrative_en: Please, I want to report something that happened here in Wa Central. My neighbor's son, Amoah, was knocked down by an aboboyaa on the road not too long ago. The boy is a young man, maybe around eighteen or nineteen years, and his leg got badly hurt in the accident — it's swollen and he can't stand on it at all. He is not unconscious or anything like that, but he is in a lot of pain and we are very worried. My name is Fatima, and we need someone to come and help us or tell us what to do next.
narrative_ewe: Meɖekuku, meɖi ŋlɔ nane bubu si woa wɔ afisia Wa Central. Amoah, ɖevi si nye na ŋutɔ-aɖa-ŋutɔ ame si nye dzɔgbe ŋunye, aboboyaa dzo na eƒe afɔ le ʋɔ dzi zi ɖeka o wɔ gake o. Ɖevi la nye ŋutsu vɔvɔ̃, eƒe ƒe le agbe ɖeka ɖeka ewo alo esiaaɖeka me, eye ɖoɖo vɔ̃ xɔ eƒe afɔ le accident ɔ me — afɔ la gbɔ, eye mate ŋu zɔ afɔ ɖo gome o. Eɖo edzi, eɖo edzi tɔtɔ, gake eyi nam vevie, eye miaƒe aɖanu le dɔ vevie. Nyeŋɔŋlɔ nye Fatima, eye miedi ame bubu a va kpe ɖe ŋumia alo ɖe mia ŋu wɔ nane gbɔna. Meɖekuku, djro nami.
narrative_ga: Ofainɛ, mifɛ report bɔɔ shi kɛ ehiɛ Wa Central. Mi neighbor bi dɛbi, Amoah, aboboyaa bumo no kpee hɛɛ — enyɛ too long ago. Oblɛ no enyɛ young man, maybe eighteen abi nineteen years, na ni gboo ehii no wɔ accident hɛɛ — eswɛɛ na oni gbi tɔɔŋ kɛ gboo no hwɛ. Enimɔ gbɛ o — onyemi le — nɛɛ ehii ni wɔ shi, ehii ni wɔ gboo, na mɛɛ worri nnɔ pɛpɛ. Mi name enyɛ Fatima, na mifɛ shi kɛ onipa bi bi shi boa mi abi mɛ shiɛ bɔɔ tɛɛ mɛ sɔ. Ofainɛ, frɛ ambulance bi bi shi!
narrative_dagbani: N daa, n bɛ yɛ ni n wilim daa ŋun bee yɛla bee ti paɣa ka gbini ka Wa Central. M shɛli biɛla, o yuli Amoah, aboboyaa daa kpɛ o nyɛŋ kɔliŋ. Biɛla bee o la ka o n-yuli ka gbɛla — o pɛliŋ bee pie ayi abi o pie awɛi wɔribu shɛŋ. O tiŋ daa nɔri daa accident puuni — o tiŋ doli ka o pa kuli la o nuu tiŋ zuɣu. O ti ti la ka o nyɛ o maa yɛla — ka o bɛ, ka o yɛligu daa, yɛligu bee o pam daa. Ti n-nyɛ yɛla la o zuɣu ka ti suhɔbɔ daa gbini. M yuli Fatima. N daa, yi sɔŋ ti — yi yiɣi ŋun yi ni paabu ti, abi yi zɔŋ ti bee ti na bɔri diri daa.
narrative_fante: Please, I want to report something that happened here in Wa Central. My neighbor's son, Amoah, was knocked down by an aboboyaa on the road not too long ago. The boy is a young man, maybe around eighteen or nineteen years, and his leg got badly hurt in the accident — it's swollen and he can't stand on it at all. He is not unconscious or anything like that, but he is in a lot of pain and we are very worried. My name is Fatima, and we need someone to come and help us or tell us what to do next.
narrative_gurene: N tɩɩma, m pɛ yɛla bi la a kɛ yɛ ka zɔŋ Wa Central. M yɩrɩ doa bia, Amoah, aboboyaa bɔɔrɩ o nyɛŋ zuɣu ka kɩɩ yɛ. Bi la bia yɛ yuɣom bia, o yome la bee asipɔɔ nɩŋ mɩŋa nɩ, ka o nʋŋ kɛ accident la poore ka o yɛligu wʋb — o nʋŋ la sʋʋŋɛ ka o zɔŋ a da. O ko a nyɛ sɔŋ la — ayo, ka yɛligu kɛ o wʋb nɩ ka ti zi o yɛlɩ wʋb. M yɩrɩ la Fatima, ka ti pɛ nɩrɩ wa sɔŋ ti, bɩ yɛ ti ka wi ka ade ti yɛ a bɩ da tuma.

narrative_en: My name is Nhyira and I'm calling to report something that happened at the Takoradi market area today. An old man, Fuseini, was attacked by some boys near the stalls — they pushed him and hit him, and he fell. He hurt his arm in the fall, but he is okay, it doesn't look too serious, just some swelling and maybe a small cut. Someone nearby helped him sit down and he is resting now, but I thought I should still call because he is an elderly man and I was worried.
narrative_ewe: Allo, yɔ nyem nye ŋkɔ nye Nhyira. Mefrɛ mi be maɖe nubɔbɔ ɖe woŋu — nudzadzra ɖeka wɔ Takoradi market gbɔ egbe. Agbenyaga ɖeka, Fuseini ŋkɔ wò le, amewo ɖe asi eŋu — ɖeviwo ɖeka nyuie o, yeyewo wɔ. Wo ɖu eŋu, wo dze eŋu fifia, gake nɛ, ewu ƒu. Ewu ƒu me, asi wò yiyi ŋu kpɔɔ — eyi eŋu ɖeke. Gake mele nuŋlɔ nyui ɖeke o abe nudzadzra gã ene ɔ, asi wò vɔm dzetɔ be asi le esiame, kple tsotso kakra le eŋu. Ame ɖeka le egbe kpɔɔ bé ne eɖo esiame. Eɖo fifia, evɔ ɖe ɖevi nɔƒe ɖeke dzi, le esiame, ale kple subɔ. Gake eƒe ta xɔ asi kakra, ene wòle agbenyaga, yeŋudede nam be maxɔ alesi na emi wuievi, eyata mafrɛ mi. Meɖekuku, mi kpɔ ame na wò adi nɔ de eŋu.
narrative_ga: Hɛloo, mi nyɛ Nhyira. Mifra oblɛ bo tsɔɔ shi naŋ enyɛ Takoradi market area shikpon. Gbɔmɔ bi, Fuseini, boys kakra fɛɛ nɔ attack — wɔbu nɔ push, wɔku nɔ, ebumo. Nɔ gbɔ ehii nɔ wɔ fall bii lɛ, noko enimɔ le, enyo too serious o — swelling kakra bɔ cut kakra eko. Onipa bi wɔ kɛɛ nɔ ba fɛɛ oshi, oni resting abɔɔ. Noko chale, gbɔmɔ lɛ, mihu nɔ worry, eyɛ naanɔ mifra.
narrative_dagbani: Allo, m yɔŋ nyɛla Nhyira. N bɛ yɛ ni frɛ, ka n yɛligu din kuli zuɣu din gbini Takoradi market yɛla kɔligu bi. Nindaan bi, din yɔŋ nyɛla Fuseini, kpɛmbila bihi naŋ nyɛ-o nuu ka naŋ bɛŋ-o, o kpeŋ o gbini tiŋ. O nuu nyɛ-o yɛligu din gbini, amma o nyɛ doo, a yɛligu da mi nyɛ gana-gana, ka nyɛ so ziim biɛla ni pam biɛla wula kuri. Doo din nyɛ be shɛɣim naŋ sɔŋ-o ka o kpɛŋ daa, ka o bɛ nyogisi la bi. Amma n bori la frɛ, bɛ o nyɛ nindaan, ka ka n maa pam nyɛ ka yɛligu daa.
narrative_fante: Me din de Nhyira, na merɛfrɛ sɛ merebu nsɛm bi a ɛsii Takoradi market mu ɛnnɛ. Opanyin bi, Fuseini, mmarima mba bi buae no stalls no ho — wɔpushae no na wɔbuae no, na ɔtuu fam. Ɔsɛee ne nsa wɔ sɛ ɔtuu fam no, nanso ne ho yɛ, ɛnyɛ den papa — swelling kakraa bi na maybe cut kumaa bi nko ara. Onipa bi a ɔbɛn no no boa no tena ase, na ɔreste saa ɛnnɛ, nanso misiie sɛ mɛfrɛ de, efisɛ ɔyɛ opanyin, na eye me hu.
narrative_gurene: M yɩɩrɩ Nhyira, ka m frɛ fo a tɩŋa n yɛla a ti a Takoradi market yɛla bine. Nindaan o yɩɩrɩ Fuseini, kpɛmba biisi bɔɔrɩ o kʋsɩ a yɔɔ buŋ nɛ — ba sʋŋɩ o ka ba tʋʋmɩ o, ka o bɔɔrɩ fam. O nɔŋ ka a yɛligu a dɔɔrɩ wɛŋ, la o bɛ nyɛ ka a yɛligu ka yaga — a tɩɩma a sɩŋɩ pʋrɩ ka nɛ zɔɔrɩ daa nɛ pʋɔ. Ninsaal o kʋsɩ sɔŋ o ka o zʋɔ o pɛlɩ, ka o bɛ nyɛ a pɛlɩ yiri bine. La m miri m ka m frɛ fo, barɩ o yɛ nindaan ka m nyɛ miri o zuɣu.

narrative_en: Please, I'm calling to report an accident that happened on the Accra-Cape Coast road, somewhere around Winneba side. My sister Akua was in a trotro that got hit by another vehicle, and she has been hurt on her leg — it's swollen and she can't really put weight on it. She's a young girl, maybe nineteen, and right now some people by the roadside are helping her. My name is Nii and I'm trying to reach her there now, but I wanted to call first so someone can come and attend to her.
narrative_ewe: Meɖekuku, mefrɛ mi be manɔ nusiwo wòwɔ ɖe Accra-Cape Coast ʋɔ dzi fia, ɖe Winneba ŋɔ la sesime. Nɔvi nyɔnu ce Akua le trotro si akɔ bubu wu ɖo dzi la me, eye wòdze ame dzi, afɔ tso ŋunye — afɔ la fo eye mate ŋu de nu ŋu afɔ la dzi o. Nyɔnu ɖevi enye, ɖo ene kple ɛ̃ ene abe sesime ene abe, eye fifia amewo ɖe ʋɔ dɔme le kpe ɖe ŋune. Nɔŋku ce enye Nii, eye mele dzɔdzɔm be mayi ƒu ŋu ye fia, gake mewɔ be manɔ mi ɖi na sɔ ansa la be ame aɖe ava gɔme kpɔ ɛ.
narrative_ga: Hɛloo, ofainɛ — mifra lɛ accident bi kɛ oshɔ Accra-Cape Coast road lɛ, somewhere Winneba side hɛ. Minaawe Akua, otsɔ trotro bi, kaa bi bumo gbɛ — ehii ni, kɛ ogboo hɛ. Ogboo ni swollen kɛ otsi fɛ gboo lɛ yɛ o. Obii oblɛ, maybe nineteen years, kɛ wɔni asɛ roadside lɛ hɛ, nɔŋ boa ni oshɔ. Miŋ Nii — mibɛ lɛ ni oshɔ aduane, nɔŋɔ mifra akɛ ɛnɛ wɔ, otsia oshi nii, nɔŋ bɛ attend to ni. Ofainɛ, frɛ ambulance mli bɛ oshɔ hɛ.
narrative_dagbani: Allo, N daa, m bɛ yɛ ni m wuɣ yɛla kɔŋ ni ka bi maalim accident din zaa dali Accra-Cape Coast nyɛŋ din, kɔŋ Winneba diɣim. M pↄplana Akua bɛ trotro din, ka kaɣa daa ŋmɛ o trotro din, ka o tiŋ yɛligu ka a fori, ka o zaa laɣim o tiŋ tooi. O nyɛ biɛla, a nyɛ piayawɛi — din gbɛra pia — ka di yɛlim. Dimsi, niba kɔŋ nyɛŋ daba bɛ sɔŋ o diɣa. M yuli nyɛ Nii, ka m bɛ yɛlim m gari o kɔŋ din, ka m nyɛ m frɛ yɛ yinmanga ka ŋun soli, n daa ti wa sɔŋ o, o diɣa.
narrative_fante: Me pa wo kyew, merefrɛ sɛ merebu nsɛm afa accident bi ho — accident no yɛɛ so wɔ Accra-Cape Coast ɔkwan so, Winneba fam hɔ. Me nuabaa Akua nà ɔwɔ trotro mu a kar fofor bi bɔɔ mu, na ɔwaa wɔ ne nan so — nan no asu na ɔntumi nsiw so yiye. Ɔyɛ ababaawa kɛkɛ — mpo mfe dunsia ne baako nkoaa — na seesei nnipa bi wɔ ɔkwan ho a wɔreboame no. Me din de Nii, na mereyɛ ɔkwan akɔ ne nkyɛn seesei, nanso mepɛɛ sɛ mefrɛ kan sɛ obiara anko hɔ ahwɛ no.
narrative_gurene: N tɩɩma, M frɛ fo tɩ bɔɔrɩ accident mɩnɛ pɛlɛ Accra-Cape Coast nyɛŋ zuɣu, Winneba kɛŋ la. M pɔɣa Akua o bɩɩ trotro mɩnɛ pɛlɛ a, kaɣa biɛlɩ nyɛna a bɩ moro. O nyuu yɛligu o nɩŋ zuɣu — o nɩŋ yɩɩla, la o nɩŋ ka nɩ wʋm o tɩɩ zuɣu la. O yɛ bia, o wʋm ayɩma zɔm nɩŋaɣa dee la. Saa mɩnɛ, nɩrɛ bihi nyɛŋ soŋ la sɔŋ o. M yɛ Nii, m toori bɛ kɛŋ la tɩ yi a, la m bɔɔrɩ m frɛ tɩ nɩrɛ na wa sɔŋ o paaboa.

narrative_en: Please, I want to report something that happened to a boy near the Hohoe-Jasikan road. My name is Efua, and the boy's name is Ibrahim — he is a young guy, maybe a teenager. He got burned on his neck area, but it doesn't look too serious, just some redness and small blisters. We think it happened from some hot liquid that touched him. He is conscious and he can talk, but we just want someone to come and check him properly.
narrative_ewe: Meɖekuku, meɖe xɔse ɖe nudzadzra si wòwɔ ɖevi ŋuti le Hohoe-Jasikan ʋɔ dzi ŋu. Ŋkɔnye enye Efua, eye ŋkɔ ɖevi la enye Ibrahim — ame yeyiɣi ɖeka, abe teenager ene. Dzo xɔe le ɖo ŋɔ, gake mele akpa vɔvɔ o — aɖeke si neze neze kple bli blibli geɖe geɖe ɖe ŋu. Míele susu be tsi tsitsi ŋuti kpɔɖeŋu wòsrɔ̃ ɖee. Eɖo edzi kpɔkpɔ eye etutue gble le, gake míeɖo gbɔ be ame aƒle va kpɔe nyuie.
narrative_ga: Ofainɛ, mehe bɔ report ni yɛ le Hohoe-Jasikan road kɛ. Mi din bi Efua, eŋ oblɛ din bi Ibrahim — oba ko, teenager ko. Shikpon ehii ni — neck area ni, kɛ enimɔ too serious o, shikpon red red nɔ kɛ blister kɛkɛ bi le ŋ. Yɛ susu ŋ eni fi liquid hot bi ni kɛ ŋ. Oni le kɛ otumi kasa, kɛ yɛ pɛ doctor aba lɛ check ŋ wɔ fɛfɛ.
narrative_dagbani: N daa, n bɛ yɛ ni n yɛl' yɛlgu bi ŋun tɔ ka o gɔliŋ Hohoe-Jasikan nyɛŋ saɣim. N yuli ni Efua, ka biɛla yuli ni Ibrahim — o nye bi, nɔɣu kpɛm biɛla dali. O zibu ka o nyɔŋ saɣim, ama a nyɛ la ka yɛligu biŋ — a nyɛ yuli yo yuli ka bihi kpara bihi be diri. Ti niŋ la ka puhi zuɣu kpiŋ yɛligu bee o. O be nyɛŋlim ka o tumi yɛlgu, ama ti bɔri la dɔɣite o ni wa nyɛ o timali.
narrative_fante: Me pa wo kyew, mepɛ sɛ meka biribi a ɛbaa abofra bi ho fa Hohoe-Jasikan ɔkwan ho. Me din de Efua, na abofra no din de Ibrahim — ɔyɛ aberante ketewa, maybe teenager. Wɔhyee no wɔ ne kɔn ho, nanso ɛnyɛ den paa — ɛte sɛ ne ho aboa kakra na nkokɔnsini ketewaa bi wɔ hɔ. Yɛdwene sɛ nsuo hyew bi na ɛka no. Ɔte nkwa mu na ɔtumi kasa, nanso yɛpɛ sɛ obi bɛba na ɔhwɛ no yiye.
narrative_gurene: N tɩɩma, m bɛ walem sɔŋ fɛ bʋŋa a la bʋŋ bia dodoo kʋn Hohoe-Jasikan nyɛŋ poore. M yʋʋrɩ la Efua, a bia yʋʋrɩ la Ibrahim — o la dalɩ biig, bɔɔrɩ o la teenager. O nyɔɔrɛ tɩɩga o nɔŋ poore, ama ka bɛ yɛligu pam — ka bɛla nyɛ ziim ayiga ŋa yaa a nɔŋ la sore bilisi. Ti kɩta o yɛligu la biig yɩɩrɛ kʋn a la tɩɩga o. O ko nyɛ la, o tɔɔrɩ la, ama ti walem ninsaal wa wa fɛ a nyɛ o la nɩɩrɩ.

narrative_en: My grandmother, Gifty, was knocked down by a car near our house here in Tarkwa. She is an old woman and the car hit her very badly — her leg is broken and there is too much blood. I am Kwabena, I am her grandson. Please, she cannot stand at all and she is crying from the pain, we are very afraid for her.
narrative_ewe: Meɖekuku, nɔvi nyɔnu sɔ gbɔ nyem, Gifty, wò nyɔnu agbenyaga — akɔ ɖeka dzo wu eɖe Tarkwa, ɖe hafi ɖe ŋɔ le xɔ mitaɖe egɔme. Eyi eɖe akɔ wu wo nyuitɔ hã o — afɔ ɖeka ƒe ɣleti vɔ, eye dzi dada le etsotso. Nyem ɖe Kwabena, meyɔ ŋkui. Meɖekuku, wò mate ŋu ƒu ɖe agɔ vi o, eye eyi nam eyata ele ŋugbɔ le. Miaɖe susu wu eɖe wu nyuitɔ — kpe ɖe ŋumiwo! Frɛ ambulance trɔ!
narrative_ga: Hɛloo! Boa mi! Boa mi! Grandma wɔ, Gifty, kaa bumo ni Tarkwa, hɛɛ fie wɔ shishi. Ogbɔmɔ ni, na kaa bumo ni ehii ni toobi — gboo wɔ breakmo na shwe etsotso pɛɛpɛɛ, too many. Mii nyɛ Kwabena, omi grandsɔŋ. Ojekoo, enyemi okɔ o — otsitsimi na ehii ni ewiemɔ shi, yɛ sɔɔ ni toobi. Ofainɛ, frɛ ambulance yi shi! Kɛ bo mii!
narrative_dagbani: M paɣa kpɛma, Gifty, kaɣa daɣa o Tarkwa ni, ti yili saɣim. O kpɛma daa, ka kaɣa daɣa o yaga yaga — o tiŋ gbɛɣɛ, ka ziim bee o paɣari. Miri Kwabena, m n'o dobihi. N daa, o zaŋ ziŋ daa kuri, ka o bɛri mi ka o goliɣa, ti bɛ miŋ daa o zaɣa.
narrative_fante: Me nana, Gifty, kar bi bɔɔ no fam fa Tarkwa ha, yɛn fie nkyɛn. Ɔyɛ opanyin, na kar no bɔɔ no yiye paa — ne nan abotɔ na mogya pii retu. Me na me yɛ Kwabena, meyɛ ne nua ba. Me pa wo kyew, ɔntumi ngyina koraa na ɔsu afi yaw no ho, yɛn suro yiye ne mu.
narrative_gurene: Yinɛ! M maŋa, Gifty, kaɣa zɔ o wʋsɩ Tarkwa, be ti yir nɛ. O yɛ nindaan, a kaɣa zɔ o yɛligu yɛligu — o nɩŋa kpaaŋ, a ziim bee o boŋa. Mɩ yɛ Kwabena, m yɛ o doŋ bia. N tɩɩma, o tɩɩ nɩŋ yeli o bɔɔrɩ, a yɛligu ka o yiieri, ti sɩɣɩm o yɛla yɛligu. Sɔŋ mam! Sɔŋ mam!

narrative_en: Please, I'm calling because my neighbor Alhassan was involved in a road accident today. He was coming back to the house here in Darkuman when an OA taxi clipped him, but thank God it was not too serious. His arm is paining him small and there is a cut there, but he is walking fine and he is conscious. We are in his room now with him, so I, Elinam, just wanted to make sure someone knows what happened in case he needs to go to the clinic.
narrative_ewe: Meɖekuku, mefre mo afi a afisia ɖe sida si Alhassan, ameɖoviwo me, wo dome wò, ewo accident ɖe ʋɔ dzi egbe. Etrɔ le fie fifia Darkuman me ɣlɔ be OA taxi ɖeka kpɔ eʋe dzi, gake Mawu sɔ ŋu, nyuie kaba — nudzadzra aɖeke sɔ wu o. Asie yi nam xo eʋe, eye kota aɖeke le afisia gome, gake elabla nyo nyo, eye eɖo edzi. Miwo fifia ne eme tso ne ŋutɔ. Enti nye Elinam, mewɔ be ame aɖeke ni nya nudzadzra si wɔa, ne eve be wòde hospital anyi le emegbe.
narrative_ga: Ofainɛ, mɛ frɛ ɛnɛ ɛfɛ sɛ mi manɔ Alhassan, accident buɛ ni kpee dzɛ ɛnɛ. Ɔ lɛ ɔ gbɔ shi Darkuman ha kɛ OA taxi buɛ ni gbɔ ŋ kakra — Onyame dɔŋ, ɛyɛ no serious ama. Gbɔ ŋ ehii ni ŋ kakra, kɛ cut bi le ɛha, shikpon ɔ tsɔɔ le kɛ ɔ tsa tsa yɛ no kɛ ɔni le. Miiŋ nɔɔ le ɔ room ŋ kɛ ŋ. Ofainɛ, mɛ — Elinam — mɛ bii sɛ mɛ nyɛ nɔ ehiɛ nyɛ dɛŋ obuɛ ni, sɛ ɔfɛ ɔ kɛ clinic ha.
narrative_dagbani: N daa, m frɛ yɛ ni bɔ m dagbanla Alhassan gbɛra accident nyɛŋ zuɣu zamaŋa. O naa o yili Darkuman, ka OA taxi zɔŋ o, amaa Naa Puɣu ti sɔŋ, ka a yɛli o yɛla mi laɣim daa. O nuu yɛligu o kani, ka kpɛm bee o zooi, amaa o yi maa mi laɣim ka o nyɛ o maa yɛla. Ti be o yili zuɣu tire, n maa, Elinam, n bori naa ŋun bi yɛ o yɛla, ti bɔri maŋ ka ziŋ, a bɛ yɛ o ni o yi gba nyɛla yili.
narrative_fante: Me pa wo kyew, merɛfrɛ ɛfiri sɛ me hɔhoɔ Alhassan na accident ba ne so wɔ ɔkwan so ɛnnɛ. Ɔrɛsan abaa efie wɔ Darkuman ha na OA taxi bi bɔɔ no kakra, nanso Nyame da ase, ɛnyɛ den paa. Ne nsa yɛ no yaw kakra na kra bi wɔ hɔ, nanso ɔnantew yiye na ɔnim ne ho. Yɛwɔ ne room mu ne no seesei, ɛno nti me, Elinam, mepɛɛ sɛ mahu sɛ obi nim deɛ asi, sɛdeɛ ɛbɛyɛ a ɔhia kɔ clinic no.
narrative_gurene: Please, I'm calling because my neighbor Alhassan was involved in a road accident today. He was coming back to the house here in Darkuman when an OA taxi clipped him, but thank God it was not too serious. His arm is paining him small and there is a cut there, but he is walking fine and he is conscious. We are in his room now with him, so I, Elinam, just wanted to make sure someone knows what happened in case he needs to go to the clinic.

narrative_en: My name is Ramatu, and I'm calling because something happened to a young girl, Adjoa, near Techiman on the Kumasi-Sunyani road. There is a small stream by the roadside there, and she somehow fell in and went under the water for a short time before people nearby pulled her out quickly. She is breathing fine now, thank God, but her neck is paining her a little and she said her head feels heavy. It was not too serious, she is sitting up and talking, but we are worried so we wanted to report it and ask if she needs to see a doctor.
narrative_ewe:
Allo, míaƒo woezɔ. Ŋkɔnyé enye Ramatu. Mefrɛ ɛ abe akɔ ɖeka si wòwa na ɖevi nyɔnu aɖe, Adjoa, atsoe. Eƒe dzɔdzɔliƒe le Techiman ŋutɔ koe, Kumasi-Sunyani ʋɔ dzi.

Le ʋɔ dzɔ ɖoɖo la, tsi agbagba aɖe le esia. Adjoa wua ƒu tsi aɖe eye wòyɔ ɖo tsi me akpa aɖe ko. Gbɔ gã o — ame siwo le esia wo zi, wokuiwo wòtsɔ eya ŋu le tsi me kaba kaba.

Fifia, edzi ƒu ame akɔ akɔ — Mawu na ŋkɔ. Gake eyi ŋutɔ ne le eƒe ɖo dzi, eye eɖo be eƒe ta nyame lolo. Eti o tso wo ŋu ɖa ɖa — edzo eye ebu gblɔgblɔ le, gake midzro ŋutɔ, ene ame mede ŋu gblɔ be eti ɖe dɔkota ɖa o, alo ɖevi la ɖi wɔ xɔse akɔ.

Meɖekuku, gbɔ nami be mianye wɔ tso fifia? Dɔkota aɖe le gã o, alo ŋdi be miaƒle Adjoa yi asiwo la?
narrative_ga: Hɛloo, mitsɔ Ramatu, mi frɛ frɛ afi kɛ bɔɔ bi etsɔ Adjoa, abɔ Techiman, Kumasi-Sunyani road kɛ shi. Kpee nɔɔ shi, asɔ bi le, na obumo atsɔ ni ji — oye ji naɔ shi ye tsɔɔ, afi kɛ nudɔɔ lɛ bɔ shi kɛ kɔ ohe amɛ gbɛiŋ ye le. Oyiwala Nyɔŋmɔ, oniɔmɔ le kɛ jooji — onyemi le, eni hɔmɔ jooji. Ɛba, ŋmɛi ehii no kɛ susu, na ni fai bi ehii no. Enye awɔ tɛ mli, oyimi le kɛ kasa kɛ shishi — ɛba, miye hewalɔ, chale, na mibɔɔ frɛ frɛ kɛ shiakɔ: otsɔ doctor bi hia?
narrative_dagbani: Allo, m yuɣu Ramatu. N frɛ ka bɛ yɛla paɣa biɛla yɔɣu, o yuɣu Adjoa, Techiman saɣim, Kumasi-Sunyani nyɛŋ doo. Nyɛŋ doo bee kpiŋ biɛla, ka Adjoa gbini o lɔɣu, ka o kɛŋ pii lɔɣim chɛɛ tɔɣili. O be doo nima paa o yibu ziŋ — o yibu n be kali. A puŋŋa Naa, o simi bee kali ziŋ, di maa ka di kpɛm. Amma o koriya yɛligu o biɛla, ka o yɛ o ŋun nyɛma dee. Di maa paa kpɛm yɛligu, o gbini ka o maa kuli o yiri, ka o daa yɛligu. Amma ti bɛri mi, ti jɛ ka n bɛ yɛ ni — dɔɣite ŋun bori la o naɣa a? O mali noya ka o nyɛ dɔɣite, wula ka dɔɣite nyɛ o.
narrative_fante: Me din de Ramatu, na mefrɛ wo efisɛ biribi asi abofra baa bi, Adjoa, ho — ɛbɛn Techiman, wɔ Kumasi-Sunyani road no so. Ɛhɔ na asu ketewa bi wɔ ɔkwan no nkyɛn, na ɛnte sɛn, Adjoa tuu fam kohwe asu no mu, na ɔkɔe ase nkɛkɛ kakra ansa na nnipa a wɔwɔ ɛhɔ tow no mfiri mu ntɛm ntɛm. Saa ara na ɔhome yiye nnɛ, Nyame adom, nanso ɔkɔn no yɛ no yaw kakra, na ɔse ne tiri yɛ duru. Ɛnyɛ den paa — ɔtena ase na ɔkasa, nanso yɛsuro kakra, enti yehia se yɛbɛka ama wo na yɛbisa sɛ, ɔhia kɔ hu oduruyɛfo anaa?
narrative_gurene: M yʋʋr la Ramatu, mam boe foŋ bɩ bia bi la yɛligu a zu. Bia no yʋʋr la Adjoa, o bee Techiman, Kumasi-Sunyani nyɛŋ zi. Nyɛŋ no zi, ziŋ bi la bee be, la Adjoa kpɛŋ fam ziŋ no pʋɔ, o kɔɔrɩ ziŋ pʋɔ ka bɩ bilim. Neba kabi bee a nyɛ be sɔŋ o yita zɔɔŋ. Yinɛ a zuɣu, o mɛŋ niŋ nyɛya la yɛlim — o nifi bee a wa. Ama o yɛligu dee o niiŋ pʋɔ bilim, la o tẽe t'a sũŋ o zuɣu wʋ nyɔɔri. Ka yɛligu a ga nyɔɔra mɔ, o togsɩ tɩ bee o yẽra, ama tɩ nyɛ wʋ bɩ bʋɔra tɩ ga. Tɩ dat'a nyɛ, d'a nyɛ la o mɩ ka doctor.

narrative_en: Please, something happened on the Tamale-Bolgatanga road, around the Walewale area. A young boy, Agyeman, was attacked by some people — they beat him badly on his head and neck. I am Amankwah, I was passing when I saw it happen, so I stopped to help him. He is conscious and talking small small, but the neck side is swelling up and he looks like he is in a lot of pain. We need someone to come or tell us where to take him fast.
narrative_ewe: Meɖekuku, nudzadzra aɖe wɔ ɖe Tamale-Bolgatanga ʋɔ dzi, ɖe Walewale ƒuƒo. Ɖevi ŋutsu aɖe, Agyeman, amesiwo aɖewo zã asi ɖe eŋu — wotsɔ wo asi ƒe tã ɖe etame ɖe ta kple ɖo dzi le nudzadzra sia me. Máwɔ ame dzoƒe ŋkeke ŋkeke. Nye ɖe Amankwah, meƒo ʋɔ ɖe hã gake mekpɔ ade afi sia, elabena nya sia wɔ le nyeme — màgblɔ be mawu na eya ɖo. Eɖo edzi hã, ele ɖo kple ame le xexe si ko si, gake ɖo ŋɔ fã ɖe eŋu xɔxɔ le, eye enézí be eyi nam le gã me. Meɖekuku, kpe ame ɖe ŋumɛ dzi abe miakɔ nami ɔ, alo gblɔ miaɖe afi miaxɔ eya ɖe asiwo abe nuwòwò nyuie ɔ ɣeɣe.
narrative_ga: Ofainɛ, something happen for Tamale-Bolgatanga road, around Walewale area. Dɛbi bi, Agyeman, nitsɛ ŋmɛi kɛ nkɔŋ — obiaa lɛ sane bubu ŋ wɔ. Mii, Amankwah, mibɔ kpee lɛ, minyɛ ehii ŋ naanɔ mi da eha. Oni le kɛ okasa kakra kakra, kɛ nkɔŋ fɛɛ lɛ shi shi fee, kɛ ehii ni hii ŋ wɔ yɛ. Chale, yɛhiɛ obiaa bi obi wa — boabi oshishi mia ta ŋ lɛ kɛ speed.
narrative_dagbani: N daa, thing paɣa Tamale-Bolgatanga nyɛŋ zuɣu, Walewale saɣim. Sobiɛla yini, Agyeman, dima niba duɣɔ o — ba gbani o ŋun ni o kpɔɣɔ zuɣu ka o paɣa viɛla. N yɛla Amankwah, n bɛŋ gɔli nyɛŋ ka n nii o ka n zani n sɔŋ o. O ti kuli ka o gollo timma timma, ama o kpɔɣɔ saɣim bee yuma ka o nyɛ o ka yɛligu daa kuli. Ti bori niba di naa kuli, ka ba di ti sɔŋ, bɑ yi maa daa ti ni ti n kara ti n yi ka ti toli o nyɛla yili ka tɔɣim.
narrative_fante: Me pa wo kyew, biribi asi wɔ Tamale-Bolgatanga ɔkwan so, kosi Walewale ho. Abofra koro, Agyeman, nnipa bi tow no — wɔbɔɔ no yaw yiye wɔ ne tiri ne ne kɔn so. Me na me yɛ Amankwah — na meretra ɔkwan so na mhunuu saa asɛm yi, enti megyinaa boa no. Ɔda hɔ na ɔrekasa kakra kakra, nanso ne kɔn fa no ahoahoa sɛɛ, na eye da adi sɛ ɔyaw no yiye. Yehia obi a ɔnba aba, anaasɛ megye wo kyew, kyerɛ yɛn baabi a yɛde no kɔ ntɛm ntɛm.
narrative_gurene: N tɩɩma, sʋka a kɛ Tamale-Bolgatanga nyɛŋ zug, Walewale nɛ. Biig a yelle Agyeman, nɩbɛ bee o — ba gɔri o yɛl o ŋun ne o noŋ yɛl zɔɣɔm. Mam yelle Amankwah, m bɛ nyɛŋ zug la m naa a, a kɛ m kpi la m sɔŋ o. O ko nyɛ la, o gom bɩtɩ bɩtɩ, la o noŋ nɛ a yɛŋ yɛŋ la a yɛligu ka yɛligu o yɛl yɛl. Ti lɛb nɩ nibi a wa, boa ti ta nɛ ti kɛ o yɛla yir ziiri. N tɩɩma, sɔŋ mam!

narrative_en: Please, something very bad has happened to a young boy here. My name is Afia, and I'm calling about a child called Adjei — he was working and a machine caught his arm, it tore it very badly, there is a lot of blood. We are on the Axim road near Half Assini. The boy is in a lot of pain and we are afraid, please send help quickly because the arm looks very, very serious.
narrative_ewe: Meɖekuku, nuwo bubu aɖe wɔ ɖevi ŋutsu aɖe afisia. Ŋkɔnya enye Afia, eye mele frɛ la nu ɖevi si wogblɔ na eɖe ŋkɔ be Adjei — ele dɔwɔwɔ me eye masini aɖe xɔ eƒe asi, ekpui egblẽ wu ɖe akpa geɖe, eye dzi geɖe le etsotso. Míle Axim ʋɔ dzi kpakple Half Assini hansã. Ɖevi la la eyi nam wu ɖe akpa geɖe, eye egblɔ mía ŋu, meɖekuku, to kpɔ ŋumía zi zi zi, elabena eƒe asi la trɔ hã kpo kpo kpo.
narrative_ga: Ofainɛ, bɔɔ kɛ ni nyɔŋ wɔ oshɔ ko ye oblɛ bi ni ohe. Miŋ dzɛŋ nyɛ Afia, mifiɛ dɛbi ko ŋ dzɛŋ nyɛ Adjei — ɔhɛ akɔ wɔ, machine bi kɛ ɔgbɔ, egbi sɛ sɛ, shwe etsotso pii pii. Mɛhɛ Axim road, Half Assini kɛ. Oblɛ bi ehii ɔgbɔ noŋŋ sɛŋ, mɛshɛ hu — ofainɛ, frɛ ambulance mli mli, kakra o, because ɔgbɔ bi eba sɛ sɛ, ehii sɛŋ.
narrative_dagbani: N daa, bɛ ti nyɛ yɛltɔɣa ziɣibu yɔŋ ka wuni din. M yuli naa Afia, ka m bɛ yɛ ni bia din bi o Adjei yɔliŋ — o bɛ tuma ka mashini kpɛŋ o nuu, o nuu yɛŋ yibu ka ziim ɣeya paai. Ti bɛ Axim nyɛŋ ni, Half Assini daana. Biɛla ka o nuu yɛligu daani ka ti sɔŋ. N daa, yi frɛ sɔŋ yi ti dini yinli, bala o nuu nyɛ yɛltɔɣa ziɣibu daani.
narrative_fante: Me pa wo kyew, bɔne kɛseɛ bi atoa abofra kumaa bi ha. Me din de Afia, na merɛfrɛ fa abofra bi ho — wɔfrɛ no Adjei. Ɔreyɛ adwuma na machine bi kyeree ne nsa, na ɛtew ne nsa no yiye yiye paa — mogya pii retu. Yɛwɔ Axim road so, Ebeano Half Assini. Abofra no yaw reyɛ no yiye yiye, na yɛsuro — me pa wo kyew, msoma mmoa bi ntɛm, efisɛ ne nsa no yɛ hu paa, paa paa.
narrative_gurene: N tɩɩma, bʋɣʋ bi-yɛri ka a taa la waa yɛ. M yʋʋr la Afia, ka m bee frɛ ka bia yʋʋr la Adjei — o bee tiim o tʋʋma ka masɩn yɛŋ a o nɔŋ, ka a taŋ a taŋ n bɔ o nɔŋ yɛri, ka ziim ɣeya pam. Ti bee Axim road zuɣu, la Half Assini nɩŋ. Bia yɛligu ka yɛligu pam, ka ti zɔɣɔ, n tɩɩma yi mam daabiisi ka a toaɣa toaɣa bala, bala — bala o nɔŋ la a yɛri ka yɛri yɛri pam.

narrative_en: My son Agyeman was working at a construction site here in Tumu when something heavy fell on him and hit him in the chest and stomach area. He is a young boy, maybe nineteen years, and he is in pain but he is still conscious and talking. I am Patience, his mother, and I was called by his coworkers when it happened. He is saying his chest is hurting him very badly when he breathes but he does not have any cuts that are bleeding too much. We need someone to come and help or tell us what to do because we are not sure if something is broken inside.
narrative_ewe:
Allo! Meɖekuku, kpe ɖe ŋunye! Nɔvi nyem Agyeman le construction site dzi afisia Tumu, eye nuɖuɖu gã aɖe wuu edo ƒu ale ɖe eƒe ɖevi ŋɔ kple eƒe abɔ dzi. Eya ɖevi wòle, eme ƒe ewoɖeka la gbɔ, eye eyi nam vevie — gake eɖo edzi kpoo, ale kple ame gome yewoƒo ƒonɔ. Nyem ɖe Patience, eƒe nɔ, eye ekeme ŋutiɖoɖo wòɖoam le nusɔsɔ la kple eƒe ablɔɖa ame siwo le construction la ŋu.

Eɖe agbe le eƒe ɖevi ŋɔ la me vevie kpoo be eye edzi ƒu agbe ɖeka ɖeka — gake dzi dɔ o, nudzikpɔ ɖekae le abe vɔ hafi o. Gake mele dzidzɔm abe ɣleti aɖe vɔ eƒe nu me o — agake mate ŋu se o. Meɖekuku, frɛ ambulance, aleke woaɖo ablɔɖa o, aleke woaɖe kpɔ ŋuwò — esia ƒa agbɔm gem! Woaƒo ŋkeke ko le Tumu afisia construction site dzi.
narrative_ga: Hɛloo, ofainɛ boa mi — dɛbi bi, Agyeman, ɔyɛ work construction site Tumu hɛ, nɛ something heavy bumo no ni kɛ ehii ni gbɔɔ kɛ shi. Oblɛ bi ye, ɔha mɔ afe ahe ye mli — mɔ mɔ nineteen years — ɛni ehii no kɛɛ, kɛ ɔni le, ɔkɛ yɛ aŋmɛɔ. Mii nyɛ Patience, ni ni — coworkers no frɛ mi ni etsɔɔ. Ɔkɛ bɔɔ ni ni gbɔɔ ehii no kɛɛ kɛɛ shi ɔhɔmɔ mli, kɛ shwe etsotso o — cuts ye o, kɛ shwe nii o. Kɛ chale, mitsɛ ni sth broken le ɔ mli o, mitsɛ o. Ofainɛ frɛ ambulance maa shi hɛ, abi bɔ mɔ yɛ shiaŋ — mitsɛ yɛ ɔ mɔŋ o. Boa mi, ofainɛ!
