narrative_dagbani: N dee, m biɛla Agyeman, o bɛ Tumu construction site ka o bi kuli bee o pam ni o digindi shibu. O bia la, o pia biɛla ni awɛi pia wula, ka o bɛri mi daa, n'o ŋmali kuli ka o gbali. N toɣa Patience, m bɛ o naanli. O yɔŋ shɛli maa n kaa mi, ka o bi shɛlibu daa ka ziim ɣeya. Amma bia o digindi ka yɛligu daa daa naa o nyɛŋ maa o pam yɛligu — ka o yɔŋ da o digindi ka o nyɛ ka yɛligu biɛla. N bori la yamba ka o yi n ti sɔŋ ma, wala ka o ni dali mi ka n nyɛ ka bɔri kuli n maa, bojina n yɛli, ka n nyɛ kuli o nyɛ bia tim o nyɛ kpalli. N daa, yi maa sɔŋ!
narrative_fante:
Me ba Agyeman, ɔrekyerɛ adwuma wɔ construction site bi ha Tumu, na biribi duru bi tuu fam guu no so — ehuruu no wɔ n'akyinkyinna bruwa ne yafunu mu. Ɔyɛ abofra kɔraaa, ɔkɔ ɛha nkɛ ɔha mmienu mmienu anaa — na ɛyɛ no yaw, nanso ɔte hɔ na ɔrekasa. Me na me ne Patience, maame no, na n'adwumayɛfoɔ frɛɛ me saa berɛ a na ɛbaa so no.

Ɔreka se n'bruwa yɛ no yaw papa papa — ma ɔhome biara a ɔhome no, ɛyɛ no yaw kɔraaa. Nanso mogya retu no dodo no — ɔwɔ mpaemu bi kakra, nanso mogya retu no dodo biara nni hɔ.

Me pa wo kyew, yehia obi a ɔbɛba boa yɛn, anaa ɔbɛka yɛn se yɛnyɛ dɛn, efisɛ yɛnnim yiye sɛ biribi twitwaee n'inside anaa ɛnyɛ. Frɛ ambulance anaa oduruyɛfo bi mmra ha ntɛm ntɛm!
narrative_gurene: My son Agyeman was working at a construction site here in Tumu when something heavy fell on him and hit him in the chest and stomach area. He is a young boy, maybe nineteen years, and he is in pain but he is still conscious and talking. I am Patience, his mother, and I was called by his coworkers when it happened. He is saying his chest is hurting him very badly when he breathes but he does not have any cuts that are bleeding too much. We need someone to come and help or tell us what to do because we are not sure if something is broken inside.

narrative_en: Please, I want to report something that happened to my younger brother. His name is Kwame, he's about nineteen years old, and he was working on a construction site somewhere along the Accra-Cape Coast road near Winneba when something fell on his arm. He is saying it's paining him but he can still move it small small, so we don't think it's too serious. I am Ofori, and I just want to know if he needs to go to a clinic or if we can manage it at home.
narrative_ewe: Meɖekuku, meɖi ɖe woŋu bɔbɔ si wɔ ɖe ŋunye dɔ̃ dɔwɔwɔ ŋu. Ŋɔŋlɔ nye dɔ̃ ƒe ŋkɔ nye Kwame, eƒe ƒe le ƒuwo ewo gome, eye eƒe dɔwɔwɔ ƒome le Accra-Cape Coast ʋɔ dzi keke Winneba gbɔ. Nu ɖeka do ƒu eƒe asi ɖe dɔwɔwɔ ƒome ɔ. Kwame be eyi eƒe asi nam, gake etɔ ŋu tutum agɔ asiwo kpem, míeɖi ŋu be ena nyui ŋutɔ nyui. Ŋkɔ nyem nye Ofori. Meɖi se be ele anyim nɔ clinic, ke mevɔ ame be míatɔ ŋu dɔ ŋkume le ŋɔtigbɔ ŋutɔm?
narrative_ga: Ofainɛ, mɛ bii shikpon kɛ wiemɔ bi eko nɔ mi nyɛ Kwame. Kwame, oha ashi kɛ afe dukonɔ nɔ — afe nineteen — eye oshi wiemo kɛ construction site bi, Accra-Cape Coast road fɛɛ, Winneba kɛ. Oshie le eŋɛ, somethin bumo kɛ ŋ gbɔ. Ni hii ŋ, ni ebi, — gbɔ hii ŋ — kɛ obi yɛ move ŋ small small, so yɛ mli he suɔ ke ni serious ni kɛ. Mii yɛ Ofori. Mɛ hɔɔ ni mli shwane — ebi ni Kwame man kɔ clinic, anaabi yɛ tumi manage ŋ kɛ fie?
narrative_dagbani: N daa, n bɛ yɛ ni m yɛl'a shɛli ka o kpɛŋ n doo biɛla. O yuli di Kwame, o wuhi kuli pia awɛi — kuli pia ni awɛi — ka o bɛ construction site zuɣu Accra-Cape Coast nyɛŋ daa, Winneba saɣim, ka shɛli gbini o nuu zuɣu. O yɛli ka a yɛligu ka o nuu din, n'o nuu tuma la biŋ biŋ naa — o nuu be wula la, ka ti nya ka o nuu di ka ti gaa o daa. Ka ti gaa, ka ti bori n bani ka ti yɛl'a shɛli — ka ti mi ka o yiɣi clinic kana o boro hospital, kana ti tol o doli yili. N yuli di Ofori. N daa, sɔŋ mi — yɛl'a shɛli a ŋmani m bori la bani.
narrative_fante: Me pa wo kyew, mepɛ sɛ meka bɔne bi a asi me nua ketewa. Ɔdin de Kwame, ɔwɔ afeɛ du nkron bɛyɛ, na ɔrefidie construction site bi so wɔ Accra-Cape Coast road no ho, Winneba nkyɛn, bere a biribi tuu ɔkɔɔ n'nsa so. Ɔreka sɛ eye no yaw, nanso ɔtumi yi n'nsa kakra kakra, enti yennhunu sɛ ɛyɛ den papa. Me din de Ofori, na mepɛ sɛ menim sɛ ɔhia kɔ clinic anaa yɛbɛtumi manageɛ no fie.
narrative_gurene: N tɩɩma, m bɛ yɛla fɔɔ bi la o niŋɩ a nɩ. O yɔri Kwame, o muri bɛ somo ayɩ-nɔɔ, ka o bɛ zɔŋ construction site baŋa Accra-Cape Coast nyɛŋ poore Winneba nɔɔ, ka bɛ yɛla o nɩ zaaŋa wɔɔ bɔɔrɩ o nɔɔŋa zugu. O bɛ yɛra ka o nɔɔŋa yɛligu, la o tara be o nɔɔŋa nyirim-nyirim, ka tɩ bɛ bɛri a sɔŋa yɛligu be zɔɣɔ dɔɔɔ. Mam yɔri Ofori, ka m bɛ bɛlɩ yɛra a fo n sɔŋ mam nyɛ — a Kwame tara be naa clinic yɩrɛ, bɩ tɩ tara tara be o yir ade?

narrative_en: Please, I want to report something that happened here in Tumu. My neighbor Nii fell down inside the compound — he slipped and hit his head on the ground. He is okay, he is sitting up and talking, but there is a small cut on his neck area and he is complaining of some pain there. I am Ibrahim, I live just next door, and I came to check on him when I heard the noise. We don't think it is too serious, but we want someone to advise us on what to do next.
narrative_ewe:
Meɖekuku, meɖe kpɔ nudzi siwo wòwɔ afisia Tumu. Agbɔŋutsu siwo ƒe ŋɔŋlɔ, Nii, edo ƒu le afɔme la — eɖi ɣlɔ͂ be ewu ƒu, eye eta dze ƒu ŋu anyigba. Eɖo edzi la — eɖo enu le dzi eye egblɔ gɔme, gake le eƒe ɖo dzi la, gaga bɔbɔ le esia. Eye eyi nam ɖo gome ɖeka la.

Nɔme nye Ibrahim. Me nɔ eƒe ŋɔŋlɔ nyuie. Megbɔ bɔbɔ kplɔ ŋutɔ le ekpɔ eƒe nu esi woè tsi nu wu de ame ɖe kpɔ. Míele be akɔnuɖeɖe aɖeke o, gake míeɖi nɔvi aɖeke nana mí susu be míaƒo nu wò ɖe ŋgɔ la afi.
narrative_ga: Ofainɛ, mifɛ report biaa shi oshie Tumu ha. Neighbor mi Nii ebumo shi compound — eslip ebumo ŋmɛi shi ground. Oni le, oshitsɔ kɛ otsɛ, kɛ cut keteke bi le ŋ ni gboo-ŋmɛi area kɛ ehii ni le ha. Mii Ibrahim, mibɛ straight next door, kɛ mibaa check ŋ shikpon mi wuliɔ noise oo. Yɛmiŋ le sɛ eni serious koraa, kɛ yɛfɛ oshie sɛ onuɔ bi advise yɛ sɛ yɛnyɛ adeɛ ŋ naaa.
narrative_dagbani: N daa, n bɛ yɛ ni kɔɣim din mɛ ka nyɛ ka Tumu. N yɛltɔɣa Nii o gbini zuɣu ni yili kpɛŋdi — o tiŋ zuɣu ka kpɛŋ ŋun, ka o ŋun nyɛ ŋun daa zuɣu. O nyɛ ni kpeŋ ŋun mɔɣa, o chɛŋdi ka zaɣa, ka o yɛldi biɛla, ama o nyɛ pɔɣili biɛla o kpɔŋ ka yɛligu o daa wuhim maa wuhim. O yɛldi ka yɛligu bi daa. N yuli Ibrahim, n chɛŋdi bi daa o kpɛŋdi, ka n kɔŋ sinli o tooi ni n gbɛyɛ la din wuhim maa. Ti bi yɛli ka o nyɛ yɛli kpɛm, ama ti bori la ni ŋun tooi sɔŋ ti yɛl ti la din tooi ni ti maa kpɛm.
narrative_fante: Me pa wo kyew, mepɛ sɛ meka biribi a asi ha wɔ Tumu. Me yonko Nii tuu fam wɔ compound no mu — ɔsree na ɔbɔɔ ne tiri wɔ fam no so. Ɔho yɛ, ɔtee ase na ɔreka asɛm, nanso wɔ cut ketewa bi wɔ ne kɔn ho na ɔreka sɛ ɛyɛ no yaw wɔ hɔ. Me din de Ibrahim, mete baabi a ɛbɛn no ara, na mebaa sɛ menhwɛ no a mietee noise no. Yennwene sɛ ɛyɛ ɔhaw kɛse, nanso yepɛ sɛ obi bɛkyere yɛn ɔkwan a yɛbɛfa so wɔ akyiri yi.
narrative_gurene: Please, I want to report something that happened here in Tumu. My neighbor Nii fell down inside the compound — he slipped and hit his head on the ground. He is okay, he is sitting up and talking, but there is a small cut on his neck area and he is complaining of some pain there. I am Ibrahim, I live just next door, and I came to check on him when I heard the noise. We don't think it is too serious, but we want someone to advise us on what to do next.

narrative_en: My name is Sulemana, I am calling to report about my neighbor, an old man called Baffour. He was involved in a car accident not too far from where we live here in Asokwa, and when they brought him back home he was holding his chest and side area. It doesn't look too serious, no bleeding or anything like that, but he is feeling some pain around his stomach and ribs. He is elderly so we just want to make sure he is okay.
narrative_ewe:
Allo, nyeŋu nye Sulemana. Mefrɛ ɖe wo ŋu abe nudɔwɔla aɖe si wòwɔ ene. Ɖevi-nyɔ aɖe si wòɖe nyem, Baffour — agbenyaga ɖeka — ekpɔ accident ɖe ƒe vavã o le Asokwa afã si míenɔ ŋu. Esi wòtsɔa eʋa ɣlɔ le ŋɔnuwo ŋu la, eɖo asi le eƒe ɖevi ŋɔ kple eƒe ɖoƒe dzidzime la ŋu.

Nudzadzra la mele vavã ŋu o abe mìsee — dzi ŋɔliŋoli mele abe nuawo bubuwo ɖe egbo o — gake eyi eŋu le eƒe abɔ kple eƒe ɣleti-ɣleti si le ɖevi ŋɔ gbɔ la teƒe. Abe enya agbenyaga la, míewɔ be mínéŋlɔ be elea nòŋu, eye míédi be emegble o.

Meɖekuku, kpe ɖe ŋumí — ambulance aɖe nava kpɔ eʋe.
narrative_ga: Hɛloo, mi din bo Sulemana. Mifra frɛ shɛ mi neighbor, gbɔmɔ bi wɔ din bo Baffour. Accident ya ne ŋ kaa bi, ehɛ Asokwa kɛ shi yɛ ni hɛɛ. Wɔ ŋmaane ne ŋ fie, ŋ gbɔ dɔŋ ŋ gbɔɔ kɛ ŋ faa. Enimɔ serious paaa o — shwe etsotso o, eŋ naakɛ baabi le — kɛ ŋ shi kɛ ŋ rib baabi ehii ne ŋ. Gbɔmɔ ni ŋ, chale, yɛ pɛ shɛ yɛ nyɛ sure shɛ ŋ le fine.
narrative_dagbani: Allo, m yuli di Sulemana. N frɛ ka n sɔŋ yɛligu n dobihi din bee m beloŋ, nindaan o yuli di Baffour. O kpɛŋ accident nyɛŋ din bɛ Asokwa, n tooi kaa dani n bɛ dini ti ti yili kama. Ka bi kpɛ o suŋ, o nuu bee o digindi ni o tibu kɔŋ. Ka yɛligu nin bɛ paai tɔŋ — o ziim ɣeya kaa, ka bɛ ŋun yɛligu kaa — ama o pam ni o tibu bɛri o yɛligu daa. O kpɛma la, dimiɣu suɣulo ka ti bori shɛli n daa n nyɛ o bɛ sali.
narrative_fante: Me din de Sulemana. Mefrɛ wo de maka wo ho fa me yɛnko, opanyin bi a wɔfrɛ no Baffour. Ɔkɔɛ accident mu wɔ Asokwa ha — ɛnyɛ akyiri papa na ɛkaa. Bere a wɔde no baa efie no, ɔkitaa ne bruwa ne ne sisi. Ɛnyɛ sum papa — mogya retu no anaa biribi te saa — nanso ɛyɛ no yaw wɔ ne yafunu ne ne nkakragyemma ho. Ɔyɛ opanyin, enti yɛpɛ sɛ yɛhunu sɛ ne ho yɛ.
narrative_gurene: My name is Sulemana, I am calling to report about my neighbor, an old man called Baffour. He was involved in a car accident not too far from where we live here in Asokwa, and when they brought him back home he was holding his chest and side area. It doesn't look too serious, no bleeding or anything like that, but he is feeling some pain around his stomach and ribs. He is elderly so we just want to make sure he is okay.

narrative_en: My name is Akua, I'm calling because something bad happened to a young girl here. She is called Afia, she is maybe around fifteen or sixteen years old, and she got burned — it happened on her neck and around her head area. We are on the Bolgatanga-Bawku road, close to Navrongo side. The burn is not small at all, the skin is looking very bad there, she is in pain and crying, but she is still awake and talking. Please we need someone to come and help her quickly.
narrative_ewe:
Allo, yeyeyeye... Ŋkɔnya enye Akua. Mefrɛ mi elabena nudzadzra bubu aɖe wɔ ɖevi nyɔnu aɖe afisia. Ŋkɔe enye Afia, eɖo ƒe ewo ɖeka alo ewo eve la gbó, eye tsiɛ wɔ ɖe eŋu — ewɔ ɖe eƒe ɖo kple eƒe ta ŋɔ. Míle Bolgatanga-Bawku ʋɔ dzi, kpó Navrongo fé gbó.

Tsi la kpɔ dzɔ o — eƒe ŋɔ vɔ dada, enézim kpɔ ŋu nufafa o, eye eyi ŋue nam. Eyi ŋue nam míagbe, esi le agbe, ele nuyɔyɔ, eye eɖo edzi — gake eyi ŋue nam titi.

Meɖekuku, kpe ame bubu ɖe míŋu akpó eŋu zi geɖe. Frɛ ambulance miane... ame ɖevi la hia kpɔ ŋuwo zi geɖe!
narrative_ga: Hɛloo, mii nyɛ Akua. Mifrɛ because something bad happen dɛbi baaba fɛ ha. Oyi wɔ Afia, ehe mli years fifteen aba sixteen — fire burn no gɔ ŋ, ni happen ŋmɛi mli kɛ neck area. Yɛ fɛ Bolgatanga-Bawku road, close Navrongo side. Burn no ye kɛɛ ooo — skin no ye bɔbɔi fɛ he, ehii ŋ, naa otsu tsu, kɛ ehii mi ooo — kɛ ŋ oni le, oshɛ kɛ yɛ. Ojekoo, yɛ hia someone ni bi kɛ boa ŋ wɔwɔ. Frɛ ambulance bi bɔɔ ha, ofainɛ!
narrative_dagbani: Allo, m yuli la Akua. N frɛ ka yɛligu daa niŋ bia puɣa o so. O yuli la Afia, o nyin ata ni anahi pia kuli, ka fiiri dɔɣim o — o kpɛŋ o ŋun doo ni o gogori doo. Ti bɛ Bolgatanga-Bawku nyɛŋ ni, Navrongo doo kpeeni. Fiiri dɔɣim doo ka bɔri kpɛŋ yaa — o ŋɔŋ bɛ ti nyɛ kpe, o bɛri mi ka o bɛ yuɣim, ama o soli ka o bɛ yɛla. N daa, ti bori dɔɣite ni o na kpe o sɔŋ ka o din kaɣa.
narrative_fante: Me din de Akua, merefrɛ efisɛ biribi bɔne ba wɔ ha. Ɔbaa ketewa bi wɔ ha — wɔfrɛ no Afia, ɔyɛ mfe aduonu-enum anaasɛ aduonu-nsia enti, na wɔtoo no — ɛkaa ne kɔn ne ne tiri ho. Yɛwɔ Bolgatanga-Bawku ɔkwan so, Navrongo beae no nkyɛn. Burn no nyɛ ketewa koraa — ne ho nam no hunu bɔne paa, eye no yaw na ɔsu, nanso ɔda hɔ rekasa na ne ani da hɔ. Me pa wo kyew, yɛhia obi mmra ha mmoa no ntɛm ara.
narrative_gurene: M yɛtɔ Akua, m frɛ bia ka a yɛligu bee nyiŋa. O yɛtɔ Afia, o bee nɩnkɛm shɩ ayima naɔ biɛŋ o ŋun nɛ o ŋɔbili zi. Ti bee Bolgatanga-Bawku nyɛŋ zi, Navrongo nɛ ka ti bee. Ka nyiŋ kpirinŋa yɛligu a yɛligu zeŋ, o yɛligu ka o ŋɔbili nɛ ka o ŋun nɛ piŋ bee dan — o pɔɔ bee yɛligu ka o gɔɣim, ama o nyɛ zee, o kɔŋ kɔŋ. N tɩɩma, ti daɣim neba la ba wa sɔŋ o zutɔ — Sɔŋ mam!

narrative_en: Good afternoon, I want to report something that happened on the Tamale-Yendi road, close to Savelugu side. My name is Ibrahim, and I was passing when I saw a young boy, Agyeman, fall down from his bicycle on the road. He hurt his leg but he is okay, not serious — he is sitting by the roadside and can move it small small. Someone is with him now but I just wanted to let you people know in case he needs to be checked by somebody.
narrative_ewe: Oshwiee, meɖi na mi se nubɔbɔ si wòwɔ ɖe Tamale-Yendi ʋɔ dzi, gbɔ Savelugu fɔm. Ŋkɔnyé enye Ibrahim. Ɣeyiɣi meyi ʋɔ dzi la, mekpɔ ɖevi ɖeka — Agyeman — ewo ƒu ɖe egbɔ bicycle dzi ʋɔ dzi. Afɔ nyawo eyi nam na eŋu, gake emeƒo o — eʋu ŋkeke bubu, edzro ɖe ʋɔ ta eye atu afɔa bɔbɔ bɔbɔ. Ame ɖeka le egbɔ fifia, gake meɖi na mi se esia, nakae ame aɖe adi kpɔe la.
narrative_ga: Hɛloo, ojekoo — afternoon. Chale, mihe shi bɛ mifɛ report ni yɛ wɔ Tamale-Yendi road wei, close to Savelugu side. Miŋ Ibrahim, ni mehe kɔ shɔŋ ni mihuu oblɛ bi — Agyeman — obumo kɔɔ bicycle ŋ wɔ kpee wei. Ogboo ehii ni, noko enye serious o — oni le, otse wɔ roadside, ni otumi shi gboo ŋ kakra kakra. Onipa bi wɔ ne ŋ saa, noko mihe shi bɛ mifɛ make una nyɛ, yei, just in case ɔdɔkɔtaa bi ni check ni.
narrative_dagbani: Allo, desiba. N bɛ yɛ n yi nyɛ la kuli n diri n maŋ yi. Ka yɛla gbini Tamale-Yendi nyɛŋ zuɣu, Savelugu dakɔri. Miri yuli di Ibrahim, ka n bɛ di nyɛŋ zuu la n nii bia din yi o yuli di Agyeman — o gbini o bicycle zuɣu nyɛŋ zuɣu. O tiŋ yɛligu o, ama ka kɔlim daa — o nyɔɣim la nyɛŋ gɔli ka o tiŋ zaŋ lɛ sɔŋ o kparaa kparaa. Nindari yini bɛ n ni o la bi. N yɛla loli yi chɛ ya maa, ka yi bɔri dɔɣite di yi nyɛ o la, yi kɔŋ ŋmɛ o.
narrative_fante: Maaha. Me pɛ sɛ me kyerɛ mo biribi a ɛtoo so wɔ Tamale-Yendi road no so, beae a ɛbɛn Savelugu side hɔ. Me din de Ibrahim, na meretwam na mehuu abofra bi, Agyeman, a ɔtuu fam fi ne bicycle so wɔ ɔkwan no so. Ɔnan yɛ no yaw kakra, nanso ɔho yɛ — ɛnyɛ serious. Ɔte ɔkwan mpon hɔ na ɔtumi yi ne nan na ɔkyekyere no kakra kakra. Obi wɔ ne ho nnɛ, nanso mepɛɛ sɛ me ma mo nyinaa ho kɔkɔ, sɛ ɛba sɛ ɔhia sɛ obi hwɛ no enti.
narrative_gurene: Laafɩ, m bɛ tɩɩma sɔŋ mam kɛ m gʋr yɛl yɛla bee a Tamale-Yendi nyɛŋ zɔŋ, Savelugu doo bee. M yɛlɛ Ibrahim, ka m nɛ la nyɛŋ zɔŋ la, m nyɛ bia biɛla yɩŋa, Agyeman, o bɔɔrɩ a o bicycle zug fam nyɛŋ zɔŋ. O nɩɩm yɛligu, la ka o bɛ yɛligu tɩŋ tɩŋ — ka o zuɣu bɛ yɛla. O bɛ chɔŋ nyɛŋ nɛ yɛlgu, ka o tɩ nɩɩm kʋs kʋs kɛŋ. Nnɩ ŋun bɛ n bee o, la m bɛ sɔŋ n gʋr yɛl nɩ yɛ nyɛ — ŋmɩŋ bia nɩ sɔŋ a o le nyɛ la, ka nindaan bɩ ŋun kɛ nyɛ o.

narrative_en: Please, I want to report something that happened here in Nalerigu. My friend Vida — she was walking near the market and she tripped over a broken piece of pavement and fell, twisting her ankle badly. The ankle is swelling up now and she cannot put any weight on it, but she is conscious and talking fine otherwise. I am Elinam, I am with her now. We need to know if she should go to the clinic or if we can manage it at home.
narrative_ewe: Meɖekuku, meɖi gblɔ nami nu siwo wòwɔ fifia kenu Nalerigu. Eƒu ɖokui le Vida — ewo le dzɔ ƒo agbeme le aɖabaƒe ŋutɔ, eye ewu nu le ɣleti biba si vɔ le ʋɔ dzi, ewu ƒu eye eɖo afɔaʋo nyuitɔ. Afɔaʋo la kpɔe le axɔxɔ fifia, eye mate ŋu tso afɔ ɖe edzi o — gake eɖo edzi eye egblɔ gbɔ nyuie. Nyeye Elinam, mele eŋu fifia. Meɖi nya be ado asiwo ɖeka la alo mate ŋu kpɔ eya le ŋɔ.
narrative_ga: Ofainɛ, mɛ yɛ kɛ report bɔɔ shi pɛɛ kɛ Nalerigu. Chale, Vida — mi ŋmaafoi — ɔhɛɛ lɛ kpee fɛɛ market kɔŋ, na ɔbumo — pavement lɛ broken shi, na obumo kɛɛ ankle ehii ŋ paaa. Ankle lɛ, ɔhɛɛ swell etsotso nɔŋ, na ɔtii gboo tɔŋ o — ankle lɛ emii ŋ weight o. Nɔkɔɔ, ɔni le, ɔyɛ kasa kɛɛ fine. Mɛ yɛ Elinam, mi hiɛ kɛ ɔlɛ nɔŋ. Chale, yɛ shɛɛ nyɛ — ɔdɛɛ yɛ clinic anaa yɛ tumi manage ŋ shi ha?
narrative_dagbani: N daa, n bɛ yɛ ni n ŋmɛ yɛla din kɔ Nalerigu. M yigba Vida — o daa be yiyɛ market saɣim, o tɔɣi kpini din gɔŋ nyɛŋ sani, o gbini, o tiŋ nuu kpɛni yɛligu tooi. O tiŋ nuu ka naŋ tooi ka ŋani daa, o ka ŋɔŋ tiŋ o tiŋ kuli o sani ka di yɛligu tooi. Amo, o nyɛ o ziŋ yɛla ŋani, o yɛla yɛligu bee o. N yuli di Elinam, n be o saɣim ka din bɛ yɛ ni. Ti bɔri n ni yɛ ti: O naa naa nyɛla yili, baa ti ŋɔŋ di naɣiti yi dii lahi yili?
narrative_fante: Please, I want to report something that happened here in Nalerigu. My friend Vida — she was walking near the market and she tripped over a broken piece of pavement and fell, twisting her ankle badly. The ankle is swelling up now and she cannot put any weight on it, but she is conscious and talking fine otherwise. I am Elinam, I am with her now. We need to know if she should go to the clinic or if we can manage it at home.
narrative_gurene: Please, I want to report something that happened here in Nalerigu. My friend Vida — she was walking near the market and she tripped over a broken piece of pavement and fell, twisting her ankle badly. The ankle is swelling up now and she cannot put any weight on it, but she is conscious and talking fine otherwise. I am Elinam, I am with her now. We need to know if she should go to the clinic or if we can manage it at home.

narrative_en: Please, something very serious has happened here and I need help urgently. My name is Memunatu, and I am calling about a young boy called Nii Kwei — he fell into the water near Kete Krachi road, the area close to Dambai. He went under and by the time we pulled him out, he was not responding at all, and his neck and head look very bad, like something is wrong there. The boy is a child, very small, and he is in a very bad condition right now — we are so afraid. Please send someone quickly because we don't know what to do for him here.
narrative_ewe: Meɖekuku, nudzadzra gãna wɔ ɖe afisia, eye meɖi kpɔ ŋunye akpokpɔ. Ŋkɔnya enye Memunatu, eye mefrɛ wo ɖe Nii Kwei ŋuti — ɖevi ɖeka feƒe. Nii Kwei edo ƒu tsi ŋutɔ ɖe Kete Krachi ʋɔ ƒe da la, afɔ si le Dambai ŋutɔ. Eyi atɔ tsi me, eye gake hafi míakɔ agblɔ eƒu kpo la, edzre dzre ame o, eɖo edzi o. Eƒe ɖo kplɛ eƒe ta kpɔ gãna — ele nuwuwu ŋu, nuse dzenna be esia wɔ ɖe afɔwɔwɔ. Ɖevi si enye ɖevi feƒefeƒe, ŋutsu ɖe viɖee — eye eƒe dɔwɔwɔ le ŋuɖoɖo bubu ŋu fifia. Míenya egbẽ o. Meɖekuku, hlɔ ame aɖewo dzo gbãtɔ, afɔwɔwɔ ene etsɔ amegbɔgblɔ aɖe dze bena wɔ nusẽ naɖe ɖevi sia, ebe míekpɔ nusẽ woaɖe naɖe eya fifia o.
narrative_ga: Ofainɛ, something very serious abɔ shi ha, mi hia bo urgently. Mi din bi Memunatu, mi frɛ mi frɛ ni ehihi Nii Kwei — obumo shi nshɔŋ kɛ Kete Krachi kpee ha, area kɛ Dambai kaa. Otsɔɔ shi nshɔŋ fɛɛ, ke ngmɛɛ yɛ yɛ jɛ opuomo, enimɔ gbɛ kpɛkɛɛ — onyemi le o wɔ. Ni ŋmɛi kɛ ŋmɛi gboo nɔŋ — something bi wrong wɔ. Dɛbi bi oblɛ fɛɛ, very small, ni condition yɛ bad koraa right now — yɛ suro. Ofainɛ, frɛ ambulance — frɛ nɔŋ tiŋ tiŋ — chale yɛ nyɛ shi bo no o wɔ.
narrative_dagbani: N daa, saha bee kɔŋ daa ti n nyɛ ka ka yɛli ziŋ, ka n bori sɔŋ ziŋ-ziŋ. N yuli di Memunatu, ka n frɛ wuhibu bia yuli Nii Kwei — o gbini kom pɔ bɔri Kete Krachi nyɛŋ wɔɣibu, kɔŋ bɔri Dambai. O kuli kom pɔ, ka ti bi o suɣulo, o nyɛ o maa yɛla kuli, o ti soli nyafu-nyafu. O ŋun ni o nigboni nyɛri ka ka yɛli ziŋ — o bɛ nyafu. Biɛla bee o ka, bia biɛla daa, ka o nyɛla ka ka yɛli ziŋ saha tia — ti zoli ka ti ti nya bɛ n ti tuma nyi o bɔri. N daa, yi kpɛ nin yini ziŋ-ziŋ, barka.
narrative_fante: Me pa wo kyew, biribi a ɛyɛ hu paa asi ha na mehia mmoa ntɛm ara. Me din de Memunatu, na mefrɛ ho fa abofra bɛn bi ho — wɔfrɛ no Nii Kwei — ɔtuu gu nsuo mu fa Kete Krachi kwan ho, beae a ɛbɛn Dambai no. Ɔkɔɔ ase nsuo mu, na bere a yɛtanee no firi mu no, ɔnnyee so hwee — ɔnhunu hwee koraa. Na n'akyi no, ne kɔn ne ne tiri yɛ hu paa — yɛhunu sɛ biribi nyɛ yiye wɔ hɔ. Abofra no yɛ ketewa paa, na ne ho tumi no yɛ bɔne kɛse paa seesei — yɛsuro na yɛntɛɛ adeɛ. Me pa wo kyew, mma onipa bi ntɛm ara efisɛ yɛnnim adeɛ biara a yɛbɛtumi ayɛ no ama no wɔ ha.
narrative_gurene: N tɩɩma, bʋ yɛ fɔɔri kɛ tɩ yi yɛligu pam kɔnɔ, ka mam yɛ sɔŋ ma foforoo. M yɩɩrɩ Memunatu, ka m bee sua bia yɩɩrɩ Nii Kwei — o bɔɔrɩ koom pʋɔ kɛ Kete Krachi nyɛŋ siiga, zɔ Dambai kɔnɔ. O kpɛŋ koom pʋɔ, ka dɛɛ tɩ yii o bɔɔrɩ, o kɔ a nyɛ pam — o sɔŋ nyɛ. Ka o ŋun ne o koɣo yɛ kɛ dʋɔrɩ pam, wʋlʋɣa bʋ bee yɛ zɔ bɩ kɔnɔ. Biia yɩɩ o bia bilisi pam, ka o yɛla yɛ ka o kɔm fɔɔri pam saa — tɩ zɔ nyɛ kaboŋ pam. N tɩɩma, tuma nnɛ foforoo, bala tɩ nyɛ ka tɩ ka a nyɛ bʋ tɩ tʋma o yin kɔnɔ.

narrative_en: Please, I want to report something that happened to a young man here in Oforikrom. My name is Kwame, and the boy's name is Appiah — he's my neighbor's son, still young. He was working at a construction site nearby when something fell and hit him on the head, and he went down. He's not unconscious but he's very confused and his neck is paining him seriously, so we are very worried about him. We need someone to come and help because the injury doesn't look small at all.
narrative_ewe: Meɖekuku, meɖi gblɔ nu siwo wòwɔ afisia le Oforikrom. Nyee Kwame, eye ɖevi sia ŋkɔe nye Appiah — nye agbɔsɔ ɖevi, eya mɔfemɔfe hã kpɔɔ. Ele construction site siɖo wò le bawo ŋu le afi siwo ƒu ŋu, eye nu ɖeka wui le eŋu gɔme yi ta ŋu, eye edo ƒu fiafia. Eɖo edzi kpɔɔ, gake enuwo ŋkɔ le eŋu, eye eɖo ale ko — eye ɖo eyi nam gboɖo gboɖo ŋutɔ. Eyata miaƒo ŋu le egɔme kpɔɔ. Meɖekuku, miaɖi ame ɖeka atrɔ vá afisia akpe ɖe ŋumia, elabena agɔ sia mele ŋunye ŋɔ o — esɔ sɔsɔ.
narrative_ga: Ofainɛ, mɛ bɛ report bii shishio le Oforikrom. Me din nɔ Kwame, nɛ dɛbi din nɔ Appiah — onyɛ mi neighbor oba, oblɛ pɛɛ. Ɔ ŋmɛ construction site bi kɛ ŋmɛi ahi shi, nɛ ebumo. Enimɔ gbɛ o, kɛ oni le, kɛ onyemi le kɛkɛ — ŋmɛi kɛ shi ŋmɛi gbɔŋ ehi ŋ aah, chale ni worry ŋ kɛkɛ. Ehii ŋ neck ehii kɛkɛ, chale yɛ tsɔ ŋ serious. Yɛ hɛ someone ni bi kɛ boa mi — injury yi eha lɛ o, chale. Frɛ ambulance!
narrative_dagbani: N daa, n bɔri yɛla din kuli ka o pahi Oforikrom. M yuli nye Kwame, ka bi yuli nye Appiah — o nye m yaba biɛla, o biɛla daa. O bi kuli ka o bi yɛlgina construction site din bɛ kɔŋ ni, ka yɛla dali gbɛri o ŋun zuɣu, ka o gbini. O nyɛ ti soli daa, n'ama o nyɛ yɛlimaligu paai, ka o nyubi yɛligu daa paai, ka ti yɛligu o zuɣu paai. N bori la ŋun din waa ni di sɔŋ ti, bimba din kuli o ti gbɛrigu bi zoli daa.
narrative_fante: Me pa wo kyew, mepɛ sɛ meka biribi a asi ɔbarima ketewa bi ho wɔ Oforikrom ha. Me din de Kwame, na abarimaba no din de Appiah — ɔyɛ me hɔhɔ ba, ɔwɔ mmere ketewa so. Ɔreye adwuma wɔ construction site bi a ɛbɛn ha, na biribi tou fam hyiaa no tiri so, na ɔtuu fam. Ɔnhunu hwee da, nanso n'adwene ayɛ ne ho pɛpɛɛpɛ, na ne kɔn yɛ no yaw pa ara, enti yɛsuro no pii. Yɛhia obi sɛ ɔmmra boa yɛn, efisɛ injury no yɛ den pa ara — ɛnyɛ ketewa.
narrative_gurene: N tɩɩma, m bɛ yɛla fo dema bee ninsaal da bi yɛligu ka da be n Oforikrom. M yɛ Kwame, ka bia yɛ Appiah — o yɛ m niba bia, o ka kɛŋ daa. O da be tuma construction site bi bee a, ka ninsaal bee sɔm o ŋun zuɣu, ka o bɔɔrɩ fam. O ko a nyɛ ye, o ye — la o ŋɔɔnɩ lari bee o ka o ka tɩɩm, ka o gbeng yɛligu o ka yɛligu be diri. Wɔ, ti ŋana bee o ka ti zɛŋ be. Ti baŋ ninsaal wa wa sɔŋ ti, bala yɛligu bee o ka yɛligu de ka o ŋɔɔŋɔ be kɛlɛ.

narrative_en: Please, something very serious has happened and I need help urgently. My name is Nii Laryea, and I'm calling about a man called Adjei who was working on the road along the Takoradi-Cape Coast highway near Anomabo. He was doing some construction work there and a heavy piece of equipment fell on his leg — it's very bad, the leg is badly crushed and he is losing a lot of blood. We have tied something around it to try to stop the bleeding but he is in a lot of pain and we are very scared for him. Please send help to that stretch of road near Anomabo as fast as you can.
narrative_ewe: Meɖekuku, nudzadzra gãã aɖe wɔ ɖe afim, eye meɖi kpɔ ŋunye enumake. Ŋkɔnyé enye Nii Laryea, eye mekpɔɖe ŋu Adjei ŋu — ŋutsu si le zu aɖewo wɔm le ʋɔ dzi le Takoradi kple Cape Coast ʋɔ dzi, kpɔ Anomabo tso. Ele construction dɔ wɔm ɖe afie la, gbe akɔ gãã si le dɔ la ta wu le egbɔ to dzi — eyi ŋutɔ wu, afɔa vɔ vavãã eye dzi le etsotso wu. Míakpe nɔviwo aɖe ɖe afɔa ta ɖe akpa be woatsi dzi la, gake eyi nam wu wu eye míevɔ̃ wu egbe. Meɖekuku, fia helpowo be wowɔ ʋɔ dzi kpɔ Anomabo ta—kpɔ enumake, kpe ɖe míaŋu ɖa!
narrative_ga: Ofainɛ, bɔɔ serious shi le hiɔŋ mli, mi hia bo mli ha. Miŋ dzɛ Nii Laryea, mi frɛ atsɛ nɔŋ Adjei dzɛŋ. Otsɔ wɔ kpee dzɛ Takoradi-Cape Coast highway bii, kɛ Anomabo fɛɛ. Ɔtsɔ construction work wɔ shia, naa heavy equipment kpee dɔɔ obumo ŋ gboo yɛ — chale, ni baa, gboo no teetee gbɛɛ, kɛ shwe etsotso plɛŋŋ. Yɛ kuta bɔɔ shi yɛ ŋ gboo so, yɛ hwɛ shwe etsotso no ni kpɛi, naa ehii mi dɛŋ, yɛ shɛbii nɔŋ ye atsɛ nɔŋ. Ofainɛ, frɛ ambulance, mli ha ha — kɛ kpee no kɛ Anomabo fɛɛ. Mli ha ha yɛ baa!
narrative_dagbani: N daa, ka diri nyɛ din yɛli n bɛri daa, ka m bori sɔŋ tooi tooi. M yuli nyɛ Nii Laryea, ka m frɛ m yɛla ni yɛligu din o daa ni la. Nii yuli nyɛ Adjei, o bɛ nyɛŋ yili ka o tuma tuma doo Takoradi-Cape Coast nyɛŋ din bee Anomabo. O bɛ nyɛla construction tuma doo, ka o tiŋ guu o din nyɛ kɔɔ ziizii — o tiŋ nyɛ yɛligu daa daa, ka ziim bee o la tooi. Ti kpɛŋ be o tiŋ so ti bori la ziim n zaŋ zani, amma o yɛligu daa la ka ti suɣuri o daa. N daa, yi frɛ sɔŋ tooi tooi ka yi wa Anomabo nyɛŋ doo — yi wa ka tooi tooi naa.
narrative_fante: Me pa wo kyew, biribi a eye hu paa aba na mehia mmoa ntɛm ara. Me din de Nii Laryea, na merefrɛ mo fa onipa bi a wɔfrɛ no Adjei ho. Ɔreye adwuma wɔ ɔkwan so wɔ Takoradi-Cape Coast highway no ho, Anomabo nkyɛn. Ɔreye construction work wɔ hɔ na heavy equipment kɛse bi tuu hwe fam wɔ ne nan so — eye hu paa, nan no aye crush kakra na mogya retu no paa. Yɛato nea yɛnya wɔ ne nan ho sɛ yɛbɛtumi asi mogya no, nanso eye no yaw paa na yɛsuro no paa. Me pa wo kyew, moma mmoa ba ntɛm wɔ ɔkwan no so Anomabo nkyɛn no — mma ntɛm ara!
narrative_gurene: N tɩɩma, bɛ yɛligu pʋɔ ka a wʋra fãa, ka mam yɛ sɔŋ ka a yɛra. M yʋɣɩrɩ Nii Laryea, ka mam frɛ yɩɩ fo zugu nɩ ba yʋɣɩrɩ Adjei. O bɛ tʋma nyɛŋ zɔŋɔ Takoradi-Cape Coast nyɛŋ pʋɔ, Anomabo taɣa. O bɛ construction tʋma bɛ la, ka tʋm kʋɣɩm gbʋɣɩm kpɛŋ o nɩŋ zugu — o nɩŋ gbɛ ŋmaaŋmaa, ka ziim bee o ka ɣeya. Ti mɛ kʋɣɩ bʋɣɩm o nɩŋ zugu ka ti gʋlɩ ziim a ɣeya, ama o yɛligu ŋmaaŋmaa ka tia nyɛ sɔŋ a wʋra zugu. N tɩɩma, yi sɔŋ Anomabo nyɛŋ taɣa la ka a yɛra yɛra — ka yɛli dɔɔrɔ!

narrative_en: "Please, I want to report an accident that happened. My name is Nii Armah, and I'm calling about my friend Abena — she was involved in a road accident on the Accra-Cape Coast road, somewhere around the Winneba area. A car hit the tro-tro she was riding in and she knocked her head against the seat in front of her, and her neck is paining her small. She is conscious and talking, but we are worried because she keeps saying the neck is stiff. We are with her now and she needs someone to check her properly."
narrative_ewe: Meɖekuku, meyi na wo nudzadzra si wɔ wɔ. Nyeŋɔŋlɔ nye Nii Armah, eye mefrɛ wo le ŋuti doƒe si tso ŋunye nyɔnu ƒe ŋkɔ nye Abena — ewɔ accident le Accra-Cape Coast ʋɔ dzi, ɖe Winneba ƒe ŋɔ ko. Akɔ ɖeka dze trotro si ele ame si ŋu eye woƒo ta ɖe ablɔɖa si le eŋɔ dzi, eye ɖo yi nam ɖe abɔbɔ xexe. Eɖo edzi eye egblɔgblɔ, gake miaƒo ƒuƒu aɖe le ame si ŋu, leyia eyi ɖo le tefe sia tefe nye. Míeƒo etɔ̃ kple eya fifia, eye eɖi ame aɖe na wo kpɔ eɖe dede dede.
narrative_ga: Ofainɛ, accident bi tɔɔ, mito gbɛ mi. Miŋ ye Nii Armah, mi frɛ bɔ Abena — chale, accident bi kɛ ŋ le Accra-Cape Coast road dɔŋ, Winneba kɛ shi. Kaa bi bumo trotro bi Abena naŋ le oshi, ehi Abena ŋmɛi bumo kɛ seat bi Eba ye ŋ atwam — ni hii ŋ gbɔɔ agya ŋmɛi gbɔ. Oni le, ŋ kɛ yɛ shikpong, kɛ ŋ gba mli le, kɛ ŋ shi le — kɛ eha ŋ, bɔ ŋ she shee ni ŋmɛi ŋ gbɔŋ ye haa. Yɛ ni le ŋ kɛ ŋ ni, kɛ yɛ bi doctor obi bo ŋ check ŋ ka ka.
narrative_dagbani: N daa, n bɛ yɛ ni n diri accident din kul la. M yɛligu din bi Nii Armah, ka m frɛ diri n doo Abena din kul la accident. O kul accident Accra-Cape Coast nyɛŋ ŋa, kul Winneba saɣim. Kaɣa doo dili trotro din o diɣa la, ka o ŋun duɣi kpanligu din bɛ o nyaɣim, ka o ŋun doo ti yɛligu o torim. O ti nyɛ o maa yɛla ka o golibu, ama ti nyaa ka o yɛla ka o ŋun doo ti saa torim. Ti bɛ n'o chɛŋ ŋa yɛlibu, ka o bori dɔɣite oo yi o doli daa.
narrative_fante: Me pa wo kyew, mepɛ sɛ meka accident bi a ɛsii ho. Me din de Nii Armah, na merɛfrɛ fa me nua Abena ho — ɔkɔ accident wɔ Accra-Cape Coast road no so, Winneba area ho nohoa. Kar bi bɔɔ trotro a ɔtene mu no, na ɔbuaa ne tiri wɔ chair a ɛwɔ ne nyinaa no so, na ne kɔn yɛ no yaw kakra. Ɔte hɔ na ɔreka asɛm, nanso yɛsuro efisɛ ɔka tumi tumi sɛ ne kɔn no hyew hɔ. Yɛwɔ ne ho seesei na ɔhia onipa bi a ɔbɛhwɛ no yiye.
narrative_gurene: N tɩɩma, m bɛ la kɔɔna accident bi nɩ da mɩɩ. M yʋʋrɩ la Nii Armah, a m bɛ frɛɛ a zuɣu m zoa Abena — o kɔɔ accident nyɛŋ zɔŋ nɩ a bɛ Accra ne Cape Coast tɩ nyɛŋ a, zɔŋ nɩ a bɛ Winneba nɩ. Kaɣa yenno nɩ bɔ tro-tro bi nɩ Abena bɛ pʋɔ, a o ŋun bɔɔrɩ kɔɔ kiti bi nɩ bɛ o yɛlɛ, a ka a yɛligu o nɔɔrɛ nɩ bilisi. O ko a nyɛ nyɛ la, a o bɛ yɛlɩ, la a yɛligu be o nɩ a ŋmɩɩrɩ, bɩ tɩ yɛligu la a o bɛ yɛlɩ la a nɔɔrɛ kpɛŋ kpɛŋ. Tɩ bɛ n yi o mɩɩ la, la a o sɔŋ nɩ nyɛ o daŋ daŋ.

narrative_en: "Please, something happened this afternoon on the Bawku road, near Zebilla. A young girl, Araba, she was knocked by a tipper truck while she was walking by the roadside. She fell and hit her head on the ground, but she is conscious and talking, just some small cuts and she is complaining of pain in her neck area. I am Patience, I am a neighbor who saw what happened. Please we need someone to come and check her because she is a young girl and we don't want to take chances."
narrative_ewe: Meɖekuku, nudzadzra ɖe afim egbe ŋdi sia Bawku ʋɔ dzi, Zebilla kɔŋ. Ɖevi nyɔnu deka, Araba, tipper truck ɖe asi eŋu abe eɖo ʋɔ xɔxɔ wòa. Ewu ƒu eye eta ɖe ŋutɔ dzi, gake eɖo edzi, le nu ƒɔ̃ɔ̃m. Eyata kutoo le nu si nɔe nye dzi ŋu tsɔtso abe nuɖuɖu vevie o — agbɔɖi ta kutoo ƒe nuvɔvɔ le eŋu, eye eyi nam le ɖo ŋɔ. Nye ŋkɔ nye Patience — menye ame si le kɔŋ bia si wɔ nudzadzra sia. Meɖekuku, midzro ame aɖe va kpɔe, efiafia, elabena nye Ɖevi nyɔnu, eye mide ɖe eŋu o.
narrative_ga: Ofainɛ, something happen this afternoon for Bawku road, near Zebilla. Oblɛ bi, Araba, tipper truck bi knock no bi wɔ okɛ kpee shishi tsɛ. Obumo, ŋmɛi otsoo fɛɛ ground, oba oni le oshi kɛ oye kasa, aba cuts kakra bi wɔ no shi, aba ehii no wɔ ni neck shishi. Minya Patience — mi nyɛ neighbour bi nɔ hii shitso happen. Ofainɛ, yɛfɛ ŋ bi aba ewo nɔ ebaahi check no, kakra bi Araba nyɛ oblɛ kakra, yɛmii fɛ yɛkɛ chances.
narrative_dagbani: N daa, ka yɛligu daa kɔhi yɛsiti ka wuntaɣa so, Bawku nyɛŋ so, Zebilla kɔhiɣu. Binsim bea, Araba, tipper truck daa o gbaŋ ka o bee nyɛŋ doo ka o be sa ka nyɛŋ. O gbini ka o ŋun daa di sahiyɛ ka yɛri, ama o maa nyɛ ka o gari, o be yɛlim. Ka yɛligu naa be o, ama ti ka kpɛŋ so shɛli, ka o be yɛlim ka o ŋun tiŋ doo yɛligu bee o. Mali maa yɛligu bee o nyɛŋ ŋuŋ kɔhiɣu. N tuma yɛligu maa Patience, maa o ji nyɛŋ naanili ŋɔ. N daa, ti bori dɔɣite ku ku naŋ come o checkri, bala ka o ti bia bea, ka ti n ti niŋ yoli ninsaɣibu so. Yi frɛ yi maa sɔŋ ka yi naŋ come.
narrative_fante: "Me pa wo kyew, biribi baa saa afternoon yi wɔ Bawku road no so, Zebilla nkyɛn. Ababaawa bi, Araba, tipper truck bi bɔɔ no wɔ saa beae no, na ɔrekɔ so na ɔte ɔkwan nkyɛn. Ɔtuu fam na wɔn tiri bɔɔ fam no, nanso ɔte ase na ɔreka asɛm — cuts kakra bi wom na ɔka sɛ ɔkɔn no yɛ no yaw. Me din de Patience, me yɛ ɔkyerɛni a mihuu deɛ esii no. Me pa wo kyew, yɛhia sɛ obi bɛba hwɛ no, efisɛ ɔyɛ abofra na yɛmpɛ sɛ yɛgye chances wɔ ne so."
narrative_gurene: N tɩɩma, bɛ yɛ yɛligu la wʋllaa Bawku nyɛŋ zuɣu, Zebilla kʋɔrɛ. Bia bi yɛla, Araba, tipper truck bɔ o la o bee nyɛŋ nɔɔrɛ. O bɔɔrɩ la o ŋun bɔ tẽ zugu, la o ko a nyɛ yɛl la — o nyɛ la o goama bee. Bɛ yɛ nɔɔbɔrɛ la a tɩɩ, la o yɛligu o ŋun nɔɔrɛ ka yɛligu. Mam yɛrɛ, m yɛrɛ Patience — m yɛ o yɛja la m niŋ o yɛligu. N tɩɩma, mam yɛ fo — ti sɔŋ yi Yɛla yir nɔɔ bi wa niŋ o gʋrɩ, bala o yɛ bia bi, la ti yɛ nɩɩ yɛl-kpɛm niŋ.

narrative_en: Please, something happened on the Ho-Accra road, close to Adidome side. My brother, Nii Kwei, he fell — I think he was on top of a vehicle or something and he lost his balance and came down hard. He hit his head and his neck area, and when I reached him he was awake but he was very confused and holding his neck, saying it was paining him a lot. He's a young boy, maybe seventeen or so. I am Agyeman, I am with him now, please we need someone to come because I don't want to move him too much.
narrative_ewe: Meɖekuku, nudzadzra ɖe afɔm le Ho-Accra ʋɔ dzi, kplim Adidome ɖo. Ƒe ŋutsu, Nii Kwei, edo ƒu — meɖo nu susu be ele akɔ dzi alo nuɖekae dzi, eye ezu asi eye ewu ƒu gã. Eta kplo eɖo ɖo xɔ ɖeka o, eye megblɔ ŋu nɔ eŋu o. Megaɖe eŋu le gake nae, eɖo edzi gake esiame gakegake eye ele eɖo ɖo tɔ, egblɔ be eyi nam ŋuɖɔ. Ɖevi ƒu ame, abe ku ɖeka ɖekaewo(17) ale. Nyeŋu Agyeman, meɖe eŋu fifia. Meɖekuku, medi ame adze be yɔ ŋutsɔ, elabena medi étrɔ akɔ eŋu gã o.
narrative_ga: Ofainɛ, something happen wo Ho-Accra road no, close to Adidome side. Me nua, Nii Kwei — obumo, mi suɔ enyo kaa paɛ ŋ baŋ, shi eda balance mli eko lɛ obumo kɛ force. Ebumo kɛ ŋmɛi ŋ kɛ neck area, shi mekɛɛ oshi lɛ, oni le, ŋgba enyɛ confused paɛ — oda neck ŋ shi ehii mi, ehii mi paɛ. Oblɛ lɛ ko, maybe seventeen years so. Mɛ yɛɛ Agyeman, mɛ kɛ oshi sane. Ofainɛ, frɛ somebody eba, because meba nɔŋ move no too much.
narrative_dagbani: N daa, accident bi bɛ Ho-Accra nyɛŋ so, Adidome baŋ doo. M dabi, Nii Kwei, o gbini — m nyɛya ko o bɛ kaɣa zugu bɔɣu, ka o kpɛŋ, ka o lɛ kpɛŋ daa. O ŋun ni o ŋun kpɛŋ baŋ doo di yɛligu, ka m tɔɣi o ŋɔɔn o bɛ nyɛlim, ama o nyɛ yɛla dali, ka o nuu kpɛŋ o ŋun kpɛŋ baŋ doo, ka o yɛla ko yɛligu ŋaani o ba. O nye biɛla, biɛla bia, o kɔligu bɛ ayɔpɔin be pia so. Maa yɛla Agyeman, m bɛ o ni ŋɔɔn. N daa, yi kpe sɔŋ maa, barka, bala m nyɛya ko m zali o ti n ba — m bɔri maa doli biɛla maa m ba yi sɔŋi maa.
narrative_fante: Me pa wo kyew, biribi asi wɔ Ho-Accra road no so, Adidome side ho fitaa. Me nua, Nii Kwei, ɔtuu fam — mese ɔwɔ kar bi so na ɔnyɛɛ ne ho den, na ɔbɛhwee ase yiye. Ɔbuae ne tiri ne ne kɔn fam, na bere a meduui ne ho no, ɔnyan na ne ti yɛ hann, nanso ɔrekyerɛ sɛ ɔyɛ ne kɔn no mu yaw paa — ɔkyeree ne kɔn daa. Ɔyɛ obarimawaa — ebia ɔwɔ afeɛ nson ne edu (17) ho. Me na me ne no wɔ ha yi, me din de Agyeman. Me pa wo kyew, yehia obi mmra ha, efisɛ menmpɛ sɛ mekyeree no yiye, ebi betumi abubu no.
narrative_gurene: N tɩɩma, something bi nɩ Ho-Accra nyɛŋ zuɣu, Adidome faan. M pio, Nii Kwei, o bɔɔrɩ — m nyɛ o bɛ kaɣa zuɣu bɩ o yɩɩrɩ a o kpɛŋ fam wʋnɩ. O daa a o ŋun nɩ o ŋoro ɣaa, la tuma o naɣa a o lee o ŋoro, o nɩ yɛligu ka o wʋsɩ nɩ o ŋoro yɛligu wʋnɩ wʋnɩ. O bɛ bia yaa, dɩɩrɩ seventeen bɩ a la. Mam nyɛrɩ Agyeman, m bɛ o nɩ yaa. N tɩɩma, ti laa nɩn faan yɛ ti sɔŋ, bɩ m yɛ o zie ka m tɩ o nɩ wʋnɩ wʋnɩ.

narrative_en: "Please, I want to report an accident that happened here at Yendi Senior High School. My name is Issah, and one of our students, Dela, was knocked down by a pragyia just at the school entrance when he was crossing to come in this morning. It's a young boy, and he's hurt on his leg but he's conscious and not bleeding too badly. We've made him sit down and someone is with him now."
narrative_ewe: Meɖekuku, meɖi gbɔ accident si wɔ ɖe afisia Yendi Senior High School. Ŋkɔnyem nye Issah, eye ɖevi ame ɖeka le ŋusinyawo me, Dela, pragyia ŋɔ eya ɖe agbe ɖe school entrance si, gbã le eƒe ŋkɔ ɖe ta dzi gbã le nu bena na ɖo ta dzi le afɔla si ɖo ŋkɔ le yeyiyi vɔ sia vɔ ne. Ɖevi ŋutsu vɛ wòna eya, eye eyi eƒe afɔ, gake eɖo edzi eye dzi le etsotso nye kakaka o. Míekɔ eme be eye na xɔ asi, eye ame ɖeka le eŋkume fifia.
narrative_ga: Ofainɛ, mɛwɔ accident report ɛ ni baa wɔ Yendi Senior High School. Nyɛ mli dzɛ Issah, na student bɛ yɛn, Dela, pragyia twe shi wɔ school entrance — ni obumo wɔ ha fɛɛ otsɛ obaa hɔ lɛ oshie. Oblɛ nyɛ, na gboo ehii mi, bo oni le, shwe etsotso kɛ bo ŋɔ bii. Yɛ ji oba tswe wɔ ha, na onipa bɛ ni ni bo wiemɔ lɛ tsɔŋ.
narrative_dagbani: N daa, n bɛ yɛ ni kɔligu accident din kul ka o bɛ Yendi Senior High School. N yuli di la Issah, ka din yɛli ni ti bi kuli yini — Dela — pragyia gbɛ o, ka o gbini, o bɛ chɔ suɣulo Yendi Senior High School zuɣu nyɛŋ doo o be ka o bɛ zaa yibu yini o. O bi yɛligu o tiŋ, ama o nyɛ o nyɛla, ka o ziim ka bee o ka la yaɣa. Ti kuli o kaa tɔbu, ka niya doo bee o la.
narrative_fante: Me pa wo kyew, mepɛ sɛ meka accident bi ho asɛm a ɛsii ha wɔ Yendi Senior High School. Me din de Issah, na yɛn student baako, Dela, pragyia bɔɔ no wɔ school entrance hɔ saa anɔpa yi — na ɔretwam baa mu no na ɛsii saa. Ɔyɛ abofra kumaa bi, na ɔnan no yɛ no yaw, nanso ɔhunu n'adwene na mogya retu no yiye koraa. Yɛama no atenase na obi wɔ ne nkyɛn seesei.
narrative_gurene: "Please, I want to report an accident that happened here at Yendi Senior High School. My name is Issah, and one of our students, Dela, was knocked down by a pragyia just at the school entrance when he was crossing to come in this morning. It's a young boy, and he's hurt on his leg but he's conscious and not bleeding too badly. We've made him sit down and someone is with him now."

narrative_en: "Please, my name is Appiah, I'm calling from Winneba Senior High School. One of our girls, Naa Dedei, she slipped on the staircase near the dormitory block and fell down, she hit her head on the edge of the step. She's conscious and talking but she's holding her neck and saying it's paining her small. It doesn't look too serious but we want to be sure."
narrative_ewe: Meɖekuku, ŋkɔnyé enye Appiah, meƒo wò ƒo ɖe Winneba Senior High School. Ɖevi nyɔnu ɖeka le ŋutɔ míawo, Naa Dedei, ewu ƒu le ataƒo ƒo ɖo te kɔ dormitory block la ŋu, eyram edu ƒu, ta xɔ asi ataƒo ƒo ŋkɔŋlɔ dzí. Eɖo edzi, le gblɔm nu, gake ele ɖo ŋu atsɔ asi, egblɔa be eyi nam ɖe ɖo gɔ. Enyo ŋutɔ didi o, gake míaɖi le ŋu be míabe nyuie le nu la ŋu.
narrative_ga: Ofainɛ, mi din bi Appiah, mifre aba Winneba Senior High School. Yɛ minu baa ŋ, Naa Dedei, oshwe kɛ staircase ni wo dormitory block faako, ebumo, obuɔ ŋmɛi ko step edge zuɔ. Oni le kɛ okan bi — enyɛ gbɛ o — kɛ okyɛ ŋ gbɔŋ neck ni ehii ŋ kakra. Enimɔ nyɛ kɛkɛ fofoi o, kɛ yɛhii sɛ yɛni nyɛ sure.
narrative_dagbani: "N daa, m yuli di Appiah, m bɛ yɛligu Winneba Senior High School. Ti pɔbiɛla ayi doŋ, Naa Dedei, o kpɛŋ stairs zuɣu ka o gbini ka o ŋun pɛɛ step nyɛbuli zuɣu. O nyɛra bee o ka o yɛlim ka o nuu kpɛm o yi diɣili zuɣu, o wuhim ka a yɛligu o daa biɛɣu. Ka yɛligu ka diɣe ka gbina ŋani — ama ti bori la o bori nyɛri dɔɣite."
narrative_fante: Me pa wo kyew, me din de Appiah, mereka firi Winneba Senior High School. Yɛn mmaabaa baako, Naa Dedei, ɔhwee fam wɔ stairs no so a ɛbɛn dormitory block no, na ɔtuu fam, na ɔtiri buiw steps no ho. Ɔte hɔ na ɔrekasa, na ɔnkolapuse, nanso ɔrekyere ne kɔn na ɔse eye ne yaw kakra. Enye dee eye hu papa, nanso yɛpɛ sɛ yɛhunu sɛ ɔho yɛ.
narrative_gurene: N tɩɩma, m yʋʋrɩ Appiah. M bɔɔrɩ fɔŋ Winneba Senior High School. Poɣa bilbiisi yenno, Naa Dedei, o bɔɔrɩ fa nyɛŋ zug dormitory block ninsaliŋ kʋŋ baŋ, o kpɛŋ fam, o ŋun bɔɔrɩ fa step gɔŋ baŋ. O ko a nyɛ yɛla, o goam dɩŋ — ama o zɔɣɔ o nori, o yɛta o yɛligu poli poli. Ka a yɛligu daa baa gure dɩŋ, ama ti bɩ yɩ a dɩma la — ti bɔrɩ a nyɛ zuum.

narrative_en: "Please, I'm calling about something very serious that just happened here at Ho Senior High School. My name is Yakubu, I'm one of the teachers. Our school cook, Elinam — she's an old woman — was crossing the compound near the dining hall when the school bus reversed suddenly and knocked her down. She hit her head on the ground very hard and her neck looks very bad. She's breathing but she's not responding properly when we call her name, and there's blood from her head. Please we need help to come quickly — she is old and the situation is very serious."
narrative_ewe: Meɖekuku, mefrɛ wo afi nu bubu aɖe si wòwɔ sɔgbe fia kple eya sia — ele Ho Senior High School. Yeyem nye Yakubu, meŋu nutsu bubuwo ŋu le school la me. Ɖevitɔ si le school la me, Elinam — nyɔnu srɔ̃ nye — le eya wòtso le compound la me afi si le dining hall kplim ko, akɔ si nye school bus la gbɔ tsi ɣeyiɣi, eye edome ko, akɔ la tsɔ ame la ƒo, eye ewu ƒu. Eta ƒo ƒu ɖoƒo la ŋutɔ nyuie nyuie, eye eƒe ɖo le nyo o. Eƒu agbe, gake megake wofrɛ eƒe ŋkɔ la, eɖo edzi nyuie o. Dzi le etsotso le eƒe ta ŋu. Meɖekuku, miɖi kpɔ mi kaba kaba — nye srɔ̃ ne, eye nu la le dzo nyuie.
narrative_ga: Ofainɛ, metsɔ phone bi frɛ ŋ shikpon yɛ kɛ shi bi oshɛɛ dɔ kɛ lɛ oshɛɛ — ni happen wɔ Ho Senior High School. Nyɛ Yakubu, mɛhɛ teacher wɔ school bi ni. Wɔ school cook, Elinam — gbɔmɔ bi — ɔhɛɛ tɔ compound bi shi, dining hall kɛ bo, school bus bi reversɛ fɛɛ kɛ ɔbumo. Ɔŋmɛi bumo fɛɛ wɔ ground bi jɛ kɛ too nyɛŋ, kɛ ɔnɛkɛ gbɔɔ shi ehii kɛ lɛ ni. Ɔhɔmɔ le, kɛ lɛ shɛ wɔ frɛ onyɛ name bi, ɔni nimɔ gbɛ pɛpɛ — ɔni respond o. Shwe etsotso ɔŋmɛi shi. Ofainɛ, yɛ hiɛ help omli kɛ lɛ — gbɔmɔ bi lɛ kɛ situation bi shikpon yɛ.
narrative_dagbani: N daa, m bɛ yɛ ni. M frɛ ni bɔri ka m nyɛla chɔli yɛla mɔɣu din kpɛŋ ka ŋun yɛ la ka din be ka Ho Senior High School. M yuli la Yakubu, m nyɛ dii shɛli yɛla o wuhirili ka. Ti nyɛla ŋun daa, Elinam — o nyɛ kpɛma — o bɛ nyɛŋa ka o bɛ dining hall siŋ din ni ka school bus o zaɣi ka o kpɛŋ o zaɣi zaa. O ŋun kpɛma ka yɛli din chɛ doo, ka o koli nyɛ nyɛla mɔɣu. O gbim bee kana, ama ka ti frɛ o yuli ka o mɔɣu yɛligu. Ka ziim bee o ŋun zuɣu. N daa, yi bori ka sɔŋ yi wa nyɛla ka yi wa nyɛŋɔbu — o nyɛ kpɛma ka yɛla nyɛ mɔɣu naa.
narrative_fante: Me pa wo kyew, merefrɛ fa biribi a ɛyɛ den paa a ɛaba aha wɔ Ho Senior High School. Me din de Yakubu, meyɛ ɔkyerɛkyerɛfo baako wɔ sukuu yi mu. Yɛn sukuu aduane noa, Elinam — ɔyɛ opanyin — ɔretwam compound no mu bɛn dining hall no ho, na sukuu bas no san kohwe n'akyi abruptly ma ɛbɔɔ no. Ɔtuu fam ma ɔtuu n'atirim wɔ fam mu den paa, na n'kɔn no hwe sɛ ɛyɛ bad paa. Ɔrehome, nanso sɛ yɛfrɛ n'din a, ɔnhunu hwee yiye. Na mogya retu firi n'atirim. Me pa wo kyew, yɛhia mmoa mmra ntɛm — ɔyɛ opanyin na situation no yɛ serious paa.
narrative_gurene: N tɩɩma, m frɛ fo sabab somɩŋa pɛlga bilɩŋ wʋla ŋa da maalɩ ka a Ho Senior High School. M yʋʋrɩ Yakubu, m yɛ school pam nɩ. Ti school pam ŋma, Elinam — o yɛ nindaan — o da be o kɔŋɔ dining hall nɔŋɔ nɩ ka school bus bɔɔrɩ za o kpɛŋ fam. O ŋun kpɛŋ fam nyɛŋ pam bilɩŋ, ka o ŋun dɔɔŋɔ tara yɛligu mɩŋa. Ziim bee o ŋun nɩ. O yɛ fo nyɛmɩ, la ka ti yɛ frɛ o yʋʋrɩ ka o bɛ nyɛ sum nɔŋɔ. N tɩɩma, sɔŋ mam yɩtɩ yɩtɩ — o yɛ nindaan, ka a zɔɣu yɛ pɛlga bilɩŋ.

narrative_en: "Please, I'm calling about a young lady who got knocked by an okada just near the main gate here at UPSA campus in Accra. My name is Amoah, I'm a lecturer here, and Yaa is one of our first-year students. She was trying to cross the road after lectures and the okada rider clipped her — she fell and hit her head on the ground but she's conscious and talking. Her neck is a bit stiff and there's a small cut on her head, but she doesn't seem too badly hurt."
narrative_ewe: Meɖekuku, meɖe ŋkɔ hã na wò — nyɔnu ɖevi ɖeka wòkpɔ accident ɖe UPSA campus ƒe ʋɔ ŋu, fó ze ŋutɔ gate la ŋutɔ afi a, Accra. Motor — okada — wo la ko ne eƒu. Ŋkɔnyе nye Amoah, manye lecturer afi sia, eye Yaa nye miƒe ŋɔli ame ɖeka — efirst year student. Elee ʋɔ ƒe ɖo ɖe akɔ ʋɔ si wò lecture vɔ la megbe, eye okada rider la kp̃o ne etsi ene — ewu ƒu, eƒe ta ko dom ƒu ƒu ƒe dzi. Gake eɖo edzi hã eye etso gble. Eƒe ɖo le didi didi kpui, eye ta ta ŋu le gbubu dzetse, gake mele nudzadzra dzo gbã o le emegbea.
narrative_ga: Ofainɛ, mibɔ frɛ eba dɛbi nyɛ — female student — oshi okada klii bɔ eha UPSA campus main gate kɛ. Miŋ dzɛ Amoah, mibɔ lecturer ha, eye Yaa yɛ wɔ first-year student. Oshi bie kɛɛ enɔ kpee shi lɛ wɔ lectures yɛɛ fii, shikpon okada rider klii bɔ shi gbɔɔ — ebumo eye ŋmɛi bɔ fɛɛ tɛtɛ ground shi. Chale, oni le eye oshi kasa, enimɔ gbɛ o — oshi kasa no. Kɛ ŋmɛi blaa gbɛŋ bɔ gbɔŋ kakra, eye shwe etsotso kakra ŋmɛi shi, kɛ enyaa hii kɛkɛ o — enaa too bad o.
narrative_dagbani: N daa, m bɛ frɛ ŋ doŋ biɛla bia yɛla. Kaɣa biɛla — okada — a nyɔɣi o ka o kpɛŋ kɔŋ UPSA campus gate kɔɣim din bɛ Accra. M yuli la Amoah, m bɛ lecturer yiŋ kɔŋ din, ka Yaa o bɛ ti first-year student yɛla. O bɛ lee nyɛŋ daa ni kpɛm lecture ti kata wuhiri ka okada rider nyɔɣi o — o kpɛŋ ka o ŋun gbɔlɔ saɣim, amaa o nyɛ o maa n-niŋ, o bee kuli. O wɔrbu bɛ baɣiri daa biɛɣu, ka ziim biɛɣu ɣeya o ŋun ni, amaa o daa nyin ka o laaɣi pam biɛɣu.
narrative_fante: Me pa wo kyew, merefrɛ wo fa ɔbaa ketewa bi ho — okada bɔɔ no fã UPSA campus ha Accra, kãkrã main gate no nkyɛn. Me din de Amoah, meyɛ lecturer ha, na Yaa yɛ yɛn first-year student baako. Ɔrɛkɔ twa ɔkwan no akyi lectures desɛ ɔkɔ fie, na okada rider no bɔɔ no — ɔtuu fam na ɔtuu ne tiri guu fam. Nanso ɔte hɔ na ɔreka asɛm. Ne kɔn no yɛ den kakraa, na wɔawo no tiri so kakra, nanso ennyɛ den papa.
narrative_gurene: N tɩɩma, m bɔbɔ bee accident — pɔɣa biig n okada bɔgɩ o UPSA campus gate poore Accra. M yɛ Amoah, m yɛ lecturer ka hali. Yaa yɛ wa bi biis din ƴɛrɛ ayenno ka n be hali. O daa nɩɩrɩ ka o bɔ nyɛŋ poore lectures zugɔ, ka okada wɛla o — o bɔɔrɩ ka o ŋun bɔ fam. Ama o ko a nyɛ daa, o gom bee o daa. O zuu yɛɛ fɛɛ biisi, ka o ŋun bee kpila biig, ama o zɩ ta yɛligu yɩɩga dɔɔ.

narrative_en: "Please I want to report something that happened here at Cape Coast University — one of our guys, Sulemana, nearly drowned in the swimming pool during a recreational swim this afternoon. He went in with some friends but he struggled and went under for a bit before they pulled him out. Kweku is the one who dragged him to the edge, so he's the one who called me to make this report. Sulemana is conscious now and he says his arm is paining him, probably from how they grabbed him when they were pulling him out. It's not serious serious but we think someone should come check him."
narrative_ewe:
Meɖekuku, meɖi gbɔ nu siwo wòwò afisia ɖe Cape Coast University — ame ɖeka le ŋumedzrɔwɔ ŋu, Sulemana, éyɔ̃ atiketi kple sii eƒe mitɔ̃wo nye ɖe agbadza aɖe ɖe egble ŋu gbãtɔ. Wòyi ɖe ame ɖevo wo kple, gake étsɔ asi vɔ̃ ɖe eme eye woɖo eƒu ɖekeke esime yewo wɔe abe ame le atiketi me ene. Kweku nye ame si dra eya ɖo ɖe fa ŋu, nenye Kweku a, éfrɛ ŋɔe ble be mɔ gbɔ nu.

Sulemana ɖo edzi fifia, gake ebe asi yi eyi nam — ebe wotsɔ eya be woɖo eya ɖe fa ŋu eƒe ʋuʋu ɖeke kpɔ nam. Mele sɔ̃ sɔ̃ bubu o, gake míeli be ame ɖeka na va kpɔ ɛ.
narrative_ga:
"Ofainɛ, mɛ shia report bii ni dɔ fɛɛ ha Cape Coast University — ni yɛ mɔ ni wɔ mi, Sulemana, ɛkɛ drown ni swimming pool lɛ wɔ shishi afternoon. Ebumo pool lɛ shi kɛ n'adɛŋ wɔ bii, ni ehii ŋ mli, ebumo down wɔ mlɔmɔ mli nɛ wɔ yaŋ ji o. Kweku ŋ ni yaŋ ji obumo edge lɛ shi, ɛ nyɛ ŋ ni frɛ mi ni mɛ ji report lɛ.

Sulemana oni le nɛ — eni gbɛ o — ni oshi mi shia ni n'gbɔ ehii ŋ, probably fɛɛ ni wɔ ji ŋ gbɔ kɛ force wɔ n'yaŋ ji time lɛ. Enyɛ serious bebele o, kɛ mi lɛ mi suɔ ni doctor bii n'aba lɛ ŋ check Sulemana."
narrative_dagbani:
N daa, n bɛ yɛ ni n yɛl'ɛ yɛla bi mani o Cape Coast University — o yɛltɔɣa yini bee ti, Sulemana, o nearly la dini swimming pool pɔ yɛsim sabab'a recreation swim ŋun o daa kɔ o so yi. O kɔ pɔ ni o yɛltɔɣba kpɛŋ pɔ, amaa o nyɛ yɛligu n'o kuli kuli kpɛŋ nuu lɔɣi, kana a ku'o pɔ baaŋ o yiyi o. Kweku la bee o yiyi o n dii o kpɛŋ bini ni, k'a bee Kweku la frɛ maa n kɔŋ yɛla ŋuna n yɛl'ɛ yɛla bi mani.

Sulemana bee o soli daa, amaa o yɛra o nu yɛligu — ka yɛligu o nuu — sabab'a bee yoo bee o naa o yiyi o tuma. O yɛla ka tɔɣ yɛligu nyan n yɛra, amaa ti nyɛ n bori la dɔɣite yini o bo n kɔ o lɛ.
narrative_fante:
Me pa wo kyew, mepɛ sɛ mebɛka biribi a ɛbaa ha wɔ Cape Coast University — ɔbarimaa bi a yɛfrɛ no Sulemana, ɔbɛhwɛɛ sɛ ɔbɛbɔ danum wɔ swimming pool no mu dɔɔso wɔ ɔkyena yi. Ɔkɔɔ mu ne nnamfonom bi, nanso ɔhwɛɛ sɛ ɔreyɛ struggling, na ɔkɔɔ ase tiaa tiaa, na wɔtuu no ase na wɔde no baa abɔn. Kweku na ɔde no tuu kɔɔ pool no ho, ɛno nti ɔfrɛɛ me sɛ meba brɔ me report yi.

Sulemana, ɔtee no anaa — na ɔka sɛ nsa no yɛ no yaw, efisɛ saa ara na wɔde tuu no na wɔreyɛ sɛ wɔrebɛtwe no aba. Enye yaw kɛseɛ biara, nanso yɛsusuw sɛ ɛsɛ sɛ oduruyɛfoɔ bi bra bɛhwɛ no.
narrative_gurene: "Please I want to report something that happened here at Cape Coast University — one of our guys, Sulemana, nearly drowned in the swimming pool during a recreational swim this afternoon. He went in with some friends but he struggled and went under for a bit before they pulled him out. Kweku is the one who dragged him to the edge, so he's the one who called me to make this report. Sulemana is conscious now and he says his arm is paining him, probably from how they grabbed him when they were pulling him out. It's not serious serious but we think someone should come check him."

narrative_en: "My name is Amankwah, I'm a student at Koforidua Technical University. So during our practical session in the workshop today, one of my classmates, Emefa, was operating a grinding machine and somehow a piece of metal flew off and hit her on the side of the head. She's conscious and talking but there's some swelling and she said her neck is stiff and painful. It's not like she's unconscious or anything serious like that, but we're worried because the swelling keeps getting bigger and she's saying she feels dizzy."
narrative_ewe: "Nyem nye Amankwah, mele student em Koforidua Technical University. Ne miaɖo practical session ɖe workshop egɔme egbɔ, classmate aɖe le ŋunye, Emefa, na operating grinding machine, eye nutitiwo la ƒe ɣedze aɖe pɛ kpɔ asi, ewu ta ɣeyiɣi dzi. Eɖo edzi eye ele gblɔm, gake swelling le eŋu, eye dzo ebe ɖo yi eŋu eye eyi nam. Abe ege la nye eɖo edzi o alo nudzodzo bubu aɖeke o, gake miaƒe wɔwɔ le ŋunye esi swelling la le gbãgbã wɔwɔm, eye ele gblɔm abe eƒe ta le yim yim."
narrative_ga: Hɛloo, name mi yɛ Amankwah, student ni mɛ Koforidua Technical University. So tɛ practical session yɛ workshop ni wɔ yɛ hii ojoŋ, classmate ni mɛ, Emefa, ɔ yɛ operate grinding machine, na somehow metal piece kɛ fly off kɛ fɛɛ ni ŋmɛi side ni ɔ. Oni le, ɔ kasa dɛ, shikaŋ swelling bi wɔ hɛ na ɔ bii ni neck ni ɔ stiff na ehii ni ɔ. Enimɔ gbɛ wɔ o, kɛ shi bi le yenaŋ, shikaŋ swelling no ɛ bi bigger bi bigger dɛ yɛ wɔ worry, na ɔ bii ni dizzy ɔ wɔ fee.
narrative_dagbani: "Allo, m yɔɣiri Amankwah, n kuli ka Koforidua Technical University pam. Zaɣa, ti practical session wɔribu, ti workshop pam, n classmate yɔɣiri Emefa, o mini grinding machine wɔribu, ka metal biɛla yini o diri gbini ka o kpɛŋ o ŋun dakali. O nyɛ o yɛligu, o bi ti soli, o guri kpaɣibu — ama o ni daa din ka o kpinsiɣu ka o kpinsiɣu yɛligu n daa. Ka biabiɛla kuli bɛ diri woribu din o ŋun biɛbu bee n daa zaɣari zaɣari, ka o ni daa din o ti soli-soli n bɛ o pam."
narrative_fante: Me din de Amankwah, meyɛ student wɔ Koforidua Technical University. Enti ɛnnɛ yɛn practical session wɔ workshop no mu, me classmate baako, Emefa, ɔreseɛ grinding machine, na ɛhɔ iron kakra bi pue firiw kohwie no wɔ ne tiri fa so. Ɔnim hunu na ɔrekasa, nanso n'anim agu ho na ɔkɔmm sɛ ne kɔn yɛ duru na eye no yaw. Ɛnyɛ sɛ ɔkolapuse anaa hwee saa, nanso yɛsuro efisɛ swelling no rekɔ so kɔ na ɔreka sɛ n'anim reper no.
narrative_gurene: M yɩɩrɩ Amankwah. M sɔŋa student Koforidua Technical University. Woto rɛ, tɩ practical session workshop pʋɔ n'yɛnda woto zaŋ, m classmate yɩɩrɩ Emefa, o bee grinding machine sɔŋɔ, ka zaam nɛ alaŋa metal yɩra o ŋun nɛ yɛlɛ fɛɛ. O ko nyɛ la, o goama la — bɛ, kɔ yɛligu bee o ŋun nɛ ka yɛlɛ doo, ka o yɩɩma o nɛɛrɛ ka yɛligu n'ara a poore a zuɣu. Ka bɛ zɔŋ a "o ko a nyɛ" la, bɛ a kɔ yela tɩŋ la — bɛ, tɩ sʋɔra ka a kɔ yɛligu bee o ŋun nɛ ka gɛŋɛ n'ara la n'ani yaa n'ara, ka o yɩɩma la o nyɛ a soro bee o.

narrative_en: "Please, I'm calling about my colleague Nii Armah — something happened to him this afternoon at T.I. Amass here in Kumasi. We were in the science lab doing our practical and one of the Bunsen burners kind of flared up and caught his arm. It's not too deep, just the lower part of his forearm, but the skin is red and he's saying it's painful. Selorm is my name, I'm his labmate, I just want to know what we should do for him right now before we take him to the clinic."
narrative_ewe: Meɖekuku, mefrɛ ŋuwò le Nii Armah dome — nudzadzra le si wòe etso eŋu egbe fiafi la kple ema kɔ T.I. Amass fii Kumasi. Míele science lab dzi le míewo practical kple ema, eye bunsen burner ɖeka gblẽ abe nuku aɖe bena xɔ tɔtɔe, eye wo da azu ɖo Nii Armah ƒe asi dzi. Eyata mekpe nyanya ka o, ele asi ƒe ɖawo ŋɔ ko — gake ŋku le woƒe asi ƒe ɖa dzi le dzi gbã, eye eyi enam, ewɔ xexlẽdzesi. Ŋkɔnyem enye Selorm, míekpọ kple ema labmate dzi — megblɔ ko bena míewɔ ŋunye ade nàm na eya fifia, eye míetso eyi yi clinic va gbɔ.
narrative_ga: Ofainɛ, mitsɔ phone bi frɛ wɔ Nii Armah shikpon — bɔ ehi naŋ ni etsɔɔ le afternoon wɔ T.I. Amass ha Kumasi. Yɛ lɛ science lab shi yɛ practical naŋ Bunsen burner kɛ eheeo, ekɔ ni otɔ Nii Armah gbɔ. Enyo deep paaa o — gbɔ ni lɛ lower part, forearm shi — kɛ skin bi etsɛɛ red, kɛ ohe kɛ ehii ni. Selorm bi nyɛ mi dzin — mi labmate bi nyɛ mi, yɛ lɛ labmates. Mifee shɔ yɛni ni yɛ dɔ aniŋ tsɔ ni aha ni lɛ clinic eŋ — sɔɔ yɛ ni ba ni naŋ ni?
narrative_dagbani: N daa, m bɛ yɛ ni m frɛ ade din kuli kpɛŋ m zo Nii Armah zuɣu — din kuli di paɣa o nyɛ daba bi ni T.I. Amass Kumasi. Ti bɛ din science lab pam n ti ti do practical din wɔri, ka Bunsen burner yini gbini ka o zuu ka o nuu — o nuu suɣu biɛla din kuli, n yɛl ba o nuu nifɔŋ bila. Ka din ka chɛ pam daabiɛla, la o nuu nifɔŋ bila sheŋ din kpahim, ka o ni nuu ka a zuu la o, ka o yɛlim o yɛligu. M yuli la Selorm, m bɛ ti lab din kuli ni o. M bɔri ban m sɔŋ mi — ti ka mi ban din ti bɛ wula be ma o zuɣu ama tiri o ka ti gba clinic.
narrative_fante: Me pa wo kyew, merefrɛ wo fa me colleague Nii Armah ho — biribi si no so nnɛ akyirikyiri wɔ T.I. Amass ha wɔ Kumasi. Yɛ ne yɛ wɔ science lab no mu yɛɛ yɛn practical na Bunsen burner baako mu flare up na ɛhyee ne nsa famu. Ɛnyɛ duru paa — efi ne forearm famu honam na honam no aba hyew na ɔreka sɛ eye no yaw. Me din de Selorm, meyɛ ne labmate. Mepɛ sɛ menim deɛ yɛbɛyɛ no ma no seesei ara ansa na yɛde no kɔ clinic no.
narrative_gurene: N tɩɩma, m frɛ fo bee Nii Armah — o la bee la a yɛligu a yɛ yɛŋa yi T.I. Amass Kumasi. Ti zɔŋ ti bɛ science lab pʋɔ, ti la bɛ ti practical tuma, la Bunsen burner baŋ yenno ŋmɩɩnɩ la a gbɛɛnɩ, la a zɩɩlɩm o nↄↄrɩ. Ka a da la ziim a kperɩ, a la o nↄↄrɩ tɛrɩ fam so — la o kpɩŋ la a yɛligu, la a zuɣu a la gʋdom. Yɩ nↄↄrɩ a bɔɔrɩ la boa, ka a ka a kɩsɩ. M yɩrɩ la Selorm, m la o lab bia. M la gɔ bɛ a yɛla — bɩ la ti sɔŋ o nↄↄrɩ ɩ zɔŋ yi la a tɩ la, bɩ la ti mɩŋ o wa ka clinic?

narrative_en: "Please, I'm calling from Kumasi Academy. My name is Wiredu. Something happened during break time — two of our students were on bicycles near the sports block and they collided trying to avoid each other, and Afia fell and landed badly on her arm. She's okay, she's conscious and talking, but her arm is swollen and she's wincing when she tries to move it. We don't think it's broken but we want someone to come and check her."
narrative_ewe: Meɖekuku, mefrɛ ɖe Kumasi Academy. Ŋkɔnyé nye Wiredu. Nudzadzra aɖe wɔa ɖe break time me — ametowo eve, yɔna woƒe ɖevinyɔ̃nyɔ̃wo eve, wòle bicycle dzi ɖa fɔfɔ sports block dzi, eye woƒo woɖe ɖeɖe hã, gake Afia edo ƒu, eye ewo ɖe asiƒe nyuitɔ o. Eɖo edzi, ele nu ɖɔɖɔ, gake asiƒe le na nyitso, eye xɔ va ɖe asinye la, eye eyi nam. Abe woɖe ɣleti vɔ o o ene, gake meɖi ame aɖe va kpɔ ame ŋuwɔwɔ.
narrative_ga: Ofainɛ, mitsɛɛ yɛ Kumasi Academy. Nyɛ dzin bi Wiredu. Bɔ bi ko kɛ break time — atsɛɛwa ni ahi ŋ mli bicycles hɛ sports block kɛŋkɛŋ, na akɛ avoid each other eha wɔ na Afia bumo, eji gbɔ wɔ fɛɛ wiemɔ. Enyemi le, oni le naŋ etoo, kɛɛ gbɔ no shi swollen na wani try move no, ehii mi eko. Wɔ sɔŋ ni fracture o, kɛɛ wɔfɛ ni onii bi wa jɛjɛ check no.
narrative_dagbani: N daa, m frɛ la Kumasi Academy kɔŋ. N yuli di Wiredu. Ka saɣim paɣisi nyin bee la — yi biɛhigu ayi bɛ nam bicycle baŋ sports block kɔŋ, ka di bɔri ka ban gari dama, ka Afia gbini ka o kpɛŋ o nuu ni daa. O ni nyɛ, o mɔɣim la ka o guri la, amma o nuu ti yɛligu la ka o sogi nuu doli ka o nyɛ zaɣa. Ti gba niŋ o nuu kpuuu, amma ti bori la dɔɣite o n ti di nyin ka o nyɛ.
narrative_fante: Me pa wo kyew, merefrɛ wo firi Kumasi Academy. Me din de Wiredu. Biribi baa so wɔ break time mu — yɛn nkurɔfoɔ baanu no wɔ bicycles so wɔ sports block ho, na wɔbɛhyiaw wɔ wɔn ho ho, na Afia tuu fam na ɔhwee fam yiye wɔ ne nsa so. Ne ho yɛ, ɔte hɔ na ɔrekan nkɔm, nanso ne nsa ahyew na sɛ ɔpɛ sɛ ɔsisi no a, ɛyɛ no yaw. Yɛnhunu sɛ fracture bi wɔ hɔ, nanso yɛpɛ sɛ obi bɛba na ɔhwɛ no.
narrative_gurene: N tɩɩma, m bee frɛ fo Kumasi Academy. M yʋʋrɩ Wiredu. Bɛ yɛla a zaa kpɛŋ a nugori yɛla nɩ — a yɛŋa biisi ayuo la bee yɩɩrɩ motor bicycle dɔɔ sports block kɔŋa nɩ, la ka a yua nyɛ a zaa saɣm a zaa, ka Afia bɔɔrɩ la o yɛsa nɩ ka o nɩɩ naboŋo n yɛ fam poɣa. O bee sɔŋa, o ko a nyɛ la o bee gom, la ka o nɩɩ naboŋo ŋoa la nɩɩ yɛligu, la ka o tɩŋ a gori a ŋmaŋa a poɣa, ka yɛligu sɩɩŋa o. Ti naa nyɛ tɩ bɩ yɛ sɔŋ la o nɩɩ naboŋo tɔrɩ, la ka ti sɔŋ sɛb fo tʋma la bi yenno wa wa lɔɣɩ la laa ka o.

narrative_en: "Please, my name is Issah, I'm calling from Adisadel College here in Cape Coast. Alhassan was coming back from town in a dropping taxi that got clipped by a car near the school gate — he fell off but he's okay, just some scrapes and a bruise on his leg. He's sitting down now and he can move it, so we don't think it's broken or anything serious like that. We just want to make sure he gets checked properly because he's still shaking a little."
narrative_ewe: Meɖekuku, ŋkɔnya nye Issah, mefrɛ mi afisia le Adisadel College le Cape Coast. Alhassan le ava le tornu le dropping taxi dzi, akɔ ɖeka ɖa akɔ aɖe vɔ esi ele egbe dɔwɔƒe ŋɔ la — ewu ƒu, gake le gbe nyui me, abe scrape aɖewo kple anyi ɖeka le eƒe afɔ dzi ene. Ele ɖo fifia la kple eƒe afɔ, ate ŋu agblɔgblɔ na, ne miƒo ta la, midzina to ŋku le ɣleti vɔ alo nu gãgã aɖe ŋu o. Miaɖi be Alhassan ni wɔ checkup nyuitɔ, elabena eƒe ŋu le kpikpɛ aɖe le.
narrative_ga: Ofainɛ, me din bi Issah, mitsɛɔ fɛɛ Adisadel College shi Cape Coast. Alhassan obeŋ fɛɛ town ni dropping taxi kɔŋ, kaa fofoi ebui taxi no kɛ Adisadel gate koko — obumo, nii eŋ amɛ kɛ ehii ŋ bo, dɛŋ scrapes kakra shi ni gboo kɔŋ. Naa otsɔ bɔɔ bɔɔ ni, ni gboo no oni wiemɔ, eŋ yebu sumo ni fracture bi bi ni, wala serious bi amɛ. Yɛhɛ yɛmɔ ofainɛ doctor bi enɔ ni check mo Alhassan nyɛŋ, because oshake kakra ni yɛ le.
narrative_dagbani: N daa, nyɛ Issah la mi yɛligu. M bɛ frɛ ŋun ka Adisadel College, Cape Coast. Alhassan naa pam ka o daa pɛlim n di kaɣa kum o doo Adisadel College saɣim saɣim. Kaɣa yɛla daa kpɛŋ o, ka o gbini, ama o kuli n daa. O tiŋ kɔɣim bi yɛligu daa, ka o bɛ zaŋ ka bɔɣ o nɛɛ, o kpam bi gbinni. N ti nyɛ ka o nyɛ o maa yɛla la ni, ama o nyɛ ka o ti dɔɣili yɛla ni daa. O ti chɛ ka o zɛŋ daa zaa, ka o nyɛ ka o ti la a din bɛ pahi pahi. N bori la dɔɣite gbɛ o ka kpɛng o mali yɛlgu bee yaa.
narrative_fante: Me pa wo kyew, me din de Issah. Merɛfrɛ wo firi Adisadel College ha Cape Coast. Alhassan no, ɔresan firi town no, dropping taxi mu na car bi bɔɔ taxi no ho beaebi kɔse school gate no nkyɛn — ɔtuu fam, nanso ne ho yɛ, scrapes kakra na bruise bi wɔ ne nan so. Ɔtena ase seesei na ɔtumi yi ne nan, enti yennhunu sɛ ɛfracture anaa biribi a ɛsɛ saa. Yɛpɛ sɛ oduruyɛfo bi bɛhwɛ no yiye, efisɛ ɔrewosow kakra da ho ara.
narrative_gurene: N tɩɩma, m yʋʋrɩ Issah. M bee frɛ fo Adisadel College la Cape Coast. Alhassan n daa pɛlɩ tɔɔna la a waɣa dropping taxi pʋɔ, ka kaɣa baŋa o nɩŋ school gate nɩ — o bɔɔrɩ fam, la o kɛŋ nyɛ, ka o yɛligu ka nɩŋ la o nɩŋ kpɩɛrɩ. O chɛŋ ka o pɔrɩ a nɩŋ la, a n wʋlɩ o nɩŋ ka a be yaga la. Ber ka tɩ nyɛ o nɩŋ ka a ka gbɛrɩ. O chɛŋ ka o zɔɣɩ sɛbɛ la wʋnɛ, la o siɛrɩ siɛrɩ ka o giri pam. Tɩ bʋɔrɩ ka o tɩ kɛŋ la hospital, la ba nɩ nyɛ ka o nɩŋ a dee la.

narrative_en: "Please, I want to report something very serious that happened here at Tamale Senior High School. I'm Nii Kwei, one of the teachers. One of our elderly kitchen workers — Yaa — was walking near the dining hall when the canteen delivery truck reversed without warning and hit her. Her leg looks very badly injured, it's twisted and there is a lot of blood. She is conscious but crying and shaking and she cannot move the leg at all. Please we need help to come here quickly because she is old and the leg is very serious."
narrative_ewe: Meɖekuku, meyi na wo bɔbɔ nu siwo wu ŋutɔ si wɔa afi sia, Tamale Senior High School ŋu. Nyeŋu nye Nii Kwei, ame aɖe le nu siwo ŋu hã. Agbenyaga aɖe si le vovotɔwo dome — Yaa — le yi ƒu abe dining hall abe katã la, akɔ si tsɔa nuidzidzi ɖo ɖe canteen la do egɔme, eye wotrɔ megbe eye ebu ame o, awɔ wo wɔm wu ewe le ŋgɔ. Eƒe afɔ nɔna kpɔ abe eyi wu ŋutɔ abe — etrɔa wɔwɔ, eye dzi le etsotso wu ŋutɔ. Eɖo edzi, gake edzi ŋu vu eye enɔna yoyom, eye mate ŋu trɔ eƒe afɔ la o, wɔa katã. Meɖekuku, miɖi kpɔ ŋunye, mi va afisia ngɔngɔ, abe eya la ye srɔ̃, eye eƒe afɔ la si wu ŋutɔ wu ŋutɔ.
narrative_ga: Ofainɛ, mɛ pɛ sɛ mɛ report bɔɔ ni ehii waa waa ni ehiɛ ha Tamale Senior High School. Mɛ nyɛ Nii Kwei, teacher kɛ wɔ ni. Gbɔmɔ kɛ wɔ work kitchen — Yaa — oba kɛ gbɛ dining hall shi, akɔ delivery truck ni canteen shɛɛ otumo yɛ warning nɔkɔ, abɔɔ no. Ogboo ni feemo waa waa — otwimu gbɛ, shwe etsotso, eni hii no kɛ gboo ŋ. Oni le, kɛ oyi owe kɛ osheke, kɛ ogboo ŋ, otumi bɔ o kpako. Ofainɛ, yɛ hia bo yɛ — mɛjɛ yɛ ba ha ha ha, kakra — bɔŋ onya gbɔmɔ, kɛ ogboo ŋ ehii waa. Frɛ ambulance ba ha wɔwɔ!
narrative_dagbani: "Allo, N daa, n bɛ yɛ ni ka nↄŋ maa yɛla kakari din kɔri ka be ka Tamale Senior High School. Maa yuli bi Nii Kwei, n nyɛ dolitaɣa daba boli yini. Nindaan din be n tɔɣirigu boli — Yaa — o nyɛla cham wuhigu wɔrigu din be dining hall kɔŋ, ka kaɣa din bi diri suɣuri din nyɛla delivery truck gbɛli o nↄŋ yɛlim, ka o kɔri ka darinli o ka zani. O tiŋ nyɛla ka yɛligu daa, o tiŋ kpɔrigu ka ziim bee o daa paɣida. O nyɛ nyɛla, amaa o taɣirima ka kpɛm ka o tiŋ bi kpɛŋ ka zaŋ. N daa, n bori sɔŋ, yi wa dↄↄri, bala o nyɛla nindaan ka o tiŋ yɛla miri daa."
narrative_fante:
Me pa wo kyew, mepɛ sɛ meka biribi a ɛyɛ den paa a ɛsii ha wɔ Tamale Senior High School. Me ne Nii Kwei, okyerɛkyerɛfo bi wɔ ha. Yɛn adwumayɛfo a ɔyɛ opanyin wɔ adidipon mu — Yaa — ɔnam ɔkwan so kɔ dining hall nkyɛn no, na canteen delivery truck no san kɔ ɛkyi a warning biara nni mu, na ɛbɔ no. Ɔnan no yɛ hu paa — ɛdan mu, na mogya retu paa. Ɔte ne ho dada, nanso ɔsu na ɔwosowoso, na ɔntumi nnane nan no koraa.

Me pa wo kyew, yehia mmoa mmrɛ yi ntɛm ntɛm, efisɛ ɔyɛ opanyin na ɔnan no yɛ den paa. Mboa yɛn!
narrative_gurene: N tɩɩma, m pɛ yɛlɩ fo kʋɔrɩ bi a yɛla a tee ka o yɛligu wʋlʋm, a bee kʋɔrɩ Tamale Senior High School. Mam yɛlɩ Nii Kwei, m yɛ ticher a bee kʋɔrɩ. Tɩ nindaan a bee nɛ kitchen — Yaa — o yɛ dɩɣa saɣa dining hall nɛ ka kaɣa a yɛ wubu kɔɔn a bee yiri zuɣu yɛlɩ o, ka o nyɛ o yɛligu. O nɩɩna a yɛligu wʋlʋm wʋlʋm — o kpɩɩsɩ ka o ziim ɣeya pam. O ko nyɛ a, o yɛ yɛligu ka o nyɛ sɔɔzɩ ka o yɛ gʋrɩ, ka o nan ka o nɩɩna koms ka o nyɛ tɩ. N tɩɩma, sɔŋ mam! Yi yi kʋɔrɩ ka yi wʋbɩ tee tee, bɔrɩ Yaa o yɛ nindaan ka o nɩɩna yɛla a tee ka o yɛligu wʋlʋm pam.

narrative_en: "My name is Naa, I'm calling from Tamale Technical University. My friend Abenaa — she's a student here — was crossing the car park near the engineering block when one of the campus buses clipped her as it was turning. She fell and hit her head on the ground, there's a small cut and her neck is sore but she's awake and talking. We want to know if we need to take her somewhere to get checked properly."
narrative_ewe: "Ŋkɔnyé nye Naa. Mefrɛ mi afi Tamale Technical University. Ƒoƒo ɖekanyé Abenaa — eya nye student afisia — ele ɖe akɔwo xɔme la gbɔ, afi engineering block tso la, gake campus bus ɖeka ɖi eŋu gã dzi be ele ɖe asixɔxɔ ƒe nu. Ewu ƒu eye eta ƒu ƒe ŋɔ. Yeyiɖi xɔ asi, eye ɖo le eyi enam kakaka, gake eɖo edzi eye egblɔa nu. Miɖi si be ele nam be mia-yi ŋu dɔ asiwo ɖe eŋu, abe be edzi bia ame ɣe egbe vevie la abe."
narrative_ga: Hɛloo, ŋ tɔŋ ŋ nyɛ Naa. Mi frɛ frɛ Tamale Technical University. Chale, ŋ wo Abenaa — student ye fɛ ŋ — enii etsɔɔ ko kaa park wɔ engineering block kɔ, akɔ campus bus bi ebuɔ nɛ wɔ etɛɛ oniŋ. Obumo, ŋmɛi omi kaa wiemɔ wɔ ground. Kɔ fɛ shwe etsetse susu — cut kakra — nɛ onyɛ nɛ ne lɔɔ ehii nɛ. Ashi oni le, eŋmɔŋ ekɔmi. Yɛ bɛ sɛkɛ yɛ ji ɔ hospital akɔ doctor bɛ check ɔ kɛ eko?
narrative_dagbani: "M yuli di Naa, n frɛ la a Tamale Technical University. M doms Abenaa — o nyɛ student dini — o bɛ di nyɛŋ mini kaɣa park bi diri engineering block kɔŋ, ani campus bus yini nini o kpiɣim ka o gbini. O gbini ka o ŋun kpɛŋ zuɣu, ziim biɛla bee o, ka o kɔŋ yɛligu, ama o mɛ nyɛ ŋmaa ka o goli. Ti bori la ŋ nyɛ, n bori la shɛli — a bori la ti kpe ti nyɛ yi o ni bia yini yini che o ni baŋ dama ka nuu nyɛla o ni?"
narrative_fante: Me din de Naa. Merefrɛ mo fi Tamale Technical University. Me friend Abenaa — ɔyɛ student wɔ ha — na ɔretu kwan wɔ car park a ɛbɛn engineering block no ho, na campus bus bi bɔɔ no fã a ɔne kar no redan. Ɔtuu fam na wɔ ne tiri bi kata a ɛketew kakra, na ne kɔn de ɛyɛ no yaw, nanso ɔda hɔ rekan asem. Yɛpɛ sɛ yɛhunu sɛ ɛsɛ sɛ yɛde no kɔ baabi na wɔhwɛ no yiye anaa.
narrative_gurene: "M yɩɩla Naa. M bee sɔŋ fo Tamale Technical University pʋɔ. M zoa Abenaa — o student la ka — o bee dɩɣ la car park soŋa, engineering block yiri, ka campus bus yengo o la o bee yiging. O bɔɔrɩ ka o ŋun kpɛŋ fam tɩɩ yiri, ŋun ziim yaga bʋɔya tɩɩ, ka o noore yɛligu — ama o nyɛ la, o bee kõ. Ti bʋɔya bɩ ti ŋɛ o yɛla yir soŋa, t'a nyɛ o la boɔrɛ?"

narrative_en: "Please, I'm calling to report something that happened here at Navrongo Senior High School. My name is Abenaa, I'm a teacher. Our school's maintenance pickup was moving through the compound towards the workshop and it clipped one of our PE teachers, Fuseini, as he was walking past. He's okay, not unconscious — just his leg is hurting him and there's a bruise there. He's sitting down now but we wanted someone to come and check him because he's saying the pain is not going down."
narrative_ewe: "Meɖekuku, mefrɛ ɖe asi le nudzadzra si wòwɔ afisia Navrongo Senior High School. Ŋkɔnyé nye Abenaa, nye sukpɔla. Mia sukuu ƒe maintenance pickup le ʋɔ dzi ɖe workshop gbɔ le agbale dzi, eye edɔ Fuseini — mia PE sukpɔla ŋɔ — gome le eƒe ŋkɔ si wòle ʋɔ dzi kɔ. Eɖo edzi — axɔ asi o — ke eƒe afɔ eyi nam ŋutɔ, eye ame le afɔa ŋɔ. Ele ame dzi fifia, ke mɔɖemɔɖe miele abe ame aƀɔ ƀɔ eŋu abe wo aɖe na va kpɔ eŋu ɔ, ɖoɖoe nye be eyi nam o le ɖeɖe."
narrative_ga: "Ofainɛ, mifra shikpon hɛloo bɔɔ akɛ mɛhɛ ni dɔɔŋ le Navrongo Senior High School ŋ. Nyɛ mi dzɛŋ yɛ Abenaa, miyɛ teacher. School ni maintenance pickup no tse kɔ compound ŋ akɔ workshop fɛɛ, na ekpee Fuseini — oyɛ PE teacher — gbɔɔ mli, esɔ ebe okpa. Enyemi le — enimɔ gbɛ o — kɛ ni gboo yɛ ehii nɔ na bruise dɔɔŋ le aba ŋŋ. Oshie le kɔ ha naanaa, kɛ yɛhɛ akɛ eba wɔ aŋɔ nɔ, ehiɛ atsɛɛ onimɔ ehii mli o."
narrative_dagbani: "N daa, m frɛ ka m yɛl' yin n bɛ la Navrongo Senior High School. M yuli di Abenaa, m ti mɔɣli. School din maintenance pickup bee sɔŋ nyɛŋ o bɛ compound pɔ, o gbiri workshop siɣibu, a o kpaga Fuseini, mɔɣli bi bɛ PE pɔ, o diri nyɛŋ o bɔri. O nyɛ la n daan bee — o ti soli ba — ka o tiŋ ka yɛligu o daa, ka gbana bɛ kɔ be lan. O chɛ zuli la diri, kaa m bori la o ti diri yɔɣu ni sɔŋmi o, din be ka o yɛl bee o siɣiri ka yɛligu be di buli ba."
narrative_fante: "Me pa wo kyew, merefrɛ de makyere saa nea asi ha wɔ Navrongo Senior High School. Me din de Abenaa, meyɛ teacher. Yen school no maintenance pickup retu kwan wɔ compound mu kɔ workshop hɔ, na ɛbuaa yen PE teacher baako, Fuseini, bere a ɔrewɔ akyi hɔ. Ɔho yɛ — ɔnkolapuse — na ɔnan nko na eye no yaw, na bruise bi wɔ hɔ. Ɔtedɔ ase seesei, nanso yɛpɛ sɛ obi bɛba ne ho nhwɛ, efisɛ ɔreka sɛ yaw no resa nkɔ."
narrative_gurene: "Please, I'm calling to report something that happened here at Navrongo Senior High School. My name is Abenaa, I'm a teacher. Our school's maintenance pickup was moving through the compound towards the workshop and it clipped one of our PE teachers, Fuseini, as he was walking past. He's okay, not unconscious — just his leg is hurting him and there's a bruise there. He's sitting down now but we wanted someone to come and check him because he's saying the pain is not going down."

narrative_en: "My name is Kwabena, I'm a student at Opoku Ware School in Kumasi. During our home economics practical this afternoon, one of the boys in my class — Baffour — accidentally brushed against a hot pot that had just been taken off the stove, and the heat caught his shirt and burned him on his stomach area. It's not too serious, the skin just looks red and a bit raw, but he's been crying and we're worried. The teacher sent me to report it straight away."
narrative_ewe: "Ŋkɔnym̃ nye Kwabena, meŋɔ sukuu le Opoku Ware School le Kumasi. Egbe efiafia, míele home economics practical wɔm le sukuu, eye ɖevi ɖeka le maɖem̃ — Baffour — wɔ nudzadzra ɖe kuɖo vɔɖi si woxɔ fiafia le apui dzi la ŋu, eye nufafa tso agbe ƒe dzi la wu eƒe ɖa, eye ewu afi si le eƒe abɔ ŋutame ɔɔ. Evivi ŋutɔ didi o — kuɖoe akɔ le ɣlɔ̃ kaka eye le eʋom xoxo, gake eyi nam, éle ŋugblẽm abe ɖevi si di asim le ene. Míeɖi nu ƒo eŋu eye míewɔm ŋutsɔtsɔ. Nufiala la ɖo mɛ be mate ŋu wɔ nu ɖe ŋunyo la."
narrative_ga: "Name mi yɛ Kwabena, mɛ yɛ student wɔ Opoku Ware School wɔ Kumasi. Ekoŋ gbɔɔ fɛɛ tɛɛ, home economics practical yɛn wɔ class — obi ŋmɛni Baffour — accident ni obutu hot pot bi, pot kɛ ba yɛ wɔ stove ashi ni wɔ faa. Ni heat no sii ofe shirt ashi ni efii ni burned shi mi. Enyɛ serious bebree, skin no keke yɛ red kɛ raw kakra, nɛ onyɛ nyɛ oblɛ enyeŋ kɔŋkɔŋ eko. Wɔ yɛ worried. Teacher no yaa mi ofainɛ mɛ ba report am straight away."
narrative_dagbani: "M yɛligu nyɛla Kwabena, mam student Opoku Ware School Kumasi. Tɔɣim yɛnda, n wuhigu class pam, ti bɛ bɛla practical wɔrigu — home economics. Bia yini ti classɣa, o nyɛligu Baffour, o nuu kɔhɣi pot diri, pot naa bee yɛnda ŋɔ o di ka ti dari tuma yili. Yɛnda naa o dɔligu o shim, ka o dɔɣiri o pam. Ka yɛligu n daa kpɛhɣi, ka ni guli bɛri mi — pihɣu bɛŋa la, ka o yiɣli kɔlibu n tɔɣim. Ka ti su o bee. Koolgu mba n noli o — teacher din tuma mam ka n bɔri yi sɔŋ."
narrative_fante: Me din de Kwabena, meyɛ student wɔ Opoku Ware School wɔ Kumasi. Nnɛ awia yi, yɛn home economics practical mu, baako wɔ me class mu — Baffour — ɔkaa hot pot bi so na wankae, na pot no yɛɛ dew paa na wɔabɛgye no fii stove no so, na ogya hoahoa no maa ne kyini huu no yaw wɔ ne yafunu ho. Ɛnyɛ kɛse ara — ne ho nhyɛn kɛkɛ na ehu red na ɛhaw kakra, nanso ɔrɛsu na yɛn ho yɛ hu. Yen teacher no somee me sɛ memmɛ bɔ amaneɛ no ntɛm ara.
narrative_gurene: M yɛla Kwabena, m yɛ student Opoku Ware School Kumasi. Gbãn dãna, ti kãa home economics practical, o biisi yi yenno — Baffour — o nii kpɛŋ pot bʋʋrɩ a kãa bʋʋrɩ, pot kɔ bɛ yɛligu bilim, ka yɛligu sɔ o sheti a bɔɔr o pʋʋrɛ zuɣu. Ka bɛ yɛlimdi pɔɔri bilim, sɩ ka o zɔɣɔ nyɛ bʋrbʋr bilim, ka fɩɩri tim, ama o bee wiim ka ti gãari. Ti kãtɩm lɛɛb nyɛ ka ninsaal sɛb mam ka n wa gɔ.

narrative_en: "My name is Alhassan, I'm calling about a young boy called Nii who got hit by a car just outside Achimota Senior High School here in Accra. He was trying to cross the road after school and a vehicle knocked him down very hard — his head hit the ground and he was not moving for some time. The neck area also looks very bad, we are afraid to move him. It is serious, please, he needs help fast."
narrative_ewe: "Allo! Nyeŋɔŋlɔ nye Alhassan. Mefrɛ ɖe ase abe ɖevi ɖeka wòɖe Nii si akɔ ɖe eŋu fɛm fa Achimota Senior High School ŋkɔme la ŋu. Eyi Accra. Ɖevi la le agbalẽ vɔ vɔm, eye ele Ʋɔ dzi la dzi ɖe akɔ ɖe eŋu teƒe — akɔ ɖe eŋu le tɔx̃e ŋu, eye eta wɔ ƒu ɣeyiɣi dzi ɣesɔ. Ede ɖe eɖo edzi o le blema si wo ɖekae. Ɖo ƒe dome noa ya nua ɖeka — mienye egbɔ ɖe eŋu la o, afɔŋtɔ o. Meɖekuku, eye meɖekuku kpɔ ŋunye — Nii di kpɔ akpakpa. Frɛ ambulance mi va ŋutɔ akpakpa!"
narrative_ga: Mii nyɛ Alhassan, mitsɔ frɛ amo oblɛ bi, ebi Nii — kaa bumo ni Achimota Senior High School ho kɛ ni Accra. Otsɔ kpee soo lɛ aŋm kɔ school nɔɔ, kaa bumo ni yawaazi — ŋmɛi ebumo fɛi fɛi. Ohe mi gbɛ nɔɔ, enyemi le na. Neck lɛ nɔɔ ehii yawaazi, yɛmidɛ wɔ ni ŋmɛ ni — yɛbɔ he ni mɔ. Chale, ehɛ lɛ keke — ofainɛ, frɛ ambulance yɛ ji. Oni hia boa ji ji!
narrative_dagbani: "M yɛligu Alhassan, m bɛ frɛ yɛ bee bia yidana, o yɛligu Nii. Kaɣa kpɛŋ o tia Achimota Senior High School kɔɣim ka dee Accra. O bɛ gbɛ nyɛŋ n yɔ ka kaɣa kpɛŋ o zaŋ paɣim — o ŋun gbini sɔɣim, ka o ti duɣ zupɔŋ. O nyɛ o maa yɛla ka zaa gbaa ka o ti lɛ ni. A nuu ŋun kpanduɣu kɔɣim bee ka a yɛligu daa paɣim, ti soribu o zaŋ nyɛ ka daɣi ka a ti kuli. Dimi suɣulo, n bori la sɔŋ paɣim — frɛ ambulance, frɛ dɔɣite. Ka bee yɛlgu! Yi maa sɔŋ!"
narrative_fante: Me din de Alhassan. Merefrɛ wo fa abofra bi ho — ne din de Nii. Kar bɔɔ no wɔ Achimota Senior High School anim ha Accra. Ɔreyɛ adwuma akɔ bra wɔ ɔkwan so wɔ sukuu akyiri no, na kar bi bɔɔ no yiye paa — ne tiri buiɛ fam maadaadaa, na ɔnnyinagyina hwee mpo. Ne kɔn no nso ehu hu — yɛnsuro sɛ yɛbɛ yi no so. Asɛm no yɛ den, me pa wo kyew — ɔhia mmoa ntɛm ara!
narrative_gurene: "My name is Alhassan, I'm calling about a young boy called Nii who got hit by a car just outside Achimota Senior High School here in Accra. He was trying to cross the road after school and a vehicle knocked him down very hard — his head hit the ground and he was not moving for some time. The neck area also looks very bad, we are afraid to move him. It is serious, please, he needs help fast."

narrative_en: "My name is Naa Shormeh, I'm calling about my friend Naa Akorkor who had an accident here at Bolgatanga Senior High School. She was coming down the staircase from the upper floor of the dormitory block and she just missed a step and fell, and she landed really hard on her side and her back. She's conscious and she can talk but she's saying there's a lot of pain around her ribs and she's finding it difficult to breathe deeply. We helped her sit up against the wall but we didn't want to move her too much because she kept wincing whenever we touched that area."
narrative_ewe: "Ŋkɔnyé nye Naa Shormeh, mefrɛ wo le Naa Akorkor tɔ me — ɖevi la xɔ accident afi sia Bolgatanga Senior High School. Ewo le da ɣli le dormitory block ƒe etɔ̃ ŋɔ va te, gake afɔ vɔ ɖe da dzi eye ewu ƒu vevie, eƒu ƒo eƒe ɖevi ŋɔ xɔ kpɔɖevimegbe. Eɖo edzi la, tso nu gblɔm kpɔ, gake eyi nam vevie ɖe eƒe ɣleti ƒe agbagbawo ŋɔ — ɖevi ŋɔ xɔ, eɖe eƒu agbe didi la vevie le eŋu. Míakpɔ ɖe ŋue míaŋlɔ eya ɖe gbɔ gbã, gake míanɔ ka mia mɔ na nu le eya ƒe ablɔɖi ŋɔ o, elabena nusiwo ŋu nya la, nane míakpɔ eya ŋɔ la, eda le ŋugbogbo."
narrative_ga: Hɛloo, mi nyɛ Naa Shormeh. Mifre shɛ Naa Akorkor ji accident aba wɔ Bolgatanga Senior High School. Obi nɔ staircase ji obumo — obi fɛɛ upper floor dormitory block ji, oko step kɛ obumo, nɔ eshi kɛ side kɛ back ji gbɛɛ. Oni le, otumi kasa, kɛ ni hii ɔ wɔ ribs ji lɛ — ehii mi anɔ gbi wɔ. Kɛ obi she breathe deep deep nɔ oho lɛ, otumi o. Mɛbɔ oshi kɛ wall ji, kɛ amɛ mii — kɛ oshɛ oho, amɛ touch lɛ ɔ wɔ place lɛ ji, oni winch gbɛɛ. Chale, frɛ ambulance sɔɔ! Boa mi!
narrative_dagbani: "Allo, m yɛligu Naa Shormeh. N bɛ yɛlim ni m dohi Naa Akorkor o gbini accident Bolgatanga Senior High School ka. O piin nyiɣi bɔŋ so, dorm yili ka biɛgu ka, ka o nyiɣi ka kuri tiim yini ka o kpɛŋ, ka o gbini yɛliŋ ka yɛliŋ — ka tiŋ nuu kpaɣa ka zuu ka. O nyɛ o maa ni o tuma yɛligu, n'o yɛlim la ka yɛligu ka o digindi kpaɣa bee o daa — ka yɛligu paai o daa, ka o bɛ tumi nyɛli daa zoli ti yɛligu. Tɔɣim o ni doli o ni yuu ni taa gbariɣu, ama n ti bori ni ti ni o kpaɣa paai baa, bala ka ti timdi o yɛliŋ, ka o yɛla ka nyunni ka o ni nyunni n'o bɛ bɛri la. N bori la n ti fre ambulance naa n yi maa sɔŋ kpeeŋ kpeeŋ!"
narrative_fante: Me din de Naa Shormeh. Merefrɛ wo fa me adamfo Naa Akorkor ho, na accident bi atoa no wɔ Bolgatanga Senior High School ha. Ɔresiw fiam wɔ dormitory block no mu stairs no so — na ɔnhuu stairs baako so yiye, ɔtuu fam, na ɔkohwe ne nkyɛn ne ne akyi ase yiye paa. Ɔhunuhunu na otumi kasa, nanso ɔreka se yaw kɛse wɔ ne nteaseɛ ho, na ɔntumi nhome yiye nso. Yɛde no tenaa ɔfasu ho, nanso yennpɛ sɛ yɛsesa no paa — bere biara a yɛka ne siw no ho no, na ɔde ne ho hyɛ mu.
narrative_gurene: M yɩɩrɛ Naa Shormeh. M bee ɣɛrɛ Naa Akorkor yɛllɛ — o accident wʋm a Bolgatanga Senior High School ka bɛ. O bee ka zɔŋɔ ka bɔɔlɩ ka waa — o bee suɣuta dormitory zɔŋɔ poore kam, ka o nyɛm fandaa ka o kpɛŋ fam naa, ka o sɔrɩ poore la a bʋr. O bee nyɛ la, o tʋma ɣɛrɛ, la ka o yɛligu ka o bʋr tɩɩm tɛɛma, ka o bʋrɩ sɩŋ ziim naa kɛɛ o mɔɣa. Ti sɔŋ o zuɣ ka o tɩɩ gaaŋa dɛɛma, la ka ti ko pɔrɩ o taŋ naa, bʋrɩ ka ti too o yɛligu kaa waat o bʋr.

narrative_en: "Please, I'm calling because something serious happened at Mfantsipim School here in Cape Coast — my name is Mensah, I'm a house prefect. Some of the senior boys got into a bad fight in the dormitory, and Nii Kwei was beaten quite badly, mostly around his stomach and ribs area. He's breathing but he's hunched over and wincing anytime someone tries to touch his side. We're not sure if something is broken inside but he doesn't look right at all."
narrative_ewe: Meɖekuku, mefɔ wò numerowo афе ŋu, elabena nusi vɔvɔ ɖeka wɔ ɖe fika Mfantsipim School me ɖe Cape Coast — nyenye Mensah, menya house prefect nyе. Senior boys ɖeɖeka wɔ dɔkui ŋutɔ ŋutɔ ɖe dormitory me, eye Nii Kwei xɔ dɔwɔwɔ vɔvɔ, abɔ kple ɖevi ŋɔ ƒe ƒu la nyuie. Edzi ƒu ame, gake eɖe edzi ɖo ta, eye afi si ame gbɔ amewo yi be woawɔ ame wu ɖe ɣeyiɣi dzi la, eyi nam ŋutɔ. Mida abe be nusi vɔ le nu me ɔ, gake eƒu amegbe nyuie o.
narrative_ga: Ofainɛ, mɛ frɛ because something serious happen Mfantsipim School Cape Coast — mi din na Mensah, mɛ be house prefect. Senior boys bi gba fight bad paa dormitory, Nii Kwei eba beaten no kɛ mi, especially shi kɛ ribs area ŋ. Ehe hɔmɔ, shi gbe, kɛ anytime obiara kyɛ touch side ŋ, ewincing paa. Yɛ nyɛ shia whether something break inside, shi gbe, kɛ ena ni good o chale — enyɛ oshiashia o.
narrative_dagbani: N daa, m frɛ ka zaɣa din gbɛya n bɛ Mfantsipim School, Cape Coast — m yuli di Mensah, m tuma house prefect. Dɔɣbihi dima kparanhi gbɛya yɛligu kum din bee dormitory, ka Nii Kwei o nyɛ yɛligu daa — pam kɔɣli ni o dindin soli niŋ. O nyɛ niŋ shɛli, ama o yuli kuli dab' o mɔɣli, ka a ban n gbɛ o tiŋdi o pam kɔbga, o kaya yɛligu kpeŋ. Ti nan bɛ soli saa din zol' pam kɔbga tɔɣa, ama o nyɛla maa kuri ni n nyɛ sumani.
narrative_fante: Me pa wo kyew, merefrɛ efisɛ biribi den bi asi Mfantsipim School ha Cape Coast — me din de Mensah, meyɛ house prefect. Senior boys bi bɛyɛɛ efuw den wɔ dormitory mu, na wɔbubuiɛ Nii Kwei yiye — na ɛyɛɛ ne yafunu ne ne sɛnkyerɛnna ho a ɛyɛ dɛn. Ɔrehome, nanso ɔkurukuru ne ho, na sɛ obi bɛdwen ne nkyɛn a, ɔsuro na ne ho sɛ ɛyɛ no yaw. Yɛnnim sɛ biribi broken wɔ ne mu anaa, nanso ne nkyɛn nyinaa, ɔntee koraa.
narrative_gurene: "Please, I'm calling because something serious happened at Mfantsipim School here in Cape Coast — my name is Mensah, I'm a house prefect. Some of the senior boys got into a bad fight in the dormitory, and Nii Kwei was beaten quite badly, mostly around his stomach and ribs area. He's breathing but he's hunched over and wincing anytime someone tries to touch his side. We're not sure if something is broken inside but he doesn't look right at all."

narrative_en: "Please, I'm calling from KNUST campus in Kumasi — my name is Owusu, I'm on the hall football team.
